succession.en: Mostly hereditary, confirmed by tribal consensus, usually passing within a chieftain's family

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构；治理由各聚落首领、长老与祭祀权威共同进行，较大聚落可统辖周边小村并形成早期酋邦
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; governance was conducted by village leaders, elders, and ritual authorities, while larger settlements could dominate nearby hamlets and form early chiefdoms

function.zh: 组织水稻种植、维护灌溉沟渠、协调播种与收获
function.en: Organized rice cultivation, maintained irrigation channels, and coordinated planting and harvest

function.zh: 管理谷仓、征集稻米和劳役、分配储备粮
function.en: Managed granaries, collected rice tribute and labor obligations, and distributed stored grain

function.zh: 主持祖灵与农耕祭仪，保管礼器，维持首领权威的神圣性
function.en: Oversaw ancestral and agricultural rites, kept ritual implements, and upheld the sacred legitimacy of leaders

function.zh: 组织村落防御、壕沟警戒、武器保管与动员青壮年
function.en: Organized village defense, ditch and palisade watch, weapon storage, and mobilization of able-bodied men

function.zh: 管理盐、玉、金属器与陶器交换，协调冶炼与手工业生产
function.en: Managed exchange in salt, jade, metal goods, and pottery, and coordinated metallurgy and craft production

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理单位为聚落与环壕村；若干村落可能臣属于较大中心聚落，由酋长亲族、长老和祭司共同管理
localAdmin.en: The basic administrative unit was the settlement or moated village; several villages might be subordinate to a larger central settlement, governed jointly by the chieftain's kin, elders, and ritual specialists

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法、亲族调解和酋长裁断为主，重视土地、水源、婚姻、血仇与贡赋义务的调停
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on custom, kin mediation, and chieftain judgment, with emphasis on settling disputes over land, water, marriage, blood-feuds, and tribute obligations

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一货币税制，主要以稻米、布帛、渔猎产品、手工制品和劳役形式向酋长或中心聚落进贡
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system; obligations were rendered chiefly as rice, cloth, fishing and hunting products, craft goods, and labor service to chieftains or central settlements

religion.zh: 以自然崇拜、祖灵信仰和农耕祭祀为核心，祭仪与丰收、土地、水源和共同体安宁密切相关
religion.en: Religion centered on nature worship, ancestral belief, and agricultural ritual, closely tied to harvests, land, water, and communal well-being

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系哲学传统，世界观主要体现为祖先敬奉、群体和谐、祭祀秩序与对自然循环的理解
philosophy.en: There was not yet a formal philosophical tradition; the worldview was expressed through ancestor veneration, communal harmony, ritual order, and an understanding of natural cycles

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字系统，信息依赖口述传统、记忆、象征器物与仪式传承
writingSystem.en: There was no native writing system; information depended on oral tradition, memory, symbolic objects, and ritual transmission

culturalAchievements.zh: 推广水稻农业、发展环壕聚落与高床仓、制作弥生陶器，并吸收来自朝鲜半岛和中国大陆的青铜与铁器技术因素
culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included the spread of wet-rice agriculture, the development of moated settlements and raised-floor granaries, the production of Yayoi pottery, and the adoption of bronze and iron technological influences from the Korean Peninsula and mainland China

languageFamily.zh: 前日语族或早期日语系成分为主，可能与列岛既有语言成分并存
languageFamily.en: Primarily proto-Japonic or early Japonic speech, possibly coexisting with earlier linguistic elements in the archipelago

unit.en: kg of rice equivalent per year
kg of rice equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以湿地稻作农业为核心，辅以粟、黍、豆类种植、渔猎、采集和家庭手工业。总体产出仍属早期农业社会水平，但相较绳文晚期已出现更稳定的余粮与区域交换网络。
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on wet-rice agriculture, supplemented by millet, beans, fishing, hunting, gathering, and household craft production. Overall output remained at an early agrarian level, but compared with the late Jomon period it showed more stable surpluses and broader regional exchange networks

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻种植、旱作农业、渔业、制陶、纺织、木工、青铜器与初期铁器加工、盐产与区域交换
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice farming, dry-field agriculture, fishing, pottery, weaving, woodworking, bronze working, early iron processing, salt production, and regional exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、盐、鱼干、贝类、布帛、木器、陶器、玉石饰物、青铜器、铁制工具原料与半岛输入品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, dried fish, shell products, cloth, wooden goods, pottery, jade ornaments, bronze goods, iron tool materials, and imported items from the peninsula

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为稻田使用权、储粮数量、农具、织物、居所质量以及是否拥有青铜礼器或铁制工具
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in rights to rice fields, size of grain stores, farm tools, textiles, quality of housing, and possession of bronze ritual goods or iron tools

unit.en: kg of rice equivalent per person per year
kg of rice equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给农业为主，兼具酋长控制的余粮再分配和有限的跨海交换，属于早期贡纳与互惠并存的经济体系
economicSystem.en: The system was based on village self-sufficient agriculture, combined with chieftain-controlled redistribution of surplus and limited maritime exchange, forming an early economy of tribute and reciprocity

unit.en: kg of rice equivalent per year
kg of rice equivalent per year
kg of rice equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非中央金库，而是分散于各中心聚落的谷仓储备、礼器库存、盐与工具储藏，主要用于灾年调剂、结盟馈赠与组织防务
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a central vault but a dispersed stock of granaries, ritual objects, salt, and tools held in major settlements, used mainly for famine relief, alliance gifts, and defense

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以储粮优先、维持水利和聚落安全为主；在丰年积累余粮，在歉年进行再分配，以增强酋长权威与社会稳定
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice prioritized food storage, irrigation upkeep, and settlement security; surpluses were accumulated in good years and redistributed in poor ones to reinforce chieftain authority and social stability

description.zh: 由中心聚落维持的常备武装，负责守卫谷仓、壕沟、栅栏与酋长居所
description.en: A small permanent armed force maintained by major settlements to guard granaries, moats, palisades, and chieftain residences

description.zh: 由农民与青壮年组成，战时临时动员，使用木矛、弓箭、石器与少量金属武器
description.en: Composed of farmers and able-bodied men, mobilized in wartime and armed with wooden spears, bows, stone tools, and limited metal weapons

description.zh: 负责河口、海湾和渡海航路的警戒，可兼任运输与联络任务
description.en: Responsible for watching estuaries, bays, and sea crossings, and could also serve in transport and communications

commanderInChief.zh: 各地酋长分别统率本部武装
commanderInChief.en: Each local chieftain commanded his own armed following

technology.zh: 武备以木矛、弓箭、石制或骨制器具为主，并开始出现青铜兵器与少量铁制工具、刀具；防御工事包括环壕、土垒与木栅
technology.en: Military equipment was based mainly on wooden spears, bows, and stone or bone implements, with the early appearance of bronze weapons and limited iron tools and blades; defensive works included moats, earthen banks, and wooden palisades

unit.en: kg of rice equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自列岛内部聚落竞争、土地与水利争夺，以及与朝鲜半岛南部海上往来的不稳定因素，而非大规模外来征服
threats.en: The main threats came from internal competition among settlements, disputes over land and irrigation, and instability in maritime contacts with the southern Korean Peninsula rather than large-scale foreign conquest

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于北九州、濑户内海沿岸与部分本州西部平原地带，稻作扩散带动聚落增多与人口上升，但地区分布仍不均衡
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated mainly in northern Kyushu, the Seto Inland Sea coasts, and parts of the western Honshu plains; the spread of rice farming increased settlement numbers and population, though distribution remained uneven

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括地方酋长与亲族上层、祭祀者、武装随从、自由农民、渔民、工匠及少量依附人口；分层正在形成，但尚未达到成熟国家社会程度
socialClasses.en: Social classes included local chieftains and elite kin, ritual specialists, armed retainers, free farmers, fishers, artisans, and a small dependent population; stratification was emerging but had not yet reached the level of a mature state society

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友；部分北九州酋邦与朝鲜半岛南部部落共同体保持通婚、技术传播与交换关系
allies.en: There were no unified external allies; some northern Kyushu chiefdoms maintained marriage, technology-transfer, and exchange ties with tribal communities in the southern Korean Peninsula

enemies.zh: 无统一国家级敌人，主要对手是邻近竞争酋邦与争夺资源的聚落集团
enemies.en: There was no single state-level enemy; the main rivals were neighboring competing chiefdoms and settlement groups contesting resources

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以近距离海上交流、婚盟、物资交换和有限冲突并存，重点在获取稻作技术、金属器与维持航道安全
foreignPolicy.en: External relations combined short-range maritime exchange, marriage alliances, material trade, and limited conflict, with emphasis on acquiring rice-growing knowledge, metal goods, and maintaining safe sea routes

keyInnovations.zh: 湿田稻作、灌溉沟渠、高床仓、环壕聚落、弥生陶器，以及经朝鲜半岛传入的青铜器和初期铁器使用
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included wet-field rice cultivation, irrigation channels, raised-floor granaries, moated settlements, Yayoi pottery, and the use of bronze and early iron introduced through the Korean Peninsula

strengths.zh: 稻作农业显著提升粮食稳定性，聚落组织能力增强，区域交换网络逐渐形成，外来技术吸收速度较快
strengths.en: Wet-rice agriculture greatly improved food stability, settlement organization was strengthening, regional exchange networks were taking shape, and foreign technologies were adopted relatively quickly

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，缺乏统一政权与文字行政体系，对气候、洪涝、歉收和地方冲突较为脆弱
weaknesses.en: Politics were highly fragmented, with no unified state or written administrative system, leaving society vulnerable to climate shifts, floods, poor harvests, and local conflict

outlook.zh: 在500 BCE时弥生社会总体稳定，并处于扩张与整合早期阶段；若稻作继续传播并且区域首领实力增强，未来数世纪可能发展出更强大的酋邦和早期国家形态
outlook.en: In 500 BCE Yayoi society was broadly stable and in an early phase of expansion and integration; if rice farming continued to spread and regional leaders grew stronger, the coming centuries could see the rise of more powerful chiefdoms and early state forms

description.zh: 日本列岛进入弥生早期，稻作扩散并形成多个地方性首领社会。
description.en: Japan was in the early Yayoi period, with wet-rice agriculture spreading among local chieftain-led societies.

name.zh: 辰韩以前的朝鲜半岛部落联盟与农耕社群
name.en: Peninsular Tribal Confederacies and Agrarian Communities of Korea

governmentForm.zh: 由多个部落、村落联盟和区域性酋邦构成的分散部落政治体系
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal political system composed of multiple tribes, village leagues, and regional chiefdoms

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族和村社为核心，上层为部落首领、战士贵族与祭司，下层为自由农民、工匠、渔猎者及少量依附人口
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on kinship groups and village communities, with tribal leaders, warrior elites, and ritual specialists at the top, and free farmers, artisans, fishers, hunters, and a small dependent population below

rulingClass.zh: 各地酋长家族、武士首领与主持祭祀的长老集团
rulingClass.en: Local chieftain lineages, warrior leaders, and councils of ritual elders

succession.zh: 通常为世袭继承，但需获得宗族长老、战士集团与附属村落的承认
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary, but required acceptance by clan elders, warrior groups, and dependent villages

structure.zh: 不存在统一中央政府，政治权力分布在河谷聚落、山间据点与沿海社群；较大的酋邦通过盟誓、婚姻和贡赋联系周边小部落
structure.en: There was no unified central government; political authority was distributed among river-valley settlements, hilltop strongholds, and coastal communities. Larger chiefdoms linked smaller tribes through oath alliances, marriage ties, and tribute

function.zh: 协调联盟决策、处理争端、确认继承与战争动员
function.en: Coordinated confederacy decisions, settled disputes, confirmed succession, and organized war mobilization

function.zh: 征集谷物、布帛、盐、鱼获与手工业品，并在祭祀和危机时重新分配
function.en: Collected grain, cloth, salt, fish, and craft goods, and redistributed them during rituals and crises

function.zh: 维持首领亲兵、组织村社民兵、守卫山城与交通通道
function.en: Maintained the chief's retainers, organized village militia, and guarded hill-forts and routes

function.zh: 主持祖先祭祀、季节仪式、农业时令与占卜活动
function.en: Oversaw ancestor rites, seasonal ceremonies, agricultural timing, and divination

function.zh: 组织修筑木栅、壕沟、仓屋、灌溉设施与道路
function.en: Organized construction of palisades, ditches, storehouses, irrigation works, and roads

function.zh: 管理与辽东、山东沿海及日本列岛社群的贸易和使节往来
function.en: Managed trade and emissary contact with Liaodong, the Shandong coast, and communities in the Japanese archipelago

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理以村落首领、宗族长老和区域酋长为核心，缺乏成文官僚体系；行政多依靠口头命令、盟约和习惯义务执行
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on village heads, clan elders, and regional chiefs, without a formal written bureaucracy. Governance was carried out through oral orders, alliance obligations, and customary duties

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，重视血缘赔偿、盟约、祭誓和首领仲裁；对盗窃、叛盟、杀伤与侵犯耕地有明确惩处传统
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, emphasizing kin compensation, oath pacts, ritual sanction, and judgment by chiefs. There were established penalties for theft, alliance-breaking, bodily harm, and encroachment on farmland

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一货币税制，主要以实物贡赋、劳役、军事服役与节庆献纳为主；较强部落还向附属聚落征收谷物、布匹、青铜器料和盐
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform monetary tax system; obligations consisted mainly of tribute in kind, labor service, military duty, and ceremonial offerings. Stronger tribes also drew grain, cloth, bronze materials, and salt from dependent settlements

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、自然神灵、山川祭祀和萨满式仪式为核心，祭祀活动与农业周期、战争和首领权威紧密相关
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor worship, nature spirits, mountain and river rites, and shamanic-style rituals, closely tied to agricultural cycles, warfare, and chiefly authority

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成如中原诸子般系统哲学，社会观念以祖训、部落习俗、长老智慧与天地神灵秩序为主
philosophy.en: No formal philosophy comparable to the Chinese schools had yet formed; social thought was grounded in ancestral precedent, tribal custom, elder wisdom, and the sacred order of heaven and earth

writingSystem.zh: 尚无本地成熟文字系统，信息依赖口传、记忆、刻画符号和器物标记；与中国接触地区可能已知晓部分外来符号传统
writingSystem.en: There was no mature native writing system; information relied on oral transmission, memory, incised signs, and object marks. Areas in contact with China may already have had limited awareness of foreign script traditions

culturalAchievements.zh: 形成支石墓文化、磨制石器与早期青铜器并行的物质传统，发展干栏或半地穴式居住、谷物贮藏、祭祀遗迹与区域性山城聚落
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included the dolmen tradition, a material culture combining polished stone tools and early bronze objects, pit-dwellings or raised structures, grain storage systems, ritual sites, and regional hill-fort settlements

languageFamily.zh: 语言状况不确定，可能属于朝鲜半岛早期土著语言群，与后世韩语前身存在复杂而未明的联系
languageFamily.en: The linguistic situation is uncertain, likely involving early indigenous peninsular language groups with complex and unclear connections to the later ancestor of Korean

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性农业为主，辅以渔猎、采集、盐产、纺织和有限青铜手工业。区域间交换正在扩大，但整体仍属低复杂度的部落农业经济
gdpDescription.en: The economy was dominated by subsistence agriculture, supplemented by fishing, hunting, gathering, salt production, weaving, and limited bronze craftwork. Interregional exchange was growing, but the overall system remained a low-complexity tribal agrarian economy

mainIndustries.zh: 粟黍与早期稻作农业、豆类种植、渔业、制盐、纺织、陶器、木作、石器和少量青铜器制作
mainIndustries.en: Millet and early rice agriculture, bean cultivation, fishing, salt-making, textile production, pottery, woodworking, stone tools, and limited bronze manufacture

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、盐、鱼干、贝饰、玉石与石材制品、布匹、陶器、青铜小器、兽皮与木材
tradeGoods.en: Grain, salt, dried fish, shell ornaments, jade and stone goods, cloth, pottery, small bronze items, hides, and timber

unitName.zh: 以谷物、布匹与青铜器物折算
unitName.en: Valued in grain, cloth, and bronze objects

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、粮仓储量、家畜、青铜礼器或武器、织物与宗族网络；贫富差距已出现，但尚未达到成熟国家的高度集中
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in access to arable land, stored grain, livestock, bronze ritual or weapon items, textiles, and clan connections. Inequality existed, but wealth was less concentrated than in mature states

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、酋长再分配和礼物交换为核心的部落再分配经济，市场性质弱，长距离贸易主要服务于威望物资和稀缺资源流通
economicSystem.en: A tribal redistributive economy centered on village production, chiefly redistribution, and gift exchange, with weak market features; long-distance trade mainly served the movement of prestige goods and scarce resources

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金银，而是分散于各酋长仓、祭祀仓和山城储藏点的谷物、盐、布匹、青铜器与威望物资储备
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a centralized stock of precious metal, but a dispersed reserve of grain, salt, cloth, bronze goods, and prestige items held in chiefly granaries, ritual stores, and hill-fort depots

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在于保证谷物储备、维系追随者忠诚、资助祭祀与应对歉收，政策偏保守，强调储藏和再分配而非持续扩张性征敛
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining grain reserves, sustaining follower loyalty, funding rituals, and coping with poor harvests. It was conservative, emphasizing storage and redistribution rather than continuous extractive expansion

description.zh: 由首领直属战士构成，装备较好，负责护卫、突袭与指挥民兵
description.en: Composed of warriors directly attached to chiefs, better equipped than common fighters, serving as guards, raiders, and leaders of militia

description.zh: 战时由农民和村社男子动员，使用矛、短弓、石镞或青铜兵器，承担主力作战和守卫任务
description.en: Mobilized in wartime from farmers and village men, using spears, short bows, stone or bronze points, and carrying the main burden of fighting and defense

description.zh: 负责驻守高地据点、河谷通道与沿海警戒，也承担联络和侦察任务
description.en: Responsible for holding elevated strongpoints, river corridors, and coastal watch posts, while also acting as messengers and scouts

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各区域酋长在联盟战争中推举主帅
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; in confederate warfare a leading chief was chosen as war leader

technology.zh: 军事技术以木栅、壕沟、山城、矛、弓、石镞和少量青铜兵器为主，尚未形成大规模骑兵或成熟铁制军备体系
technology.en: Military technology relied on palisades, ditches, hill-forts, spears, bows, stone points, and limited bronze weapons, without large cavalry forces or a mature iron military system

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部落间冲突、资源竞争、沿海与海峡地带争夺，以及来自辽东方向的外来压力和迁徙波动
threats.en: Major threats came from intertribal conflict, competition over resources, struggles over coastal and strait zones, and external pressure or migratory shifts from the Liaodong direction

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布在西北与西南河谷平原、南部沿海盆地及部分内陆盆地，聚落规模普遍较小，但若干区域已出现数千至上万人的中心性聚落
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across northwestern and southwestern river plains, southern coastal basins, and some inland basins. Most settlements were small, but several regions had already developed central places with populations in the thousands to low tens of thousands

socialClasses.zh: 主要包括酋长家族、武士与祭司、自由农民、工匠与渔盐生产者，以及依附于强宗族或首领的从属人口
socialClasses.en: The main social classes included chiefly families, warriors and ritual specialists, free farmers, artisans and fish-salt producers, and dependent people attached to stronger clans or chiefs

allies.zh: 无稳定的统一盟国，对外关系以邻近部落联盟、海峡对岸社群和辽东沿海交换伙伴为主
allies.en: There were no stable unified allies; external relations centered on neighboring tribal leagues, communities across the straits, and exchange partners along the Liaodong coast

enemies.zh: 无长期统一敌国，敌对对象多为邻近竞争部落、争夺渔盐资源的沿海社群，以及偶发的北方外来势力
enemies.en: There was no permanent unified enemy; hostile relations were mostly with neighboring rival tribes, coastal groups competing over fish and salt resources, and occasional northern intruding forces

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持贸易通道、平衡部落关系、通过婚盟与礼物巩固联盟为主；尚未形成成熟国家式对外战略
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining trade routes, balancing tribal relations, and reinforcing alliances through marriage and gift exchange; no fully developed state-style grand strategy yet existed

era.zh: 轴心时代、朝鲜半岛青铜时代晚期向早期铁器时代过渡阶段
era.en: Axial Age, a transitional phase from the Late Bronze Age toward the Early Iron Age on the Korean Peninsula

keyInnovations.zh: 支石墓建筑、青铜短剑传统、磨制石器与复合农具、谷仓储藏、水田和旱田并行耕作、山城与木栅防御体系
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included dolmen construction, the bronze dagger tradition, polished stone tools with composite farming implements, granary storage, mixed wet-rice and dry-field cultivation, and hill-fort with palisade defensive systems

strengths.zh: 社会组织具有较强地方韧性，农业、渔盐和山海资源互补明显，祭祀和亲族网络有助于维持内部稳定，区域交换正在增长
strengths.en: The society had strong local resilience, with clear complementarity between agriculture, fishing-salt production, and mountain-coastal resources. Ritual and kinship networks helped maintain internal stability, and regional exchange was expanding

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，缺乏统一文字、常备官僚与成熟税制，军事协调有限，对气候波动、歉收和外来压力的应对能力不均衡
weaknesses.en: Politics were highly fragmented, lacking a unified script, standing bureaucracy, and mature tax system. Military coordination was limited, and responses to climate stress, crop failure, and outside pressure were uneven

outlook.zh: 在公元前后数世纪内，随着铁器传播、稻作扩展、对华交流增强和区域权力整合推进，这些部落联盟有望逐步演化为更稳定的酋邦和早期国家形态
outlook.en: Over the following centuries, with the spread of iron, the expansion of rice farming, increased contact with China, and stronger regional consolidation, these tribal confederacies were likely to evolve into more stable chiefdoms and early state formations

description.zh: 朝鲜半岛此时尚未形成后世三国，主要由早期农业社群与部族联盟组成。
description.en: The Korean peninsula had not yet formed the later Three Kingdoms and was dominated by early agrarian communities and tribal confederacies.

governmentForm.zh: 君主集权的多民族帝国，依托总督区体系治理
governmentForm.en: A centralized multiethnic monarchy governed through a satrapal system

socialStructure.zh: 帝国社会由王室与波斯贵族、各地总督与军政官员、祭司与书吏、城市商人与手工业者、自由农民、附庸社群以及奴隶与战俘构成；各民族在帝国框架下保留相当程度的地方习俗与宗教传统。
socialStructure.en: Imperial society consisted of the royal house and Persian nobility, satraps and administrative officers, priests and scribes, urban merchants and artisans, free peasants, dependent communities, and slaves or captives; many peoples retained substantial local customs and religious traditions within the imperial framework.

rulingClass.zh: 阿契美尼德王族、波斯与米底大贵族、总督、高级军官及宫廷官僚
rulingClass.en: The Achaemenid royal house, Persian and Median great nobles, satraps, senior military officers, and court administrators

succession.zh: 名义上为王室世袭，由在位君主指定继承人并依靠宫廷、贵族与军队支持确认
succession.en: Nominally hereditary within the royal house, with the reigning king designating an heir whose accession was secured through support from the court, nobility, and army

structure.zh: 帝国由国王统治，中央宫廷掌控财政、军务、道路通信与王室地产；地方划分为多个总督区，由总督、军事统帅与财政官相互制衡，另有“王之眼与耳”巡察体系监督地方。
structure.en: The empire was ruled by the king, while the central court controlled finance, military affairs, roads and communications, and royal estates; the provinces were divided into satrapies, where satraps, military commanders, and financial officers checked one another, and a system of royal inspectors, the 'King's Eyes and Ears,' monitored local administration.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保管文书、使用阿拉米文及其他行政语言处理帝国政务
function.en: Drafted decrees, maintained archives, and handled imperial administration in Imperial Aramaic and other administrative languages

function.zh: 征收各总督区贡赋、管理金银储备、核算王室收入与支出
function.en: Collected tribute from satrapies, managed gold and silver reserves, and accounted for royal income and expenditure

function.zh: 维护御道、驿站与信使系统，保障军政命令快速传递
function.en: Maintained royal roads, relay stations, and the courier system to ensure rapid transmission of military and administrative orders

function.zh: 管理禁卫军、各地驻军调动、军粮补给与兵员登记
function.en: Managed the royal guard, deployment of provincial garrisons, military supplies, and troop registers

function.zh: 管理王室土地、宫殿工程、灌溉设施及征发劳役与工匠
function.en: Managed royal lands, palace construction, irrigation works, and the mobilization of laborers and craftsmen

localAdmin.zh: 地方以总督区为核心治理单元，下辖旧有王国、城邦、神庙地产和部族区域。总督负责民政与征赋，军事指挥与财政监督常由独立官员分掌，以防地方坐大。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on satrapies, which encompassed former kingdoms, city-states, temple estates, and tribal zones. Satraps handled civil administration and tribute collection, while military command and fiscal supervision were often entrusted to separate officials to prevent excessive provincial autonomy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系兼具王令、波斯习惯法与各地传统法。帝国通常允许巴比伦、埃及、犹大和小亚细亚等地区继续依本地法审理民事与宗教事务，重大叛乱、贡赋与军政案件则由王权直接裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decree, Persian customary law, and local legal traditions. The empire generally allowed Babylonia, Egypt, Judah, and Anatolian regions to continue using local law for civil and religious matters, while major cases involving rebellion, tribute, and military affairs were judged directly under royal authority.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以总督区定额贡赋、实物徭役、关税、道路税、王室地产收入与附属民族贡纳组成。大流士一世对各区贡额进行制度化与标准化，是帝国财政稳定的重要基础。
taxationSystem.en: The taxation system consisted of fixed satrapal tribute quotas, payments in kind and labor obligations, customs duties, road tolls, income from royal estates, and tribute from subject peoples. Darius I regularized and standardized provincial assessments, forming a major basis of imperial fiscal stability.

religion.zh: 王室意识形态以阿胡拉·马兹达崇拜为核心，体现早期祆教化倾向，但帝国整体采取宗教宽容政策，容纳巴比伦、埃及、犹太、安纳托利亚与伊朗各地信仰。
religion.en: Royal ideology centered on the worship of Ahura Mazda, reflecting an early Zoroastrianizing tendency, but the empire as a whole pursued religious tolerance, accommodating Babylonian, Egyptian, Jewish, Anatolian, and Iranian cults.

philosophy.zh: 帝国思想强调王权的正当性、秩序与真理对抗谎言，以及维护天下安定的普世君权观；同时吸收美索不达米亚、埃及与伊朗传统的伦理和宇宙秩序观念。
philosophy.en: Imperial thought emphasized the legitimacy of kingship, the struggle of order and truth against the lie, and a universal kingship responsible for maintaining stability; it also absorbed ethical and cosmic-order concepts from Mesopotamian, Egyptian, and Iranian traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 宫廷碑铭使用古波斯楔形文字，行政系统广泛使用帝国阿拉米文，此外巴比伦楔形文字、埃及文字与希腊字母在地方仍并行使用。
writingSystem.en: Court inscriptions used Old Persian cuneiform, while the administrative system relied heavily on Imperial Aramaic; Babylonian cuneiform, Egyptian scripts, and the Greek alphabet also remained in local use.

culturalAchievements.zh: 大流士时期的文化成就包括波斯波利斯宫殿群建设、贝希斯敦铭文、标准化行政文书体系、王家御道网络以及融合伊朗、美索不达米亚、埃及和安纳托利亚风格的宫廷艺术。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements under Darius included the construction of the Persepolis palace complex, the Behistun Inscription, a standardized administrative documentary system, the royal road network, and court art blending Iranian, Mesopotamian, Egyptian, and Anatolian styles.

languageFamily.zh: 统治族群语言属印欧语系伊朗语族；行政通用语阿拉米文属闪米特语族
languageFamily.en: The ruling population spoke an Indo-European Iranian language; the principal administrative lingua franca, Aramaic, belonged to the Semitic family

gdpDescription.zh: 帝国经济在公元前500年前后处于古代世界顶峰之一，依托两河流域、埃及与安纳托利亚等高产农业区，以及横跨欧亚非的税赋和贸易网络。其财富高度集中于王室、神庙、地方精英和商人阶层。
gdpDescription.en: Around 500 BCE the imperial economy stood among the strongest in the ancient world, drawing on the productive agricultural zones of Mesopotamia, Egypt, and Anatolia as well as tax and trade networks spanning Eurasia and Africa. Wealth was highly concentrated in the royal house, temples, regional elites, and merchant strata.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、谷物生产、畜牧、金银采冶、纺织、陶器、石材建筑、木材与造船、香料与奢侈品贸易、税赋征收与转运
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, grain production, pastoralism, gold and silver extraction, textiles, pottery, stone construction, timber and shipbuilding, spice and luxury trade, and tax collection and redistribution

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、枣椰、葡萄酒、亚麻、羊毛织物、地毯、金银、青金石、木材、腓尼基紫染品、埃及纸草、香料、象牙、马匹与奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Grain, dates, wine, flax, wool textiles, carpets, gold, silver, lapis lazuli, timber, Phoenician purple goods, Egyptian papyrus, spices, ivory, horses, and slaves

unitName.zh: 金币大流克、银币西格洛斯

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。乡村农户主要拥有土地使用权、牲畜、农具与粮食储备；城市富商和地方贵族则掌握银锭、货币、奴仆、仓储与远途贸易资本。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied enormously. Rural families mainly held land-use rights, livestock, tools, and grain reserves, while urban merchants and regional nobles controlled bullion, coin, servants, storage facilities, and capital for long-distance trade.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为以农业税基为核心、由王室再分配和地方市场交换并存的帝国经济。国家控制贡赋、道路、战略物资与部分铸币，而地方神庙、庄园、商团和城市市场保持活跃。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was an imperial economy centered on an agrarian tax base, combining royal redistribution with local market exchange. The state controlled tribute, roads, strategic resources, and part of coinage, while temples, estates, merchant groups, and urban markets remained highly active.

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室国库储备庞大，主要以金银锭、贵金属器、贡纳珍品与粮食储备形式保存在苏萨、埃克巴坦那、巴比伦及新建的波斯波利斯等核心据点，用于战争、工程与王室赏赐。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was substantial, stored chiefly as bullion, precious metal vessels, tribute valuables, and grain reserves in major centers such as Susa, Ecbatana, Babylon, and the newly developed Persepolis, to be used for war, construction, and royal patronage.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以稳定贡赋、保持国库积累、资助大型工程并确保边疆驻军供给为主。大流士一世重视度量衡、税额和货币标准化，以减少地方征收混乱。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on stabilizing tribute, maintaining treasury accumulation, financing major works, and ensuring supply for frontier garrisons. Darius I emphasized standardization of weights, measures, tax assessments, and coinage to reduce provincial irregularities.

description.zh: 包括“不死军”等精锐近卫及常备步兵，承担中军、防御与王室护卫任务，装备长矛、弓箭、短剑和柳条盾。
description.en: Included elite guards such as the Immortals and standing infantry, serving as the core field force and royal protectors, equipped with spears, bows, short swords, and wicker shields.

description.zh: 由各总督区按民族与地域征发，包括巴比伦、埃及、安纳托利亚与东方各地兵员，装备与战法差异很大。
description.en: Raised by the satrapies on ethnic and regional lines, including troops from Babylonia, Egypt, Anatolia, and eastern provinces, with highly varied equipment and fighting styles.

description.zh: 由波斯、米底、巴克特里亚、萨迦等地提供，是帝国机动力量核心，用于侦察、包抄、追击与边疆作战；战车在部分平原战场仍保有象征与实战功能。
description.en: Supplied by Persians, Medes, Bactrians, Saka, and others, these formed the empire's main mobile arm for reconnaissance, encirclement, pursuit, and frontier warfare; chariots still retained symbolic and occasional battlefield roles on suitable plains.

description.zh: 主要依赖腓尼基、埃及、塞浦路斯与爱奥尼亚诸地水手和船只，负责东地中海制海、运兵与补给。
description.en: Depended chiefly on Phoenician, Egyptian, Cypriot, and Ionian sailors and ships, providing control and transport capacity in the eastern Mediterranean.

commanderInChief.zh: 大流士一世（万王之王）
commanderInChief.en: Darius I (King of Kings)

technology.zh: 军事实力建立在复合弓、长矛步兵、重视机动的骑兵、攻城工程、道路后勤和多民族协同作战之上；海军技术主要来自腓尼基与爱奥尼亚传统。
technology.en: Military capability rested on composite bows, spear infantry, mobility-oriented cavalry, siege engineering, road-based logistics, and multiethnic coordination; naval technology drew primarily from Phoenician and Ionian traditions.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自爱琴海希腊城邦的反抗倾向、埃及周期性不稳、斯基泰—萨迦草原势力、帝国内部边远总督区的离心，以及西部海疆可能出现的联合叛乱。
threats.en: Major threats came from rebellious tendencies among the Greek poleis of the Aegean, recurring instability in Egypt, the Scythian-Saka steppe world, centrifugal tendencies in distant satrapies, and the possibility of coordinated unrest along the western maritime frontier.

populationDescription.zh: 帝国人口约二千五百万，为当时世界上人口最多的国家之一，涵盖伊朗高原、两河流域、尼罗河流域、小亚细亚、高加索南缘、中亚绿洲与印度河西部地区，多民族、多语言、多宗教并存。
populationDescription.en: With about 25 million people, the empire was one of the most populous states of its age, encompassing the Iranian plateau, Mesopotamia, the Nile valley, Anatolia, the southern Caucasus, Central Asian oases, and regions west of the Indus, with extensive ethnic, linguistic, and religious diversity.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室与大贵族、总督与军官、祭司与书吏、商人与手工业者、自由农民、依附劳工以及奴隶。不同地区社会结构差异明显，如巴比伦和埃及的神庙机构、小亚细亚城邦公民群体和伊朗部族贵族传统并存。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata included the royal family and high nobility, satraps and officers, priests and scribes, merchants and artisans, free peasants, dependent laborers, and slaves. Social organization varied notably by region, with Babylonian and Egyptian temple establishments, Anatolian civic communities, and Iranian aristocratic tribal traditions coexisting.

allies.zh: 帝国主要依赖附庸王、公认效忠的腓尼基诸城、塞浦路斯势力、部分安纳托利亚地方精英及若干阿拉伯与边疆部族作为合作力量，而非现代意义上的固定同盟。
allies.en: The empire relied mainly on subject kings, loyal Phoenician cities, Cypriot powers, segments of Anatolian local elites, and certain Arabian and frontier tribes as cooperative partners rather than fixed allies in the modern sense.

enemies.zh: 潜在或现实敌对者包括爱奥尼亚与爱琴海部分希腊城邦、反叛的埃及地方势力、北方斯基泰—萨迦游牧集团，以及任何试图脱离帝国控制的边远总督区。
enemies.en: Potential or actual enemies included some Ionian and Aegean Greek poleis, rebellious Egyptian local forces, northern Scythian-Saka nomadic groups, and any distant satrapy seeking to break from imperial control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以扩张后的整合与边疆稳固为重点，强调通过道路、驻军、婚姻政治、赏赐和宗教宽容将各地纳入王权秩序；在西方则逐步加强对爱琴海沿岸和海上通道的控制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on consolidating expansion and stabilizing frontiers, using roads, garrisons, marriage politics, rewards, and religious tolerance to integrate diverse regions into royal order; in the west it increasingly sought tighter control over the Aegean coast and maritime routes.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度创新包括标准化铸币体系、王家御道与驿传网络、跨区域行政文书体系、大型石造宫殿工程、灌溉与运河整修、复合弓骑兵战法以及多语种帝国治理技术。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included a standardized coinage system, the Royal Road and courier network, a transregional administrative documentary system, large-scale stone palace construction, irrigation and canal improvements, composite-bow cavalry methods, and multilingual imperial governance techniques.

strengths.zh: 帝国最大优势在于广阔疆域、高度发达的贡赋与交通体系、充足人口和资源、较成熟的总督制、强大的财政动员能力，以及对多民族与多宗教地区相对灵活的统治方式。
strengths.en: The empire's greatest strengths lay in its vast territory, highly developed tribute and transport systems, abundant population and resources, relatively mature satrapal administration, strong fiscal mobilization, and a comparatively flexible mode of rule over multiethnic and multireligious lands.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于疆域过大导致沟通与控制成本高昂，地方总督可能坐大，军队在精锐核心之外高度异质化，西部希腊沿岸和埃及等地反复不稳，王位继承也可能引发宫廷斗争。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included the high costs of communication and control over an enormous territory, the risk of overmighty satraps, a military highly heterogeneous beyond its elite core, repeated instability in regions such as the Greek coast and Egypt, and the possibility of court struggles over succession.

outlook.zh: 在公元前500年，大流士一世治下的波斯帝国前景极为强盛，短期内仍将保持近东霸权，并继续完善行政、财政与道路体系；但若西部海疆冲突升级或地方叛乱频发，帝国未来将面临更高的军事与治理压力。
outlook.en: In 500 BCE, the Persian Empire under Darius I had an exceptionally strong outlook and was likely to maintain Near Eastern supremacy in the near term while further refining its administrative, fiscal, and road systems; however, intensified conflict on the western maritime frontier or recurrent provincial revolts would impose growing military and governance pressures.

description.zh: 大流士一世统治下的波斯帝国达到鼎盛，横跨伊朗高原、两河、埃及、安纳托利亚与中亚。
description.en: Under Darius I, the Persian Empire reached a peak, spanning Iran, Mesopotamia, Egypt, Anatolia, and Central Asia.

ideology.zh: 中央集权、总督制行省管理、御道交通、琐罗亚斯德教国教地位、帝国多元统一
ideology.en: Centralized administration, satrapal provincial governance, Royal Road communications, Zoroastrian state religion, imperial unity in diversity

relationship.zh: 大王宫廷是帝国的绝对权力核心。大流士一世通过行省制、御道驿站、「王之眼与耳」巡察体系和统一度量衡货币，建立了古代世界最高效的多民族帝国治理体系。宫廷直接掌控财政、军队调度和对外战争决策，各总督区和附庸国均须向大王臣服纳贡。
relationship.en: The Great King's court was the absolute center of imperial power. Darius I established the most efficient multiethnic governance system of the ancient world through provincial satrapies, the Royal Road relay system, the 'King's Eyes and Ears' inspectors, and unified weights, measures, and coinage. The court directly controlled finance, military deployment, and foreign war decisions; all satrapies and vassal states owed submission and tribute to the Great King.

description.zh: 大流士一世在公元前522年夺取王位后，对帝国进行了全面改革。他将帝国划分为约二十至三十个总督区，建成连接苏萨至撒迪斯的御道驿站体系，统一银币（大流克）的铸造与流通，并通过王室巡察官监督各地总督。波斯波利斯的宏伟宫殿群象征着帝国的权威与各民族的朝贡秩序。大流士以阿胡拉·玛兹达之名统治，将王权与琐罗亚斯德教紧密结合，构建了从尼罗河到印度河的超级帝国。
description.en: After seizing the throne in 522 BCE, Darius I undertook comprehensive reform of the empire. He divided it into some twenty to thirty satrapies, built the Royal Road relay system connecting Susa to Sardis, standardized the minting and circulation of the silver daric, and monitored provincial governors through royal inspectors. The grand palace complex at Persepolis symbolized imperial authority and the tributary order of subject peoples. Ruling in the name of Ahura Mazda, Darius bound kingship to Zoroastrianism and constructed a superpower stretching from the Nile to the Indus.

role.zh: 帝国缔造者与改革者，建立行省制与御道体系
role.en: Imperial architect and reformer who established the satrapal system and the Royal Road

role.zh: 大流士核心亲信之一，参与推翻伪巴尔迪亚，掌管巴比伦总督区
role.en: One of Darius's core confidants, participant in overthrowing False Bardiya, governor of Babylonia

ideology.zh: 地方自治与帝国效忠并行，维护总督权威与家族利益
ideology.en: Local autonomy alongside imperial loyalty, preserving satrapal authority and family interests

relationship.zh: 总督贵族是帝国治理体系的执行层，也是大王维持广阔帝国运转不可或缺的合作伙伴。各总督在其辖区内拥有广泛的军政和司法权力，大流士通过军事长官、财政官与「王之眼与耳」巡察体系加以制衡。多数总督忠诚执行中央政令，是帝国行省治理的关键支柱。
relationship.en: The satrapal aristocracy was the executive layer of imperial governance and an indispensable partner in maintaining the vast empire's operations. Each satrap possessed broad military, administrative, and judicial powers within his province, with Darius counterbalancing them through separate military commanders, financial officers, and the 'King's Eyes and Ears' inspection system. Most satraps faithfully implemented central directives and served as crucial pillars of provincial governance.

description.zh: 阿契美尼德帝国的总督制是古代最精密的行省治理体系之一。总督（哈沙特拉帕万）由大王任命，负责征税、司法、维持秩序和地方军事动员。他们多出身波斯或米底贵族，有时为王室宗亲。总督在辖区内权倾一方，拥有私人宫廷和武装随从，部分总督家族形成了世袭化的地方势力。总督叛乱在帝国史上屡见不鲜——大流士即位初年即平定了多起总督起义。中央对总督的控制力是帝国存续的关键因素之一。
description.en: The Achaemenid satrapal system was one of the most sophisticated provincial governance structures of the ancient world. Satraps (khshathrapavan) were appointed by the Great King and responsible for tax collection, justice, maintaining order, and local military mobilization. They were typically of Persian or Median noble birth, sometimes royal kinsmen. Satraps wielded immense power in their provinces, maintaining private courts and armed retinues; some satrapal families developed into hereditary local powers. Satrapal revolts recurred throughout imperial history — Darius himself suppressed multiple uprisings upon his accession. Central control over the satraps was a critical factor in the empire's survival.

role.zh: 爱奥尼亚总督区下的希腊僭主，即将发动反波斯起义，体现总督体系下的地方叛乱风险
role.en: Greek tyrant under the Ionian satrapy, about to launch the anti-Persian revolt, exemplifying the rebellion risk within the satrapal system

leader.zh: 爱奥尼亚各城邦僭主与贵族

ideology.zh: 希腊城邦自治主义——追求希腊式城市自由与自治，抵制波斯直接行政干预，渴望恢复政治独立
ideology.en: Greek polis autonomy — pursuing Greek-style civic freedom and self-governance, resisting Persian direct administrative interference, and yearning to restore political independence

relationship.zh: 爱奥尼亚希腊城邦名义上臣服于波斯帝国，通过波斯任命的僭主治理，但对波斯统治的不满日益增长。希腊人珍视的城邦自治传统与波斯帝国的集权征税体制存在深层矛盾。叛乱的种子已经播下，公元前499年的爱奥尼亚起义即将爆发，成为希波战争的导火索。
relationship.en: The Ionian Greek cities were nominally subject to the Persian Empire, governed through Persian-appointed tyrants, but discontent with Persian rule was growing. The Greek tradition of polis autonomy stood in deep tension with the empire's centralized taxation system. The seeds of revolt had been sown, and the Ionian Revolt of 499 BCE was about to erupt, becoming the trigger for the Greco-Persian Wars.

description.zh: 爱奥尼亚希腊城邦位于小亚细亚西海岸，是希腊世界最富庶、最具文化创造力的地区之一。米利都、以弗所、萨摩斯等城市曾是希腊哲学、科学和艺术的先驱中心——泰勒斯、阿那克西曼德和赫拉克利特都出自这一地区。自公元前546年居鲁士大帝征服吕底亚后，爱奥尼亚诸城即被纳入波斯帝国版图。波斯通过在各城扶持顺从的僭主来维持控制，同时征收贡赋和征发军事劳役。然而希腊人对政治自由和城邦自治的执着追求使他们对波斯统治愈发不满。公元前500年前后，反波斯情绪达到顶峰，米利都僭主阿里斯塔戈拉斯正在策划一场大规模叛乱。这场即将爆发的爱奥尼亚起义将彻底改变希腊世界与波斯帝国的关系，开启长达半个世纪的希波战争时代。
description.en: The Ionian Greek cities on the western coast of Asia Minor were among the wealthiest and most culturally creative regions of the Greek world. Cities like Miletus, Ephesus, and Samos had been pioneering centers of Greek philosophy, science, and art — Thales, Anaximander, and Heraclitus all came from this region. Since Cyrus the Great's conquest of Lydia in 546 BCE, the Ionian cities had been incorporated into the Persian Empire. Persia maintained control by installing compliant tyrants in each city, while exacting tribute and conscripting military labor. Yet the Greek commitment to political freedom and polis autonomy made them increasingly resentful of Persian rule. By around 500 BCE, anti-Persian sentiment had reached a peak, and Aristagoras, tyrant of Miletus, was planning a major revolt. The imminent Ionian Revolt would fundamentally alter the relationship between the Greek world and the Persian Empire, inaugurating half a century of Greco-Persian warfare.

role.zh: 爱奥尼亚起义的发起者，将放弃僭主职位并号召希腊城邦联合反波斯
role.en: Instigator of the Ionian Revolt, who would resign his tyranny and call on Greek cities to unite against Persia

role.zh: 被大流士扣留在苏萨宫廷的前僭主，据传秘密鼓动阿里斯塔戈拉斯叛乱
role.en: Former tyrant detained at Darius's court in Susa, traditionally credited with secretly encouraging Aristagoras to revolt

rulerTitle.en: Pharaoh and Great King of Persia

governmentForm.zh: 波斯帝国辖下的行省殖民统治，保留法老名号与埃及地方神庙秩序
governmentForm.en: Satrapal colonial administration under the Persian Empire, retaining pharaonic titulature and the Egyptian temple order

socialStructure.zh: 社会由波斯总督与驻军、埃及祭司集团、地方贵族与书记官、城市工匠与商人、广大农民及劳役人口构成；神庙体系仍是地方社会组织核心。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the Persian satrap and garrisons, Egyptian priestly elites, local nobles and scribes, urban artisans and merchants, and a broad mass of peasants and laborers; the temple system remained the core of local social organization.

rulingClass.zh: 波斯帝国任命的总督、军政官员，以及与其合作的埃及高级祭司和地方显贵
rulingClass.en: Imperially appointed Persian satrapal and military officials together with cooperating Egyptian high priests and local notables

succession.zh: 最高统治权依阿契美尼德王朝世袭，埃及法老身份随波斯帝位转承；地方总督由皇帝任免
succession.en: Supreme authority followed hereditary Achaemenid succession, with the Egyptian pharaonic title attached to the Persian throne; the local satrap was appointed and removed by the king

structure.zh: 埃及在名义上为法老统治王国，实际上为阿契美尼德帝国的行省，由总督统辖税赋、司法、治安与军务；神庙与传统书吏机构继续承担基层登记与征收。
structure.en: Egypt was nominally a pharaonic kingdom but in practice an Achaemenid province governed by a satrap overseeing taxation, justice, order, and military affairs; temples and traditional scribal offices continued to handle local registration and collection.

function.zh: 统筹全省行政命令、与王廷往来、监督地方官与军政协调
function.en: Coordinated provincial orders, correspondence with the royal court, supervision of local officials, and civil-military coordination

function.zh: 征收谷物、白银、实物贡赋与运输配额，核算王室收入
function.en: Collected grain, silver, payments in kind, and transport quotas, and accounted for royal revenue

function.zh: 管理土地丈量、灌溉记录、神庙特权与农业产出登记
function.en: Managed land surveys, irrigation records, temple privileges, and registration of agricultural output

function.zh: 保存契约文书、主持诉讼、执行王令与地方习惯法
function.en: Preserved contracts, handled litigation, and enforced royal decrees alongside local customary law

function.zh: 管理驻防部队、尼罗河要地、三角洲要塞与通往利比亚、西亚的防线
function.en: Administered garrison troops, key Nile positions, Delta fortresses, and defenses toward Libya and Southwest Asia

function.zh: 负责尼罗河运输、国家粮仓、补给调配与工程劳役组织
function.en: Oversaw Nile transport, state granaries, supply distribution, and organization of corvée labor

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以埃及传统州县体系为基础，各州由地方官、书记和神庙网络协助征税、司法和劳役组织；波斯驻军与总督代表控制战略城市和交通节点。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on the traditional Egyptian nome system, with local officials, scribes, and temple networks assisting in taxation, justice, and labor organization; Persian garrisons and satrapal representatives controlled strategic cities and transport nodes.

legalSystem.zh: 法律实行帝国王令、总督裁断与埃及习惯法并行，土地、婚姻、继承和债务多依埃及文书传统处理，涉及叛乱、赋税和军务则受帝国强制法约束。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined imperial decree, satrapal judgment, and Egyptian customary law; land, marriage, inheritance, and debt were often handled through Egyptian documentary traditions, while rebellion, taxation, and military matters fell under stricter imperial authority.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地税、谷物征收、畜产与手工业课税、河运与贸易征费为主，并要求部分地区承担劳役与军需供给；高等级神庙和部分特许地享有减免。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on land tax, grain levies, livestock and craft dues, and riverine and trade tolls, with some districts also required to provide labor and military supplies; major temples and certain privileged lands enjoyed exemptions.

religion.zh: 传统埃及多神信仰仍占主导，阿蒙、卜塔、拉、奥西里斯、伊西斯等神崇拜持续兴盛；波斯统治总体容忍并资助部分神庙，以维持合法性。
religion.en: Traditional Egyptian polytheism remained dominant, with the cults of Amun, Ptah, Ra, Osiris, Isis, and others continuing strongly; Persian rule was generally tolerant and at times supportive of temples in order to maintain legitimacy.

philosophy.zh: 思想以玛阿特秩序观、祭司神学、实用书记官伦理和古老智慧文学传统为主，同时受帝国环境影响，接触波斯王权观念与更广泛东地中海交流。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by the concept of ma'at, priestly theology, practical scribal ethics, and the long tradition of wisdom literature, while the imperial setting brought contact with Persian ideas of kingship and wider eastern Mediterranean exchange.

writingSystem.zh: 书写并用圣书体、僧侣体和通俗行政文书；帝国层面常使用阿拉米语处理跨地区行政联络。
writingSystem.en: Writing employed hieroglyphic, hieratic, and vernacular administrative scripts; at the imperial level Aramaic was commonly used for interregional administration.

culturalAchievements.zh: 神庙建筑与修复、石碑铭文、木乃伊防腐技术、历法与测量传统、尼罗河农业管理及精细文书制度持续发展；波斯时期的王室铭文常结合埃及与帝国语言政治象征。
culturalAchievements.en: Temple building and restoration, monumental inscriptions, mummification techniques, calendrical and surveying traditions, Nile agricultural management, and sophisticated documentary practices continued to develop; royal inscriptions of the Persian period often combined Egyptian and imperial political symbolism.

languageFamily.zh: 主体语言属埃及语支的晚期埃及语；行政与帝国往来中并见阿拉米语，统治精英中有古波斯语使用
languageFamily.en: The main language was Late Egyptian within the Egyptian branch; Aramaic also appeared in administration and imperial communication, while Old Persian was used among the ruling elite

gdpDescription.zh: 经济高度依赖尼罗河农业与国家再分配体系，谷物盈余支撑大规模人口、神庙、驻军和对帝国财政的贡纳。其人均产出在古代大型农业社会中属中等偏上，但财富分配极不均衡。
gdpDescription.en: The economy depended heavily on Nile agriculture and a state redistributive system, with grain surpluses supporting a large population, temples, garrisons, and tribute to the empire. Per-capita output was moderate to moderately high for a large ancient agrarian society, though wealth distribution was highly unequal.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、亚麻与纺织、纸草加工、啤酒与面包制作、石材采掘、金属与木工手工业、河运物流、神庙经济与仓储管理
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, flax and textiles, papyrus processing, beer and bread production, stone quarrying, metal and woodworking crafts, river transport logistics, and temple-based storage and redistribution

tradeGoods.zh: 小麦、大麦、亚麻布、纸草、玻璃器、香油、金石器物、奴隶、象牙、乌木及来自非洲与阿拉伯的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Wheat, barley, linen, papyrus, glassware, oils, worked gold and stone goods, slaves, ivory, ebony, and luxury products arriving from Africa and Arabia

name.zh: 大流克与银重量货币并用

householdWealth.zh: 普通农村家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备和工具上；城市与神庙精英则积累白银、首饰、奴仆及多处地产收益。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary rural household wealth lay chiefly in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, and tools, while urban and temple elites accumulated silver, jewelry, servants, and income from multiple estates.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业税收、神庙地产、国家仓储与帝国贡纳为核心的混合再分配经济，辅以市场交换、河运贸易和跨帝国商业网络。
economicSystem.en: A mixed redistributive economy centered on agrarian taxation, temple estates, state granaries, and imperial tribute, supplemented by market exchange, river transport, and trans-imperial commercial networks.

treasuryDescription.zh: 省级财政储备以白银、谷物与贵重实物混合存在，部分留存孟斐斯与主要仓储中心，部分则定期输送帝国核心地区。
treasuryDescription.en: The provincial treasury was held as a mix of silver, grain, and valuable goods, with part retained in Memphis and major storage centers and part regularly remitted to the imperial core.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在稳定征收、保障驻军、维持水利和按时向帝国缴纳贡赋；对神庙采取拉拢与控制并行的务实方针。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed at reliable collection, support for garrisons, maintenance of irrigation, and punctual remittance of imperial tribute; toward temples it followed a pragmatic blend of conciliation and control.

description.zh: 由波斯、西亚与本地部队混编，负责要塞驻防、治安与野战主力，装备长矛、弓、盾和短剑。
description.en: Mixed Persian, Southwest Asian, and local troops serving as fortress garrisons, security forces, and the main field infantry, equipped with spears, bows, shields, and short swords.

name.zh: 埃及本地步兵与征发兵

description.zh: 主要用于守备、运粮、工程和地方防务，战斗力因地区与训练而异。
description.en: Used chiefly for local defense, supply movement, engineering, and garrison service, with effectiveness varying by region and training.

description.zh: 骑兵在波斯军事体系中更重要，承担侦察、传令和边境机动；传统战车已属次要象征与有限战术用途。
description.en: Cavalry held greater importance within the Persian system, handling reconnaissance, dispatches, and frontier mobility; traditional chariots survived only in minor symbolic and limited tactical roles.

name.zh: 尼罗河舰队与运输部队

description.zh: 利用河船与轻型舰艇执行运兵、护运、巡河和三角洲水道控制任务。
description.en: Used river craft and light vessels for troop transport, convoy protection, patrol, and control of Delta waterways.

description.zh: 驻于关键据点和边境，具较强近战与守城能力，是波斯在埃及的重要专业兵力。
description.en: Stationed at key strongpoints and frontiers, they provided strong close-combat and defensive capability and formed an important professional element of Persian power in Egypt.

commanderInChief.zh: 大流士一世为名义最高统帅，实际由埃及总督与驻军将领统辖
commanderInChief.en: Darius I was the nominal commander-in-chief, while practical command rested with the satrap of Egypt and garrison generals

technology.zh: 军事技术处于铁器时代成熟阶段，广泛使用复合弓、铁制矛头与刀剑、鳞甲和大型要塞体系，并借助帝国道路与驿传增强调动效率。
technology.en: Military technology was at a mature Iron Age level, with widespread use of composite bows, iron spearheads and swords, scale armor, and substantial fortress systems, aided by imperial road and courier networks for better mobility.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自埃及本土反波斯叛乱风险、三角洲与边境不稳、来自利比亚方向的袭扰，以及爱琴海世界局势变化可能带来的海上和雇佣兵忠诚问题。
threats.en: Major threats included the risk of anti-Persian revolt within Egypt, instability in the Delta and frontier zones, raids from the Libyan direction, and changing conditions in the Aegean that could create maritime pressure and mercenary loyalty problems.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于尼罗河谷与三角洲，农业村落密布，沙漠与边境绿洲人口稀少；孟斐斯、赛伊斯和底比斯等城市与神庙中心是主要人口节点。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Nile Valley and Delta, with dense agricultural villages and sparse settlement in deserts and frontier oases; Memphis, Sais, and Thebes, along with temple centers, were the principal population nodes.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为波斯统治者、军官、埃及祭司和地方贵族；中层为书记官、军人、商人和熟练工匠；下层为农民、佃户、劳役者与奴仆。神庙附属人口和军镇居民构成重要特殊群体。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata comprised Persian rulers, officers, Egyptian priests, and local nobles; the middle strata included scribes, soldiers, merchants, and skilled artisans; the lower strata consisted of peasants, tenants, laborers, and servants. Temple dependents and military-settlement populations formed important special groups.

allies.zh: 作为阿契美尼德帝国的一部分，其盟友即帝国所属诸行省、腓尼基沿海城邦及受波斯控制的近东地区。
allies.en: As part of the Achaemenid Empire, its allies were effectively the empire’s other provinces, the Phoenician coastal cities, and Persian-controlled Near Eastern territories.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌对力量包括反波斯的埃及地方势力、部分希腊城邦，以及不时威胁边境交通的利比亚与沙漠部族。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included anti-Persian Egyptian local forces, certain Greek poleis hostile to Persia, and Libyan and desert tribes that periodically threatened frontier communications.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策从属于波斯帝国大战略：确保埃及税赋与粮食稳定输出，控制东地中海和红海联系，维持三角洲、尼罗河与西奈通道安全，并通过安抚神庙与地方精英降低叛乱风险。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was subordinate to the grand strategy of the Persian Empire: securing stable Egyptian revenue and grain output, controlling eastern Mediterranean and Red Sea connections, maintaining security in the Delta, Nile corridor, and Sinai routes, and reducing revolt risk by accommodating temples and local elites.

era.zh: 轴心时代铁器时代晚期
era.en: Late Iron Age of the Axial Age

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的流域灌溉与地籍测量、石工与神庙建筑技术、纸草文书系统、木乃伊防腐工艺、复合弓和铁制兵器，以及帝国尺度的驿传与行政整合能力。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mature basin irrigation and cadastral surveying, advanced stoneworking and temple-building techniques, papyrus-based documentation systems, mummification methods, composite bows and iron weaponry, and imperial-scale courier and administrative integration.

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于尼罗河农业的高稳定性、庞大人口基础、成熟书记官与神庙制度，以及纳入阿契美尼德帝国后获得的跨区域军事与贸易网络支持。
strengths.en: Its chief strengths were the stability of Nile agriculture, a large population base, mature scribal and temple institutions, and access to the military and trade networks of the Achaemenid Empire.

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点是殖民统治合法性不足、对地方祭司和精英合作依赖很深、税负与驻军压力易激化不满，且远离帝国核心导致危机时控制成本高。
weaknesses.en: Its main weaknesses were the limited legitimacy of colonial rule, heavy dependence on cooperation from local priests and elites, taxation and garrison burdens that could inflame discontent, and the high cost of control due to distance from the imperial core.

outlook.zh: 到公元前500年局势总体稳定，财政与农业基础稳固，但中长期前景取决于波斯中央政权强弱、对神庙政策是否审慎，以及能否压制反复出现的埃及独立倾向。
outlook.en: By 500 BCE the situation was broadly stable, with solid fiscal and agrarian foundations, but the medium-term outlook depended on the strength of the Persian central monarchy, prudent management of temple policy, and the ability to suppress recurring Egyptian independence movements.

description.zh: 埃及此时为波斯帝国行省，保留法老名号但受帝国总督体系管理。
description.en: Egypt was then a Persian province, retaining pharaonic titulature but governed through the imperial satrapal system.

name.zh: 波斯治下黎凡特与腓尼基诸城
腓尼基、犹地亚与叙利亚行省性政体
name.en: Phoenician, Judean, and Syrian Provincial Polities

ruler.zh: 大流士一世与诸地方王公

governmentForm.zh: 阿契美尼德帝国宗主权下的行省—城邦混合统治，结合腓尼基城市王国、犹地亚圣殿社群与叙利亚地方共同体
governmentForm.en: A satrapal-city-state hybrid under Achaemenid imperial suzerainty, combining Phoenician city-kingdoms, the Judean temple community, and Syrian local civic polities

socialStructure.zh: 社会由城市王室与贵族、祭司集团、帝国任命官员、商人船主、神庙人员、工匠、农民、佃户与奴隶构成；沿海城市商业化程度高，内陆地区则更依赖农业与神庙组织
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of urban royal houses and nobles, priestly groups, imperial appointees, merchants and shipowners, temple personnel, artisans, peasants, tenant farmers, and slaves; the coast was highly commercialized, while inland districts relied more on agriculture and temple organization

rulingClass.zh: 腓尼基城邦王室、地方贵族家族、犹地亚祭司与文书精英，以及受波斯认可的地方行政首长
rulingClass.en: Phoenician city-king dynasties, local noble families, Judean priestly and scribal elites, and Persian-recognized provincial administrators

succession.zh: 腓尼基诸城多实行王族世袭并需获大王承认；犹地亚地方权威更多围绕总督与大祭司职能运作，重大任命受帝国干预
succession.en: In the Phoenician cities succession was generally dynastic and hereditary but required recognition by the Great King; in Judah authority centered more on the governor and high-priestly office, with major appointments subject to imperial oversight

structure.zh: 该地区隶属阿契美尼德帝国西部行省体系，政治上并非统一国家，而是由西顿、推罗、阿拉多斯、比布鲁斯等腓尼基城市王国，耶胡德省的圣殿—总督体制，以及叙利亚各地方城镇共同组成；帝国通过贡赋、驻军、海军征调与道路通讯维持控制
structure.en: The region belonged to the western satrapal system of the Achaemenid Empire and was not a single unified state; rather, it comprised Phoenician city-kingdoms such as Sidon, Tyre, Arwad, and Byblos, the temple-governor system of Yehud, and various Syrian local communities; imperial control was maintained through tribute, garrisons, naval levies, and road communications

function.zh: 协调地方王室与帝国总督之间的关系，管理诏令、朝贡、使节与任命确认
function.en: Coordinated relations between local kings and imperial satrapal authority, managing decrees, tribute, embassies, and confirmation of appointments

function.zh: 征收银赋、谷物、木材、染料与其他实物税，并管理城市与神庙仓储
function.en: Collected silver tribute, grain, timber, dyes, and other payments in kind, and managed civic and temple storage facilities

function.zh: 监管港口装卸、船舶登记、海关、船只征调以及对帝国舰队的海上服务
function.en: Supervised port handling, ship registration, customs, naval levies, and maritime service to the imperial fleet

function.zh: 管理神庙地产、祭司登记、奉献物、祭礼日历及与地方社群认同相关的宗教事务
function.en: Administered temple estates, priestly registers, offerings, ritual calendars, and religious matters tied to local communal identity

function.zh: 保存契约、仲裁商业纠纷、执行地方习惯法，并以阿拉米语和地方文字处理行政文书
function.en: Kept contracts, arbitrated commercial disputes, enforced local customary law, and processed administration in Aramaic and local scripts

function.zh: 组织沿海守备、城墙维护、道路警备与帝国驻军后勤
function.en: Organized coastal defense, wall maintenance, road security, and logistics for imperial garrisons

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政呈强烈分区性：腓尼基沿海由城市王国及其议事贵族管理港口和商业；犹地亚由耶路撒冷总督、祭司与长老体系管理；叙利亚内陆则由城镇首长、地主贵族与帝国官员协作治理。帝国层级通常通过总督、税吏和军官监督，而非彻底取代地方制度
localAdmin.en: Local administration was strongly regionalized: the Phoenician coast was governed by city-kings and aristocratic councils overseeing ports and commerce; Judah was managed by the Jerusalem governor, priests, and elders; inland Syria was administered through town chiefs, landed elites, and imperial officials. The empire generally supervised through satrapal agents, tax collectors, and military officers rather than fully replacing local institutions

legalSystem.zh: 法律制度为帝国法、王室敕令、地方习惯法与神庙法并行。商业合同、债务、海运、地产与婚姻常由文书记录并在地方法庭裁决；犹地亚宗教共同体内部事务带有较强圣律色彩，而腓尼基城市则更重视商法与契约惯例
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined imperial law, royal decrees, local customary law, and temple law. Contracts, debts, shipping, property, and marriage were commonly recorded by scribes and judged in local courts; internal affairs in Judah carried a stronger sacred-law character, while Phoenician cities placed greater emphasis on commercial law and contractual custom

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以向帝国缴纳银赋与实物贡纳为核心，辅以港口关税、航运费、土地税、神庙献纳和劳役。沿海城市常通过贸易税和船舶服务承担较大份额，内陆地区则更多以粮食、橄榄油、葡萄酒和畜产缴纳
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on silver tribute and payments in kind to the empire, supplemented by port customs, shipping dues, land tax, temple contributions, and corvée labor. Coastal cities bore a major share through trade taxes and naval service, while inland areas contributed more in grain, olive oil, wine, and livestock

religion.zh: 宗教高度多元：腓尼基诸城崇拜巴力、阿斯塔特、埃什蒙、梅尔卡特等神祇；犹地亚以耶和华信仰和耶路撒冷圣殿为核心；叙利亚地方则崇拜哈达德、阿塔加提斯及其他本地神。波斯统治总体容许地方祭祀延续，只要政治忠诚和贡赋稳定
religion.en: Religion was highly plural: the Phoenician cities worshipped deities such as Baal, Astarte, Eshmun, and Melqart; Judah centered on the worship of Yahweh and the Jerusalem Temple; Syrian communities revered Hadad, Atargatis, and other local gods. Persian rule generally allowed local cults to continue so long as loyalty and tribute remained secure

philosophy.zh: 该地区思想传统以神庙智慧、王权伦理、先知训诫、实用商贸经验和抄写文化为主，不存在类似希腊式系统哲学，但在犹地亚已形成强烈的伦理一神论和历史神学，在腓尼基与叙利亚则表现为实用主义、宗教传统与跨文化吸收并存
philosophy.en: The region’s intellectual traditions were rooted in temple wisdom, royal ethics, prophetic admonition, practical mercantile knowledge, and scribal culture rather than Greek-style systematic philosophy; in Judah a strong ethical monotheism and historical theology had emerged, while Phoenicia and Syria combined pragmatism, religious tradition, and cross-cultural borrowing

writingSystem.zh: 书写体系并存：腓尼基字母广泛用于沿海商业和铭文，帝国行政主要使用阿拉米字母文书，犹地亚使用希伯来文传统并逐渐更多采用帝国阿拉米语；这些文字体系共同支撑贸易、税务、外交和宗教文本传播
writingSystem.en: Multiple scripts coexisted: the Phoenician alphabet was widely used in coastal commerce and inscriptions, Imperial Aramaic served as the chief administrative script, and Judah maintained Hebrew traditions while increasingly using Imperial Aramaic; together these writing systems supported trade, taxation, diplomacy, and religious texts

culturalAchievements.zh: 主要成就在于字母文字传统的传播、成熟的港口贸易文化、染紫工艺、玻璃与象牙细工、木材与造船技术，以及犹地亚圣殿重建时期的宗教文书整理。该地区在埃及、美索不达米亚、爱琴海与阿拉伯之间充当文化中介
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements lay in the spread of alphabetic writing traditions, advanced port commerce, purple-dye production, glass and ivory craftsmanship, timber and shipbuilding, and the religious textual consolidation associated with the rebuilt Judean Temple period. The region served as a cultural intermediary between Egypt, Mesopotamia, the Aegean, and Arabia

languageFamily.zh: 以西北闪米特语支为主，包括腓尼基语、希伯来语、亚兰语及其地方变体；帝国官方沟通中阿拉米语地位尤为重要
languageFamily.en: The dominant language family was Northwest Semitic, including Phoenician, Hebrew, Aramaic, and local variants; Aramaic held particular importance in imperial communication

gdpDescription.zh: 经济在公元前500年前后处于帝国西部较活跃水平，依托沿海港口、内陆农业腹地与贯通埃及—两河—安纳托利亚的商路。总产出以农业为基础，但财富高度集中于港市贸易、海运与税收节点
gdpDescription.en: By 500 BCE the economy was among the more active in the western empire, relying on coastal ports, inland agricultural hinterlands, and routes linking Egypt, Mesopotamia, and Anatolia. Total output was fundamentally agrarian, but wealth was heavily concentrated in port trade, shipping, and fiscal chokepoints

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物农业、葡萄酒、橄榄油、枣与果园经营、木材采运、造船、航运、染紫、纺织、玻璃制造、金属与象牙工艺、神庙地产经营及转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain agriculture, wine, olive oil, date and orchard cultivation, timber extraction, shipbuilding, shipping, purple dye, textiles, glassmaking, metal and ivory crafts, temple estate management, and entrepôt trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黎巴嫩木材、紫色染料、玻璃器、金属制品、橄榄油、葡萄酒、谷物、盐鱼、香料、乳香转运品、埃及亚麻、塞浦路斯铜、爱琴海陶器与美索不达米亚奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Lebanon timber, purple dye, glassware, metal goods, olive oil, wine, grain, salted fish, spices, incense transshipments, Egyptian linen, Cypriot copper, Aegean pottery, and Mesopotamian luxury products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。沿海商人、船主和神庙相关精英积累了大量贵金属、地产与货物库存；多数乡村家庭仅拥有小块耕地、牲畜、橄榄树或葡萄园，并易受歉收与赋税影响
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Coastal merchants, shipowners, and temple-linked elites accumulated substantial bullion, land, and inventories, while most rural households possessed only small plots, livestock, and olive or vine holdings, and remained vulnerable to bad harvests and taxation

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系属于农业基础上的商业化贡赋经济：土地、神庙与王室控制关键资源，城市商人和船主经营跨海贸易，帝国通过税赋、道路和安全保障吸纳剩余并促进流通
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a commercialized tribute economy resting on an agrarian base: land, temples, and royal interests controlled key resources; urban merchants and shipowners managed overseas trade; and the empire extracted surplus while facilitating circulation through taxation, roads, and security

source.zh: 航运费、船只征调与海事收入
source.en: Shipping Fees, Naval Levies, and Maritime Income

category.zh: 向帝国上缴贡赋与王室义务

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备分散于西顿等港市王室库藏、神庙库房及帝国监督下的银储中，并非单一中央国库。贵金属、谷物和高价值贸易货物共同构成实际储备
treasuryDescription.en: Fiscal reserves were dispersed among royal stores in ports such as Sidon, temple treasuries, and silver stocks under imperial supervision rather than held in a single central treasury. Bullion, grain, and high-value trade goods together formed the practical reserve

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调稳定贡赋、保护海运、维持港口运转和避免地方叛乱。帝国通常优先确保收入与舰船供给，对地方则允许较高自治度，只要税赋按期足额征收
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized stable tribute, secure shipping, functioning ports, and the avoidance of local revolt. The empire prioritized revenue and naval supply, granting significant local autonomy so long as taxes and obligations were collected on time

description.zh: 由腓尼基、叙利亚与犹地亚各地守军组成，负责城墙、关隘、仓储和治安，装备以矛、盾、弓和简易甲胄为主
description.en: Composed of garrison forces from Phoenician, Syrian, and Judean districts, responsible for walls, passes, storage depots, and security, armed mainly with spears, shields, bows, and light armor

description.zh: 依托西顿、推罗、阿拉多斯等城征发的舰船与船员，是阿契美尼德帝国西地中海与东地中海行动的重要海上力量
description.en: Drawn from ships and crews levied from Sidon, Tyre, Arwad, and related cities, this was a major maritime arm of Achaemenid operations in the eastern Mediterranean

description.zh: 战时由乡村社群、地主附庸和城镇居民征发，适合守备、短程作战和补充帝国军行动
description.en: Raised in wartime from rural communities, landed dependents, and townsmen, suitable for defense, short-range campaigning, and reinforcement of imperial armies

description.zh: 数量有限，多用于侦察、通讯、护送与道路控制，较内陆帝国核心区骑兵传统弱
description.en: Limited in number, used mainly for scouting, communications, escort, and road control, and weaker than cavalry traditions in the empire’s inland core regions

name.zh: 帝国驻军与精锐分遣队

description.zh: 由波斯帝国派驻的少量精锐与军官组成，确保忠诚、监管战略据点并支援地方统治者
description.en: Consisted of small detachments of imperial troops and officers sent by Persia to ensure loyalty, supervise strategic points, and support local rulers

commanderInChief.zh: 大流士一世经由西部总督与腓尼基诸王统辖
commanderInChief.en: Darius I through western satrapal commanders and the Phoenician kings

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁器时代水平为主，广用铁矛、复合弓、盾牌、鳞甲和攻城器具；海军技术尤为发达，腓尼基人在三列桨战船、船体构造、航海和补给上具突出优势
technology.en: Military technology was broadly Iron Age, using iron spearheads, composite bows, shields, scale armor, and siege equipment; naval technology was especially advanced, with Phoenicians excelling in triremes, hull construction, seamanship, and logistical support

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括爱琴海希腊势力在东地中海的海上竞争、埃及方向的反波斯动荡、地方城市对重税与强制征船的不满，以及内陆沙漠边缘部族袭扰
threats.en: Main threats included maritime competition from Greek powers in the eastern Mediterranean, anti-Persian unrest in the Egyptian direction, local resentment over heavy taxation and forced naval service, and raids from tribes along the desert margins

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约二百五十万，分布于腓尼基沿海城邦、叙利亚内陆城镇与乡村、犹地亚高地及若干混合贸易聚落。人口结构多元，族群与宗教差异明显，但被帝国税制、贸易网络和阿拉米语行政体系部分整合
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 2.5 million, distributed among the Phoenician coastal city-states, inland Syrian towns and villages, the Judean highlands, and mixed trading settlements. The population was diverse in ethnicity and religion, but partially integrated by imperial taxation, trade networks, and Aramaic administration

socialClasses.zh: 上层为地方王室、贵族地主、富商与祭司；中层包括船主、商人、文书、工匠首领与较富裕农户；下层包括普通农民、码头工人、手工业者、佃农、债务依附者和奴隶。不同地区中，神庙和家族网络对阶层流动影响显著
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of local royal houses, landed nobles, wealthy merchants, and priests; the middle strata included shipowners, traders, scribes, master artisans, and better-off farmers; the lower strata comprised ordinary peasants, dockworkers, craftsmen, tenant cultivators, debt dependents, and slaves. In different regions, temples and kinship networks strongly shaped social mobility

allies.zh: 其正式盟友即阿契美尼德帝国体系内的诸行省与附属政体，尤其与波斯本部、安纳托利亚总督区和部分腓尼基姊妹城邦保持密切合作
allies.en: Its formal allies were the provinces and subordinate polities of the Achaemenid imperial system, especially Persia proper, the Anatolian satrapies, and fellow Phoenician city-kingdoms

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量为挑战波斯海权和西方影响力的部分希腊城邦，以及在特定时期反抗帝国统治的埃及势力和地方叛乱集团
enemies.en: The principal enemies were Greek poleis that challenged Persian sea power and western influence, as well as Egyptian forces and local rebel groups that resisted imperial authority at particular times

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以服务阿契美尼德帝国战略为核心：保障海上交通、输送舰船、维持埃及—叙利亚走廊稳定、保护跨区域贸易，同时尽量保存地方城邦和宗教共同体的内部自治
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was centered on serving Achaemenid strategy: securing maritime routes, providing ships, maintaining stability in the Egypt-Syria corridor, protecting long-distance trade, and preserving as much internal autonomy as possible for local city-states and religious communities

era.zh: 轴心时代铁器时代晚期
era.en: Late Iron Age of the Axial Age

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度优势包括字母书写传统、帝国阿拉米语文书体系、远程航海与港口管理、三列桨战船与造船工艺、紫染与玻璃制造、标准化度量衡以及连接帝国西部的道路—驿传网络
keyInnovations.en: Key technological and institutional strengths included alphabetic writing traditions, the Imperial Aramaic documentary system, long-distance seafaring and port management, trireme and shipbuilding techniques, purple-dye and glass production, standardized weights and measures, and the road-courier network linking the empire’s west

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于地处东西贸易枢纽、港口体系成熟、海军与航海技术发达、文化中介能力强、地方自治弹性高，并能依托波斯帝国提供的广域安全和市场整合
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were its position at the crossroads of east-west trade, mature port infrastructure, strong naval and seafaring capacity, powerful cultural brokerage, flexible local autonomy, and access to the broad security and market integration provided by the Persian Empire

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点是政治碎片化、对帝国保护和贸易畅通高度依赖、城乡与地区差异显著、地方忠诚分散，以及在帝国大规模征税或征船时容易积累不满
weaknesses.en: Its main weaknesses were political fragmentation, heavy dependence on imperial protection and uninterrupted trade, sharp urban-rural and regional disparities, divided local loyalties, and the ease with which resentment could build under heavy taxation or naval levies

outlook.zh: 在公元前500年前后，其前景总体繁荣而稳健：只要阿契美尼德帝国保持海陆秩序，该地区将继续作为东地中海贸易与舰队供应中心；但若帝国卷入对希腊和埃及的长期冲突，地方城邦与省区的财政和政治压力将迅速上升
outlook.en: Around 500 BCE the outlook was broadly prosperous and stable: so long as the Achaemenid Empire maintained order on land and sea, the region would continue as a major eastern Mediterranean trade and naval supply center; however, prolonged imperial conflict with Greece or Egypt would quickly raise fiscal and political pressures on the local city-states and provinces

description.zh: 黎凡特由波斯帝国统辖，下辖腓尼基海上城市、犹地亚和叙利亚诸地方共同体。
description.en: The Levant was governed within the Persian Empire through Phoenician maritime cities, Judea, and Syrian local communities.

ruler.zh: 各城邦的执政官、国王与僭主
ruler.en: Archons, Kings, and Tyrants of Individual Poleis

rulerTitle.zh: 执政官 / 国王 / 僭主

governmentForm.zh: 由众多自治城邦组成的分散政治体系，政体涵盖贵族制、寡头制、僭主制、混合政体与早期公民民主
governmentForm.en: A decentralized political world of autonomous city-states, including aristocracies, oligarchies, tyrannies, mixed constitutions, and early citizen democracies

socialStructure.zh: 社会以公民共同体为核心，其下有妇女、外邦侨民、自由非公民、佃农、农民、工匠、商人以及数量可观的奴隶；斯巴达等地还存在独特的斯巴达人、边民与希洛人等级
socialStructure.en: Society centered on the male citizen body, with women, resident foreigners, free non-citizens, tenant farmers, peasants, artisans, merchants, and large slave populations beneath it; Sparta also maintained the distinct hierarchy of Spartiates, perioikoi, and helots

rulingClass.zh: 各城邦的公民贵族、富裕地主、武装重装步兵阶层与显赫家族
rulingClass.en: Citizen aristocrats, wealthy landholders, hoplite elites, and prominent families within each polis

succession.zh: 无统一继承规则；有的城邦通过选举或抽签产生官员，有的由世袭王室继承，有的由寡头集团轮替，僭主政权则依武力与家族安排延续
succession.en: No single rule of succession; some poleis selected officials by election or lot, some retained hereditary kingship, some rotated office among oligarchic families, and tyrannies relied on force and family arrangements

structure.zh: 希腊世界不存在统一中央政府，而是由雅典、斯巴达、科林斯、底比斯等独立城邦各自设有公民大会、议事会、执政官员、将军与地方法庭；在宗教、节庆与外交上偶有泛希腊协调
structure.en: The Greek world lacked a unified central state and instead consisted of independent poleis such as Athens, Sparta, Corinth, and Thebes, each with its own assembly, council, magistrates, generals, and local courts; pan-Hellenic coordination appeared mainly in religion, festivals, and diplomacy

name.zh: 公民大会与议事会事务

function.zh: 组织公民大会、议程、法令审议与议事会运作，主要见于雅典等较制度化城邦
function.en: Managed citizen assemblies, agendas, review of decrees, and council operations, especially in more institutionalized poleis such as Athens

function.zh: 征收关税、港口税、矿业收入、罚金、战争分摊与公共收入，管理神庙与国库支出
function.en: Collected customs, harbor dues, mining income, fines, wartime contributions, and other public revenues, and managed temple and civic treasury expenditures

function.zh: 维护成文法与习惯法，组织陪审法庭、执法官和跨城邦仲裁
function.en: Maintained written and customary law, organized jury courts and magistrates, and handled inter-polis arbitration

function.zh: 负责征召重装步兵、骑兵、舰船船员、武器储备与边防要塞
function.en: Oversaw mobilization of hoplites, cavalry, crews for ships, arms reserves, and frontier fortifications

function.zh: 主持祭祀、神谕往来、公共节庆、竞技会与圣所财产管理
function.en: Supervised sacrifices, communication with oracles, public festivals, games, and the management of sacred property

name.zh: 港务、市场与度量衡监督

function.zh: 监管港口、集市秩序、进口谷物、商业契约和标准度量衡
function.en: Regulated harbors, market order, imported grain, commercial contracts, and standard weights and measures

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以城邦为基本单位，城邦下辖市区、乡村与附属港口或属地；阿提卡等地区有村社与部落组织，斯巴达则以军事化共同体和附属居民体系管理周边
localAdmin.en: Local administration was centered on the polis, which governed an urban core, rural hinterland, and often dependent harbors or territories; Attica used demes and tribal organization in evolving form, while Sparta governed through a militarized citizen body and subordinate populations

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系因城邦而异，兼具成文法与习惯法；雅典继承梭伦改革后的法制传统，重视公民诉讼与陪审裁判；斯巴达更依赖祖制、习惯与监察官权威
legalSystem.en: The legal system varied by polis and combined written law with custom; Athens followed the legal tradition shaped by Solonian reform, emphasizing citizen litigation and jury judgment, while Sparta relied more heavily on ancestral custom and ephoral authority

taxationSystem.zh: 常态下直接税有限，财政主要依赖关税、港务税、市场税、矿山收入、国有土地、罚金与祭产；战时则征收非常税、盟费、富人公共捐献与军费分摊
taxationSystem.en: Routine direct taxation was limited; revenue came chiefly from customs, harbor dues, market taxes, mining income, public land, fines, and sacred property, while wartime brought extraordinary levies, allied contributions, elite public liturgies, and military assessments

religion.zh: 希腊多神教占主导，崇拜宙斯、雅典娜、阿波罗、阿耳忒弥斯、赫拉、波塞冬、得墨忒耳等神祇，城邦守护神崇拜、祭祀、竞技节庆与神谕中心如德尔斐具有重要地位
religion.en: Greek polytheism predominated, with worship of Zeus, Athena, Apollo, Artemis, Hera, Poseidon, Demeter, and others; civic patron cults, sacrifice, athletic festivals, and oracular centers such as Delphi were of major importance

philosophy.zh: 处于前苏格拉底思想繁荣时期，伊奥尼亚自然哲学、南意大利毕达哥拉斯学派、爱利亚学派与理性探究兴起，对自然、数、存在与宇宙秩序提出系统思考
philosophy.en: This was the flourishing age of pre-Socratic thought, including Ionian natural philosophy, the Pythagorean tradition in southern Italy, the Eleatic school, and broader rational inquiry into nature, number, being, and cosmic order

writingSystem.zh: 使用源自腓尼基字母的希腊字母，各地方言书写形式略有差异，已广泛用于法令、碑铭、诗歌、商业记载与私人标记
writingSystem.en: The Greeks used the Greek alphabet derived from the Phoenician script, with some local variants, and it was widely employed for laws, inscriptions, poetry, commercial records, and private marks

culturalAchievements.zh: 荷马史诗传统持续塑造共同文化；抒情诗、戏剧雏形、历史记述萌芽、神庙建筑、黑绘与红绘陶器、泛希腊竞技与公共纪念艺术高度发展
culturalAchievements.en: The Homeric epic tradition continued to shape shared identity; lyric poetry, early drama, proto-historiography, temple architecture, black-figure and red-figure pottery, pan-Hellenic athletics, and civic commemorative art were highly developed

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系希腊语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Hellenic branch

gdpDescription.zh: 希腊城邦世界经济在地中海东部属高度活跃水平，以小农农业、葡萄酒和橄榄油生产、手工业、矿业、航运与跨海贸易为支柱；雅典、科林斯与爱琴海诸港口在区域交换中地位突出
gdpDescription.en: The economy of the Greek poleis was highly active by eastern Mediterranean standards, based on smallholder agriculture, wine and olive oil production, craft manufacture, mining, shipping, and overseas trade; Athens, Corinth, and Aegean ports held prominent positions in regional exchange

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、橄榄与葡萄栽培、陶器、金属加工、造船、纺织、石材建筑、银矿与铜铁采冶
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, olive and vine growing, pottery, metalworking, shipbuilding, textiles, stone construction, and silver, copper, and iron mining and smelting

tradeGoods.zh: 出口橄榄油、葡萄酒、陶器、金属制品、工艺品与雇佣兵服务；进口谷物、木材、腓尼基染料、埃及纸草、金属原料、奴隶与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included olive oil, wine, pottery, metal goods, crafted wares, and mercenary service; imports included grain, timber, Phoenician dyes, Egyptian papyrus, metal raw materials, slaves, and luxury goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距极大：多数公民家庭拥有小块土地、牲畜与基础器具；富裕家族控制大庄园、船只、矿业承包与奴隶劳动；贫民、佃户与无地者抗风险能力较弱
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply: most citizen households held small plots, livestock, and basic tools, while wealthy families controlled larger estates, ships, mining leases, and slave labor; the poor, tenants, and landless had limited resilience

economicSystem.zh: 以私有土地和家庭经营为基础的市场化城邦经济，辅以奴隶劳动、宗教财产、公共工程和战争掠夺；海上贸易网络对财富积累尤为关键
economicSystem.en: A market-oriented polis economy based on private landholding and household production, supplemented by slave labor, sacred property, public works, and wartime plunder; maritime trade networks were especially important for wealth accumulation

source.en: Customs, harbor dues, and market taxes
Fines, court income, and public property

source.zh: 富人公共捐献与非常征发

category.zh: 外交使节、神谕与应急储备

treasuryDescription.zh: 希腊世界没有统一国库，此处为主要城邦与圣所可动用储备的综合估计；财富分散于城邦库藏、神庙金银、铸币金属和贵族私人财富之中
treasuryDescription.en: The Greek world had no single treasury; this is a combined estimate of mobilizable reserves held by major poleis and sanctuaries, with wealth dispersed among civic treasuries, temple bullion, coined metal, and aristocratic private fortunes

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政趋于地方化与事件驱动，平时维持低常设负担，战时依靠非常征发、富人义务和临时借用神庙资源；港口贸易与矿业是最稳定的现金来源
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was localized and event-driven, keeping peacetime burdens relatively low while relying in war on emergency levies, elite obligations, and occasional use of sacred resources; port commerce and mining were the most dependable cash revenues

description.zh: 由有产公民为主构成，装备圆盾、长矛、头盔与胸甲，是各城邦会战的核心兵种
description.en: Composed mainly of propertied citizens equipped with shield, spear, helmet, and cuirass, forming the core arm in inter-polis warfare

description.zh: 包括标枪兵、投石兵与较轻装的步兵，多由贫民、边民或附属人口担任，用于骚扰、追击和复杂地形作战
description.en: Included javelin-men, slingers, and lightly equipped infantry, often drawn from poorer groups or dependent populations, useful for skirmishing, pursuit, and rough terrain

description.zh: 规模较小，主要集中于拥有马匹资源的城邦与地区，用于侦察、掩护和追击
description.en: Relatively small in scale, concentrated in poleis and regions able to maintain horses, and used for reconnaissance, screening, and pursuit

description.zh: 以桨帆战船和武装商船为基础，科林斯、雅典及爱琴海岛邦尤为重视海上力量，用于护航、劫掠与控制海峡航线
description.en: Based on oared warships and armed merchant vessels; Corinth, Athens, and island poleis placed special emphasis on sea power for escort, raiding, and control of straits and shipping lanes

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅；各城邦由将军、国王、执政官或监察官分别统军
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; each polis was led by its own generals, kings, archons, or ephors

technology.zh: 军事技术以青铜和铁制武器、重装步兵方阵、城墙防御、攻守梯具、三列桨战船早期发展以及纪律化集体作战为特征；斯巴达陆战训练尤为突出
technology.en: Military technology featured bronze and iron weaponry, the hoplite phalanx, fortified walls, simple siege ladders and assault tools, early development of trireme warfare, and disciplined collective fighting; Sparta stood out for land-war training

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自波斯帝国对爱琴海与小亚细亚希腊人的扩张压力，以及城邦之间长期竞争，如雅典、斯巴达、科林斯、底比斯及岛邦间的冲突；北方与西方边缘地区也有色雷斯人、伊利里亚人和其他部族压力
threats.en: The principal threat came from Persian imperial pressure on the Aegean and the Greeks of Asia Minor, along with chronic rivalries among poleis such as Athens, Sparta, Corinth, Thebes, and island states; frontier pressures from Thracian, Illyrian, and other tribal groups also mattered

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于希腊本土、爱琴海诸岛、小亚细亚沿岸及西地中海殖民网络；多数居民居于乡村，集中在雅典、斯巴达腹地、科林斯地峡、彼奥提亚和平原港口周边
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across mainland Greece, the Aegean islands, the coast of Asia Minor, and wider colonial networks in the western Mediterranean; most people lived rurally, concentrated around Attica, Laconia, the Corinthian Isthmus, Boeotia, and coastal plains

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括公民贵族、普通公民农兵、工匠与商人、外邦居留者、自由贫民、奴隶；斯巴达体系下则分为斯巴达人、边民和希洛人。妇女在家庭与宗教中重要，但普遍缺乏正式政治权利
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included citizen aristocrats, ordinary citizen farmers and hoplites, artisans and merchants, resident foreigners, free poor, and slaves; in the Spartan system these corresponded to Spartiates, perioikoi, and helots. Women were important in household and religious life but generally lacked formal political rights

allies.zh: 无统一盟友；城邦间联盟不断变化。斯巴达在伯罗奔尼撒同盟中居主导，雅典与爱琴海部分城邦及伊奥尼亚希腊人保持联系，泛希腊圣所为调解与结盟提供空间
allies.en: There were no unified allies; alliances shifted constantly among poleis. Sparta dominated the Peloponnesian League, Athens maintained ties with parts of the Aegean and Ionian Greeks, and pan-Hellenic sanctuaries provided venues for mediation and alliance-making

enemies.zh: 最大外敌是波斯帝国；此外各城邦彼此常为竞争者，尤其在贸易航线、殖民地、边界和同盟影响力上冲突频繁
enemies.en: The greatest external enemy was the Persian Empire; beyond that, the poleis were often rivals with one another, especially over trade routes, colonies, frontiers, and alliance influence

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以城邦利益为中心，重视结盟、仲裁、婚姻联系、神谕咨询、殖民扩张与海上控制；面对波斯威胁时虽可能形成临时合作，但持久统一极为罕见
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was polis-centered, emphasizing alliances, arbitration, marriage ties, consultation of oracles, colonial expansion, and maritime control; temporary cooperation against Persia was possible, but durable political unity was rare

keyInnovations.zh: 希腊字母的成熟应用、标准化铸币流通、重装步兵方阵、早期三列桨战船、几何与自然哲学思辨、石制神庙建筑技术、陶器烧制与绘画工艺精进
