languageFamily.zh: 以东伊朗语族诸方言为主，边缘地区混有其他草原与森林地带语言
languageFamily.en: Primarily Eastern Iranian dialect groups, with other steppe and forest-zone languages present in frontier regions

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以牧业为主，辅以狩猎、掠夺、边境贸易与对商路的控制。由于游牧生产不以城市账簿计量，财富更多体现在马、牛、羊、奴隶、金器与战利品，而非稳定货币流通。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on pastoralism, supplemented by hunting, raiding, frontier trade, and control of trade routes. Because nomadic production was not measured through urban accounting, wealth was expressed more in horses, cattle, sheep, slaves, gold objects, and booty than in steady monetary circulation.

mainIndustries.zh: 马匹与畜群牧养、皮革与毛毡制作、金属工艺、武器制造、狩猎、护商与战利品再分配
mainIndustries.en: Horse and herd pastoralism, leather and felt production, metalworking, weapon manufacture, hunting, caravan protection, and redistribution of war booty

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、羊毛、毛毡、皮革、畜产品、奴隶、金器、兽皮，以及从农耕世界换入的谷物、酒、金属器、织物与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Horses, wool, felt, leather, animal products, slaves, gold objects, and furs, exchanged for grain, wine, metal goods, textiles, and luxury items from agrarian societies

unitName.en: head of livestock and silver weight

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要以马、羊、牛、帐幕、武器与家用器具体现；贵族则拥有大量畜群、奴隶、金器和附庸。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in horses, sheep, cattle, tents, weapons, and domestic equipment; nobles possessed large herds, slaves, goldwork, and dependents.

economicSystem.zh: 以游牧家庭生产和部族再分配为基础的混合经济，结合贡赋、掠夺、互市和长途商路中介功能。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on pastoral household production and tribal redistribution, combined with tribute, raiding, exchange markets, and mediation of long-distance trade routes.

source.zh: 畜群抽取与王族牧场收益
source.en: Livestock levies and royal pasture income

source.zh: 商道保护费与边贸关收
source.en: Caravan protection payments and frontier trade dues

category.en: Maintenance of royal households and noble retinues

category.zh: 盟约馈赠与部族再分配

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备并非集中于单一都城金库，而是分散于王族营帐、贵族墓藏、畜群储备和可移动金银器物之中，具高度机动性但集中调度能力有限。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were not concentrated in a single capital vault but dispersed among royal camps, noble hoards, herd reserves, and portable gold and silver goods, giving high mobility but limited central control.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持王族威望与联盟凝聚，通过分配战利品、馈赠盟友和保护商路来巩固统治，而非建立稳定官僚税制。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining royal prestige and confederational cohesion through the distribution of booty, gifts to allies, and protection of trade routes rather than building a stable bureaucratic tax system.

description.zh: 由王族与贵族率领的精锐骑手，装备复合弓、短矛、战斧与鳞甲或皮甲，是草原机动作战核心。
description.en: Elite riders led by royal and noble lineages, equipped with composite bows, short spears, battle axes, and scale or leather armor, forming the core of steppe mobile warfare.

description.zh: 由自由牧民战士组成，负责侦察、包抄、追击、袭扰与远程消耗。
description.en: Composed of free pastoralist warriors, used for scouting, encirclement, pursuit, harassment, and ranged attrition.

description.zh: 由营地随从、较贫困牧民及附属人口组成，守卫车营、牲畜和补给线，在防御战中较为重要。
description.en: Made up of camp followers, poorer pastoralists, and dependent populations, guarding wagon camps, livestock, and supply lines, especially important in defense.

description.zh: 由附属部族、森林地带战士或绿洲盟友提供，战力不一，但可补充兵力与地域适应性。
description.en: Supplied by subordinate tribes, forest-zone fighters, or oasis allies; uneven in quality but useful for added manpower and regional adaptability.

commanderInChief.zh: 联盟最强王族之王或战时共主
commanderInChief.en: The king of the dominant royal lineage or a wartime paramount chief

technology.zh: 军事技术以骑马机动、复合弓、鞍垫、马具、短剑、矛、战斧与部分鳞甲为特色；虽缺乏后世马镫，但其骑术已足以支持高效骑射与快速远征。
technology.en: Military technology was characterized by mounted mobility, composite bows, saddle pads, harness systems, short swords, spears, battle axes, and some scale armor; although lacking the later stirrup, riding skill already supported highly effective horse archery and rapid campaigning.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自阿契美尼德波斯在中亚与黑海北岸的扩张压力、部族间竞争、东部草原新兴势力迁动，以及与定居边境政权围绕贸易和掠夺的冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from Achaemenid Persian pressure in Central Asia and near the Black Sea, intertribal competition, movements of emerging eastern steppe groups, and conflict with settled frontier states over trade and raiding.

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于黑海北岸、哈萨克草原、咸海周边、中亚草原与天山以西诸地，以流动营地和季节性牧场为主，人口密度低而分散。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across the north Pontic steppe, the Kazakh steppe, the Aral region, Central Asian grasslands, and lands west of the Tian Shan, with low-density and dispersed settlement in mobile camps and seasonal pasture zones.

name.zh: 无固定都城；主要人口集中于季节性王帐与贸易营地
name.en: No fixed capital; population concentrated in seasonal royal camps and trade encampments

name.zh: 黑海草原希腊边贸聚落
中亚绿洲边缘贸易营地

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王族、贵族武士、自由牧民、工匠、祭司、附属部众与奴隶；阶层身份常与血缘、战功、畜群规模和对王族的依附关系相连。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata included royalty, noble warriors, free pastoralists, artisans, priests, dependent groups, and slaves; status was closely tied to kinship, martial achievement, herd size, and dependence on royal lineages.

allies.zh: 盟友关系多为流动且务实，可包括部分中亚绿洲政体、黑海北岸贸易伙伴、以及与某些邻近部族的临时军事联盟。
allies.en: Alliances were fluid and pragmatic, including at times certain Central Asian oasis polities, trading partners on the north Black Sea coast, and temporary military arrangements with neighboring tribes.

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括试图控制草原边缘与商道的阿契美尼德波斯，以及与其争夺牧场、贸易和霸权的其他草原联盟；与部分定居国家的关系亦时战时和。
enemies.en: Main opponents included the Achaemenid Persian Empire where it sought to control steppe margins and trade routes, as well as rival steppe confederations contesting pasture, trade, and supremacy; relations with some settled states also alternated between war and peace.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保持牧场安全、控制迁徙通道、从边境贸易中获利、利用机动优势压迫定居邻邦并在必要时结盟为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on securing pasture zones, controlling migration corridors, profiting from frontier trade, exploiting mobility to pressure settled neighbors, and making alliances when useful.

era.zh: 轴心时代早期铁器草原文化
era.en: Early Iron Age steppe culture of the Axial Age

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括成熟骑乘传统、复合弓、改良马具、毛毡帐幕、游牧后勤体系、动物纹金工艺术与适应长距离迁徙的草原战争组织。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mature horse-riding traditions, the composite bow, improved harness equipment, felt tents, nomadic logistics systems, animal-style goldworking, and steppe war organization adapted to long-distance mobility.

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于高度机动的骑射战力、广阔草原纵深、灵活联盟结构、跨区域贸易中介能力以及适应严酷环境的牧业经济。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were highly mobile mounted archery, vast strategic depth across the steppe, flexible confederational organization, the ability to mediate long-distance trade, and a pastoral economy adapted to harsh environments.

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点是政治统一性不足、财政集中度低、缺乏固定行政中心和书面官僚体系，联盟常受继承争端、部族分裂和外部收买影响。
weaknesses.en: Its principal weaknesses were limited political unity, low fiscal centralization, and the absence of fixed administrative centers and a written bureaucracy; confederations were often vulnerable to succession disputes, tribal fragmentation, and outside manipulation.

outlook.zh: 就公元前500年前后而言，斯基泰—萨迦世界整体稳定且仍具扩张和掠夺能力，但未来将持续面临波斯压力、东西草原人口迁动以及与定居帝国更深层接触所带来的重组。
outlook.en: Around 500 BCE, the Scythian-Saka world was broadly stable and still capable of expansion and raiding, but it would continue to face Persian pressure, east-west steppe population movements, and reorganization driven by deeper contact with settled empires.

description.zh: 欧亚草原由斯基泰、萨迦等骑牧联盟控制，与波斯、中国和黑海世界持续互动。
description.en: The Eurasian steppe was dominated by Scythian and Saka pastoral confederations interacting with Persia, China, and the Black Sea world.

name.zh: 阿拉伯半岛诸部族与绿洲王国

dynasty.zh: 因地区而异的部族与地方王系
dynasty.en: Regionally varied tribal lineages and local royal houses

governmentForm.zh: 以部族联盟、绿洲城邦与南部香料王国并存的分散型酋邦—君主制体系
governmentForm.en: A decentralized system of tribal confederacies, oasis polities, and southern incense kingdoms combining chiefdoms and local monarchies

socialStructure.zh: 社会由部族血缘网络构成，上层为酋长、王室、祭司与富商，中层为牧民、绿洲农民、手工业者与商队经营者，底层包括依附民、债务劳工与奴隶。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized through tribal kinship networks, with chiefs, royal houses, priests, and wealthy merchants at the top; pastoralists, oasis farmers, artisans, and caravan operators in the middle; and dependents, debt laborers, and slaves at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 部族贵族、绿洲城镇长老、神庙祭司集团与南阿拉伯地方王室
rulingClass.en: Tribal aristocracies, oasis town elders, temple priesthoods, and local royal houses of South Arabia

succession.zh: 多依部族协商与家族继承并行，南部王国更倾向世袭，北部酋邦常需长老与武士支持确认
succession.en: Succession combined family inheritance with tribal consultation; southern kingdoms were more strongly hereditary, while northern chiefdoms often required recognition by elders and warrior supporters

structure.zh: 整体无统一中央国家。北部以部族联盟和绿洲聚落自治为主，南部若干王国在灌溉农业区形成较稳固的宫廷、神庙与税收机构。
structure.en: There was no single centralized state. Northern Arabia was dominated by tribal confederacies and self-governing oasis settlements, while several southern kingdoms in irrigated agricultural zones developed more stable palatial, temple, and revenue institutions.

function.zh: 处理部族争端、盟约、继承、放牧路线与对外和解或报复事务
function.en: Handled tribal disputes, alliances, succession, pasture routes, and decisions on reconciliation or retaliation with outsiders

function.zh: 管理商队通行、绿洲市场、道路保护费、过境税与仓储
function.en: Managed caravan passage, oasis markets, road protection fees, transit duties, and storage

function.zh: 在南阿拉伯管理梯田、堤坝、水渠、井泉与农产征收
function.en: Oversaw terraces, dams, canals, wells, and agricultural collection in South Arabia

function.zh: 主持神庙地产、祭品、誓约见证、宗教节庆与部分文书记录
function.en: Supervised temple lands, offerings, oath witnessing, religious festivals, and part of the scribal record

function.zh: 负责城镇护卫、商路巡逻、部族征召与战时集结
function.en: Managed town guards, trade-route patrols, tribal levies, and wartime mustering

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要依靠部族首领、城镇长老、神庙管理者与南部王国任命的地方官。北方偏重习惯法与协商，南方在农业核心区有更正式的征收和工程管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on tribal chiefs, town elders, temple managers, and in the south officials appointed by local kings. The north leaned on customary law and negotiation, while the south had more formal collection and hydraulic management in core agricultural zones.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以部族习惯法、血亲赔偿、誓约、神庙见证和地方王命共同构成。商旅合同与债务关系在主要绿洲和南部城镇已较规范。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted of tribal customary practice, kin-based compensation, oaths, temple witnessing, and local royal decrees. Caravan contracts and debt relations were relatively well developed in major oases and southern towns.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收来源包括商道过境税、绿洲市场税、灌溉农业实物征收、牲畜贡赋、神庙奉献以及战时特别征敛。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation drew on transit duties along caravan routes, oasis market dues, in-kind agricultural collection from irrigated land, livestock tribute, temple offerings, and extraordinary wartime levies.

religion.zh: 以多神信仰为主，崇拜地方神、天体神与守护神，神庙和圣所是誓约、贸易和政治合法性的核心空间；部分北阿拉伯地区受阿拉米、叙利亚和美索不达米亚宗教影响。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly polytheistic, centered on local deities, astral gods, and protective gods, with temples and sanctuaries serving as key spaces for oath-making, trade, and political legitimacy; parts of North Arabia were influenced by Aramaean, Syrian, and Mesopotamian religious traditions.

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系哲学学派，思想主要体现为部族荣誉、祖先传统、誓约伦理、商业信誉与王权—神权结合的实用观念。
philosophy.en: There was no fully systematized philosophical tradition; thought was expressed mainly through tribal honor, ancestral custom, oath ethics, mercantile trust, and pragmatic ideas linking kingship with divine sanction.

writingSystem.zh: 南部广泛使用古南阿拉伯字母书写，北部则见早期北阿拉伯铭文传统，并在贸易环境中接触阿拉米字母文书。
writingSystem.en: The south widely used Ancient South Arabian alphabetic scripts, while the north employed early North Arabian inscriptional traditions and encountered Aramaic documentary writing in trade contexts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要成就包括沙漠长途商队组织、绿洲定居文化、南阿拉伯碑铭传统、灌溉与梯田农业、香料贸易网络以及跨地区宗教圣所体系。
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included long-distance desert caravan organization, oasis settlement culture, South Arabian inscriptional traditions, irrigation and terraced agriculture, incense trade networks, and a system of transregional sanctuaries.

languageFamily.zh: 主要属于闪米特语族，包括古南阿拉伯诸语言与北阿拉伯方言，并广泛接触阿拉米语
languageFamily.en: The main languages belonged to the Semitic family, including Ancient South Arabian languages and North Arabian dialects, with widespread contact with Aramaic

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以牧业、绿洲农业和中介贸易为基础。北部依赖骆驼与羊群放牧、绿洲补给和商道服务，南部凭借乳香、没药和灌溉农业形成较高剩余。整体财富分布不均，集中于商路节点、神庙与南部王室中心。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on pastoralism, oasis agriculture, and intermediary trade. The north depended on camel and sheep herding, oasis provisioning, and caravan services, while the south generated greater surplus through frankincense, myrrh, and irrigated agriculture. Wealth was unevenly distributed, concentrating at trade nodes, temples, and southern royal centers.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、绿洲农业、香料采集与转运、灌溉农作、皮革与织物加工、盐与铜等地方资源贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, oasis agriculture, incense harvesting and transport, irrigated cultivation, leather and textile processing, and trade in local resources such as salt and copper

tradeGoods.zh: 乳香、没药、香木、皮革、羊毛、椰枣、葡萄酒、盐、铜器、骆驼、奴隶以及转口的外来奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Frankincense, myrrh, aromatics, leather, wool, dates, wine, salt, copper goods, camels, slaves, and re-exported foreign luxuries

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富高度依赖牲畜、井泉使用权、枣椰林、商队份额与部族保护关系。南部定居家庭较能积累土地和灌溉设施，北部游牧家庭则以骆驼与羊群为核心资产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth depended heavily on livestock, access rights to wells, date groves, shares in caravans, and tribal protection ties. Sedentary southern households were better able to accumulate land and irrigation works, while northern nomadic households centered wealth on camels and flocks.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为部族牧业、神庙再分配、地方王室征收与长途商贸相结合的混合型前货币经济。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a mixed pre-monetary economy combining tribal pastoral production, temple redistribution, local royal extraction, and long-distance commerce.

source.zh: 农业与灌溉区实物征收

source.en: Temple offerings and confederate tribute gifts

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非集中于单一都城，而是分散保存在主要绿洲、王室仓库、神庙金库与商路节点中，多以金属、香料、谷物和牲畜形式储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not concentrated in a single capital but dispersed among major oases, royal storehouses, temple treasuries, and trade nodes, with reserves held in metal, aromatics, grain, and livestock.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视维持商路安全、保障水利和通过祭祀与馈赠稳固部族联盟，避免对游牧群体征收过重。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, emphasizing trade-route security, maintenance of waterworks, and the stabilization of tribal alliances through ritual and gift exchange while avoiding excessive burdens on pastoral groups.

description.zh: 由部族成员和绿洲居民组成，装备长矛、短剑、弓与皮盾，适于守卫聚落、伏击和短期集结作战。
description.en: Composed of tribesmen and oasis inhabitants, armed with spears, daggers, bows, and leather shields, suited to settlement defense, ambush, and short-term mobilization.

description.zh: 主要用于侦察、护送商队、快速袭扰与沙漠机动，是阿拉伯军事与治安体系的关键力量。
description.en: Used chiefly for reconnaissance, caravan escort, rapid raiding, and desert mobility, they were a key arm of Arabian military and security systems.

description.zh: 驻守泰玛及其他主要绿洲和南部城镇，负责城门、仓库、市场和水源保护。
description.en: Stationed in Tayma and other major oases and southern towns, they guarded gates, storehouses, markets, and water sources.

name.en: Royal Guards and Southern Elite Troops

description.zh: 由地方诸王和显贵供养，装备较佳，承担宫廷警卫、攻坚与关键节点防御任务。
description.en: Maintained by local kings and magnates, these better-equipped forces served as court guards and were used in assaults and defense of key positions.

commanderInChief.zh: 各部酋长与地方国王分掌军权，战时由盟主或最强首领统领
commanderInChief.en: Military command was divided among tribal chiefs and local kings, with a confederate leader or strongest chief assuming overall command in wartime

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁器兵刃、复合弓或简易弓、皮盾、骆驼骑乘和沙漠后勤经验为主，攻城能力有限，但在旱地机动、追踪和补给组织方面优势明显。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, composite or simple bows, leather shields, camel riding, and desert logistical expertise. Siege capabilities were limited, but strengths in arid mobility, tracking, and supply organization were pronounced.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间争斗、商道控制竞争、南部王国彼此冲突，以及北方受阿契美尼德帝国边缘势力和黎凡特政治变化的间接影响。
threats.en: Main threats came from intertribal conflict, competition over trade-route control, rivalries among southern kingdoms, and indirect pressure from the Achaemenid imperial frontier and political changes in the Levant.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布高度不均，北部多为游牧或半游牧部族，集中在绿洲和季节性水源周边；南部则在高地和灌溉谷地形成更稳定、密集的定居人口。
populationDescription.en: Population was highly unevenly distributed: the north was dominated by nomadic and semi-nomadic tribes clustered around oases and seasonal water sources, while the south supported denser and more stable settled populations in highlands and irrigated valleys.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室与酋长家族、部族贵族、祭司和书吏、商人、牧民、农民、手工业者、依附民与奴隶；但部族身份常比单纯财富更能决定地位和保护。
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included royal and chiefly families, tribal nobles, priests and scribes, merchants, pastoralists, farmers, artisans, dependents, and slaves; however, tribal affiliation often mattered more than wealth alone in determining status and protection.

allies.zh: 主要盟友为半岛内部的部族联盟、南阿拉伯诸王国之间的临时协约，以及与黎凡特、埃及和美索不达米亚商人的互利关系。
allies.en: Principal allies were internal peninsular tribal confederacies, temporary agreements among South Arabian kingdoms, and mutually beneficial ties with merchants from the Levant, Egypt, and Mesopotamia.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象多为争夺水源、牧场和商路的邻近部族与竞争性绿洲政体，而非持续统一的外部国家敌人。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually neighboring tribes and rival oasis polities competing over water, pasture, and trade routes rather than a single enduring foreign state enemy.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护商路畅通、平衡南北部族、通过婚姻和馈赠巩固盟约、并避免直接卷入强大帝国冲突为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on keeping trade routes open, balancing northern and southern tribal interests, reinforcing alliances through marriage and gift exchange, and avoiding direct entanglement in conflicts with major empires.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括骆驼驯用与商队体系、深井与蓄水设施、南阿拉伯灌溉工程、石刻与字母铭文传统、沙漠导航与长程补给组织。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included camel domestication and caravan systems, deep wells and water-storage facilities, South Arabian irrigation engineering, stone and alphabetic inscriptional traditions, and advanced desert navigation and long-range supply organization.

strengths.zh: 优势在于控制连接南阿拉伯、黎凡特、埃及与美索不达米亚的商道，拥有强韧的部族组织、出色的沙漠机动能力，以及南部较成熟的灌溉农业与香料经济。
strengths.en: Strengths lay in control of trade routes linking South Arabia with the Levant, Egypt, and Mesopotamia, resilient tribal organization, excellent desert mobility, and the more developed irrigated agriculture and incense economy of the south.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分裂、中央权力薄弱、财政和军力分散、城市规模有限，并高度依赖水源稳定与跨区域贸易需求。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included political fragmentation, weak central authority, dispersed finances and military power, limited urban scale, and strong dependence on water stability and external trade demand.

outlook.zh: 在公元前500年前后局势总体稳定，只要商路维持畅通、南部灌溉体系得以维护，诸政体仍可继续繁荣；但若帝国势力改变周边贸易格局，或内部部族冲突升级，整体平衡将迅速脆弱化。
outlook.en: Around 500 BCE the overall situation was stable; as long as trade routes remained open and southern irrigation systems were maintained, these polities could continue to prosper. But if imperial powers altered regional trade patterns or internal tribal conflicts intensified, the balance would become fragile very quickly.

description.zh: 阿拉伯半岛北部以游牧和绿洲商路共同体为主，南部则出现更稳定的香料贸易王国。
description.en: Northern Arabia was dominated by nomads and oasis trade communities, while the south saw more stable incense-trade kingdoms.

name.zh: 南阿拉伯文明（萨巴王国）

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为核心、结合部族贵族与神庙权威的南阿拉伯灌溉贸易王国
governmentForm.en: A South Arabian irrigation-and-trade kingdom centered on monarchy and supported by tribal aristocracy and temple authority

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与穆卡里卜、部族贵族、神庙祭司、灌溉地地主、商队商人、工匠、农民、牧民及依附劳役人口组成；部族身份与灌溉共同体成员资格对政治与经济地位极为重要。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house and mukarribs, tribal nobility, temple priests, owners of irrigated land, caravan merchants, artisans, farmers, pastoralists, and dependent laborers; tribal affiliation and membership in irrigation communities were central to political and economic status.

rulingClass.zh: 萨巴王室、主要部族首领、大神庙祭司集团以及控制马里卜坝灌区与乳香贸易线路的贵族家族
rulingClass.en: The Sabaean royal house, leading tribal chiefs, major temple priesthoods, and aristocratic families controlling the Marib Dam irrigation zone and incense routes

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部世袭，但实际继承常需得到主要部族联盟、贵族与宗教权威的承认，因此并非绝对长子继承。
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the royal line, but effective succession often required recognition by leading tribal federations, nobles, and religious authorities, making it less rigid than strict primogeniture.

structure.zh: 中央政权设于马里卜，由国王或穆卡里卜统领，依靠王室家臣、部族议事机制、神庙机构与地方总督共同治理；国家组织重点围绕灌溉管理、商路安全、祭祀秩序与对附属地区的征收。
structure.en: The central government was based at Marib and led by the king or mukarrib, relying on royal retainers, tribal consultative mechanisms, temple institutions, and local governors; state organization focused on irrigation management, caravan security, ritual order, and exaction from dependent territories.

function.zh: 起草王令、保存奉献题记、记录工程、条约、征税与宗教奉献事项。
function.en: Drafted royal orders and kept records of dedications, public works, treaties, taxation, and religious offerings.

function.zh: 监督马里卜坝、渠道、闸门、分水制度与农田修缮，组织季节性劳役。
function.en: Supervised the Marib Dam, canals, sluices, water allocation rules, and field repairs, and organized seasonal corvée labor.

function.zh: 管理香料商路、驿站、护卫、关卡与沙漠补给点，保障长距离贸易运行。
function.en: Managed incense routes, waystations, escorts, checkpoints, and desert supply points to sustain long-distance trade.

function.zh: 协调大神庙财政、祭司任命、公共祭祀与王权合法性的宗教仪式。
function.en: Coordinated major temple finances, priestly appointments, public cults, and rituals legitimizing royal authority.

function.zh: 征收农产、香料税、牲畜贡赋与贸易关税，并管理谷仓与储备物资。
function.en: Collected agricultural produce, incense taxes, livestock tribute, and customs dues, and managed granaries and reserve goods.

function.zh: 联络附属城镇与部族首领，处理盟约、徭役分配、纠纷调停与边地统治。
function.en: Liaised with subordinate towns and tribal chiefs, handling alliances, labor quotas, dispute mediation, and frontier governance.

function.zh: 统筹驻军、征召、武器储备、边境据点与商路护卫堡垒。
function.en: Oversaw garrisons, levies, arms stores, frontier outposts, and fortified stations guarding caravan routes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托绿洲城镇、灌溉共同体与部族领地展开；王权通过任命地方代理人、承认部族首领与要求贡赋和劳役来实施统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through oasis towns, irrigation communities, and tribal territories; royal control was exercised by appointing local agents, recognizing tribal chiefs, and demanding tribute and labor.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王令、部族仲裁与神庙誓约共同构成，重视水权、土地界限、债务、贡赋义务与商队安全；铭文和公开奉献文可作为权利与义务的见证。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined customary law, royal decrees, tribal arbitration, and temple oaths, with emphasis on water rights, land boundaries, debt, tribute obligations, and caravan security; inscriptions and public dedication texts could serve as witnesses to rights and duties.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自灌溉农业产出分成、牲畜贡赋、香料与没药贸易税、商路关卡费、附属地区贡品及公共工程徭役。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was drawn mainly from shares of irrigated agricultural output, livestock tribute, incense and myrrh trade duties, caravan tolls, tribute from dependent regions, and labor obligations for public works.

religion.zh: 宗教以南阿拉伯多神信仰为核心，重要神祇包括阿勒玛卡、阿塔尔与沙姆斯；王权通过神庙奉献、祭祀与铭文宣示获得神圣合法性。
religion.en: Religion centered on South Arabian polytheism, with important deities including Almaqah, Athtar, and Shams; kingship gained sacred legitimacy through temple dedications, rituals, and inscriptions.

philosophy.zh: 其思想传统并非轴心时代意义上的系统哲学，而以宗教秩序、部族荣誉、契约、公共水利责任和王权—神权关系的实践伦理为主。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual tradition was not a systematic philosophy in the Axial Age sense, but rather a practical ethic centered on sacred order, tribal honor, covenant, communal irrigation duty, and the relationship between royal and divine authority.

writingSystem.zh: 使用古南阿拉伯文字中的萨巴字母系统书写萨巴语，主要见于石刻、建筑铭文、祭祀题记与行政短文。
writingSystem.en: Sabaic, written in the Ancient South Arabian musnad alphabet, was used mainly for stone inscriptions, building texts, dedicatory records, and short administrative texts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 最突出的成就是大型灌溉工程，尤其是马里卜坝及其配套渠道体系；此外在石建筑、神庙建筑、纪念铭文、长程商队组织和沙漠绿洲经营方面亦高度发达。
culturalAchievements.en: Its foremost achievement was large-scale irrigation engineering, especially the Marib Dam and its associated canal system; it also excelled in stone construction, temple architecture, commemorative inscription, long-distance caravan organization, and oasis management in arid environments.

languageFamily.zh: 闪米特语系南闪米特分支（古南阿拉伯语群）
languageFamily.en: Semitic language family, South Semitic branch (Ancient South Arabian group)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以灌溉农业与乳香贸易双重支柱构成。与多数阿拉伯内陆社会相比，萨巴具有异常高的剩余产出与商贸组织能力，但总体规模仍低于同时代的阿契美尼德帝国等大型农商帝国。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on twin pillars of irrigated agriculture and the incense trade. Compared with most inland Arabian societies, Saba had unusually high surplus output and commercial organization, though its overall scale remained below that of major agrarian-commercial empires such as the Achaemenid Empire.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、乳香与没药转运、商队贸易、畜牧、石工建筑、陶器制作、金属加工、神庙经济与仓储管理
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, transshipment of frankincense and myrrh, caravan trade, pastoralism, stone construction, pottery production, metalworking, temple economy, and storage management

tradeGoods.zh: 乳香、没药、香膏、葡萄干、枣、谷物、葡萄酒、牲畜、皮革、盐、铜器、石器制品，以及自外部输入的纺织品、奢侈器物和金属
tradeGoods.en: Frankincense, myrrh, aromatics, raisins, dates, grain, wine, livestock, leather, salt, copper goods, stone products, and imported textiles, luxury items, and metals

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异显著。灌溉地地主、商队投资者和神庙相关家族拥有土地、牲畜、储粮与香料收益；普通农户主要持有小块农地、果园使用权、牲畜与陶器工具。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied greatly. Owners of irrigated land, caravan investors, and temple-connected families held land, herds, stored grain, and incense profits; ordinary households mainly possessed small plots, orchard rights, livestock, and ceramic and agricultural tools.

economicSystem.zh: 经济属于以王权、部族网络与神庙机构共同调节的再分配—市场混合体系；农业剩余由水利组织保障，远程贸易则通过商队联盟、关卡和契约运行。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a redistributive-market hybrid regulated jointly by monarchy, tribal networks, and temple institutions; agricultural surplus was secured through irrigation organization, while long-distance trade operated via caravan alliances, checkpoints, and contract-like arrangements.

source.en: Irrigated agriculture taxes and land dues

category.en: Army, caravan escorts, and frontier defense

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以金属贮藏、香料库存、谷物储备和神庙保管财富共同构成。由于国家财富相当部分以实物、牲畜与贸易存货形式存在，流动性并不完全等同于铸币财政。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of stored metals, incense inventories, grain reserves, and wealth held in conjunction with temples. Since much state wealth existed in goods, herds, and trade stock, liquidity did not function like a fully monetized coin-based treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守，优先确保坝体维护、灌溉连续性和商道安全；在丰年积蓄实物和金属储备，在歉收或道路受阻时以减免、调拨和赈济稳定核心灌区。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, prioritizing dam upkeep, irrigation continuity, and caravan-route security; in good years the kingdom accumulated material and metal reserves, while in poor harvests or trade disruptions it used remissions, reallocations, and relief to stabilize the core irrigated zone.

description.zh: 由王室直属卫队、城防步兵与持矛持盾部队组成，是守卫马里卜、坝区和神庙群的核心兵力。
description.en: Composed of royal guards, garrison infantry, and spear-and-shield troops, this was the core force protecting Marib, the dam district, and temple complexes.

description.zh: 由附属与盟属部族按义务提供，平时分散，战时用于野战、护道与边地行动。
description.en: Supplied by dependent and allied tribes according to obligation, these forces were dispersed in peacetime and mobilized for field operations, route protection, and frontier actions.

description.zh: 以骆驼与部分马匹机动，负责沙漠侦察、商路巡逻、追击袭扰者和远程联络。
description.en: Mobile on camels and some horses, they conducted desert reconnaissance, route patrols, pursuit of raiders, and long-distance communications.

description.zh: 驻守关隘、仓库、闸门和关键灌溉设施，兼具军事与治安职能。
description.en: Stationed at passes, storehouses, sluice points, and key irrigation installations, they served both military and policing functions.

commanderInChief.zh: 穆卡里卜／国王
commanderInChief.en: Mukarrib / King

technology.zh: 军备以铁制与青铜制长矛、短剑、弓箭、皮革护具、木盾和石土堡垒为主；擅长沙漠行军、绿洲防御和商路据点控制，但不具备同时代大帝国的复杂攻城体系。
technology.en: Armament centered on iron and bronze spears, short swords, bows, leather armor, wooden shields, and stone-earth fortifications; Saba excelled in desert movement, oasis defense, and control of caravan strongpoints, but lacked the complex siege systems of the great empires of the age.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南阿拉伯竞争王国如盖塔班、马因与哈德拉毛之间的商路争夺、北阿拉伯和半岛部族袭扰，以及乳香产地与转运通道控制权的冲突。
threats.en: The main threats came from rival South Arabian kingdoms such as Qataban, Ma'in, and Hadramawt in struggles over trade routes, from raids by North Arabian and peninsular tribal groups, and from conflicts over control of incense-producing regions and transit corridors.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在马里卜及其灌溉农业腹地、若干绿洲聚落和商路节点，另有相当数量牧民和半定居部族分布于高地与荒漠边缘。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in Marib and its irrigated agricultural hinterland, several oasis settlements, and caravan nodes, with significant pastoral and semi-settled tribal populations spread across uplands and desert margins.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、部族贵族、神庙祭司与大商人；中层包括灌溉地农庄主、商队组织者、工匠和地方首领；下层为普通农民、牧民、劳役人口、债务依附者与家内奴仆。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included the royal house, tribal aristocracy, temple priests, and major merchants; the middle strata comprised irrigated estate holders, caravan organizers, artisans, and local notables; the lower strata consisted of common farmers, pastoralists, laborers, debt dependents, and household slaves or servants.

allies.zh: 主要盟友为部分南阿拉伯附属部族与阶段性结盟的邻近商路政体；外交关系通常以通商、婚盟、共同护道和神庙誓约维系。
allies.en: Its main allies were subordinate South Arabian tribes and, at times, neighboring trade-route polities; diplomatic relations were sustained through commerce, marriage alliances, joint route protection, and temple-backed oaths.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是争夺乳香贸易主导权的南阿拉伯邻国，尤其是盖塔班、马因与哈德拉毛，以及不受控制的沙漠部族势力。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were neighboring South Arabian states competing for dominance of the incense trade, especially Qataban, Ma'in, and Hadramawt, as well as uncontrolled desert tribal forces.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制乳香产地至红海和北阿拉伯方向的商道为核心，偏好通过盟约、附庸关系、经济封锁、护道驻军与有限战争巩固区域优势。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was centered on controlling the routes linking incense-producing regions to the Red Sea and North Arabia, favoring alliances, vassalage, commercial pressure, caravan garrisons, and limited warfare to secure regional advantage.

keyInnovations.zh: 大型拦洪坝与渠道灌溉技术、旱地与绿洲复合农业、骆驼商队后勤体系、石刻文字传统、干旱地区仓储与水资源分配管理
keyInnovations.en: Large flood-control dams and canal irrigation, combined dryland-oasis agriculture, camel-caravan logistics, a developed stone-inscription tradition, and sophisticated storage and water-allocation management in arid lands

strengths.zh: 萨巴的核心优势在于马里卜坝支撑的高产灌溉农业、对乳香贸易的中介控制、成熟的部族联盟政治和适应沙漠环境的商路组织能力。
strengths.en: Saba’s core strengths lay in the productive irrigated agriculture supported by the Marib Dam, intermediary control of the incense trade, mature tribal-alliance politics, and caravan organization adapted to desert conditions.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于经济对水利维护和贸易畅通高度依赖，政治上受部族联盟稳定性制约，且人口与军力规模不足以像近东大帝国那样长期发动大规模征服。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were the economy’s heavy dependence on irrigation maintenance and uninterrupted trade, political reliance on stable tribal coalitions, and a demographic and military scale too limited for sustained large-scale conquest like that of Near Eastern empires.

outlook.zh: 在公元前500年前后，萨巴处于繁荣阶段，只要马里卜坝得到良好维护并保持对商道与附属部族的控制，仍可继续作为南阿拉伯最重要的强国之一；但若水利失修或贸易路线转移，其优势将迅速削弱。
outlook.en: Around 500 BCE, Saba was in a thriving phase; so long as the Marib Dam remained well maintained and the kingdom preserved control over trade routes and subordinate tribes, it could continue as one of South Arabia’s foremost powers. But if irrigation decayed or trade routes shifted, its advantages would erode quickly.

description.zh: 示巴依托灌溉工程与乳香贸易，成为南阿拉伯最重要的王国之一。
description.en: Saba, sustained by irrigation and incense trade, was one of the principal kingdoms of South Arabia.

ruler.zh: 地方诸王或波斯任命的统治者

governmentForm.zh: 区域性君主制，在部分时期受阿契美尼德波斯宗主权或总督监督
governmentForm.en: Regional monarchy, in some periods under Achaemenid Persian suzerainty or satrapal oversight

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与地方贵族、祭司阶层、城市商人、工匠、农民、牧民与劳役人口构成；边境地带保留较强的部族与氏族组织
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of royal and local noble houses, priestly groups, urban merchants, artisans, cultivators, pastoralists, and laboring populations; frontier zones retained strong tribal and clan organization

rulingClass.zh: 王室、地方首领、波斯体系认可的总督官员、军事贵族与富裕商人家族
rulingClass.en: Royal houses, local chieftains, Persian-recognized satrapal officials, military aristocrats, and wealthy mercantile families

succession.zh: 通常为世袭继承，但在波斯影响地区可能需获得宗主国认可；地方豪族与军事力量对继承亦有影响
succession.en: Usually hereditary succession, though in Persian-influenced areas recognition by the overlord could matter; local noble and military support also influenced succession

structure.zh: 犍陀罗的统治结构兼具印度地方王权与阿契美尼德帝国边疆行政特征。核心地区由王廷直接控制，周边城镇、山谷与交通节点由地方首领、城镇官员及税务代理人管理；若处于波斯势力之下，则需向更大行省体系输送贡赋、兵员与情报
structure.en: Gandhara’s governing structure combined local Indic kingship with frontier administrative features associated with the Achaemenid Empire. The core zone was controlled directly by the royal court, while surrounding towns, valleys, and transit points were managed by local chiefs, town officials, and tax agents; when under Persian influence, tribute, manpower, and intelligence were forwarded into the wider satrapal system

function.zh: 记录敕令、贡赋、边界协议、商贸文书与外交往来，可能同时使用阿拉米文与当地语言书写传统
function.en: Recorded decrees, tribute, boundary agreements, commercial documents, and diplomatic correspondence, likely using Aramaic alongside local linguistic traditions

function.zh: 征收土地税、商税、道路关税、牲畜税及附属部族贡纳，并核算向上级政权或王室输送的财政份额
function.en: Collected land dues, market taxes, road customs, livestock levies, and tribute from subordinate tribes, while accounting for remittances to higher imperial authority or the royal household

function.zh: 维护塔克西拉及其他聚落的道路、驿站、市场秩序、城门与储粮设施，保障跨区域商路运行
function.en: Maintained roads, relay points, market order, gates, and granaries in Taxila and other settlements, ensuring the operation of long-distance trade routes

function.zh: 组织王室卫队、地方征召兵、边境守军与山口驻军，监视来自草原、山区与邻国的威胁
function.en: Organized the royal guard, local levies, frontier garrisons, and pass troops, monitoring threats from the steppe, mountain zones, and neighboring states

function.zh: 处理财产、债务、商贸纠纷、部族冲突与刑事案件，依照习惯法、王命与宗教规范裁决
function.en: Handled property, debt, trade disputes, tribal conflicts, and criminal cases, judging according to customary law, royal decree, and religious norms

function.zh: 协调祭祀、支持祭司与学者、管理部分圣所和教育活动，巩固王权合法性与文化声望
function.en: Coordinated rituals, supported priests and scholars, and supervised some sanctuaries and educational activity, reinforcing royal legitimacy and cultural prestige

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以城镇、农业村社、山谷部族区和商路据点为单位。塔克西拉周边由王廷直属官员管理，远郊则更多依靠地方贵族、部族首领和纳贡关系维系秩序；在波斯影响下，部分地区可能纳入更广泛的行省征税网络
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through towns, agrarian villages, valley tribal districts, and trade-route stations. Areas around Taxila were managed by officers closer to the court, while outlying zones relied more heavily on local nobles, tribal chiefs, and tributary arrangements; under Persian influence, some districts were integrated into a broader satrapal tax network

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以习惯法为基础，受王令、部族传统、祭司规范与商业契约共同影响。城市中较重视债务、贸易、重量衡量与契约执行，乡村与山地则更多依赖长老调解、赔偿与誓约。严重叛乱、抢掠与拒贡会受到军事镇压
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on customary law, shaped by royal commands, tribal tradition, priestly norms, and commercial contracts. In towns there was stronger concern for debt, trade, weights and measures, and contract enforcement, while rural and mountain areas relied more on elder mediation, compensation, and oath-taking. Serious rebellion, banditry, and refusal of tribute could trigger military suppression

taxationSystem.zh: 税制包括农产实物征收、灌溉或土地赋、市场税、道路与渡口关税、牲畜贡纳以及附属地区定额贡赋。部分赋税以谷物、畜产、纺织品、木材、金属或银锭缴纳；跨区域商队是重要税源
taxationSystem.en: Taxation included agricultural payments in kind, land or irrigation dues, market taxes, road and ferry customs, livestock tribute, and fixed obligations from subordinate districts. Some dues were paid in grain, animal products, textiles, timber, metals, or silver by weight; long-distance caravans were an especially important source of revenue

religion.zh: 宗教生活高度多元，以早期婆罗门传统、地方神祇崇拜、祖先祭祀与伊朗文化影响并存；祆教观念可能通过波斯行政与驻军间接传播，但尚未完全取代本地信仰结构。此时期佛教尚未形成国家层面的重要力量
religion.en: Religious life was highly plural, combining early Brahmanical traditions, local deity cults, ancestor rites, and Iranian cultural influences; Zoroastrian ideas may have spread indirectly through Persian administration and garrisons, but did not replace local belief structures. Buddhism had not yet emerged as a major state-level force at this date

philosophy.zh: 思想处于轴心时代背景中的交汇地带，包含吠陀祭祀思想、早期奥义书式思辨、政治实用主义以及来自伊朗世界的王权与秩序观念。塔克西拉逐渐成为跨地区知识交流中心
philosophy.en: Thought in Gandhara stood at an Axial Age crossroads, including Vedic ritual ideas, early Upanishadic-style speculation, political pragmatism, and notions of kingship and order arriving from the Iranian world. Taxila was becoming a center of transregional intellectual exchange

writingSystem.zh: 书写使用并不完全统一。官方与跨区域行政往来可能采用帝国阿拉米文，地方口语属于早期印度-雅利安语支，文书传统尚未形成后世标准化的犍陀罗佉卢文体系；口述传承仍极为重要
writingSystem.en: Writing was not fully standardized. Official and long-distance administrative exchange may have used Imperial Aramaic, while local speech belonged to early Indo-Aryan varieties; the later standardized Gandhari Kharosthi tradition had not yet fully formed. Oral transmission remained extremely important

culturalAchievements.zh: 塔克西拉作为教育与商贸中心开始积累声望，促成祭司、学者、医生、商人与官员的交流；犍陀罗在建筑、手工业、跨文化审美与道路网络组织方面表现突出，为后世著名的犍陀罗文化奠定基础
culturalAchievements.en: Taxila was beginning to gain renown as a center of learning and commerce, facilitating exchange among priests, scholars, physicians, merchants, and officials; Gandhara stood out in architecture, crafts, cross-cultural aesthetics, and road-network organization, laying foundations for the later celebrated Gandharan cultural sphere

languageFamily.zh: 主要为印度-伊朗语系，其中地方俗语偏向早期印度-雅利安语，行政与外交层面受伊朗语和阿拉米语影响
languageFamily.en: Primarily Indo-Iranian, with local vernaculars leaning toward early Indo-Aryan speech, while administration and diplomacy were influenced by Iranian languages and Aramaic

gdpDescription.zh: 犍陀罗经济在当时属中上水平，依托肥沃河谷农业、畜牧、手工业与横贯欧亚交通线的中转贸易。其财富并非仅来自粮食生产，更来自塔克西拉等城市对商旅、税收、仓储、转运和文化服务的整合能力
gdpDescription.en: Gandhara’s economy was above average for its time, drawing on fertile river-valley agriculture, pastoralism, crafts, and transit trade across Eurasian routes. Its wealth came not only from food production but from the ability of Taxila and other centers to integrate merchants, taxation, warehousing, redistribution, and cultural services

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧业、纺织、金属加工、木器与车具制造、宝石与装饰品加工、贸易转运、驿站服务
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, textiles, metalworking, woodworking and cart-making, gemstone and ornament production, trade transshipment, and relay-station services

tradeGoods.zh: 大麦、小麦、稻米、芝麻、羊毛、皮革、马匹、牛、木材、铜器、铁器、半宝石、象牙制品、香料与来自印度内地、伊朗和中亚的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Barley, wheat, rice, sesame, wool, leather, horses, cattle, timber, copper goods, iron tools, semi-precious stones, ivory goods, spices, and luxury items moving between inner India, Iran, and Central Asia

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备、工具与家族劳动力；城市富户则拥有仓储、商队股份、金属器、珠饰及可流通银锭。整体贫富差距明显，但中层商人和手工业者数量较周边纯农业地区更多
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly held in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, tools, and family labor; urban elites possessed warehouses, caravan shares, metal goods, ornaments, and movable silver bullion. Inequality was marked, but the number of middling merchants and artisans was larger than in purely agrarian neighboring regions

economicSystem.zh: 以农业基础上的市场交换和贡赋体系并行为特征，城市贸易受王权、地方豪族与跨地区商人共同支配；货币化程度有限但高于许多内陆农业地区
economicSystem.en: Characterized by a mix of agrarian production, market exchange, and tributary obligations, with urban trade shaped jointly by royal authority, local elites, and transregional merchants; monetization was limited but higher than in many inland agrarian regions

source.en: Tribute from Subordinate Tribes and Districts

source.zh: 王室地产、罚金与杂项收入
source.en: Royal Estates, Fines, and Miscellaneous Income

category.en: Royal Household, Bureaucracy, and Local Administration

category.zh: 道路、驿站、仓储与城市维护
category.en: Roads, Relay Stations, Storage, and Urban Maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以银锭、谷物、牲畜、纺织品及其他实物形式分散储存于塔克西拉和若干战略据点。由于边疆政治环境复杂，保持可调动的实物储备比单纯积累贵金属更重要
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was stored in a mixed form of silver bullion, grain, livestock, textiles, and other goods dispersed between Taxila and several strategic points. Because the frontier political environment was complex, maintaining mobilizable stores in kind was more important than merely hoarding precious metal

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重视稳定征收、保障交通线和维持边防。统治者倾向于在不破坏商贸活力的前提下征收关税，并通过馈赠、减免与协商维系地方豪族和部族忠诚
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized stable collection, secure routes, and frontier defense. Rulers tended to tax commerce without crushing trade, while using gifts, remissions, and negotiation to maintain the loyalty of local nobles and tribes

description.zh: 以矛兵、盾兵、弓箭手为主，来源包括王室直属兵、城市守军与乡村征召兵，是主要作战力量
description.en: Made up mainly of spearmen, shield-bearers, and archers drawn from royal troops, town garrisons, and rural levies; this was the main combat arm

description.zh: 依托西北边地传统和伊朗影响发展，适合侦察、追击与边境快速机动，在本地区具有较高军事价值
description.en: Developed through northwestern frontier traditions and Iranian influence, suitable for scouting, pursuit, and rapid border response, and of high military value in the region

description.zh: 战车仍具仪式与战术用途，但重要性已下降，多用于平坦地带和王室展示
description.en: Chariots still had ceremonial and tactical roles, but their importance had declined; they were used mainly on flatter ground and in royal display

description.zh: 驻守山口、河渡与要道，熟悉地形，负责截击袭扰者和保护商旅通道
description.en: Stationed at passes, river crossings, and key routes, these troops knew the terrain and were tasked with blocking raiders and protecting trade corridors

description.zh: 负责护卫统治者、都城与国库，也在关键战役中担任精锐预备队
description.en: Protected the ruler, capital, and treasury, and served as an elite reserve in major engagements

commanderInChief.zh: 国王或受命总督
commanderInChief.en: The King or Appointed Governor

technology.zh: 军备处于铁器时代区域先进水平，使用铁制兵器、复合弓、皮革与织物护具、战车和一定规模骑兵；受波斯帝国军事组织影响，重视道路、补给和多族群协同
technology.en: Military technology stood at a regionally advanced Iron Age level, using iron weapons, composite bows, leather and textile armor, chariots, and a meaningful cavalry arm; under Persian influence, organization emphasized roads, logistics, and cooperation across multiple ethnic groups

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自边疆部族叛乱、山地袭扰、斯基泰—萨迦草原力量的间接压力，以及在波斯帝国内部权力变化时可能引发的统治重组；同时需警惕恒河流域强国西进对贸易与外交平衡的影响
threats.en: Main threats came from frontier tribal unrest, mountain raiding, indirect pressure from Scythian-Saka steppe groups, and possible administrative upheaval when power shifted within the Persian Empire; Gandhara also had to watch the westward and northwestern implications of stronger Gangetic states for trade and diplomacy

populationDescription.zh: 人口约一百万，集中于河谷、农耕平原、交通节点和塔克西拉周边。人口构成多元，包含定居农业社群、城市居民、商旅群体、部族人口和一定数量的外来行政或军事人员
populationDescription.en: The population was about one million, concentrated in river valleys, farming plains, transport nodes, and around Taxila. It was socially and ethnically mixed, including settled agrarian communities, urban residents, merchant groups, tribal populations, and a smaller number of foreign administrative or military personnel

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族、祭司和高级官员；中层为商人、书记、工匠首领、富农和军官；下层为普通农民、牧民、工匠、雇工与劳役人口。部族社会中则同时存在氏族首领与自由战士结构
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal house, nobles, priests, and senior officials; the middle strata included merchants, scribes, master artisans, prosperous farmers, and officers; the lower strata comprised ordinary cultivators, pastoralists, artisans, laborers, and dependent workers. Tribal society also preserved structures of clan chiefs and free warriors

allies.zh: 在实际政治上更接近阿契美尼德波斯帝国体系，并与若干地方部族、商人共同体和邻近北印度政权保持务实关系
allies.en: In practical politics Gandhara was aligned most closely with the Achaemenid Persian imperial system, while maintaining pragmatic ties with local tribes, merchant communities, and neighboring North Indian polities

enemies.zh: 没有长期单一宿敌，但边疆山地部族、拒贡势力、草原游动群体以及任何威胁商路控制权的邻近强权都可能成为敌手
enemies.en: It had no single permanent archenemy, but mountain tribes on the frontier, tribute-refusing groups, mobile steppe peoples, and any nearby power threatening control of trade routes could become enemies

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持交通线安全、在印度与伊朗世界之间保持中介地位、避免卷入不必要大战并灵活承认更强宗主权为核心。犍陀罗善于通过通婚、馈赠、纳贡、商贸协定和边境调解来维护稳定
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on securing routes, preserving an intermediary position between the Indian and Iranian worlds, avoiding unnecessary large-scale wars, and flexibly acknowledging stronger overlordship when required. Gandhara relied on marriage ties, gifts, tribute, trade agreements, and border mediation to maintain stability

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具与兵器的普及、较成熟的道路与驿站网络、跨区域称量银和标准化度量习惯、复合弓使用、城市性教育中心的形成以及阿拉米文行政文书技术的引入
keyInnovations.en: The spread of iron tools and weapons, relatively developed road and relay networks, transregional use of weighed silver and more standardized measures, employment of the composite bow, emergence of urban learning centers, and the introduction of Aramaic administrative writing techniques

strengths.zh: 地理位置极佳，控制印度、伊朗和中亚之间的关键通道；贸易和文化交流活跃；塔克西拉具备长期成长为学术与商业中心的潜力；在波斯与印度世界之间拥有战略缓冲价值
strengths.en: Its location was exceptional, controlling key corridors between India, Iran, and Central Asia; trade and cultural exchange were vibrant; Taxila had strong long-term potential as a scholarly and commercial center; and Gandhara possessed strategic buffer value between the Persian and Indian worlds

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度有限，地方豪族、部族势力和外部宗主权并存，易导致行政碎片化；边疆广阔而难守；经济虽然繁荣，但对商路安全和外部大国稳定高度敏感
weaknesses.en: Political integration was limited, with local nobles, tribal forces, and external overlordship coexisting in ways that could fragment administration; the frontier was broad and hard to defend; and although prosperous, the economy was highly sensitive to route security and the stability of larger surrounding powers

outlook.zh: 到公元前500年前后，犍陀罗总体稳定并具有上升潜力。只要波斯宗主体系未发生严重崩解、商路维持畅通且地方精英继续合作，犍陀罗将继续作为西北印度最重要的枢纽地区之一；但若帝国权力更替或草原压力加剧，其边疆性也会迅速转化为脆弱性
outlook.en: Around 500 BCE, Gandhara was broadly stable and possessed upward potential. So long as the Persian imperial framework did not suffer severe collapse, trade routes remained open, and local elites continued to cooperate, Gandhara would remain one of the key hub regions of northwestern India; but if imperial succession struggles or steppe pressures intensified, its frontier character could quickly become a vulnerability

description.zh: 犍陀罗位于印度河上游，处于印度、伊朗与中亚交汇地带，部分地区受波斯影响。
description.en: Gandhara lay at the crossroads of India, Iran, and Central Asia, with parts of the region under Persian influence.

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭君主为核心、辅以宫廷官僚与地方酋长网络的扩张型君主制
governmentForm.en: An expansionist hereditary monarchy centered on the king, supported by a court bureaucracy and networks of local chiefs

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、婆罗门祭司、刹帝利武士与官员、城市商人与手工业者、村社农民、雇工与奴婢构成；同时保留森林部族与边缘社群的半整合结构。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and nobles, Brahmin ritual specialists, Kshatriya warriors and officials, urban merchants and artisans, village cultivators, laborers and servants, while forest tribes and marginal communities remained only partly integrated.

rulingClass.zh: 诃黎王迦王室、宫廷贵族、军事首领、婆罗门顾问以及受王权扶植的地方豪强
rulingClass.en: The Haryanka royal family, court nobles, military commanders, Brahmin advisers, and locally powerful chiefs patronized by the crown

succession.zh: 父子世袭为常态，但王位继承常受宫廷派系、诸王子竞争与贵族支持所影响
succession.en: Succession was normally patrilineal, though court factions, rival princes, and noble backing often influenced the actual transfer of power

structure.zh: 王权集中于王舍城宫廷之中，由国王统领军事、司法、外交与赋税。中央依赖近臣、书记、财政征收者、城防官与地方总管；新并入地区常以臣服诸侯、封地首领或直接派驻官员的混合方式治理。
structure.en: Authority was concentrated at the court of Rajagriha, where the king directed military, judicial, diplomatic, and fiscal affairs. The center relied on close advisers, scribes, revenue collectors, city officials, and provincial overseers; newly incorporated areas were governed through a mixed system of subordinate rulers, landed chiefs, and directly appointed officials.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室财产、侍从、接见与诏令传达
function.en: Managed the palace, royal estates, attendants, audiences, and transmission of royal orders

function.zh: 征收土地税、贡赋、关税，管理粮仓与实物储备
function.en: Collected land tax, tribute, and customs dues, and managed granaries and stores in kind

function.zh: 统筹常备军、征召兵、象队、堡垒与边防
function.en: Coordinated the standing army, levies, elephant corps, forts, and frontier defense

function.zh: 监督属地、村社首领、道路、渡口与交通节点
function.en: Oversaw territories, village headmen, roads, ferries, and transport nodes

function.zh: 处理争讼、惩罚、拘押、城市治安与市场秩序
function.en: Handled disputes, punishments, detention, urban policing, and market regulation

function.zh: 接待使节、安排婚盟、谈判停战与处理朝贡关系
function.en: Received envoys, arranged marriage alliances, negotiated truces, and managed tributary relations

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托村社首领、地区豪族与王室委任官并行。核心地带实行更直接的税收与司法控制，边缘地区则常通过盟约、婚姻与驻军维系统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined village headmen, regional notables, and royal appointees. Core territories were subjected to more direct fiscal and judicial control, while frontier zones were often held through alliances, marriages, and garrisons.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命、赔偿原则与宗教规范并存。案件多在地方层级裁决，重案或涉贵族事务由国王及宫廷审断；刑罚包括罚金、没收、体罚、监禁与处死。
legalSystem.en: The legal order combined customary law, royal commands, compensation principles, and religious norms. Most cases were settled locally, while major crimes or noble disputes were judged by the king and court; penalties included fines, confiscation, corporal punishment, imprisonment, and execution.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖农业收成分成、土地贡赋、牧养与林产征收、贸易关税、渡口费以及附庸供献。税收多以粮食、牲畜、金属与劳役形式缴纳。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal income came chiefly from shares of agricultural produce, land tribute, levies on pastoral and forest products, trade customs, ferry tolls, and contributions from dependents. Taxes were often paid in grain, livestock, metals, and labor service.

religion.zh: 摩揭陀的宗教生活以晚期吠陀祭祀传统、地方神祇崇拜、祖先礼仪与新兴沙门运动并存为特征。频婆娑罗时代，佛教与耆那教在王舍城及周边获得显著活动空间。
religion.en: Religious life in Magadha was marked by the coexistence of late Vedic sacrificial traditions, local deity cults, ancestor rites, and emerging shramana movements. In the age of Bimbisara, Buddhism and Jainism gained notable space for activity in Rajagriha and its environs.

philosophy.zh: 思想上处于“轴心时代”转折点，既有婆罗门关于祭祀、社会秩序与达摩的讨论，也有沙门群体对业、轮回、苦、解脱、禁欲与伦理自律的深刻反思。
philosophy.en: Intellectually, Magadha stood at an Axial Age turning point, with Brahmanical discussions of sacrifice, social order, and dharma alongside shramana reflections on karma, rebirth, suffering, liberation, asceticism, and ethical self-discipline.

writingSystem.zh: 文字使用尚不普及，行政与商业仍以口述、记忆和符记为主。学术传统主要依赖口传；若有书写，多可能使用早期地方记号系统，而成熟的婆罗米文字尚未广泛见于国家行政。
writingSystem.en: Writing was not yet widely institutionalized, and administration and commerce still relied heavily on oral record, memory, and marks or tokens. Learned traditions were chiefly transmitted orally; where writing existed, it was likely limited and local, while mature Brahmi script was not yet broadly visible in state administration.

culturalAchievements.zh: 王舍城成为恒河中游重要的宗教辩论与思想交流中心；城市防御、石垒工程、宫廷赞助、商旅网络以及对佛陀与耆那教导师活动的包容，使摩揭陀在北印度文化史上地位突出。
culturalAchievements.en: Rajagriha became a major center of religious debate and intellectual exchange in the middle Ganges. Urban fortification, stone ramparts, court patronage, merchant networks, and relative openness to the activities of the Buddha and Jain teachers gave Magadha a prominent place in North Indian cultural history.

languageFamily.zh: 印度-伊朗语族之印度-雅利安语支，以东部中古印度雅语前身和俗语为主，祭祀与学术场合并使用古印度雅语传统
languageFamily.en: Indo-Iranian, specifically Indo-Aryan, with eastern vernacular predecessors of Middle Indo-Aryan dominant in daily life, alongside Old Indo-Aryan traditions in ritual and learned contexts

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 摩揭陀经济以高产农业为核心，辅以铁器生产、林产采集、手工业和区域贸易。中恒河流域肥沃土地、河运与政治扩张共同支撑其繁荣，但整体仍是以农村自给和实物交换为主体的前货币经济。
gdpDescription.en: Magadha’s economy was centered on productive agriculture, supplemented by ironworking, forest extraction, craft production, and regional trade. The fertile middle Ganges plain, river transport, and political expansion underpinned its prosperity, though it remained fundamentally a pre-monetized economy dominated by rural subsistence and payments in kind.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻与杂粮农业、畜牧业、铁器冶炼与锻造、木材与林产、陶器、纺织、车辆制造、盐与地方贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice and mixed grain agriculture, animal husbandry, iron smelting and forging, timber and forest products, pottery, textiles, cart-making, salt exchange, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、大麦、豆类、油料、布匹、陶器、铁制农具与兵器、木材、蜂蜜、漆、象牙、牲畜、盐和半宝石
tradeGoods.en: Rice, barley, pulses, oilseeds, cloth, pottery, iron tools and weapons, timber, honey, lac, ivory, livestock, salt, and semi-precious stones

name.zh: 秤量金属与早期打记银片
name.en: Weighed metal and early punch-marked silver pieces

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富体现为耕地使用权、谷物储备、牛只、农具和亲属劳动力。城市商人和宫廷供给者拥有较高积累，边缘部族则更多依赖林地资源和互惠交换。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth took the form of cultivation rights, grain stocks, cattle, tools, and family labor. Urban merchants and palace suppliers possessed greater accumulations, while frontier tribes depended more on forest resources and reciprocal exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业为基础、由王权征收实物税并通过城市市场和长途商路整合的混合经济；货币使用有限但在精英交换和远距贸易中逐步增加。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on village agriculture, with the crown extracting taxes in kind and integrating production through urban markets and long-distance routes; monetary use was limited but increasingly present in elite exchange and long-distance trade.

source.en: Contributions from subordinates and local chiefs
Fines, war booty, and miscellaneous receipts

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以粮仓、牲畜、金属储藏、贡物与少量贵金属器具构成，并非完全货币化。王舍城及周边要塞储备可用于战争、赈济和宫廷赏赐。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of granary reserves, livestock, stored metals, tribute goods, and a limited stock of precious metal objects rather than a fully monetized hoard. Reserves at Rajagriha and nearby strongholds could be used for war, relief, and royal rewards.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持军备、扩大核心耕地控制、巩固贸易节点并积累实物储备；在丰年储粮、在战争或扩张时强化征发。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining military power, extending control over core farmland, securing trade nodes, and building reserves in kind; grain was stockpiled in good years, while requisitioning intensified during war or expansion.

description.zh: 军队主体，由持矛、弓、盾与刀斧的农兵和职业兵组成，适于守城、列阵与区域征伐。
description.en: The main body of the army, composed of peasant levies and professional fighters armed with spears, bows, shields, and blades, suited for garrison duty, line combat, and regional campaigning.

description.zh: 保留旧式贵族战争传统，用于指挥、冲击和战场威望展示，但在恒河流域复杂地形中的作用已相对有限。
description.en: A survival of older aristocratic warfare, used for command, shock, and battlefield prestige, though its practical role was increasingly limited in the complex terrain of the Ganges region.

description.zh: 数量不如后世印度帝国，但已用于侦察、追击、侧翼机动和联络。
description.en: Smaller than in later Indian empires, but already useful for reconnaissance, pursuit, flank movement, and communications.

description.zh: 摩揭陀最具威慑力的兵种之一，用于冲击敌阵、破坏士气和体现王权实力。
description.en: One of Magadha’s most intimidating arms, used to break formations, shatter morale, and symbolize royal power.

description.zh: 负责王舍城、边境据点、粮道、仓储和工程保障，维持长期作战能力。
description.en: Responsible for Rajagriha, frontier posts, supply routes, storage, and engineering support, sustaining long campaigns.

commanderInChief.zh: 频婆娑罗王
commanderInChief.en: King Bimbisara

technology.zh: 军事技术处于铁器时代成熟阶段，广泛使用铁制兵器、弓箭、战车、象军、土石城防与山地堡垒。王舍城多重石垒与天然山势相结合，形成强大防御体系。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a mature Iron Age stage, with widespread use of iron weapons, bows, chariots, elephants, earthen and stone fortifications, and hill strongholds. Rajagriha’s multiple stone ramparts combined with natural hills to create a formidable defensive system.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自恒河中游竞争强国，如拘萨罗、阿般提、跋耆联盟与波罗奈—跋沙一带势力；此外，新征服地区的反叛、边缘部族袭扰和交通线脆弱性也构成持续挑战。
threats.en: Major threats came from rival powers of the middle Ganges such as Kosala, Avanti, the Vajji confederacy, and powers around the Varanasi-Vatsa sphere; rebellions in newly conquered territories, raids by frontier tribes, and vulnerable communication lines were also persistent challenges.

populationDescription.zh: 人口规模在当时北印度诸国中属上升且庞大的水平，集中于恒河及其支流流域的村庄、农田和新兴城镇。人口增长受益于农业扩展、森林开垦与国家兼并。
populationDescription.en: Its population was large and growing by the standards of contemporary North Indian states, concentrated in villages, fields, and emerging towns along the Ganges and its tributaries. Growth was supported by agricultural expansion, forest clearance, and political consolidation.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族、祭司与高级武士；中层包括商人、地主、手工业者和地方首领；下层主要是农民、佃作者、劳工、仆役与依附民。种姓秩序在东部地区仍在调整，与部族身份和地方传统交织。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the royal family, nobles, priests, and senior warriors; the middle included merchants, landholders, artisans, and local chiefs; the lower strata were mainly cultivators, tenants, laborers, servants, and dependents. Varna hierarchy in the eastern regions was still evolving and interacted with tribal identities and local traditions.

allies.zh: 通过婚盟与务实外交，摩揭陀在不同时期与拘萨罗及部分邻近势力维持暂时合作；同时积极争取商人集团、边缘酋长和新附属地区的支持。
allies.en: Through marriage alliances and pragmatic diplomacy, Magadha maintained periods of cooperation with Kosala and certain neighboring powers; it also actively sought the support of merchant groups, frontier chiefs, and newly subordinated territories.

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括阿般提、跋耆联盟及中恒河地区其他竞争性大国；与安伽虽已取得优势，但其整合仍需持续压制地方反抗。
enemies.en: Its principal opponents included Avanti, the Vajji confederacy, and other competing great states of the middle Ganges; although Magadha had gained the upper hand over Anga, its integration still required continued suppression of local resistance.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策兼具扩张、联姻、贸易控制与现实结盟。频婆娑罗倾向先以外交稳定一线对手，再集中兵力吞并关键河谷与商路节点，从而建立区域霸权。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy combined expansion, intermarriage, trade control, and pragmatic alliance. Bimbisara tended to stabilize one rival through diplomacy while concentrating force on annexing key river valleys and trade nodes, thereby building regional hegemony.

era.zh: 轴心时代铁器时代北印度国家形成期
era.en: Axial Age Iron Age state-formation period in North India

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具促进森林开垦与农业扩张；铁兵器提升军队战力；石垒与山城防御技术增强都城安全；河谷交通、仓储和早期货币化交换促进区域整合。
keyInnovations.en: Iron agricultural tools promoted forest clearance and agrarian expansion; iron weapons improved military effectiveness; stone ramparts and hill-fort defenses strengthened the capital; riverine transport, storage systems, and early monetized exchange supported regional integration.

strengths.zh: 摩揭陀拥有肥沃土地、增长中的人口、较强的铁器生产基础、稳固的山地都城、象军优势以及善于兼并和结盟的王权。这些因素使其成为北印度最具统一潜力的国家之一。
strengths.en: Magadha possessed fertile land, a growing population, a relatively strong ironworking base, a secure hill capital, an elephant advantage, and a monarchy skilled in conquest and alliance. These factors made it one of the North Indian states with the greatest potential for unification.

weaknesses.zh: 国家整合仍不彻底，地方豪强与新附属地区忠诚有限；行政书写和官僚制度尚未高度成熟；财政多依赖实物征收，远程统治成本较高。
weaknesses.en: State integration remained incomplete, with limited loyalty from local magnates and newly subordinated regions; administrative writing and bureaucracy were not yet highly developed; and the fiscal system relied heavily on payments in kind, making long-distance rule costly.

outlook.zh: 若继续维持王室稳定、控制恒河中游商路并压制阿般提与跋耆等竞争者，摩揭陀极可能进一步扩张，并在后续数代成为北印度统一帝国的核心。
outlook.en: If it could maintain dynastic stability, control the trade routes of the middle Ganges, and overcome rivals such as Avanti and the Vajji confederacy, Magadha was highly likely to expand further and become the core of a later North Indian unifying empire.

description.zh: 摩揭陀是恒河中下游最具扩张性的国家之一，日后将成为统一北印的核心。
description.en: Magadha was one of the most expansionist states in the middle Ganges and would later become the core of North Indian unification.

name.zh: 诃黎旃陀罗/频婆娑罗王朝

ideology.zh: 军事扩张与恒河霸权——以武力吞并周边诸国，控制恒河流域贸易路线，建立以华氏城为中心的区域帝国
ideology.en: Military expansion and Ganges hegemony — annexing neighboring states by force, controlling Ganges basin trade routes, and building a regional empire centered on Pataliputra

relationship.zh: 诃黎旃陀罗王朝是摩揭陀国家权力的核心。频婆娑罗通过联姻、外交与征伐建立了恒河流域的霸权基础，其子阿阇世则以更加激进的军事手段继续扩张，先后吞并安伽和迦尸，并与跋耆联邦和拘萨罗展开争夺。王朝对国家政策拥有最高决定权，但必须与婆罗门祭司阶层和新兴沙门教团维持微妙平衡。
relationship.en: The Haryanka dynasty was the core of Magadhan state power. Bimbisara built the foundations of Ganges hegemony through marriage alliances, diplomacy, and conquest, while his son Ajatashatru continued expansion with more aggressive military means, annexing Anga and Kashi and contending with the Vajji confederacy and Kosala. The dynasty held supreme authority over state policy but had to maintain a delicate balance with the Brahmanical priestly class and the emerging shramana movements.

description.zh: 诃黎旃陀罗王朝是摩揭陀崛起为北印度霸主的关键王朝。频婆娑罗（约前544-前492年在位）被视为摩揭陀帝国化进程的奠基者，他通过与拘萨罗、跋耆和犍陀罗的联姻建立了广泛的外交网络，吞并了东方的安伽国，控制了通往孟加拉湾的贸易通道。频婆娑罗还是佛教和耆那教的重要王室赞助者，与佛陀和大雄都保持了密切关系。阿阇世据传弑父篡位后继承王国，但他证明了自己是一位更为进取的军事领袖。他改进了摩揭陀的军事技术——包括使用新型投石器和带刀轮的战车——先后征服了跋耆联邦和拘萨罗王国，将摩揭陀的领土扩大了一倍以上。阿阇世下令修建华氏城（巴特那利普特拉）的城防，这座城市后来成为古代世界最大的城市之一。诃黎旃陀罗王朝统治下的摩揭陀积累了巨大的军事和财政资源，为后来的孔雀帝国统一印度奠定了基础。
description.en: The Haryanka dynasty was the pivotal dynasty in Magadha's rise to North Indian hegemony. Bimbisara (r. c. 544-492 BCE) is regarded as the founder of Magadha's imperialization, building an extensive diplomatic network through marriages with Kosala, the Vajji confederacy, and Gandhara, annexing Anga to the east, and controlling trade routes to the Bay of Bengal. Bimbisara was also an important royal patron of Buddhism and Jainism, maintaining close relations with both the Buddha and Mahavira. Ajatashatru allegedly usurped the throne by killing his father but proved an even more aggressive military leader. He improved Magadhan military technology — including new catapults and scythed chariots — and successively conquered the Vajji confederacy and Kosala, more than doubling Magadha's territory. Ajatashatru ordered the construction of fortifications at Pataliputra, which would become one of the largest cities of the ancient world. Under Haryanka rule, Magadha accumulated immense military and fiscal resources that laid the groundwork for the later Maurya Empire's unification of India.

role.zh: 军事扩张的推动者，征服跋耆与拘萨罗，奠定摩揭陀霸权
role.en: Driver of military expansion who conquered the Vajji confederacy and Kosala, establishing Magadhan hegemony

role.zh: 摩揭陀帝国化的奠基者，以外交联姻和渐进征服建立区域霸权
role.en: Founder of Magadha's imperialization, building regional hegemony through diplomatic marriages and gradual conquest

ideology.zh: 佛法——四圣谛、八正道、中道、业报轮回与涅槃解脱，主张众生平等，反对婆罗门种姓特权
ideology.en: The Dharma — Four Noble Truths, Noble Eightfold Path, the Middle Way, karma-samsara and nirvana liberation, advocating equality of all beings and opposing Brahmanical caste privilege

headquarters.zh: 王舍城竹林精舍 / 毗舍离
headquarters.en: Bamboo Grove Monastery at Rajagriha / Vaishali

relationship.zh: 佛教僧伽与摩揭陀王室形成了互利的赞助关系。频婆娑罗是佛陀最重要的王室赞助者之一，赠予竹林精舍作为僧团驻地。阿阇世虽一度与佛教疏远，但后来也成为佛陀的重要支持者。佛教为摩揭陀王权提供了超越婆罗门种姓体系的道德合法性，而王室赞助则为僧团的扩张提供了物质基础。
relationship.en: The Buddhist sangha formed a mutually beneficial patronage relationship with the Magadhan royal house. Bimbisara was one of the Buddha's most important royal patrons, donating the Bamboo Grove monastery as a monastic residence. Though Ajatashatru initially distanced himself from Buddhism, he later became an important supporter of the Buddha. Buddhism provided Magadhan kingship with moral legitimacy transcending the Brahmanical caste system, while royal patronage gave the sangha a material foundation for expansion.

description.zh: 公元前500年前后，释迦牟尼佛正处于其传法事业的鼎盛时期。佛陀于约前528年在菩提伽耶悟道后，在恒河流域各地传播佛法，建立了一个严密组织的僧侣共同体（僧伽）。佛教的核心教义——四圣谛（苦、集、灭、道）和八正道——提供了一条不依赖婆罗门祭祀权威的解脱之路，吸引了从国王到商人、工匠乃至贱民的广泛信众。王舍城和毗舍离是佛教活动的两大中心，频婆娑罗赠予的竹林精舍成为最早的佛教寺院之一。佛教僧伽的组织特色在于其民主化的决策方式——僧团事务通过集体讨论和投票解决，这在当时的印度社会中极为独特。佛教对种姓制度的挑战和对个人修行的强调，使其在商人阶层和城市居民中尤其受到欢迎。到佛陀于约前483年涅槃时，佛教已发展为北印度最有影响力的宗教运动之一。
description.en: Around 500 BCE, Gautama Buddha was at the height of his teaching career. After attaining enlightenment at Bodh Gaya around 528 BCE, the Buddha spread the Dharma across the Ganges basin, establishing a rigorously organized monastic community (sangha). Buddhism's core teachings — the Four Noble Truths (suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path) and the Noble Eightfold Path — offered a path to liberation independent of Brahmanical sacrificial authority, attracting a wide following from kings and merchants to artisans and outcasts. Rajagriha and Vaishali were the two great centers of Buddhist activity, with the Bamboo Grove donated by Bimbisara becoming one of the earliest Buddhist monasteries. The sangha's distinctive feature was its democratic decision-making — monastic affairs were resolved through collective discussion and voting, a practice highly unusual in Indian society at the time. Buddhism's challenge to the caste system and its emphasis on individual practice made it particularly popular among the merchant class and urban populations. By the time of the Buddha's parinirvana around 483 BCE, Buddhism had become one of the most influential religious movements in northern India.

role.zh: 佛教创始人，正在恒河流域传法，建立僧团组织
role.en: Founder of Buddhism, currently teaching the Dharma across the Ganges basin and establishing the monastic order

role.zh: 佛陀最重要的弟子之一，精通教理，协助教团组织管理
role.en: One of the Buddha's most important disciples, master of doctrine, and aide in sangha organization

leaderTitle.zh: 第二十四祖提尔丹嘉拉

ideology.zh: 耆那教法——极端非暴力（阿希姆萨）、严格苦修、灵魂净化、业力解脱，反对吠陀祭祀与种姓歧视
ideology.en: Jain Dharma — radical non-violence (ahimsa), strict asceticism, purification of the soul, liberation from karma, opposing Vedic sacrifice and caste discrimination

relationship.zh: 耆那教社团与摩揭陀王室及商人阶层保持着紧密的赞助关系。大雄与频婆娑罗和阿阇世都有交往记录，耆那教的非暴力和苦修理念在恒河流域商人群体中尤其受到尊崇。耆那教与佛教在争取王室赞助方面形成了温和的竞争关系。
relationship.en: The Jain community maintained close patronage ties with the Magadhan royal house and the merchant class. Mahavira had recorded interactions with both Bimbisara and Ajatashatru, and Jain ideals of non-violence and asceticism were especially revered among the Ganges valley merchant communities. Jainism and Buddhism formed a moderate rivalry in competing for royal patronage.

description.zh: 公元前500年前后，大雄（筏驮摩那）正处于其传教事业的晚期阶段。大雄是跋耆联邦中刹帝利贵族出身的苦行者，约于前527年放弃世俗生活，经过十二年极端苦修后达到全知（凯瓦利亚），此后三十年间在恒河流域各地传播耆那教义。耆那教主张极端的非暴力——不仅不伤害人类和动物，甚至努力避免对昆虫和微生物的伤害。其修行体系强调五大誓言：不杀生、不妄语、不偷盗、不淫欲、不执著。耆那教对商人阶层具有特殊吸引力，因为其伦理体系强调诚信交易和财富的正当积累，同时非暴力原则使商业成为最值得尊敬的职业之一。大雄建立的教团分为僧侣和在家居士两个层次，形成了完整的宗教共同体。在摩揭陀，耆那教与佛教并行发展，两者在教义上存在重要分歧——尤其是关于苦修的必要性和灵魂本质的问题——但在反对婆罗门种姓特权和祭祀体系方面立场一致。大雄约于前468年在华氏城附近入灭，但其教团延续至今。
description.en: Around 500 BCE, Mahavira (Vardhamana) was in the late phase of his teaching career. Mahavira was an ascetic of Kshatriya noble birth from the Vajji confederacy who renounced worldly life around 527 BCE and, after twelve years of extreme asceticism, attained omniscience (kevala). For the following thirty years he spread Jain teachings across the Ganges basin. Jainism advocated radical non-violence — not only avoiding harm to humans and animals but striving to prevent injury even to insects and microorganisms. Its practice system centered on five great vows: non-violence, truthfulness, non-stealing, chastity, and non-attachment. Jainism held particular appeal for the merchant class, as its ethical system emphasized honest trade and legitimate accumulation of wealth, while non-violence principles made commerce one of the most respected occupations. Mahavira's religious community was organized in two tiers — monks and lay followers — forming a complete religious community. In Magadha, Jainism developed alongside Buddhism; the two traditions had important doctrinal disagreements — especially regarding the necessity of asceticism and the nature of the soul — but shared common ground in opposing Brahmanical caste privilege and sacrificial systems. Mahavira passed away around 468 BCE near Pataliputra, but his order endures to this day.

title.zh: 第二十四祖提尔丹嘉拉

role.zh: 耆那教传统的最后一位祖师，正在恒河流域传法，建立僧团
role.en: The last Tirthankara of Jain tradition, currently teaching across the Ganges basin and establishing the order

role.zh: 原为婆罗门学者，皈依耆那教后成为大雄十一名首席弟子（甘陀罗）之首
role.en: Originally a Brahmin scholar, converted to Jainism and became the first of Mahavira's eleven chief disciples (ganadharas)

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制的列国王国，依靠宫廷、地方官与贵族网络统治
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchical mahajanapada governed through the royal court, local officials, and aristocratic networks

socialStructure.zh: 社会大体遵循后吠陀时期的等级秩序，由王族与刹帝利贵族、婆罗门祭司、富裕商人和手工业者、农民、佣工与奴婢构成，同时吸纳城镇移民与边缘部族人口
socialStructure.en: Society broadly followed the late Vedic hierarchical order, consisting of the royal family and Kshatriya nobility, Brahmin ritual specialists, wealthy merchants and artisans, cultivators, laborers and servants, while also incorporating urban migrants and peripheral tribal peoples

rulingClass.zh: 以国王、王室宗族、刹帝利贵族、军事首领和有影响力的婆罗门顾问为核心
rulingClass.en: The ruling class centered on the king, royal kin, Kshatriya nobles, military commanders, and influential Brahmin advisers

succession.zh: 王位通常由王族男性世袭继承，但仍需获得宫廷贵族、军队首领与祭司集团承认
succession.en: The throne normally passed hereditarily within the male royal line, though recognition by court nobles, military leaders, and priestly elites remained important

structure.zh: 国家以国王为核心，宫廷负责决策，下设财政、征税、军务、司法、城防、仓储和交通商贸等职能；地方由城市官员、乡村首领和受封贵族共同治理
structure.en: The state was centered on the king, with the court directing policy through fiscal, revenue, military, judicial, urban defense, granary, and transport-trade functions; local administration was shared by city officials, village headmen, and subordinate nobles

function.zh: 管理宫廷礼仪、文书、王室财产、使者接待与日常政令传达
function.en: Managed court ceremony, records, royal property, diplomatic reception, and daily transmission of royal orders

function.zh: 统计耕地、征收农产份税、监督灌溉地和王田收入
function.en: Recorded farmland, collected agricultural shares, and supervised irrigated lands and royal estates

function.zh: 管理谷仓、军粮、赈济储备和向城市市场投放粮食
function.en: Administered granaries, army supplies, relief reserves, and release of grain to urban markets

function.zh: 负责征兵、武器储备、战象与战车调度、边境防务和将领任命
function.en: Handled levies, weapons stores, deployment of elephants and chariots, frontier defense, and appointment of commanders

function.zh: 处理盗窃、债务、土地纠纷、刑罚执行与城市治安
function.en: Dealt with theft, debt, land disputes, penal enforcement, and urban security

function.zh: 监督市集、商税、道路与河渡收费，并维护沿恒河—阎牟那河走廊的贸易秩序
function.en: Supervised markets, trade dues, road and ferry charges, and maintained commercial order along the Ganges-Yamuna corridor

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托憍赏弥等城市官署、周边城镇守备点、乡村首领与税役征收人。王权在核心河谷地区较强，在边缘地带则更多借助地方豪强与附属首领。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on city offices at Kausambi and other towns, fortified outposts, village headmen, and tax collectors. Royal authority was strongest in the core riverine zone and depended more heavily on local notables and subordinate chiefs in peripheral areas.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命和婆罗门法观念并行为特征，案件多由地方首领、法官或宫廷裁断。常见事项包括土地边界、债务、婚姻、继承、盗窃与伤害，刑罚从罚金、没收到体罚不等。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined customary law, royal decree, and Brahmanical legal ideas. Cases were commonly judged by local headmen, magistrates, or the royal court. Typical matters included land boundaries, debt, marriage, inheritance, theft, and bodily injury, with punishments ranging from fines and confiscation to corporal penalties.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠农业收成份额、王田产出、商税、市集与渡口税、手工业贡纳以及战争时的特别征发。实物征收占主导，货币和金属支付在城市商贸中逐渐增加。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue relied chiefly on shares of agricultural produce, output from royal estates, customs and market dues, ferry tolls, artisan obligations, and extraordinary wartime levies. Collection in kind predominated, though monetary and bullion payments were becoming more common in urban commerce.

religion.zh: 以婆罗门传统和吠陀祭祀为主，同时活跃着沙门思潮，包括早期佛教与耆那教活动。城市中的商人和游方修行者使憍赏弥成为多种宗教实践并存之地。
religion.en: Religion was dominated by Brahmanical tradition and Vedic sacrificial practice, while shramana movements were also active, including early Buddhism and Jainism. Merchants and wandering ascetics made Kausambi a place where multiple religious practices coexisted.

philosophy.zh: 思想处于轴心时代的北印度辩论环境中，既有关于祭祀、王权与社会秩序的婆罗门思考，也有关于业、轮回、解脱、苦与戒律的沙门讨论。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life belonged to the North Indian Axial Age debate culture, including Brahmanical reflection on sacrifice, kingship, and social order as well as shramana discussions of karma, rebirth, liberation, suffering, and discipline.

writingSystem.zh: 行政和商业中可能使用早期书写习惯，但大多数经典文化仍以口传为主；正式铭文传统尚未广泛发展，文字实践可能与北印度后来的婆罗米书写前身有关
writingSystem.en: Early writing practices may have been used in administration and commerce, but elite textual culture remained predominantly oral; formal inscriptional habits were not yet widespread, and literacy practices may have been linked to precursors of later North Indian Brahmi usage

culturalAchievements.zh: 跋蹉以憍赏弥的城市发展、陶器与手工业、宫廷传说和戏剧化的优陀延那故事而知名；其地处交通要道，也促进了思想交流与宗教团体传播。
culturalAchievements.en: Vatsa was notable for the urban development of Kausambi, pottery and craft production, courtly legends and later dramatized stories about Udayana; its position on major routes also fostered intellectual exchange and the spread of religious communities.

languageFamily.zh: 印度-雅利安语支，中部印度雅利安方言环境
languageFamily.en: Indo-Aryan, within the Middle Gangetic and central Indo-Aryan dialect continuum

gdpDescription.zh: 跋蹉经济以灌溉和雨养农业为基础，辅以河运贸易、城市手工业和交通课税。憍赏弥位于恒河—阎牟那交通枢纽，使该国在北印度列国中具有相当强的市场整合能力。
gdpDescription.en: The Vatsa economy rested on irrigated and rain-fed agriculture, supplemented by river trade, urban crafts, and transit taxation. Kausambi's location on the Ganges-Yamuna communications nexus gave the kingdom relatively strong market integration among the North Indian mahajanapadas.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻麦种植、畜牧、陶器、纺织、金属加工、木工、车具制造、河运与商贸中转
mainIndustries.en: Rice and barley cultivation, pastoralism, pottery, textiles, metalworking, carpentry, vehicle-making, river transport, and commercial brokerage

tradeGoods.zh: 粮食、棉织品、陶器、金属器、木材、盐、油料、珠饰、象牙制品与转口奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, cotton textiles, ceramics, metal tools, timber, salt, oils, beads, ivory goods, and re-exported luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、谷物储备、牲畜、工具与首饰。城镇商人和工匠家庭更可能持有银钱、库存货物和债权。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was held in land-use rights, grain stores, livestock, tools, and jewelry. Urban merchant and artisan households were more likely to hold silver coin, trade inventories, and credit claims.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业贡赋为基础、市场交换日益活跃的早期国家经济；王权控制交通节点、市场税和部分王田，但生产仍主要由村社、家族与商人网络驱动
economicSystem.en: An early state economy based on agrarian tribute with increasingly active market exchange; the monarchy controlled transit nodes, market taxes, and some royal estates, but production was still driven mainly by villages, kin groups, and merchant networks

source.en: Agricultural share taxes and land dues
Trade dues, market taxes, and ferry tolls
Royal estates, forests, and crown assets

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由银钱、金属锭、谷物储备、牲畜和贵重礼品组成。稳定时期可维持数年常规军政支出，但若与摩揭陀或阿槃提爆发长期大战，财政会迅速承压。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of silver coin, bullion, grain reserves, livestock, and valuable gifts. In stable conditions it could support several years of ordinary military and administrative spending, but a prolonged war with Magadha or Avanti would quickly strain it.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视维持粮仓、保障军费与控制商路。王权倾向避免对核心农业区过度征敛，以免引发逃亡和减产，同时通过商税与通行税提高现金收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, emphasizing granaries, military funding, and control of trade routes. The monarchy tended to avoid excessive extraction from core farming districts, to prevent flight and falling output, while increasing cash receipts through customs and transit taxes.

description.zh: 军队主力，由持矛、弓、盾和短兵器的征召农民与职业卫士组成
description.en: The main arm, composed of levy farmers and professional retainers armed with spears, bows, shields, and sidearms

description.zh: 由贵族武士主导，用于机动冲击、指挥和战场威望展示，但在复杂地形中效能受限
description.en: Led by aristocratic warriors, used for mobile shock, command, and battlefield prestige, though limited in difficult terrain

description.zh: 数量较少，主要承担侦察、追击和联络任务，骑兵尚非决定性兵种
description.en: Limited in number and used mainly for reconnaissance, pursuit, and communications; cavalry was not yet the decisive arm

description.zh: 数量不大但威慑力强，用于冲击敌阵、保护国王和提升军队士气
description.en: Not large in number but highly intimidating, used to break enemy lines, protect the king, and raise morale

description.zh: 负责憍赏弥及渡口、仓城和边境据点的守备，也在战时支援后勤和道路安全
description.en: Responsible for defending Kausambi, ferries, fortified depots, and frontier posts, while supporting logistics and road security in wartime

commanderInChief.zh: 优陀延那王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: King Udayana as commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军备处于北印度铁器时代水平，使用铁制矛头、箭镞、刀剑，辅以弓、盾、战车和战象；攻城能力有限，作战依赖野战、围困和交通线控制
technology.en: Military technology reflected the North Indian Iron Age, using iron spearheads, arrowheads, blades, bows, shields, chariots, and elephants; siege capability was limited, and warfare relied on field battles, blockades, and control of routes

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自摩揭陀、拘萨罗与阿槃提等强邻的扩张，以及恒河—阎牟那通道上城镇和渡口控制权的竞争。若国内贵族离心，王权也可能面临内部军事挑战。
threats.en: The principal threats came from expansionist neighbors such as Magadha, Kosala, and Avanti, and from competition over towns and crossings along the Ganges-Yamuna corridor. If aristocratic cohesion weakened, the monarchy could also face internal military challenges.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在阎牟那河流域与可耕平原，村庄密布，憍赏弥为最大的城市中心。整体人口规模使跋蹉成为中等偏强的列国，但仍逊于后起扩张中的摩揭陀。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Yamuna basin and cultivable plains, with dense village settlement and Kausambi as the largest urban center. This made Vatsa a moderately strong mahajanapada, though still smaller than the expanding power of Magadha.

name.en: Market Town Cluster of the Vatsa Corridor

socialClasses.zh: 王族与刹帝利贵族居于顶层，婆罗门享有宗教与教育威望；其下为富商、放债者、工匠行会和独立农民，最广大人口为佃农、佣工、牧民与仆役。城镇化推动了商人阶层的重要性上升。
socialClasses.en: The royal house and Kshatriya nobility occupied the top layer, while Brahmins held religious and educational prestige; beneath them were wealthy merchants, moneylenders, craft groups, and independent cultivators, with tenant farmers, laborers, herders, and servants forming the broadest base. Urbanization was increasing the importance of merchant strata.

allies.zh: 跋蹉的对外关系以婚姻、使节和临时均势安排为主，没有绝对稳定的长期盟友；可与部分恒河中游列国及商贸网络维持合作
allies.en: Vatsa's diplomacy relied on marriage ties, embassies, and temporary balance-of-power arrangements rather than permanent alliances; it could maintain cooperation with selected middle Ganges states and commercial networks
