threats.en: The main threats came from neighboring valleys or highland groups competing for water, farmland, pilgrimage routes, and exchange nodes rather than from large imperial invasions.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于秘鲁海岸绿洲谷地、高地盆地和更南方安第斯地区，聚落规模普遍较小，但若干礼仪中心与灌溉谷地形成较高人口密度。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across Peruvian coastal oasis valleys, highland basins, and farther southern Andean regions; most settlements were small, though several ceremonial centers and irrigated valleys achieved relatively high densities.

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为祭司与酋长精英、工匠和专职仪式人员、普通农民与牧民、渔民，以及依附性较强的劳役群体；阶层差异存在但未形成成熟官僚贵族国家。
socialClasses.en: Society can broadly be divided into priestly and chiefly elites, artisans and specialized ritual personnel, common farmers and herders, fishers, and more dependent laboring groups; inequality existed, but a fully developed bureaucratic aristocratic state had not emerged.

allies.zh: 无固定整体盟友，常见的是邻近谷地、共享圣地的朝圣网络和高地—海岸交换伙伴关系
allies.en: There were no fixed overall allies; more common were neighboring valley partnerships, shared pilgrimage networks, and highland-coast exchange relationships

enemies.zh: 敌对对象主要是争夺水源、耕地和贸易路线的邻近酋邦；在更广安第斯范围内，既受沙文文化影响，也与其他形成期中心存在竞争。
enemies.en: Enemies were mainly neighboring chiefdoms competing over water, farmland, and exchange routes; across the broader Andes, these societies were influenced by Chavín culture while also competing with other Formative centers.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持地区平衡、通过婚姻与祭祀结盟、保障跨生态区交换，以及在必要时进行有限报复性战争为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining regional balance, using marriage and ritual alliance, securing exchange across ecological zones, and conducting limited retaliatory warfare when necessary.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括山地梯田与沿海灌溉、石与夯土平台建筑、精细纺织、玉米与藜麦栽培改良、羊驼驮运利用，以及金、铜的早期加工。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mountain terracing and coastal irrigation, stone and rammed-earth platform architecture, fine textile production, improved maize and quinoa cultivation, use of llamas for transport, and early working of gold and copper.

strengths.zh: 优势在于跨生态区资源互补、成熟的水利与礼仪动员能力、强大的地方适应性，以及由宗教中心维系的区域整合力量。
strengths.en: Strengths included complementary resources across ecological zones, mature capacity for irrigation and ritual mobilization, strong local adaptability, and regional integration maintained through religious centers.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治碎片化、对气候波动和厄尔尼诺事件高度敏感、缺乏统一军政机构，以及长距离协调能力有限。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included political fragmentation, high vulnerability to climatic fluctuation and El Niño events, lack of unified military-administrative institutions, and limited capacity for long-distance coordination.

outlook.zh: 就公元前500年前后而言，这些社会总体稳定并持续发展，部分地区将进一步复杂化，向更强的区域酋邦和早期国家形态演进，但整合仍将保持多中心和不均衡特征。
outlook.en: Around 500 BCE these societies were broadly stable and still developing; some regions would become more complex, evolving toward stronger regional chiefdoms and early state forms, though integration would remain multicentered and uneven.

description.zh: 秘鲁高地、海岸与更南方地区存在多个灌溉农业和礼仪中心型酋邦。
description.en: The Peruvian highlands, coast, and farther southern regions hosted multiple irrigation-based and ceremonial chiefdoms.

governmentForm.zh: 以宗教圣所为核心、由祭司集团统治的神权体系，依靠仪式权威、朝圣网络与地方首领合作维系统治。
governmentForm.en: A theocratic system centered on a major ceremonial sanctuary, ruled by priestly elites and sustained through ritual authority, pilgrimage networks, and cooperation with local chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈分层结构，上层为掌控神庙与仪式知识的祭司和地方精英，中层为工匠、专业建造者与运输组织者，下层为广大农民、牧民与劳役群体。
socialStructure.en: Society was stratified, with priests and local elites controlling temples and ritual knowledge at the top, artisans, specialized builders, and transport organizers in the middle, and the broad mass of farmers, herders, and laborers below.

rulingClass.zh: 神庙祭司集团与与之结盟的区域首领
rulingClass.en: Temple priesthood and allied regional chiefs

succession.zh: 领导权并非严格世袭，更可能在祭司家族、宗教资历和神谕认可的基础上于精英内部传承。
succession.en: Leadership was not strictly dynastic; it likely passed within elite priestly lineages through religious seniority, hereditary prestige, and ritual legitimation.

structure.zh: 中央核心是查文·德万塔尔神庙复合体，由大祭司及其祭司议事圈管理祭祀、仓储、工程和对外宗教联系；外围地区则由地方社群首领在宗教影响下维持自治。
structure.en: The central core was the temple complex at Chavin de Huantar, where the High Priest and a council of ritual specialists oversaw worship, storage, construction, and religious outreach; outlying regions remained locally governed by community chiefs under Chavin religious influence.

function.zh: 主持祭典、神谕解释、朝圣接待与宗教历法安排。
function.en: Conducted ceremonies, interpreted oracles, received pilgrims, and organized the ritual calendar.

function.zh: 负责神庙扩建、地下廊道、排水系统、石雕安置与公共工程维护。
function.en: Managed temple expansion, underground galleries, drainage systems, stone monuments, and maintenance of public works.

function.zh: 征集粮食、纺织品、劳役和礼品，并进行储藏与再分配。
function.en: Collected food, textiles, labor obligations, and ritual gifts, then stored and redistributed them.

function.zh: 管理石匠、金工、陶工、织工和用于宗教威权展示的圣器制作。
function.en: Supervised stonemasons, metalworkers, potters, weavers, and the production of sacred objects used to display religious authority.

name.zh: 地方联络与朝圣网络事务

function.zh: 协调山地、谷地与沿海社群首领，维系宗教传播与物资往来。
function.en: Coordinated with chiefs from highland, valley, and coastal communities to sustain religious influence and material exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由各谷地和高地社群首领承担，查文中心通过宗教威望、祭司使者、定期朝圣和仪式贡赋来施加影响，而非建立高度统一的领土官僚制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was chiefly handled by valley and highland community leaders. Chavin exercised influence through religious prestige, priestly envoys, periodic pilgrimage, and ritual tribute rather than through a tightly unified territorial bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 没有成文法典，秩序主要依靠习惯法、祭司裁断、亲属协商和宗教禁忌维持；对亵渎圣所、拒绝贡赋或破坏公共工程的行为可能施以宗教与社会惩戒。
legalSystem.en: There was no formal written law code; order was maintained through customary law, priestly judgment, kin-based mediation, and religious taboo. Desecration of sacred space, refusal of obligations, or damage to communal works likely brought ritual and social sanctions.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础以实物贡赋和劳役为主，包括玉米、块根作物、骆马制品、纺织品、贝壳、石材和季节性工程劳作；这更接近宗教再分配而非统一货币税收。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base rested on in-kind tribute and labor service, including maize, tubers, camelid products, textiles, shells, stone, and seasonal work obligations; this resembled religious redistribution more than monetized taxation.

religion.zh: 核心信仰围绕查文宗教展开，崇拜具猫科、蛇类、猛禽等复合特征的神灵与祖灵力量，重视神庙、神谕、幻象体验、祭献与朝圣。
religion.en: Religion centered on the Chavin cult, venerating deities and sacred powers with composite feline, serpent, and raptor traits, and emphasizing temples, oracles, visionary experience, offerings, and pilgrimage.

philosophy.zh: 查文没有已知成体系的哲学文本，其世界观强调宇宙秩序、自然力量转化、人与神灵沟通以及祭司对神圣知识的垄断。
philosophy.en: Chavin had no known formal philosophical literature, but its worldview emphasized cosmic order, transformation of natural forces, communication between humans and sacred beings, and priestly control of esoteric knowledge.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无成熟文字系统；信息传递主要依靠口述传统、图像符号、建筑空间、纪念石刻与仪式表演。
writingSystem.en: There was no mature writing system; information was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, iconography, architectural space, carved monuments, and ritual performance.

culturalAchievements.zh: 查文在石雕、宗教图像体系、地下廊道建筑、复杂水利声学设计、金工与跨区域风格传播方面成就突出，形成了安第斯早期最具影响力的宗教艺术传统之一。
culturalAchievements.en: Chavin excelled in stone carving, religious iconography, subterranean gallery architecture, sophisticated hydraulic-acoustic design, metallurgy, and the spread of an interregional visual style, creating one of the most influential early religious-art traditions in the Andes.

languageFamily.zh: 语言情况不明，可能涉及安第斯多种地方语言；查文影响是宗教与图像上的统一，而非语言上的单一化。
languageFamily.en: The linguistic situation is uncertain and likely involved multiple local Andean languages; Chavin unity was primarily religious and visual rather than linguistic.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、牧养、手工业和宗教再分配为主，整体处于前货币、低城市化但具有明显专业分工和跨生态交换的繁荣阶段。估值仅为便于跨文明比较的近似换算。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, herding, craft production, and religious redistribution. It was largely pre-monetary and weakly urbanized, yet showed clear specialization and exchange across ecological zones. The estimate is only an approximate comparative conversion.

mainIndustries.zh: 山地农业、灌溉谷地种植、骆马牧养、纺织、陶器、石雕建筑、金工与宗教服务
mainIndustries.en: Highland agriculture, irrigated valley cultivation, camelid herding, textiles, pottery, stone construction, metallurgy, and ritual services

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、藜麦、块根作物、辣椒、纺织品、羊驼与美洲驼毛、贝壳（尤其外来海贝）、黑曜石、石材、颜料、金属小件与宗教器物
tradeGoods.en: Maize, quinoa, tubers, chili peppers, textiles, alpaca and llama wool, shells (especially exotic marine shells), obsidian, stone, pigments, small metal goods, and ritual objects

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、亲属网络、劳动力、储粮、纺织品、骆马类牲畜和祭祀交换关系，而非金属货币积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed chiefly in land-use access, kin networks, labor capacity, stored food, textiles, camelid herds, and ritual exchange ties rather than in monetary savings.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产为基础、由神庙中心进行部分汇集与再分配的混合经济；跨山地、谷地和沿海的垂直生态互补是其关键特征。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on village production with partial collection and redistribution through temple centers; vertical exchange across highland, valley, and coastal ecological zones was a defining feature.

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要不是铸币储备，而是神庙仓储系统中积累的粮食、纺织品、牲畜、原材料和贵重礼器。其真正力量在于动员与再分配能力。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a coin reserve but rather accumulated food, textiles, livestock, raw materials, and prestige ritual objects stored in temple-controlled facilities. Its real strength lay in mobilization and redistribution.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守稳健，优先维持宗教中心、公共工程和储备能力，通过节庆再分配与工程动员巩固地方忠诚。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and stability-oriented, prioritizing the religious center, public works, and reserve capacity, while using festive redistribution and labor mobilization to reinforce local loyalty.

description.zh: 负责保护圣所、仓储、祭司与重要仪式场所，是最接近常备武装的力量。
description.en: Protected sanctuaries, storehouses, priests, and major ritual spaces; this was the force closest to a standing army.

description.zh: 由地方社群在需要时征召，用于防御山谷通道、护送物资和处理部落冲突。
description.en: Raised from local communities as needed to defend valley routes, escort goods, and manage inter-group conflicts.

description.zh: 由祭司集团和地方首领控制的小规模精锐随从，兼具威慑、护卫和礼仪展示职能。
description.en: Small elite retainers controlled by priests and local chiefs, serving deterrent, protective, and ceremonial display roles.

commanderInChief.zh: 大祭司与地方首领联席指挥
commanderInChief.en: High Priest with allied local chiefs in joint command

technology.zh: 军事技术较为基础，依靠木石武器、投石索、长矛、棍棒、简易防具和山地地形优势；查文更擅长象征性威慑与组织动员，而非大规模征服战争。
technology.en: Military technology was relatively basic, relying on wooden and stone weapons, slings, spears, clubs, simple armor, and the defensive advantages of mountain terrain; Chavin was stronger in symbolic deterrence and mobilization than in large-scale conquest warfare.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自区域间竞争、地方社群脱离宗教网络、交通线中断、资源紧张和山地冲突，而非来自拥有常备国家军队的外部帝国。
threats.en: The main threats came from interregional rivalry, local communities drifting away from the cult network, disruption of routes, resource stress, and highland conflict rather than from external empires with professional state armies.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约五十万，分布于北中安第斯的山地、谷地与部分沿海关联社群。人口并非集中在单一国家疆域内，而是处于受查文宗教中心强烈影响的广域文化圈之中。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 500,000, spread across highland, valley, and some connected coastal communities of the north-central Andes. This population was not concentrated in a single tightly bounded state, but within a broad cultural sphere strongly influenced by the Chavin religious center.

socialClasses.zh: 大致可分为祭司与宗教精英、地方首领家族、专业工匠与建筑者、商旅与运输组织者、普通农民牧民以及劳役依附群体。
socialClasses.en: Social classes may be broadly divided into priests and religious elites, local chiefly families, specialized artisans and builders, traders and transport organizers, ordinary farmers and herders, and labor-dependent groups.

allies.zh: 没有现代意义上的正式盟邦；查文通过与北中安第斯各谷地首领、沿海礼仪中心和高地社群建立宗教合作关系来形成影响网络。
allies.en: There were no formal allies in the modern sense; Chavin built an influence network through religious cooperation with valley chiefs, coastal ceremonial centers, and highland communities across the north-central Andes.

enemies.zh: 没有可明确识别的单一宿敌，潜在对手主要是拒绝接受查文宗教权威、争夺区域资源或维持独立仪式传统的地方势力。
enemies.en: There was no clearly identifiable single arch-enemy; likely rivals were local powers that resisted Chavin religious authority, competed for resources, or maintained independent ceremonial traditions.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以宗教扩散、象征整合和礼仪交流为核心，而非领土吞并；通过朝圣、圣物、婚姻联系、工艺风格和神谕声望扩大影响。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on religious diffusion, symbolic integration, and ceremonial exchange rather than territorial annexation; influence spread through pilgrimage, sacred objects, marriage ties, craft style, and oracular prestige.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括大型石构建筑、地下通道系统、精心设计的排水与水声工程、金银与铜的早期加工、复杂纺织技术以及跨生态区资源整合。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included monumental stone construction, subterranean passage systems, carefully engineered drainage and hydraulic-acoustic works, early working of gold, silver, and copper, sophisticated textile production, and integration of resources across ecological zones.

strengths.zh: 查文最大的优势在于强大的宗教号召力、跨区域文化影响、成熟的纪念性建筑传统、对精英认同的塑造能力以及把不同生态区资源纳入同一礼仪网络的整合力。
strengths.en: Chavin's greatest strengths were its powerful religious appeal, broad cultural influence, mature monumental architecture, ability to shape elite identity, and capacity to integrate resources from different ecological zones into a single ritual network.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于政治整合有限、缺乏成熟文字与统一官僚体系、军事征服能力较弱，并高度依赖宗教威望、交通联系和中心圣所的持续权威。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in limited political integration, the absence of mature writing and a unified bureaucracy, weak military-conquest capacity, and heavy dependence on religious prestige, transport links, and the continued authority of the central sanctuary.

outlook.zh: 在公元前500年前后，查文仍处于繁荣阶段，能够继续作为安第斯广域宗教中心发挥作用；但从长期看，若区域网络松动、环境压力上升或地方中心增强，其统一文化地平线可能逐步分化。
outlook.en: Around 500 BCE, Chavin was still in a thriving phase and could continue functioning as a major pan-Andean religious center; in the longer term, however, loosening regional ties, environmental stress, or the rise of stronger local centers could gradually fragment its shared cultural horizon.

description.zh: 安第斯北中部由查文宗教中心连接，形成跨区域文化网络与朝圣体系。
description.en: North-central Andean societies were linked by the Chavin cult center, creating a broad religious and cultural horizon.

governmentForm.zh: 由若干世袭酋长领导的松散部族酋邦体系，以仪式中心和亲族关系维系权威。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal chiefdom system led by hereditary chiefs, with authority maintained through ritual centers and kinship ties.

socialStructure.zh: 阿德纳社会以氏族与村落为基本单位，上层为主持祭祀与分配礼物的酋长家族。普通成员从事狩猎、采集、园圃农业与土木工程。社会分层尚有限，但在墓葬随葬品与土丘规格上已能看出地位差异。
socialStructure.en: Adena society was organized around clans and villages, with chiefly families at the top directing rituals and gift distribution. Common members engaged in hunting, gathering, horticulture, and earthwork construction. Social stratification remained limited, but differences in grave goods and mound size already reveal status distinctions.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是掌握祭祀权威、贸易联系与动员劳役能力的酋长家族。其权力更多依赖声望、馈赠与祖先崇拜，而非成文官僚制度。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted mainly of chiefly families who controlled ritual authority, trade ties, and the ability to mobilize labor. Their power depended more on prestige, gifting, and ancestor veneration than on a formal bureaucracy.

succession.zh: 继承多半在显赫家族内部进行，通常偏向父系或近亲继承，但也受长老认可与个人威望影响。若继承人无法维持联盟与祭祀网络，酋长权威可能迅速削弱。
succession.en: Succession usually remained within prominent families, often favoring patrilineal or close-kin inheritance, though elder approval and personal prestige were important. If an heir failed to sustain alliances and ritual networks, chiefly authority could weaken quickly.

structure.zh: 阿德纳并无统一国家机构，而是由若干区域性酋邦组成。公共事务围绕土丘营建、祭祀集会、馈赠交换与冲突调解展开。决策通常由酋长、长老与宗教主持者共同完成。
structure.en: Adena had no unified state apparatus but consisted of several regional chiefdoms. Public affairs centered on mound construction, ritual gatherings, gift exchange, and conflict mediation. Decisions were typically made by chiefs together with elders and ritual specialists.

function.zh: 组织祭祀、祖先葬礼、土丘建造与跨村劳役动员。
function.en: Organized rituals, elite burials, mound construction, and labor mobilization across villages.

function.zh: 管理礼物流通、外来物资交换与部落间争端调解。
function.en: Managed gift circulation, exchange of exotic goods, and mediation of inter-group disputes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由村落长老和氏族首领承担，各聚落保有高度自治。区域酋长只在重大祭祀、工程或冲突时期集中权力。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was handled by village elders and clan heads, and settlements retained high autonomy. Regional chiefs concentrated authority mainly during major rituals, public works, or periods of conflict.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，依靠氏族传统、长老裁决与赔偿机制维持秩序。严重冲突通常通过和解、礼物补偿或驱逐解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, with order maintained through clan traditions, elder arbitration, and compensation practices. Serious disputes were usually settled by reconciliation, gift compensation, or expulsion.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有固定税制，酋长通过贡礼、劳役与祭祀献纳获取资源。公共工程所需物资多以季节性征集方式取得。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed tax system; chiefs obtained resources through tribute, labor dues, and ritual offerings. Materials for public works were gathered by seasonal requisition.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿德纳以大型土丘、墓葬礼仪与长距离交换网络著称，是北美东部早期仪式中心传统的重要代表。其石器、铜器与装饰工艺显示出相当成熟的礼仪文化。
culturalAchievements.en: The Adena are noted for large earthen mounds, mortuary ceremonies, and long-distance exchange networks, making them a major representative of early ceremonial center traditions in eastern North America. Their stonework, copper objects, and decorative crafts reflect a well-developed ritual culture.

languageFamily.zh: 未知，可能属于北美东部早期未定语系群体。
languageFamily.en: Unknown; possibly part of early undetermined language groups of eastern North America.

philosophy.zh: 泛灵信仰与祖先崇拜，强调死亡仪式、神圣景观与酋长威望的神圣化。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor veneration, emphasizing mortuary ritual, sacred landscapes, and the sacralization of chiefly prestige.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，知识通过口传传统、仪式象征与工艺样式传承。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, ritual symbolism, and craft styles.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性狩猎采集、园圃农业和手工业为主，市场化程度很低。财富集中体现为食物剩余、装饰品、外来铜料与动员劳役的能力。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based mainly on subsistence hunting, gathering, horticulture, and handicrafts, with very low marketization. Wealth was expressed through food surplus, ornaments, exotic copper, and the ability to mobilize labor.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎采集、园圃农业、土木营建、石器与铜器加工
mainIndustries.en: Hunting and gathering, horticulture, earthwork construction, stone and copper craftworking

tradeGoods.zh: 铜料、海贝、云母、燧石、装饰石珠、兽皮
tradeGoods.en: Copper, marine shells, mica, flint, decorative stone beads, hides

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富有限，主要以粮食储备、工具、皮毛和亲族支持体现。显赫家族则拥有更多外来礼器与葬仪资源。
householdWealth.en: Most households held limited wealth, mainly in stored food, tools, hides, and kin support. Prominent families possessed more exotic prestige goods and mortuary resources.

unit.en: grams of silver per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以亲族互惠、酋长再分配和礼物交换为核心的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on kin reciprocity, chiefly redistribution, and gift exchange.

grams of silver equivalent in reserve

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备并非集中金库，而是分散在酋长家族、祭祀中心和储藏设施中的食物、工艺品与威望物资。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a centralized vault but dispersed among chiefly households, ritual centers, and storage facilities in the form of food, crafted goods, and prestige items.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持祭祀声望与公共营建，尽量避免过度征集以免破坏氏族忠诚。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining ritual prestige and public construction while avoiding excessive extraction that could damage clan loyalty.

description.zh: 由酋长亲族与随从组成，负责护卫、执法与战时核心战斗。
description.en: Composed of the chief's kin and followers, responsible for protection, enforcement, and core combat in wartime.

description.zh: 由各聚落成年男子临时集结，使用矛、棍棒、石刃与弓箭作战。
description.en: Temporarily raised from adult men of the settlements, fighting with spears, clubs, stone blades, and bows.

commanderInChief.zh: 最高酋长
commanderInChief.en: Paramount Chief

technology.zh: 军事技术较基础，以木制与石制武器为主，少量铜器主要具礼仪和威望性质，防御依赖地形与聚落协作。
technology.en: Military technology was basic, relying mainly on wooden and stone weapons; limited copper objects were largely ceremonial and prestige-oriented, while defense depended on terrain and settlement cooperation.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部落竞争、狩猎地争夺与内部联盟松动，而非大规模外来征服。
threats.en: The main threats came from neighboring tribal competition, disputes over hunting grounds, and weakening internal alliances rather than large-scale foreign conquest.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于河谷与林地区域的小型聚落，季节性流动较明显。缺乏真正城市，人口重心在村落与仪式中心周边。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed among small settlements in river valleys and forested regions, with noticeable seasonal mobility. There were no true cities, and population was concentrated in villages and around ceremonial centers.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族、祭祀主持者、自由部落成员、依附家庭与俘获者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, ritual specialists, free tribal members, dependent households, and captives.

allies.zh: 与周边林地部族维持以通婚、馈赠与仪式往来为主的松散联盟。
allies.en: Maintained loose alliances with neighboring woodland groups through marriage, gifting, and ritual exchange.

enemies.zh: 无固定宿敌，但与邻近猎场竞争者和边缘部落常有摩擦。
enemies.en: No fixed arch-enemy, but frequent friction existed with rivals over nearby hunting grounds and frontier groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持交换网络和避免长期仇杀为主，必要时通过展示祭祀声望与武力威慑稳定边界。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining exchange networks and avoiding long blood feuds, while using ritual prestige and occasional force to stabilize frontiers when necessary.

keyInnovations.zh: 土丘工程、礼仪性铜器加工、区域性长距离交换、园圃农业与储藏技术。
keyInnovations.en: Earthen mound engineering, ceremonial copperworking, regional long-distance exchange, horticulture, and storage techniques.

strengths.zh: 仪式整合能力强，社会合作可支持大型工程，区域交换网络较广。
strengths.en: Strong ritual integration, social cooperation capable of supporting large works, and relatively broad regional exchange networks.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏集中政权与常备军，经济剩余有限，政治统一度较低。
weaknesses.en: Lacked centralized state authority and a standing army, had limited economic surplus, and possessed low political unity.

outlook.zh: 短期内可继续维持稳定的区域仪式文化，但长期可能分化并演变为新的土丘与礼仪传统。
outlook.en: In the short term it could maintain a stable regional ceremonial culture, but over time it was likely to fragment and evolve into new mound-building and ritual traditions.

description.zh: 阿德纳文化是北美东部以土丘、祭祀和部落酋邦网络为核心的早期林地文明形态。
description.en: The Adena Culture was an Early Woodland ceremonial civilization of eastern North America centered on mounds, ritual life, and tribal chiefdom networks.

governmentForm.zh: 以王权为核心的早期岛屿君主制，依靠宗族贵族、地方首领与宫廷随从治理全国。
governmentForm.en: An early island monarchy centered on royal authority, governing through kin nobles, local chiefs, and palace retainers.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族与贵族居上，地方首领、战士、农民、工匠和仆役分层分布。沿海与河谷地区定居人口较多，内陆与边缘区域仍保留较强部族结构。来自北印度的移民传统与本地部族社会正在融合。
socialStructure.en: Society was layered with the royal house and nobles at the top, followed by local chiefs, warriors, farmers, artisans, and servants. Coastal and riverine areas supported more settled populations, while inland and peripheral zones retained stronger tribal structures. Migrant traditions from northern India were in the process of blending with local tribal society.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由毗阇耶家族、其追随者后裔以及归附的地方首领构成。王权依赖控制土地、港口、贡赋与宗教合法性来维系统治。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the family of Vijaya, descendants of his followers, and incorporated local chiefs. Royal power depended on controlling land, ports, tribute, and religious legitimacy.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部继承，偏重父系王统，但仍可能受宫廷贵族和武装支持影响。若王位继承不稳，地方首领有较大机会干预王权更替。
succession.en: The throne generally passed within the royal family, favoring patrilineal succession, though court nobles and armed support remained important. If succession became unstable, local chiefs had significant opportunities to influence royal replacement.

structure.zh: 王国的治理核心在坦巴潘尼宫廷，国王通过近臣、税吏与地方首领实施统治。中央机构尚不复杂，但已具备宫廷、财政、军事和港务等基本职能。地方行政依靠村社长与区域首领协同执行王令。
structure.en: The kingdom was governed from the court at Tambapanni, where the king ruled through close retainers, tax collectors, and local chiefs. The central apparatus was still relatively simple, but it already possessed basic court, fiscal, military, and port functions. Local administration was carried out jointly by village headmen and regional chiefs enforcing royal orders.

function.zh: 负责宫廷管理、王命传达、贵族接待与宗教仪式协调。
function.en: Managed the court, transmitted royal orders, received nobles, and coordinated religious ceremonies.

function.zh: 征收谷物、劳役和港口贡赋，并管理王室仓储。
function.en: Collected grain, labor dues, and port tribute, and managed royal storage facilities.

function.zh: 组织王家卫队、征召地方兵力并维持沿海与内陆边界秩序。
function.en: Organized the royal guard, levied local forces, and maintained order along coastal and inland frontiers.

function.zh: 监管登陆点、海上交换、商旅安全及外来物资征收。
function.en: Supervised landing places, maritime exchange, merchant security, and collection of dues on incoming goods.

localAdmin.zh: 地方上由村社长、部族首领和王室任命的征收者共同治理。偏远地区对王室控制较弱，多通过结盟和贡赋承认王权。
localAdmin.en: At the local level, village headmen, tribal chiefs, and royal collectors governed jointly. In remote regions royal control was weaker and was acknowledged mainly through alliance and tribute.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命和贵族裁决为基础，尚未形成成熟成文法典。土地争议、婚姻与伤害案件多由地方首领和王廷共同裁断。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on custom, royal decree, and noble judgment, without yet forming a mature written code. Land disputes, marriage issues, and injury cases were often decided jointly by local chiefs and the royal court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以谷物、渔获、劳役和港口贡赋为主，部分地区还以象牙、木材和珍珠等实物纳贡。王室对沿海贸易征收较高比例的关津收入。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on grain, fish, labor dues, and port tribute, while some regions paid in ivory, timber, pearls, and other goods. The crown drew a relatively high share of customs revenue from coastal trade.

religion.zh: 佛教
religion.en: Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 王国早期的重要成就在于建立岛上较稳定的王权中心，并整合外来移民与本地社群。港口聚落、礼仪传统与灌溉雏形为后来的僧伽罗文明奠定基础。
culturalAchievements.en: A major early achievement of the kingdom was the establishment of a relatively stable center of royal power on the island and the integration of migrant and local communities. Port settlements, ritual traditions, and the first outlines of irrigation laid foundations for later Sinhalese civilization.

languageFamily.zh: 印度-雅利安语族早期僧伽罗语形成阶段，并与本地语言成分混合
languageFamily.en: Early Indo-Aryan proto-Sinhalese in formation, mixed with local linguistic elements

philosophy.zh: 早期僧伽罗社会处于婆罗门传统、本地神祇崇拜与后起佛教影响交汇阶段；若按传统纪年置于此时，佛教尚属精英层面的新兴合法性资源。
philosophy.en: Early Simhala society stood at the intersection of Brahmanical traditions, local deity worship, and later Buddhist influence; if placed at this traditional date, Buddhism would still be an emerging source of legitimacy among elites.

writingSystem.zh: 可能使用早期口传行政与记忆传统，正式文字使用尚不普及；北印度文字观念可能已开始传入。
writingSystem.en: Administration likely relied mainly on oral and mnemonic practices, with formal literacy not yet widespread; concepts of North Indian writing may already have begun to spread.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础是稻作农业、渔业、林产与沿海贸易，整体生产力高于一般部族社会。由于地处海上航线节点，外来交换在王室财政中占有相当比重。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on rice agriculture, fishing, forest products, and coastal trade, giving it productivity above that of a typical tribal society. Because it sat on maritime routes, external exchange formed a significant part of royal finance.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、渔业、木材采集、珍珠与贝产、手工业、海港贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, fishing, timber extraction, pearls and shell products, handicrafts, port trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、鱼干、木材、象牙、珍珠、贝壳、香料、宝石、金属器
tradeGoods.en: Rice, dried fish, timber, ivory, pearls, shells, spices, gemstones, metal goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为土地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备和渔具。沿海商人与贵族家庭则更可能拥有金属器、珠饰和外来奢侈品。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary farming households held wealth mainly in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, and fishing equipment. Coastal merchants and noble households were more likely to possess metal goods, jewelry, and imported luxuries.

unit.en: grams of silver per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室控制港口、地方贡赋和村社农业为核心的早期再分配型王国经济。
economicSystem.en: An early redistributive kingdom economy centered on royal control of ports, local tribute, and village agriculture.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent in reserve

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室储备主要由谷物仓储、珍珠宝石、金属器和港口所得构成，集中于王都及若干沿海仓库。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves consisted mainly of grain stocks, pearls and gemstones, metal goods, and port income, concentrated at the capital and several coastal storehouses.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健，重视港口收入与农业储备，并通过公共工程提升王权控制力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, emphasizing port income and agricultural reserves while using public works to strengthen royal control.

description.zh: 负责保卫国王、王都与宫廷，装备较精良。
description.en: Protected the king, capital, and court, and was better equipped than ordinary forces.

description.zh: 由地方首领征召的主力兵种，使用矛、盾、弓和短刀作战。
description.en: The main arm raised by local chiefs, fighting with spears, shields, bows, and short blades.

description.zh: 用于沿海巡逻、运输和打击小规模海上袭扰。
description.en: Used for coastal patrol, transport, and suppression of small-scale maritime raiding.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王毗阇耶
commanderInChief.en: King Vijaya

technology.zh: 军备以铁器和木制兵器为主，具备基本海上运输能力，但尚未形成高度专业化军制。战术依赖步兵、地方动员和沿海机动。
technology.en: Armament relied on iron weapons and wooden gear, with basic seaborne transport capability but no highly specialized military system. Tactics depended on infantry, local mobilization, and coastal mobility.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括岛内未完全臣服的部族、宫廷继承纷争以及来自南印度海上势力的潜在干预。
threats.en: Main threats included incompletely subdued island tribes, court succession struggles, and potential interference from South Indian maritime powers.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于西北沿海、河口和平原农业区，王都周边密度较高。内陆山地和森林地区人口较稀疏，仍保留较多地方部族成分。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated mainly in the northwestern coast, river mouths, and agricultural plains, with higher density around the capital. Inland hills and forests were more sparsely populated and retained more local tribal elements.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族、地方首领、战士、商人和工匠、自由农民、仆役与附属部族。
socialClasses.en: Royal family and nobles, local chiefs, warriors, merchants and artisans, free farmers, servants, and subordinate tribal groups.

allies.zh: 与部分沿海商人团体和岛内归附首领保持合作，并与南印度若干港口存在务实贸易联系。
allies.en: Maintained cooperation with some coastal merchant groups and subordinate island chiefs, and had pragmatic trade contacts with several South Indian ports.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌对者包括岛内抗拒王权的部族与可能争夺海峡贸易的南印度势力。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included island tribes resisting royal authority and South Indian powers that might compete for strait trade.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制海上交换节点、吸纳移民资源和防止外部干预为重点。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling maritime exchange nodes, absorbing migrant resources, and preventing outside interference.

era.zh: 南亚早期铁器时代岛屿王国
era.en: Early Iron Age South Asian Island Kingdom

keyInnovations.zh: 稻作扩张、早期灌溉与仓储、海港组织、铁器使用、王权再分配体系。
keyInnovations.en: Expansion of rice cultivation, early irrigation and granaries, port organization, use of iron, and a royal redistributive system.

strengths.zh: 地处海上交通要冲，农业与港口收入兼备，王权能够整合多元族群。
strengths.en: It occupied a strategic maritime position, combined agricultural and port income, and possessed a monarchy capable of integrating diverse populations.

weaknesses.zh: 中央控制尚浅，制度化程度有限，继承与地方离心风险较大。
weaknesses.en: Central control remained shallow, institutionalization was limited, and succession and provincial centrifugal risks were substantial.

outlook.zh: 若能稳固王统并扩大灌溉与港务体系，未来有望发展为更成熟的僧伽罗王国。
outlook.en: If it could stabilize the royal line and expand irrigation and port administration, it had the potential to develop into a more mature Sinhalese kingdom.

description.zh: 僧伽罗王国是斯里兰卡早期以王权、稻作与海港贸易为基础的岛屿政权。
description.en: The Simhala Kingdom was an early island polity in Sri Lanka built on monarchy, rice agriculture, and maritime trade.

governmentForm.zh: 由贵族战士阶层主导的凯尔特部族联盟，王权受战士贵族与部落会议制衡。
governmentForm.en: A Celtic tribal confederation dominated by a warrior aristocracy, with kingship balanced by noble warriors and tribal assemblies.

socialStructure.zh: 社会上层为部落王、公侯贵族和武士，宗教权威由德鲁伊或祭司群体掌握。中层包括自由农民、牧民与工匠，下层则有依附者、债役者和奴隶。血缘关系与战士追随网络共同构成社会组织基础。
socialStructure.en: The upper stratum consisted of tribal kings, nobles, and warriors, while religious authority rested with druids or priestly figures. The middle layers included free farmers, herders, and artisans, and the lower strata contained dependents, debt-bondsmen, and slaves. Kinship and warrior followings together formed the basis of social organization.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层是拥有土地、牲畜和武装追随者的贵族家族。其权威来自战功、财富分配、宗教赞助和在部族会议中的影响力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was composed of noble families that held land, livestock, and armed followers. Their authority derived from military success, distribution of wealth, religious patronage, and influence in tribal assemblies.

succession.zh: 王位多在显贵家族中选立，常兼具世袭与推举性质。若候选人缺乏战功或贵族支持，部族会议可转而拥立他支贵族。
succession.en: Kingship was often chosen within leading families and combined hereditary and elective elements. If a candidate lacked military prestige or noble backing, the tribal assembly could support another noble branch.

structure.zh: 波伊人没有高度集中的国家官僚体系，而是以部族王庭、贵族议事和地方氏族领袖共治。重大事务如战争、迁徙、结盟和贡赋分配，通常在贵族集会中决定。王权更多是军事与仲裁核心，而非全面行政机器。
structure.en: The Boii did not possess a highly centralized bureaucratic state, but were governed through the king's court, noble deliberation, and local clan leaders. Major matters such as war, migration, alliances, and tribute distribution were usually decided in aristocratic gatherings. Kingship served more as a military and arbitral center than as a comprehensive administrative machine.

function.zh: 组织部族会议、传达王令、处理贵族协商与盟约。
function.en: Organized tribal assemblies, conveyed royal commands, and handled noble negotiations and agreements.

function.zh: 征收贡物、分配战利品并维持贵族追随网络。
function.en: Collected tribute, distributed spoils, and maintained networks of noble retainers.

function.zh: 统计可战之民、召集战士并筹备迁徙或远征。
function.en: Recorded fighting manpower, summoned warriors, and prepared migrations or campaigns.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由氏族首领和贵族庄园主负责，聚落拥有相当自治。中心权力主要通过人质、盟誓、馈赠和军事召集来维持。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by clan chiefs and noble estate holders, and settlements retained considerable autonomy. Central authority was maintained mainly through hostages, oaths, gifts, and military summons.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以凯尔特习惯法和口头裁决为主，德鲁伊或长老在仲裁中具有重要作用。赔偿、誓约和家族责任是常见的法律原则。
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on Celtic customary practice and oral judgment, with druids or elders playing important roles in arbitration. Compensation, oath-taking, and family responsibility were common legal principles.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有普遍的定额税，统治者主要依靠贡赋、牲畜、战利品和随从供养。战时可额外征集粮食、车辆和劳役。
taxationSystem.en: There was no universal fixed tax; rulers relied chiefly on tribute, livestock, war booty, and support from followers. In wartime, extra grain, wagons, and labor could be requisitioned.

religion.zh: 凯尔特多神教
religion.en: Celtic polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 波伊人文化以精良铁器、武器工艺、车战传统和装饰艺术著称。其迁徙与贸易活动使其成为中欧至北意大利之间凯尔特文化传播的重要力量。
culturalAchievements.en: Boii culture was noted for fine ironworking, weapon craft, chariot traditions, and decorative art. Their migrations and trade made them an important force in transmitting Celtic culture between central Europe and northern Italy.

languageFamily.zh: 凯尔特语族大陆凯尔特语支
languageFamily.en: Celtic language family, Continental Celtic branch

philosophy.zh: 凯尔特多神信仰强调神圣自然、祖先、战士荣誉与祭司传统，德鲁伊知识具有重要社会权威。
philosophy.en: Celtic polytheism emphasized sacred nature, ancestors, warrior honor, and priestly tradition, with druidic knowledge carrying major social authority.

writingSystem.zh: 日常政治与法律主要依赖口传，可能接触到伊特鲁里亚或早期意大利字母影响，但本族广泛书写尚不普及。
writingSystem.en: Daily politics and law relied mainly on oral tradition, though contact with Etruscan or early Italic scripts was possible; widespread native literacy was not yet common.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济建立在谷物农业、畜牧业、铁器制造和区域贸易之上，生产力在同时代欧洲内陆部族中较强。财富集中在贵族所控制的土地、牲畜、武器和战利品网络中。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was built on grain farming, livestock husbandry, ironworking, and regional trade, with relatively strong productivity for inland European tribes of the era. Wealth was concentrated in aristocratic control of land, herds, weapons, and networks of spoils.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、铁器冶铸、木工、制车、盐与区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, herding, iron smelting and forging, woodworking, wagon-making, salt, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、武器、盐、牲畜、皮革、琥珀、谷物、奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Iron goods, weapons, salt, livestock, leather, amber, grain, slaves

householdWealth.zh: 自由家庭的主要财富是土地使用权、牲畜、谷仓和铁制工具。贵族家庭则拥有战车、武器、宴饮器具和更多依附人口。
householdWealth.en: Free households held wealth mainly in land-use rights, herds, granaries, and iron tools. Noble households possessed chariots, weapons, feasting equipment, and larger dependent populations.

unit.en: grams of silver per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以贵族庄园、部族贡赋、战利品再分配和区域交换相结合的凯尔特部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A Celtic tribal economy combining aristocratic estates, tribal tribute, redistribution of spoils, and regional exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备多以银块、牲畜、谷物和战利品形式存在，分散于王庭与贵族据点，而非统一国库。
treasuryDescription.en: Fiscal reserves existed mainly as silver bullion, livestock, grain, and spoils, dispersed among the royal court and noble strongholds rather than kept in a single state treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政策略重在通过战利品和馈赠维持贵族忠诚，并在必要时以贸易通道收益补充王庭资源。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal strategy focused on maintaining noble loyalty through spoils and gifts, while supplementing royal resources with income from trade routes when necessary.

description.zh: 由贵族及其扈从组成，是冲击力最强的核心武装，常配长剑、长矛和盾牌。
description.en: Composed of nobles and their retainers, this was the hardest-hitting core force, commonly equipped with long swords, spears, and shields.

description.zh: 由自由民临时集结，人数最多，负责主战线和防御聚落。
description.en: Raised from free men on a temporary basis, they formed the largest body and held the main battle line and settlement defenses.

description.zh: 数量较少但具威望，用于机动、追击和展示贵族身份。
description.en: Few in number but prestigious, used for mobility, pursuit, and display of noble status.

commanderInChief.zh: 部落王
commanderInChief.en: Tribal King

technology.zh: 军备以铁剑、矛、盾、锁甲雏形和木制战车为特点，冶铁水平在欧洲内陆部族中较先进。作战风格重个人勇武、贵族冲击与部族集群作战。
technology.en: Military equipment featured iron swords, spears, shields, early mail, and wooden chariots, with ironworking comparatively advanced for inland European tribes. Their style of war emphasized personal valor, aristocratic shock action, and tribal mass fighting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近凯尔特竞争部族、伊利里亚与意大利北部势力，以及部族联盟内部的分裂倾向。
threats.en: The main threats came from rival Celtic tribes, Illyrian and northern Italian powers, and the tendency of the confederation itself to fragment.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以乡村聚落、山间据点和河谷农牧社区为主，尚未形成真正大城市。若干设防高地聚落与贸易节点具有准城市功能。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in rural settlements, hill strongholds, and river-valley agro-pastoral communities, without true large cities. Several fortified hill settlements and trade nodes served quasi-urban roles.

name.zh: 波希米亚中部设防聚落

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族、德鲁伊与祭司、自由战士与农民、工匠和商人、依附民、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royal family and nobles, druids and priests, free warriors and farmers, artisans and merchants, dependents, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分凯尔特邻族保持临时军事合作，并通过婚盟和贸易维系跨区域关系。
allies.en: Maintained temporary military cooperation with some neighboring Celtic groups and sustained wider regional ties through marriage alliances and trade.

enemies.zh: 与争夺草场、盐路和贸易通道的邻近部族及意大利北部社群常有敌对。
enemies.en: Frequently clashed with neighboring tribes and northern Italian communities competing for pasture, salt routes, and trade corridors.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在控制迁徙通道、扩大贸易优势并以战争获取声望和财富。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized control of migration corridors, expansion of trade advantage, and the pursuit of prestige and wealth through warfare.

era.zh: 中欧拉坦文化早期铁器时代
era.en: Early La Tène Iron Age Central Europe

keyInnovations.zh: 高质量铁器锻造、装饰金属工艺、战车传统、设防高地聚落、长距离盐与金属贸易。
keyInnovations.en: High-quality iron forging, decorative metalwork, chariot traditions, fortified hill settlements, and long-distance salt and metal trade.

strengths.zh: 武士传统强盛，铁器工艺优良，人口与动员能力在凯尔特世界中较为可观。
strengths.en: They possessed a strong warrior tradition, high-quality ironworking, and considerable population and mobilization capacity within the Celtic world.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合有限，王权依赖贵族支持，财政和行政集中度不足。
weaknesses.en: Political integration was limited, kingship depended on noble backing, and fiscal and administrative centralization was weak.

outlook.zh: 若能维持联盟并掌握贸易走廊，波伊人可继续扩张影响；但内部分裂和外部强敌将长期制约其发展。
outlook.en: If they could maintain the confederation and control trade corridors, the Boii could continue expanding their influence; however, internal fragmentation and powerful external enemies would constrain long-term development.

description.zh: 波伊人是中欧早期铁器时代以贵族武士、农牧经济和跨区域贸易著称的凯尔特部族联盟。
description.en: The Boii were a Celtic tribal confederation of early Iron Age central Europe known for aristocratic warriors, agro-pastoral wealth, and long-distance trade.

governmentForm.zh: 以血缘与营地共识为基础的分散狩猎采集部族治理。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized hunter-gatherer tribal order based on kinship and camp consensus.

socialStructure.zh: 科伊桑诸群体多以小型游群、亲族网络与季节性营地构成社会基本单位。社会分层较浅，财富差异有限，但熟练猎人、医者、占卜者与长者享有较高威望。婚姻与互赠维系群体间关系，并缓和资源波动带来的风险。
socialStructure.en: Khoisan societies were organized around small mobile bands, kin networks, and seasonal camps. Social stratification was shallow and wealth inequality limited, though skilled hunters, healers, diviners, and elders held greater prestige. Marriage ties and gift exchange linked groups and helped buffer ecological risk.

rulingClass.zh: 严格意义上并无固定统治阶层，长老、资深猎手与仪式人物在决策中较有影响力。其权威主要来自经验、说服力与调解能力，而非强制权力。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed ruling class in a strict sense; elders, experienced hunters, and ritual specialists carried influence in decision-making. Their authority rested mainly on experience, persuasion, and mediation rather than coercive power.

succession.zh: 领导地位通常不世袭，而是在营地或游群内部由声望较高者自然形成。若原有长者失去支持，群体可通过分营或共识转向其他领袖。
succession.en: Leadership was usually not hereditary but emerged informally around high-prestige individuals within a band or camp. If an elder lost support, the group could shift allegiance through consensus or by fissioning into separate camps.

structure.zh: 政治组织极为松散，日常事务在营地层级通过讨论与共识处理。跨营地合作多围绕婚姻、共享水源、狩猎路线与争端调解展开，并无常设官僚系统。
structure.en: Political organization was highly loose, with daily matters settled at the camp level through discussion and consensus. Inter-band coordination focused on marriage ties, shared water sources, hunting routes, and dispute mediation, with no permanent bureaucracy.

function.zh: 协调营地迁徙、分配水源使用、调解家庭与群体纠纷。
function.en: Coordinated camp movements, regulated access to water, and mediated family and inter-group disputes.

function.zh: 主持治病、占卜、成年礼及与祖灵相关的仪式活动。
function.en: Oversaw healing, divination, initiation, and rituals connected to spirits and ancestors.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即营地自治，由数名长者与有威望者主持讨论。不同游群在旱季与丰水季的聚散具有很高弹性。
localAdmin.en: Local administration took the form of self-governing camps guided by a few elders and respected figures. Bands aggregated and dispersed flexibly between dry and wet seasons.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，强调赔偿、和解与群体压力。严重冲突若无法调解，常以分离迁徙而非制度化惩罚解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, and group pressure. When serious conflicts could not be mediated, separation and relocation were more common than formal punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，公共需求通过共享猎获、互赠与共同劳动满足。仪式性馈赠和对长者的尊重性供给可视为准财政机制。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; communal needs were met through shared game, reciprocal exchange, and collective labor. Ritual gifts and support for elders functioned as quasi-fiscal mechanisms.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 南部非洲岩画传统与复杂的动物象征体系是其突出文化成就。其追踪术、生态知识与治病舞传统在非文字社会中高度发达。
culturalAchievements.en: The rock art traditions of southern Africa and their complex animal symbolism were major cultural achievements. Tracking skill, ecological knowledge, and trance-healing dance were highly developed within a non-literate society.

languageFamily.zh: 科伊桑诸语言（含多种搭嘴音语言支系）
languageFamily.en: Khoisan language groups (including several click-language branches)

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、祖灵联系、人与动物及地景相互嵌合的世界观为核心，重视梦兆、治病舞与口传伦理。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, ancestral connection, and an embedded relationship among people, animals, and landscape, with strong emphasis on dreams, trance healing dances, and oral ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣、岩画与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, song, rock art, and ritual.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、采集和小规模互换为主，货币化程度极低。产出虽不集中，但在适宜生态条件下可维持稳定生计与较高机动性。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on hunting, gathering, and small-scale exchange, with very little monetization. Output was not concentrated, but under favorable ecological conditions it supported stable subsistence and high mobility.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、兽皮加工、骨角器制作、岩画与仪式用品制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, hide processing, bone and horn toolmaking, rock art, and ritual item production

tradeGoods.zh: 鸵鸟蛋壳珠、兽皮、骨器、石料、颜料、药草
tradeGoods.en: Ostrich eggshell beads, hides, bone tools, stone materials, pigments, and medicinal plants

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在工具、饰物、皮具、人际互助网络与可获取的水源知识，而非土地或积储金属。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth lay mainly in tools, ornaments, leather goods, reciprocal social ties, and knowledge of water sources rather than land or stored metal wealth.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year equivalent

economicSystem.zh: 以共享性采集经济和互惠交换为核心的生计体系，辅以季节性区域互通。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence system centered on sharing-based foraging and reciprocal exchange, supplemented by seasonal regional interaction.

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无集中金库，储备主要表现为珠饰、皮革、工具和互助义务网络。危机时依赖亲族与邻近群体的再分配。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves existed mainly as beads, hides, tools, and reciprocal obligations. In times of crisis, redistribution through kin and neighboring groups was crucial.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政逻辑以维持共享、避免过度积累和保障旱季生存为主。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal logic prioritized sharing, avoidance of excessive accumulation, and survival during dry seasons.

description.zh: 以弓箭和毒箭为主，兼具狩猎与防卫功能，适合伏击与追踪。
description.en: Primarily armed with bows and poisoned arrows, serving both hunting and defense, well suited to ambush and tracking.

description.zh: 负责营地、妇孺与水源点警戒，多在局部冲突中动员。
description.en: Responsible for guarding camps, families, and water points, usually mobilized in localized conflicts.

commanderInChief.zh: 无固定总司令，由资深长老与猎手临时统率
commanderInChief.en: No fixed commander-in-chief; senior elders and hunters led ad hoc

technology.zh: 军事技术较低，但弓箭、毒药、隐蔽接近、追踪识迹和地形利用能力极强。
technology.en: Military technology was simple, but skill in bows, poisons, stealthy approach, tracking, and terrain use was very strong.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自争夺水源与猎场的邻近群体冲突，以及生态波动导致的生存压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from conflicts with neighboring groups over water and hunting grounds, as well as ecological stress from environmental fluctuation.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布稀疏，集中于南部非洲干旱、半干旱与沿海资源带。聚落规模小，流动性高，季节迁徙明显。
populationDescription.en: Population was sparse, spread across arid, semi-arid, and coastal resource zones of southern Africa. Settlements were small, highly mobile, and marked by clear seasonal movement.

socialClasses.zh: 分层较弱，可区分为长老与仪式者、成年猎采者、青年与儿童；并无严格世袭等级。
socialClasses.en: Stratification was weak, broadly distinguishable into elders and ritual specialists, adult foragers, and youth and children; there was no rigid hereditary hierarchy.

allies.zh: 无稳定国家盟友，主要依靠邻近游群、通婚伙伴与交换网络。
allies.en: No stable state allies; support came mainly from neighboring bands, marriage partners, and exchange networks.

enemies.zh: 无固定宿敌，但与争夺水源、猎场和采集区的邻群会发生周期性摩擦。
enemies.en: There were no fixed long-term enemies, but periodic frictions occurred with neighboring groups competing for water, hunting grounds, and gathering zones.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系重在回避大规模冲突、维持通婚和共享资源规则。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations prioritized avoiding large-scale conflict while preserving marriage ties and shared resource norms.

era.zh: 晚期石器时代南部非洲狩猎采集文化
era.en: Later Stone Age southern African foraging culture

keyInnovations.zh: 毒箭、微石器组合工具、精细追踪术、鸵鸟蛋壳珠制作、岩画颜料技术。
keyInnovations.en: Poisoned arrows, microlithic composite tools, advanced tracking, ostrich eggshell bead production, and rock-art pigment techniques.

strengths.zh: 生态适应力强、机动性高、共享网络稳固、追踪与荒地生存技能卓越。
strengths.en: Strong ecological adaptation, high mobility, resilient sharing networks, and exceptional tracking and survival skills.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、缺乏集中权力、难以长期集结兵力，且易受旱灾影响。
weaknesses.en: Sparse population, lack of centralized authority, difficulty sustaining large mobilizations, and vulnerability to drought.

outlook.zh: 在公元前500年前后仍能维持稳定区域生计，但难形成更高层级政治整合。
outlook.en: Around 500 BCE these peoples could maintain stable regional livelihoods, but were unlikely to form higher-order political integration.

description.zh: 科伊桑诸群体是南部非洲以狩猎采集、营地共识和灵性仪式维系社会的分散部族世界。
description.en: The Khoisan peoples formed a dispersed southern African tribal world sustained by foraging, camp consensus, and spiritual ritual.

dynasty.zh: 无王朝氏族与部落网络

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族、部落和仪式法权为核心的分散长老治理体系。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized elder-led order centered on clans, tribes, and ritual law.

socialStructure.zh: 澳大利亚原住民社会由众多语言群、氏族和地缘群体组成，土地权与亲属关系深度嵌合。成年礼、婚配规则、图腾身份与圣地义务决定社会位置。各地社会形态差异很大，但整体上都强调长者权威与口传法则。
socialStructure.en: Australian Aboriginal society consisted of many language groups, clans, and territorial communities in which land rights were deeply tied to kinship. Initiation, marriage rules, totemic identity, and obligations to sacred places shaped social position. Social forms varied greatly by region, but elder authority and oral law were broadly central.

rulingClass.zh: 并无统一贵族阶层，男性长老、知识持有者、仪式主持者和部分资深女性在不同地区掌握权威。权威来自对歌线、圣地、婚姻规则与习惯法的掌握。
rulingClass.en: There was no unified aristocratic class; male elders, custodians of knowledge, ritual leaders, and in some regions senior women held authority. Prestige derived from control of songlines, sacred sites, marriage regulations, and customary law.

succession.zh: 权威通常按年龄、知识和亲属资格递进，不以王位世袭形式传承。某些仪式职责可在特定宗族或血缘群体内延续。
succession.en: Authority usually advanced through age, knowledge, and kin qualification rather than dynastic inheritance. Some ritual roles could continue within specific descent groups.

structure.zh: 政治结构分散，核心单位是地方氏族群与仪式联盟。长老会议负责处理婚姻、边界、圣地、复仇与和解等事务，但不存在覆盖全大陆的中央政权。
structure.en: Political structure was decentralized, with local clan communities and ritual alliances as the core units. Councils of elders managed marriage, boundaries, sacred sites, revenge, and reconciliation, but there was no continent-wide central authority.

function.zh: 裁定习惯法、安排婚姻规则、调解争端与组织季节性集会。
function.en: Adjudicated customary law, arranged marriage rules, mediated disputes, and organized seasonal gatherings.

function.zh: 保管歌线知识、主持成年礼与祭仪，并监督对圣地的使用。
function.en: Preserved songline knowledge, led initiation and ceremonies, and supervised use of sacred sites.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政即氏族与营地层级的自治，各地依据生态环境形成不同规模的领地组织。河谷、海岸与资源丰富区常有更稳定的季节聚居点。
localAdmin.en: Local administration consisted of clan- and camp-level self-rule, with territorial organization varying by ecology. Riverine, coastal, and resource-rich zones often had more stable seasonal aggregation sites.

legalSystem.zh: 法律为口传习惯法，依托梦世法则、祖先道路和长者裁决。惩戒方式包括赔偿、仪式处罚、决斗、婚姻调整与群体和解。
legalSystem.en: Law was oral customary law grounded in Dreaming principles, ancestral tracks, and elder judgment. Sanctions included compensation, ritual punishment, dueling, marriage adjustment, and communal reconciliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税收，公共义务通过食物分享、仪式供给、劳作协助与婚姻交换承担。大型集会时会有集中馈赠与资源再分配。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; public obligations were met through food sharing, ritual contributions, labor assistance, and marriage exchange. Larger gatherings involved concentrated gift-giving and redistribution.

religion.zh: 梦幻时代信仰
religion.en: Dreamtime religion

culturalAchievements.zh: 其歌线网络将广阔空间整合为可记忆、可通行的文化地图，是极高水平的非文字知识体系。岩画、木器、编织与火管理技术亦极具代表性。
culturalAchievements.en: Its songline networks integrated vast space into a memorable and navigable cultural map, representing a highly sophisticated non-literate knowledge system. Rock art, woodcraft, weaving, and fire management were also major achievements.

languageFamily.zh: 澳大利亚原住民诸语言（以帕马-纽恩加语系及多种非帕马-纽恩加语言为主）
languageFamily.en: Australian Aboriginal languages (dominated by Pama-Nyungan and several non-Pama-Nyungan families)

philosophy.zh: 核心为梦世信仰，认为祖先在创世过程中塑造地貌、律法与生命秩序，人与土地负有相互维护义务。
philosophy.en: Its core was Dreaming belief, in which ancestral beings shaped landforms, law, and the order of life, creating reciprocal obligations between people and country.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，知识通过歌线、口述叙事、身体彩绘、岩画与仪式表演传承。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; knowledge was transmitted through songlines, oral narrative, body painting, rock art, and ceremonial performance.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎采集、渔猎、火耕式环境管理和区域交换为主，部分地区存在较稳定的定期集市。虽然缺乏国家财政和城市市场，但生态知识大幅提高了资源利用效率。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on hunting, gathering, fishing, fire-managed landscapes, and regional exchange, with some areas supporting relatively regular meeting-trade sites. Though lacking state finance and urban markets, ecological knowledge significantly increased resource efficiency.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎采集、渔猎、火管理、石器木器制作、篮编、赭石开采与交换
mainIndustries.en: Hunting and gathering, fishing, fire management, stone and wooden tool production, basketry, and ochre extraction and exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 赭石、贝壳、石斧坯料、木矛、树脂、编织品、珍贵羽饰
tradeGoods.en: Ochre, shells, stone axe blanks, wooden spears, resin, woven goods, and valued feathers

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富体现为对土地与圣地的使用权、工具储备、婚姻关系、歌线知识和交换伙伴网络。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed through rights in country and sacred places, tool stores, marriage ties, songline knowledge, and exchange partnerships.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year equivalent

economicSystem.zh: 以土地管理、共享分配和远距离礼物交换结合的部落生计经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal subsistence economy combining land management, communal distribution, and long-distance gift exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 无集中金库，储备主要体现在食物知识、工具、赭石、珍贵交换物与亲族互助义务之中。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves were embodied in food knowledge, tools, ochre, valued exchange items, and kin-based mutual obligations.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 资源分配重在维持圣地义务、季节平衡和跨群体互惠，避免个人过度占有。
fiscalPolicy.en: Resource distribution emphasized sacred obligations, seasonal balance, and inter-group reciprocity while discouraging excessive private accumulation.

description.zh: 由成年男子临时组成，使用矛、盾、投矛器和棍棒进行部落冲突与复仇行动。
description.en: Temporary bodies of adult men using spears, shields, spear-throwers, and clubs for tribal conflict and revenge expeditions.

description.zh: 负责守护营地、妇孺和集会地点，多在突发袭击时行动。
description.en: Protected camps, families, and gathering sites, usually acting during sudden raids.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一统帅，由资深战士与长老临时领队
commanderInChief.en: No unified supreme commander; senior warriors and elders led temporarily

technology.zh: 军事技术以木石兵器为主，但投矛器、地形熟悉、伏击和远距离行进能力很强。
technology.en: Military technology was based on wood and stone weaponry, but spear-throwers, knowledge of terrain, ambush, and long-distance movement were strong advantages.

threats.zh: 威胁主要来自部落边界争端、复仇循环、资源匮乏时的区域冲突和局部环境压力。
threats.en: Threats mainly came from territorial disputes, cycles of revenge, regional conflict during scarcity, and localized environmental pressure.

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于全澳洲大陆及塔斯马尼亚周边海域，密度极低但文化多样性极高。沿海、河流和资源富集区聚居较多，内陆荒漠地带更为分散。
populationDescription.en: Population was spread across the Australian continent and nearby Tasmanian waters, with very low density but extremely high cultural diversity. Coastal, riverine, and resource-rich areas supported greater concentration, while the arid interior was far more dispersed.

socialClasses.zh: 无严格阶级制，可概括为长老与仪式知识持有者、成年男女劳动者、青年受训者与儿童；部分地区还区分更细的婚姻分组。
socialClasses.en: There was no rigid class system; broadly one may distinguish elders and ritual knowledge holders, adult male and female workers, initiated youth, and children, with some regions also maintaining finer marriage sections.

allies.zh: 以邻近语言群、婚姻交换伙伴和共同仪式网络为主要盟系。
allies.en: Alliances were mainly formed through neighboring language groups, marriage exchange partners, and shared ritual networks.

enemies.zh: 无永久性大陆级敌人，但邻地氏族和竞争资源群体之间常有长期仇怨。
enemies.en: There were no permanent continent-wide enemies, but neighboring clans and rival resource groups often maintained long-standing feuds.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持领地边界、遵守礼物交换和通过仪式重建和平为原则。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations emphasized maintaining territorial boundaries, honoring gift exchange, and restoring peace through ceremony.

era.zh: 澳洲全新世后期原住民石木器与火管理时代
era.en: Late Holocene Aboriginal stone-wood and fire-management era

keyInnovations.zh: 投矛器、系统性火棒农业、歌线导航、复杂渔猎设施、岩画与身体彩绘传统。
keyInnovations.en: Spear-throwers, systematic fire-stick farming, songline navigation, complex fishing structures, and traditions of rock art and body painting.

strengths.zh: 环境适应极强、知识体系深厚、远距离文化联系广、土地管理有效。
strengths.en: Very strong environmental adaptation, deep knowledge systems, broad long-distance cultural links, and effective land management.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、金属技术和大规模粮食储备，不利于集中动员与复杂国家形成。
weaknesses.en: The absence of unified state structures, metal technology, and large grain surpluses limited centralized mobilization and complex state formation.

outlook.zh: 在公元前500年前后，其社会能够长期稳定延续并保持高度文化复杂性，但不会向统一国家形态发展。
outlook.en: Around 500 BCE these societies were capable of long-term stability and high cultural complexity, but were unlikely to develop into a unified state form.

description.zh: 澳大利亚原住民是以梦世法则、氏族领地和长老权威维系的广域而多样的非国家文明体系。
description.en: Australian Aboriginal society was a vast and diverse non-state civilization sustained by Dreaming law, clan territories, and elder authority.

governmentForm.zh: 由诸多岛屿酋邦、村社首领和航海共同体构成的分散酋长制网络。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of island chiefdoms, village headmen, and seafaring communities.

socialStructure.zh: 南岛语族世界横跨台湾、菲律宾、印尼群岛、太平洋西部及部分印度洋沿岸，社会组织从平等村社到分层酋邦不等。多数地区以氏族、家族和村社为核心，首领、航海者、祭司与工匠拥有较高地位。部分岛屿已出现较稳定的贡赋关系和首领再分配体系。
socialStructure.en: The Austronesian world stretched across Taiwan, the Philippines, the Indonesian archipelago, the western Pacific, and parts of the Indian Ocean, with social forms ranging from relatively egalitarian villages to stratified chiefdoms. In most areas clans, households, and village communities were central, while chiefs, navigators, ritual specialists, and craftsmen held higher status. Some islands had already developed stable tribute relations and chiefly redistributive systems.

rulingClass.zh: 统治者主要是岛屿酋长、家族首领和祭仪领袖，部分地区存在世袭贵族化倾向。其权威依托祖先谱系、航海组织能力、武力和对贸易节点的控制。
rulingClass.en: Rulers were mainly island chiefs, lineage heads, and ritual leaders, with some regions tending toward hereditary aristocracy. Their authority rested on ancestral genealogy, ability to organize voyages, force, and control of trade nodes.

succession.zh: 继承通常在首领家族内部进行，但常需得到长老、亲族和战士支持。若继承争议激化，分支家族往往通过迁徙建新聚落解决。
succession.en: Succession usually occurred within chiefly families, but often required support from elders, kin groups, and warriors. When disputes intensified, branch lineages often resolved them by migration and founding new settlements.

structure.zh: 政体以村社和酋邦为主，缺乏统一帝国式中央机构。较大的岛屿或海峡地区可形成多村联盟，由酋长、祭司和航海长共同处理贸易、防务与宗教事务。
structure.en: Polities were based chiefly on villages and chiefdoms, without a unified imperial center. In larger islands or strait zones, multi-village alliances could form, with chiefs, ritual specialists, and master navigators jointly handling trade, defense, and religion.

function.zh: 管理贡赋、土地与渔场分配，并调解村社间争端。
function.en: Managed tribute, allocation of land and fishing grounds, and mediation between communities.

function.zh: 组织远航、维护海上交换网络并安排船只与货物调度。
function.en: Organized long-distance voyages, maintained maritime exchange networks, and coordinated vessels and cargo.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、守护祖灵崇拜并确认首领合法性。
function.en: Led ceremonies, upheld ancestral worship, and affirmed chiefly legitimacy.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理多由村长、宗族首领和酋长代表负责，行政单位通常是村落、海湾或岛屿群。偏远岛屿保有较高自治性，核心港湾地区则更集中。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was usually handled by village heads, lineage leaders, and chiefly representatives, with villages, bays, or island clusters as the main units. Remote islands retained substantial autonomy, while core harbor zones were more concentrated.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法和祖先规范为基础，涵盖婚姻、土地、渔场、航海义务与赔偿制度。首领裁决重要纠纷，必要时通过贡礼、盟誓或迁徙化解。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary rules and ancestral norms, covering marriage, land, fishing grounds, voyaging obligations, and compensation. Chiefs judged major disputes, which could be settled through tribute, oath-taking, or migration.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制多为贡赋与劳役，形式包括稻谷、块根、鱼获、纺织品、独木舟劳作与节庆供奉。海上贸易节点还可征收象征性港贡。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation largely took the form of tribute and labor dues, including rice, root crops, fish catches, textiles, canoe labor, and ceremonial offerings. Maritime trade nodes could also levy symbolic harbor dues.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 双体船、支架船与恒星导航构成杰出的航海传统，使广阔海域得以文化连通。纺织、木雕、房屋建筑和谱系口述传统亦十分发达。
culturalAchievements.en: Double canoes, outrigger craft, and stellar navigation formed an extraordinary maritime tradition that connected wide oceanic spaces culturally. Weaving, woodcarving, house construction, and oral genealogy were likewise highly developed.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系
languageFamily.en: Austronesian language family

philosophy.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、万物有灵、海洋宇宙观和谱系记忆为核心，强调人与海、岛、风向和祖先之间的连续关系。
philosophy.en: Centered on ancestor veneration, animism, an oceanic cosmology, and genealogical memory, emphasizing continuity between people, sea, islands, winds, and ancestors.

writingSystem.zh: 公元前500年前后大多无本土成熟文字，知识主要依靠口传谱系、航海歌诀、纹饰符号与仪式记忆保存。
writingSystem.en: Around 500 BCE most Austronesian societies had no mature indigenous writing system; knowledge was preserved through oral genealogies, navigational chants, symbolic motifs, and ritual memory.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济结合刀耕火种农业、园圃作物、渔业、海运和岛际贸易，整体产出高于一般纯采集社会。沿海与海峡地带通过航海交换积累较多财富，并推动酋邦权威增长。
gdpDescription.en: The economy combined shifting agriculture, horticultural crops, fishing, seaborne transport, and inter-island trade, producing higher output than purely foraging societies. Coastal and strait zones accumulated more wealth through maritime exchange, strengthening chiefly authority.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作与块根农业、椰子与香蕉种植、渔业、独木舟造船、纺织、陶器、木雕和海上贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice and root-crop cultivation, coconut and banana growing, fishing, canoe building, weaving, pottery, woodcarving, and maritime trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石、贝饰、盐、干鱼、陶器、树脂、香料前体、鸟羽、织物、木材
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian, shell ornaments, salt, dried fish, pottery, resin, proto-spice products, feathers, textiles, and timber

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为舟船、耕地使用权、果树、纺织品、贝饰、奴役或依附者数量以及贸易关系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed in boats, use-rights in cultivated land, fruit trees, textiles, shell valuables, numbers of dependents or captives, and trade connections.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year equivalent

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、酋长再分配和海上交换网络相结合的岛屿经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An island economic system combining village production, chiefly redistribution, and maritime exchange networks.

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备多为贝饰、粮食、船材、织物和酋长掌控的珍贵贸易品，而非金属铸币金库。较大酋邦可在丰年积累较多再分配物资。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves consisted mainly of shell valuables, food stores, boat timber, textiles, and prized trade goods under chiefly control rather than a coined-metal treasury. Larger chiefdoms could accumulate notable redistributive stores in good years.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政侧重通过贡赋与宴飨巩固首领权威，并优先投资航海与跨岛联系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on reinforcing chiefly authority through tribute and feasting, while prioritizing voyaging and inter-island connectivity.

description.zh: 以矛、木盾、弓箭和短刃为主，负责村社冲突、袭击和酋长护卫。
description.en: Armed mainly with spears, wooden shields, bows, and short blades, they handled village warfare, raids, and chiefly protection.

description.zh: 乘独木舟或支架船进行跨岛袭扰、护航和海峡控制。
description.en: Using canoes or outrigger craft, they conducted inter-island raids, escorts, and strait control.

description.zh: 守卫港湾、粮仓和酋长居地，战时可快速扩编。
description.en: Defended harbors, storehouses, and chiefly residences, and could expand rapidly in wartime.

commanderInChief.zh: 各岛酋长与航海战首领
commanderInChief.en: Island chiefs and maritime war leaders

technology.zh: 技术水平属新石器至早期金属接触边缘阶段，以造船、航海、渔猎器具和木石兵器见长，部分西部岛区可能已接触少量青铜或铁器流入。
technology.en: Technology ranged from Neolithic to early metal-contact fringes, excelling in boatbuilding, navigation, fishing gear, and wood-stone weaponry; some western islands may already have seen limited bronze or iron inflow.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自岛际竞争、海峡控制权争夺、海盗式袭扰、资源不足和风暴导致的航海损失。
threats.en: Main threats came from inter-island rivalry, struggles over strait control, raiding, resource shortfalls, and storm-related maritime losses.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于广阔岛屿世界，密度在肥沃火山岛、三角洲、沿海平原和渔业海湾更高。社会聚落多为村庄、港湾聚落和小型酋长中心。
populationDescription.en: Population was dispersed across a vast island world, with higher density on fertile volcanic islands, deltas, coastal plains, and rich fishing bays. Settlements were mainly villages, harbor communities, and small chiefly centers.

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为酋长与贵胄、祭司与航海专家、自由平民、依附者及少量战俘奴役者；各岛差异明显。
socialClasses.en: Broadly, society could include chiefs and nobles, ritual specialists and navigators, free commoners, dependents, and small numbers of war captives or slaves, though island-to-island variation was substantial.

allies.zh: 盟友多为通婚岛群、贸易伙伴和共享航线的酋邦联盟。
allies.en: Allies were mainly marriage-linked island groups, trade partners, and chiefdom alliances sharing sea routes.

enemies.zh: 敌对者通常是争夺海峡、渔场、港湾和贸易中转地的邻近岛群。
enemies.en: Enemies were typically neighboring island groups competing for straits, fishing grounds, harbors, and trade entrepôts.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以扩展航线、稳固交换关系、控制港湾节点并通过婚盟缓和冲突为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on expanding sea routes, stabilizing exchange ties, controlling harbor nodes, and easing conflict through marriage alliances.

era.zh: 南岛航海扩张时代晚期新石器—早期金属接触阶段
era.en: Late Austronesian expansion era, Neolithic to early metal-contact phase

keyInnovations.zh: 支架船与双体船、恒星与海流导航、远洋定居、精细纺织、独木舟造船和园圃复合农业。
keyInnovations.en: Outrigger and double-hulled craft, stellar and current navigation, long-distance settlement, refined weaving, canoe construction, and mixed horticultural agriculture.

strengths.zh: 航海能力卓越、贸易网络广、农业与渔业互补、社会扩张性强。
strengths.en: Exceptional seafaring, broad trade networks, complementary agriculture and fishing, and strong capacity for expansion.

weaknesses.zh: 政治碎片化明显、岛际协调困难、易受风暴与海上风险影响，且重型陆战能力有限。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation was marked, inter-island coordination was difficult, societies were vulnerable to storms and maritime risk, and heavy land-warfare capability was limited.

outlook.zh: 公元前500年前后，南岛诸社会最有可能继续依靠海上迁徙和贸易增长，逐步形成更复杂的地方酋邦。
outlook.en: Around 500 BCE Austronesian societies were most likely to continue growing through maritime migration and trade, gradually producing more complex local chiefdoms.

description.zh: 南岛语族在公元前500年前后构成了横跨岛屿与海洋、以航海、酋邦和交换网络为核心的动态文明圈。
description.en: Around 500 BCE, the Austronesian peoples formed a dynamic island-oceanic civilization centered on seafaring, chiefdoms, and exchange networks.

governmentForm.zh: 以巴底利斯为共主的部落联盟君长制，依靠个人威望、军事成功与部族协商维系统治。
governmentForm.en: A tribal hegemonic kingship under Bardyllis, maintained through personal authority, military success, and negotiation among clans.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、部落与贵族武士集团为核心，自由民承担放牧、耕作与战时征召。下层包括依附民、战俘与奴役人口，但其地位在各部之间并不完全一致。山地聚落与谷地社区之间联系紧密，婚姻与盟誓是整合的重要手段。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on clans, tribes, and aristocratic warrior groups, while free commoners handled herding, farming, and wartime levies. Lower strata included dependents, captives, and enslaved persons, though their status varied by tribe. Mountain settlements and valley communities were tied together by marriage alliances and oath-bonds.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由王族、部落首领与贵族骑战精英构成。其权力建立在控制牧场、战利品分配与对随从武装的领导之上。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the royal household, tribal chiefs, and aristocratic mounted-warrior elites. Their power rested on control of pasturelands, distribution of booty, and leadership over armed retainers.

succession.zh: 继承通常倾向于王族内部的男性亲属，但并非严格长子继承。继位往往需获得主要部族首领承认，并以军事能力证明正当性。
succession.en: Succession generally favored male members of the ruling lineage, but it was not strict primogeniture. Accession often required recognition by leading tribal chiefs and validation through military capability.

structure.zh: 政体结构较为简约，中央仅有王廷与若干核心随从机构，真正的治理仍依赖各部首领。巴底利斯通过召集部族会议、分配战利品与仲裁争端来维持联盟凝聚力。边远地区的服从程度取决于军事压力与互利关系。
structure.en: The polity had a simple structure, with only a royal court and a few core retinue offices at the center, while real governance depended on tribal chiefs. Bardyllis maintained cohesion by convening councils, distributing booty, and arbitrating disputes. Obedience in outlying areas depended on military pressure and mutual advantage.

function.zh: 负责王廷管理、部落会议召集、盟誓确认与贵族协调。
function.en: Managed the royal court, convened tribal councils, confirmed alliances, and coordinated nobles.

function.zh: 负责征收牲畜、谷物、金属与战利品份额，供王廷与军队使用。
function.en: Collected livestock, grain, metals, and shares of booty for the court and army.

function.zh: 负责集结战士、传达征召命令，并与边境部族保持联络。
function.en: Mustered warriors, transmitted levy orders, and maintained contacts with frontier tribes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由各部首领、氏族长老与要塞头目承担。王权对核心地区影响较强，对外围则多采取贡赋与军事附庸式管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was mainly exercised by tribal chiefs, clan elders, and fortress heads. Royal authority was stronger in the core areas, while peripheral zones were managed more through tribute and military vassalage.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、血亲义务与赔偿原则为基础。重大案件通常由首领与长老裁断，涉及复仇、牲畜赔偿、婚姻与盟约违背等事项。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice, kin obligations, and principles of compensation. Major cases were judged by chiefs and elders, especially those involving feud, livestock compensation, marriage, and breach of alliance.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政征收以贡赋而非统一税制为主，内容包括牲畜、谷物、金属、奴隶与战利品分成。战时征发和随军供给是王权的重要收入来源。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal extraction relied more on tribute than on a uniform tax system, including livestock, grain, metals, slaves, and shares of booty. Wartime requisitions and army provisioning were major sources of royal income.

religion.zh: 伊利里亚多神教
religion.en: Illyrian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 伊利里亚文化在山地防御聚落、青铜与铁器装饰、武士葬俗及部族音乐传统方面较有特色。贵族墓葬与武器、饰针、马具反映出明显的身份等级与区域风格。
culturalAchievements.en: Illyrian culture was notable for fortified hill settlements, bronze and iron ornamentation, warrior burial customs, and tribal musical traditions. Elite graves with weapons, fibulae, and horse gear reveal clear status distinctions and regional styles.

languageFamily.zh: 古巴尔干语群中的伊利里亚语支
languageFamily.en: Illyrian branch of the Paleo-Balkan languages

philosophy.zh: 以祖先崇拜、战士荣誉、盟誓伦理与多神信仰为核心，尚未形成系统化哲学传统。
philosophy.en: Centered on ancestor veneration, warrior honor, oath ethics, and polytheistic belief, without a formalized philosophical tradition.

writingSystem.zh: 本地书写传统薄弱，主要依赖口述；与希腊世界接触地区可能有限使用希腊字母作记号与贸易用途。
writingSystem.en: Native writing was weak and largely oral; areas in contact with the Greek world may have made limited use of the Greek alphabet for marks and trade purposes.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以混合型山地畜牧、谷地农业、采矿与掠夺收益构成。与马其顿、伊庇鲁斯及亚得里亚海沿岸的交换活动提升了贵族层的财富集中度。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on mixed upland pastoralism, valley agriculture, mining, and gains from raiding. Exchange with Macedon, Epirus, and the Adriatic coast increased wealth concentration among the aristocracy.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、谷物种植、采矿、冶铁、木材采伐、武器制造
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, grain cultivation, mining, ironworking, timber extraction, weapon production

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、皮革、木材、蜂蜡、金属矿石、奴隶、盐、武器
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, hides, timber, beeswax, metal ores, slaves, salt, weapons

unitName.en: head of livestock and weight of silver

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以牛羊、马匹、金属器、武器与亲族网络衡量。贵族家庭拥有较多战马、奴仆与储粮，自由民则以小规模畜群和土地使用权为主。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in cattle, sheep, horses, metal goods, weapons, and kin connections. Noble households held more warhorses, dependents, and grain stores, while commoners relied on smaller herds and access to land.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以部族再分配、家庭自给与有限区域贸易并行的混合经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining tribal redistribution, household subsistence, and limited regional trade.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
