grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非集中化铸币财政，而是由银重、金属器、牲畜储备、粮食与战利品共同构成。王廷财富流动性有限，但可在战争中迅速转化为军需。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a centralized coin treasury but a combination of weighed silver, metal goods, livestock reserves, grain, and booty. Court wealth had limited liquidity, but it could be converted quickly into military supply in war.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持战时动员与贵族忠诚，优先将收入用于馈赠、征召与边地控制。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining wartime mobilization and elite loyalty, prioritizing gift-giving, levies, and frontier control.

description.zh: 由贵族及其随从组成，是突击、追击与指挥核心。
description.en: Composed of nobles and retainers, serving as the main shock, pursuit, and command element.

description.zh: 由自由民征召构成，使用长矛、标枪、盾牌与短剑，适合山地作战。
description.en: Raised from free tribesmen, using spears, javelins, shields, and short swords, well suited to mountain warfare.

description.zh: 机动性强，承担侦察、伏击、劫掠与交通线破坏任务。
description.en: Highly mobile groups used for scouting, ambush, raiding, and disruption of communications.

commanderInChief.zh: 巴底利斯
commanderInChief.en: Bardyllis

technology.zh: 军备处于铁器时代区域平均以上，擅长标枪、盾牌与山地机动，但缺乏成熟攻城技术与统一重装体系。
technology.en: Military equipment stood somewhat above the regional Iron Age average, with strength in javelins, shields, and mountain mobility, but lacked advanced siegecraft and a standardized heavy infantry system.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自马其顿的扩张、伊庇鲁斯势力竞争、部落内部离心以及沿海希腊殖民城邦的政治干预。
threats.en: Main threats came from Macedonian expansion, rivalry with Epirus, internal tribal fragmentation, and political intervention from Greek coastal colonies.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于西巴尔干山地、河谷与防御性高地聚落。人口密度不高，但适龄男性比例足以支撑频繁的部族战争与季节性征召。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the western Balkans across mountains, river valleys, and fortified hill settlements. Population density was low, but the share of fighting-age males was enough to support frequent tribal warfare and seasonal levies.

name.zh: 佩拉戈尼亚地区要塞聚落

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族、部落首领、自由民战士、依附民与奴役人口。
socialClasses.en: Royal and noble elites, tribal chiefs, free warrior-commoners, dependents, and enslaved persons.

allies.zh: 与部分伊利里亚部族及反马其顿势力保持临时结盟，也可能与伊庇鲁斯内部派系建立机会性合作。
allies.en: Maintained temporary alliances with some Illyrian tribes and anti-Macedonian forces, and may have formed opportunistic ties with factions in Epirus.

enemies.zh: 马其顿诸王、竞争性的伊利里亚部族以及部分受希腊影响的边境势力。
enemies.en: The Macedonian kings, rival Illyrian tribes, and some border powers influenced by the Greek world.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以扩张影响范围、控制山地通道并通过战争与婚盟巩固霸权为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on expanding influence, controlling mountain corridors, and consolidating hegemony through war and marriage alliances.

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制武器普及、山地防御聚落、骑战装备改良、金属饰件与纤维工艺发展。
keyInnovations.en: Widespread iron weaponry, fortified hill settlements, improved riding equipment, and advances in metal ornaments and textile crafts.

strengths.zh: 军事动员力强，山地作战经验丰富，首领个人威望高，并能整合多个部族资源。
strengths.en: Strong military mobilization, extensive mountain warfare experience, high personal authority of the ruler, and an ability to combine the resources of multiple tribes.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度有限，财政与行政制度不稳，继承与部族忠诚容易引发分裂。
weaknesses.en: Political integration was limited, fiscal and administrative institutions were unstable, and succession and tribal loyalty could easily trigger fragmentation.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍具地区强权地位，但若遭遇更集中化的马其顿军事改革与持续进攻，其联盟结构将承受巨大压力。
outlook.en: In the short term it remained a significant regional power, but its league structure would come under severe strain if faced with more centralized Macedonian military reform and sustained offensives.

description.zh: 巴底利斯统合下的伊利里亚诸部，是公元前5世纪末巴尔干西部一支以战士贵族和部族联盟为基础的强势政治力量。
description.en: The Illyrian tribes under Bardyllis formed a strong western Balkan power in the late 5th century BCE, built on warrior aristocracy and a tribal league.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族长老会议为核心的分散部族治理形态，缺乏固定王权与常设中央。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal order centered on councils of clan elders, lacking a fixed kingship or permanent central authority.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、家族与季节性迁徙营地为基本单位。渔猎、采集、驯鹿或早期畜养及刀耕火种农业共同维持生计，各群体之间通过婚姻、祭仪与互助网络联系。萨满、长老和经验丰富的猎手享有较高威望。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, extended families, and seasonal camps. Fishing, hunting, gathering, reindeer or early livestock management, and slash-and-burn cultivation sustained life, while groups were connected through marriage, ritual, and mutual aid networks. Shamans, elders, and skilled hunters held high prestige.

rulingClass.zh: 并无严格世袭贵族，地位较高者多为长老、祭司型萨满与富有猎牧资源的家族首领。权威主要来自年龄、知识、灵性声望与分配能力。
rulingClass.en: There was no strict hereditary nobility; higher status usually belonged to elders, shamanic ritual specialists, and family heads with greater access to hunting or pastoral resources. Authority derived mainly from age, knowledge, spiritual prestige, and distributive ability.

succession.zh: 领导地位通常由氏族内部协商产生，可由资深亲属或最有能力者承担。继承重在认可而非正式加冕，若领导失德或无力保护族群，地位可被替代。
succession.en: Leadership was usually determined by clan consensus, often passing to senior kin or the most capable individual. Succession depended more on recognition than formal enthronement, and authority could be replaced if a leader failed to protect the group.

structure.zh: 没有稳定的中央官僚体系，治理依靠氏族会议、长老议事和祭仪场合上的集体决断。遇到迁徙、争端或外部威胁时，会由若干家族推举临时首领协调行动。不同地域群体之间联系松散，但共享语言亲缘与仪式传统。
structure.en: There was no stable central bureaucracy; governance relied on clan councils, elders' deliberation, and collective decisions made in ritual settings. During migration, disputes, or external threats, several families might choose a temporary leader to coordinate action. Groups in different regions were loosely connected but shared linguistic kinship and ritual traditions.

function.zh: 处理迁徙安排、资源分配、婚姻协定与对外协调。
function.en: Handled migration planning, resource allocation, marriage arrangements, and external coordination.

function.zh: 负责祭仪、禁忌维护、狩猎时节安排与萨满活动。
function.en: Oversaw rituals, taboos, hunting seasons, and shamanic activities.

localAdmin.zh: 地方事务由各营地家长、氏族长老和萨满共同调节。由于缺乏固定城市与宫廷，治理更多体现为面对面的协商与习惯约束。
localAdmin.en: Local matters were regulated jointly by camp heads, clan elders, and shamans. With no fixed cities or courts, governance was expressed mainly through face-to-face negotiation and customary restraint.

legalSystem.zh: 法律属于口传习惯法，强调亲属赔偿、资源共享边界与禁忌规范。杀伤、婚配、渔猎范围和偷盗问题多由长老调解，严重冲突则可能导致分离迁徙。
legalSystem.en: Law was oral customary law emphasizing kin compensation, boundaries of resource sharing, and taboo rules. Cases involving injury, marriage, fishing or hunting ranges, and theft were usually mediated by elders, while severe conflicts might lead to group separation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，公共需求依靠家庭贡献、共同劳动与节庆性献纳满足。临时领袖只能获得有限的食物、皮毛或工具支持，难以形成持续财政。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; public needs were met through household contributions, communal labor, and ceremonial offerings. Temporary leaders could obtain only limited food, furs, or tools, making sustained public finance difficult.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 芬兰-乌戈尔诸群体在口头史诗传统、木骨器制作、皮毛加工、舟具与雪地生存技术方面表现突出。其神话叙事和季节仪式为后世乌拉尔文化提供了深厚基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Finno-Ugric groups excelled in oral epic traditions, wood and bone crafting, fur processing, boat gear, and snow-region survival techniques. Their mythic narratives and seasonal rituals provided a deep foundation for later Uralic cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 乌拉尔语系
languageFamily.en: Uralic language family

philosophy.zh: 世界观以万物有灵、祖灵敬畏、自然禁忌与萨满沟通灵界为核心。
philosophy.en: The worldview centered on animistic vitality, reverence for ancestral spirits, natural taboos, and shamanic mediation with the spirit world.

writingSystem.zh: 无成体系文字，知识主要通过歌谣、叙事、纹饰和仪式口传保存。
writingSystem.en: There was no systematic writing; knowledge was preserved mainly through songs, narrative, ornament, and ritual oral transmission.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济高度自给，依赖渔猎、采集、皮毛加工与有限的迁徙农业。剩余产出很低，但在毛皮、琥珀、蜂蜡和森林资源方面具有区域交换价值。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was highly self-sufficient, relying on fishing, hunting, gathering, fur processing, and limited mobile agriculture. Surplus output was low, but furs, amber, beeswax, and forest products held value in regional exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔猎、采集、皮毛加工、木器骨器制作、小规模刀耕火种农业
mainIndustries.en: Fishing and hunting, gathering, fur processing, wood and bone craft, small-scale slash-and-burn agriculture

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、蜂蜡、鱼干、琥珀、兽牙、木材、骨角器
tradeGoods.en: Furs, beeswax, dried fish, amber, animal teeth, timber, bone and antler goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在毛皮储备、渔具、猎具、舟具、家畜数量与亲族合作网络。财富差距存在但不极端，成功猎手与祭仪专家较易积累声望性财富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth lay mainly in fur stocks, fishing gear, hunting tools, watercraft, livestock where present, and kin cooperation networks. Inequality existed but was not extreme, and successful hunters and ritual specialists more easily accumulated prestige wealth.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给、氏族共享和低强度交换为主的生计型经济。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence economy based on household self-sufficiency, clan sharing, and low-intensity exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓公共储备主要是毛皮、干鱼、工具、蜂蜡和季节性食物库存，并非金属货币库。其功能在于渡过冬季与资助祭仪，而非进行大规模国家支出。
treasuryDescription.en: The public reserve consisted mainly of furs, dried fish, tools, beeswax, and seasonal food stores rather than a metal treasury. Its purpose was to survive winter and support rituals, not to finance large state expenditures.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政习惯强调低征收、共同分担和季节性再分配，避免破坏氏族内部平衡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized low extraction, shared burdens, and seasonal redistribution, avoiding disruption of clan balance.

description.zh: 由经验猎手和成年男子轮值承担，负责防卫营地与储备。
description.en: Drawn from experienced hunters and adult men on rotation, defending camps and stores.

description.zh: 平时从事狩猎，战时以弓、矛、斧和伏击战术作战。
description.en: Normally engaged in hunting, they fought in war with bows, spears, axes, and ambush tactics.

description.zh: 在外敌入侵或资源冲突时由多个营地联合组成。
description.en: Formed by several camps together in times of invasion or resource conflict.

commanderInChief.zh: 无固定最高统帅，由资深长老或战时领袖担任
commanderInChief.en: No permanent commander-in-chief; a senior elder or war leader served in emergencies

technology.zh: 军备简陋，以木弓、骨角器、石铁混用工具和轻便近战武器为主，适合森林、沼泽与冰雪环境中的袭扰防御。
technology.en: Equipment was simple, centered on wooden bows, bone-antler gear, mixed stone and iron tools, and light close-combat weapons, suitable for raiding defense in forests, marshes, and snowy terrain.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族对渔猎资源的争夺、气候波动造成的生计压力，以及草原或森林边缘群体的零散袭扰。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring groups contesting fishing and hunting resources, subsistence stress caused by climate fluctuations, and scattered raids from steppe or forest-edge peoples.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于森林、湖沼和北方河流流域，聚落规模很小且多具季节性。总体寿命受寒冷、营养波动和疾病影响，但人口结构相对年轻。
populationDescription.en: The population was scattered across forests, lakes, marshes, and northern river basins, with very small and often seasonal settlements. Life expectancy was affected by cold, fluctuating nutrition, and disease, though the overall age structure was relatively young.

socialClasses.zh: 氏族长老、萨满与祭仪专家、自由家庭成员、依附亲属与战俘。
socialClasses.en: Clan elders, shamans and ritual specialists, free household members, dependent kin, and captives.

allies.zh: 与语言和婚姻网络相近的邻近氏族保持松散互助关系，必要时共同狩猎或防御。
allies.en: Maintained loose mutual-aid ties with neighboring clans linked by language and marriage, cooperating in hunts or defense when necessary.

enemies.zh: 并无长期国家级宿敌，主要对手是争夺资源的邻近部族与偶发掠夺者。
enemies.en: There were no long-term state-level enemies; main opponents were neighboring groups competing for resources and occasional raiders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外策略以回避冲突、维持通婚交换与守护传统活动范围为主。
foreignPolicy.en: External strategy aimed primarily at avoiding conflict, maintaining intermarriage and exchange, and defending traditional activity zones.

era.zh: 北方森林地带铁器时代早段
era.en: Early Iron Age of the northern forest zone

keyInnovations.zh: 轻舟与冰雪交通技术、骨角复合工具、毛皮加工、季节性储藏与林地生存知识。
keyInnovations.en: Light boat and snow transport techniques, bone-antler composite tools, fur processing, seasonal storage, and forest survival knowledge.

strengths.zh: 环境适应力强，社区凝聚依靠亲族与祭仪，生存技术适合严寒森林地带。
strengths.en: Strong environmental adaptability, community cohesion rooted in kinship and ritual, and survival skills suited to cold forest regions.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少且分散，缺乏集中政治和稳定余粮，难以组织大规模战争与工程。
weaknesses.en: The population was small and dispersed, with no centralized politics or stable surplus, making large-scale war and construction difficult.

outlook.zh: 在无强大外来扩张压力时可长期维持稳定，但其发展速度将明显慢于南方农业文明和草原军事联合体。
outlook.en: It could remain stable for long periods in the absence of strong outside expansion, but its developmental pace would remain much slower than that of southern agrarian states and steppe military confederations.

description.zh: 公元前500年前后的芬兰-乌戈尔诸群体，是分布于北方森林与河湖地带、以氏族协商和萨满传统维系的低密度部族社会。
description.en: Around 500 BCE, the Finno-Ugric peoples formed low-density tribal societies across northern forests and river-lake zones, sustained by clan consensus and shamanic tradition.

governmentForm.zh: 以小型游动群体和亲族网络为基础的部落式首领制，权力松散且高度依赖个人声望。
governmentForm.en: A tribal headman system based on small mobile bands and kin networks, with loose authority highly dependent on personal prestige.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由若干流动性较强的狩猎采集群体构成，核心单位是扩大家庭与亲属群。劳动分工具有年龄与性别差异，男性多从事狩猎与防卫，女性承担采集、加工与家庭组织。地位差异存在但较弱，优秀猎手、祭仪主持者和年长者影响较大。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of several highly mobile hunter-gatherer groups whose core unit was the extended family and kin cluster. Labor was divided by age and sex, with men more involved in hunting and defense, and women in gathering, processing, and household organization. Social differences existed but were modest, while skilled hunters, ritual leaders, and elders had greater influence.

rulingClass.zh: 没有固定贵族阶层，领导者多为资深猎手、善于调解者或在交换网络中有影响的人物。其权威需要通过慷慨分配、勇武与成功的季节组织不断维持。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed aristocratic class; leaders were often senior hunters, skilled mediators, or influential figures in exchange networks. Their authority had to be maintained continually through generosity, bravery, and successful seasonal coordination.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常由亲属群认可的有能力者继承，不一定按长子原则。若领导无力组织狩猎或失去支持，群体可分裂并另择首领。
succession.en: Leadership usually passed to a capable person recognized by the kin group, not necessarily by primogeniture. If a leader failed to organize hunts or lost support, the band could split and choose another head.

structure.zh: 政治结构极为简约，没有固定首都、官僚或常设行政中心。重大事务在营地聚会中由长者、首领和家族代表协商决定，尤其是迁徙路线、狩猎区域与婚姻交换。跨群体合作通常只在资源丰沛季节或外部冲突时出现。
structure.en: The political structure was extremely simple, with no fixed capital, bureaucracy, or permanent administrative center. Major matters were decided in camp gatherings by elders, leaders, and family representatives, especially migration routes, hunting grounds, and marriage exchange. Cooperation across bands usually appeared only during resource-rich seasons or external conflict.

function.zh: 讨论迁徙、冲突调解、婚姻协定和资源使用。
function.en: Discussed migration, conflict mediation, marriage arrangements, and resource use.

function.zh: 协调季节狩猎、祖灵仪式、食物分配与营地安全。
function.en: Coordinated seasonal hunts, ancestral rites, food distribution, and camp safety.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即营地内部治理，由首领、长者和家庭代表直接处理。由于居住形态流动，行政边界并不固定，而是围绕传统活动区域形成。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was simply camp-level governance handled directly by leaders, elders, and household representatives. Because settlement was mobile, administrative boundaries were not fixed but formed around traditional activity zones.

legalSystem.zh: 规范主要来自习俗、禁忌、亲属义务与口头调解。争端多通过赔礼、重新分配猎获物、婚姻安排或分群迁移来解决。
legalSystem.en: Norms derived mainly from custom, taboo, kin obligations, and oral mediation. Disputes were usually settled through apology, redistribution of game, marriage arrangements, or band separation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税收，公共需求依靠共同劳动、猎获分享和仪式性馈赠满足。首领能调动的资源有限，主要是食物、皮张和工具。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; public needs were met through communal labor, sharing of game, and ritual gifting. Resources a leader could mobilize were limited mainly to food, hides, and tools.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 潘帕斯诸文化擅长草原环境中的狩猎组织、石骨器制作、皮革处理和仪式性身体装饰。其口头地理知识与季节性迁徙经验对维持群体生存极为关键。
culturalAchievements.en: Pampas cultures excelled in organizing hunts in grassland environments, making stone and bone tools, hide processing, and ritual body decoration. Their oral geographical knowledge and seasonal mobility experience were crucial to collective survival.

languageFamily.zh: 南锥地区多样语言，可能包括早期丘翁语系及其他未定属语言
languageFamily.en: Diverse Southern Cone languages, possibly including early Chonan and other unattested families

philosophy.zh: 文化观念以万物有灵、祖灵联系、土地记忆和季节循环为核心。
philosophy.en: Cultural thought centered on animism, bonds with ancestral spirits, memory of the land, and seasonal cycles.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，知识通过口述传统、身体装饰、岩画或器物纹样传递。
writingSystem.en: There was no writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, body decoration, rock art, and object designs.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以草原狩猎、采集、沿河渔捞和小规模地区交换为主，物质剩余有限。财富更多表现为食物安全、皮张储备、石料来源与亲族互助，而非可积累的货币性资产。
gdpDescription.en: The economy relied on grassland hunting, gathering, riverine fishing, and small-scale regional exchange, with little material surplus. Wealth was expressed more in food security, hide reserves, access to stone sources, and kin support than in accumulable monetary assets.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、渔捞、皮革加工、石器骨器制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, fishing, hide processing, stone and bone tool making

tradeGoods.zh: 皮张、羽毛、石料、骨角器、贝壳饰物、颜料
tradeGoods.en: Hides, feathers, stone materials, bone and antler tools, shell ornaments, pigments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富体现在工具质量、皮张储量、采集容器、营地位置和结亲范围。群体整体较平等，但掌握优质猎场和交换渠道的家庭更具影响力。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was reflected in tool quality, stored hides, gathering containers, camp location, and marriage connections. Society was broadly egalitarian, though families with access to better hunting grounds and exchange routes had more influence.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以流动狩猎采集、自给消费和礼物交换为主的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on mobile hunting-gathering, subsistence consumption, and gift exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备主要是干肉、皮张、石料工具和少量装饰物，不存在固定国家金库。储备主要服务于歉收季节、祭仪和临时冲突。
treasuryDescription.en: The communal reserve consisted mainly of dried meat, hides, stone tools, and small ornaments; there was no fixed state treasury. These reserves served poor seasons, rituals, and temporary conflict.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政行为重在分享与风险分散，避免任何持续性强制征收。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal behavior focused on sharing and risk distribution, avoiding any form of sustained coercive extraction.

description.zh: 负责营地安全、夜间警戒和突发冲突处置。
description.en: Responsible for camp safety, night watch, and handling sudden clashes.

description.zh: 平时用于集体围猎，战时使用长矛、投射器、棍棒和石刃进行近距作战。
description.en: Used for collective hunting in peacetime and for close combat in war with spears, throwing devices, clubs, and stone blades.

commanderInChief.zh: 无固定最高统帅，由游群首领临时率领
commanderInChief.en: No permanent commander-in-chief; band leaders commanded temporarily

technology.zh: 技术水平属新石器至早期金属接触阶段，以石器、骨器、木矛和皮革工艺为主，军事用途主要限于部族冲突与狩猎协同。
technology.en: Technology was at a late lithic to early metal-contact level, centered on stone tools, bone implements, wooden spears, and hide craft, with military use largely limited to tribal clashes and coordinated hunting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近群体的猎场争夺、气候和动物迁徙变化，以及局部报复性冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from competition over hunting grounds with neighboring groups, climatic and animal migration changes, and localized revenge conflicts.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分布于南美温带草原、河流和湿地边缘，多数聚落为季节性营地。营地规模通常较小，因资源压力与迁徙节律而频繁分合。
populationDescription.en: The population was thinly spread across temperate South American grasslands, rivers, and wetland margins, with most settlements being seasonal camps. Camps were usually small and frequently merged or split due to resource pressures and migration rhythms.

socialClasses.zh: 首领与长者、祭仪主持者、自由家庭成员、俘获或依附者。
socialClasses.en: Leaders and elders, ritual specialists, free household members, and captives or dependents.

allies.zh: 与通婚和共享季节路线的邻近游群保持松散合作，必要时联合围猎或防御。
allies.en: Maintained loose cooperation with neighboring bands connected by intermarriage and shared seasonal routes, joining together for hunts or defense when needed.

enemies.zh: 没有固定国家级敌人，冲突多发生于相邻游群之间的资源竞争与复仇链条。
enemies.en: There were no fixed state-level enemies; conflict usually arose from resource competition and revenge cycles among neighboring bands.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持活动空间、控制冲突规模和保存交换联系为主。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations aimed at preserving movement space, limiting the scale of conflict, and maintaining exchange ties.

era.zh: 南美潘帕斯地区晚期前陶/早期地方复杂化阶段
era.en: Late pre-ceramic to early local complexification stage in the South American Pampas

keyInnovations.zh: 草原协同围猎、石骨复合工具、皮革处理、营地季节流动组织与环境记忆传承。
keyInnovations.en: Coordinated grassland hunting, stone-bone composite tools, hide processing, seasonal camp mobility organization, and transmission of environmental memory.

strengths.zh: 机动性高，适应草原生态，群体平等性较强，能够快速调整营地与资源策略。
strengths.en: Highly mobile, well adapted to grassland ecology, relatively egalitarian, and able to adjust camps and resource strategies quickly.

weaknesses.zh: 人口规模小、技术积累有限、政治整合度低，难以形成持久的大型组织和高剩余经济。
weaknesses.en: Small population, limited technological accumulation, and low political integration made durable large organizations and high-surplus economies difficult.

outlook.zh: 在生态条件稳定时可长期延续其生活方式，但面对更高密度农业社会或气候剧变时，脆弱性将明显上升。
outlook.en: Its way of life could endure for long periods under stable ecological conditions, but vulnerability would rise sharply when faced with denser agricultural societies or major climatic change.

description.zh: 公元前500年前后的潘帕斯文化，是南美温带草原上以流动狩猎采集、亲族协作和季节性营地为基础的低密度部族社会。
description.en: Around 500 BCE, the Pampas cultures were low-density tribal societies of the temperate South American grasslands, built on mobile hunting-gathering, kin cooperation, and seasonal camps.

governmentForm.zh: 以季节性猎队与亲族营地为核心、由资深猎手和长者协商决策的游猎式部落治理。
governmentForm.en: A nomadic hunter-band system centered on seasonal bison-hunting groups and kin camps, governed through consensus by experienced hunters and elders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以扩展家庭、亲族群和临时集结的猎队组成，平时分散，猎牛季节则合并成较大营地。地位主要取决于狩猎能力、慷慨分配、精神威望与亲属网络，而非固定世袭贵族。男女分工较明确，但老年人、萨满和熟练工匠在营地中拥有较高声望。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around extended families, kin groups, and temporarily assembled hunting bands; groups were dispersed for much of the year and gathered into larger camps during bison hunts. Status depended mainly on hunting skill, generosity in distribution, spiritual prestige, and kin connections rather than on a fixed hereditary nobility. Gendered labor roles were distinct, but elders, shamans, and skilled craftspeople held notable influence within camp life.

rulingClass.zh: 并无严格意义上的统治阶级，实际领导者通常是成功猎手、营地长者与宗教人物。其权威依赖说服力、分配战利品的能力和危机中的表现。
rulingClass.en: There was no strict ruling class; effective leadership rested with successful hunters, camp elders, and ritual specialists. Their authority depended on persuasion, ability to distribute spoils, and performance during times of danger.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常不完全世袭，而是在有威望家族中由营地共识推举最合适者。若领导者失去支持，猎队可分裂或改随他人。
succession.en: Leadership was usually not strictly hereditary, though prominent families could supply candidates; the camp generally recognized the most capable individual by consensus. If a leader lost support, bands could split off or follow another person.

structure.zh: 政治结构松散，以营地会议和猎前协商为核心，没有常设首都或固定官僚机构。季节性大集会时，不同猎队会共同决定迁徙路线、狩猎秩序、婚姻交换和对外防御。
structure.en: Political organization was loose, centered on camp councils and pre-hunt deliberation, with no permanent capital or fixed bureaucracy. During seasonal gatherings, different hunting bands jointly determined migration routes, hunting order, marriage exchange, and external defense.

function.zh: 协调迁徙、营地秩序、纠纷调解与对外关系。
function.en: Coordinated migration, camp order, dispute mediation, and external relations.

function.zh: 组织围猎、分配肉食与皮张，并安排冬储。
function.en: Organized communal hunts, distributed meat and hides, and arranged winter stores.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理即各营地和猎队内部自治，由长者、家族代表和猎手首领临时协调。权力随季节和营地规模变化，没有独立行政层级。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was simply the self-government of each camp and hunting band, coordinated temporarily by elders, family representatives, and hunt leaders. Authority shifted with the seasons and size of the encampment, without a distinct administrative hierarchy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，强调分享义务、婚姻规则、猎场秩序和对暴力行为的赔偿。严重冲突通常通过调解、分营而居或象征性补偿解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, emphasizing obligations of sharing, marriage rules, hunting order, and compensation for violence. Serious conflicts were typically resolved through mediation, camp separation, or symbolic compensation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，公共需要依靠猎获分成、礼物交换和危机时期的共同劳动承担。首领不能强制征税，只能依靠声望组织再分配。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; public needs were met through shares of game, gift exchange, and collective labor in times of need. Leaders could not compel taxes and relied instead on prestige-based redistribution.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出高度适应草原环境的集体围猎技术、皮革加工和便携式营地生活方式。口述史、猎歌与灵视仪式构成其文化连续性的核心。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed highly adapted communal bison-hunting techniques, hide processing, and a portable camp lifestyle suited to the plains. Oral histories, hunting songs, and vision rituals formed the core of cultural continuity.

languageFamily.zh: 多种北美土著语系前身，可能包括早期阿尔冈昆或苏语相关方言群
languageFamily.en: Various ancestral North American indigenous language groups, possibly including early Algonquian- or Siouan-related dialect clusters

philosophy.zh: 以萨满式宇宙观、动物灵力观念和人与野牛之间的互惠关系为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on a shamanic worldview, belief in animal power, and a reciprocal relationship between people and bison.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，依靠口述传统、仪式歌谣、记忆地景和器物纹饰传承知识。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, ritual songs, remembered landscapes, and decorative motifs on objects.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以野牛狩猎、采集、小型猎物和皮张加工为主，市场化程度极低。财富主要体现在食物安全、皮革储备、工具和亲族互助，而非金属货币积累。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on bison hunting, gathering, small-game procurement, and hide processing, with very low monetization. Wealth was expressed mainly in food security, hide reserves, tools, and kin support rather than accumulation of metal currency.

mainIndustries.zh: 野牛狩猎、皮革加工、骨角器制作、采集与营地手工业
mainIndustries.en: Bison hunting, hide processing, bone and antler toolmaking, gathering, and camp handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、干肉、骨器、颜料石、贝壳饰品与燧石原料
tradeGoods.en: Hides, dried meat, bone tools, pigment stones, shell ornaments, and flint raw material

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常表现为优质皮帐、储存肉干、骨石工具、驮运能力和广泛的亲族交换关系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth usually took the form of good hide lodges, stored dried meat, bone and stone tools, carrying capacity, and broad kin-based exchange ties.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 以亲族共享、礼物交换和季节性再分配为主的游猎采集经济。
economicSystem.en: A nomadic hunting-gathering economy based on kin sharing, gift exchange, and seasonal redistribution.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent in reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金库，而是由首领家庭和营地共同持有的食物、皮张、优质工具与礼品储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a central vault but a distributed reserve of food, hides, quality tools, and gift goods held by leading households and camps.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以最低集中化为特征，优先维持冬储、集体狩猎组织和危机互助。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice was minimally centralized, prioritizing winter stores, organization of communal hunts, and mutual aid in times of crisis.

description.zh: 以步行为主，使用投矛、标枪、弓箭和石制武器，既能狩猎也能作战。
description.en: Primarily foot-mobile bands using spears, javelins, bows, and stone weapons, capable of both hunting and combat.

description.zh: 负责营地警戒、迁徙护卫和突袭预警，多由成年猎手临时编成。
description.en: Responsible for camp watch, migration escort, and raid warning, usually formed temporarily from adult hunters.

commanderInChief.zh: 资深猎队首领
commanderInChief.en: Senior hunting-band leader

technology.zh: 军事技术处于石器—骨角器水平，擅长伏击、围猎式战术和地形利用，但缺乏金属武器、骑兵和攻城能力。
technology.en: Military technology remained at a stone, bone, and antler level; they excelled in ambush, encirclement-style tactics, and terrain use, but lacked metal weapons, cavalry, and siege capacity.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近猎群竞争、资源波动、极端气候和局部复仇冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring hunting groups, resource fluctuation, severe weather, and localized revenge conflicts.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分布于北美大平原边缘与河谷地带，多数时间以小营地生活。季节性聚会时人口会短暂集中，形成较大的狩猎聚落。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparsely spread across prairie margins and river valleys in North America, living most of the year in small camps. During seasonal gatherings, people concentrated temporarily into larger hunting settlements.

socialClasses.zh: 以普通家庭、熟练猎手、萨满、长者和有声望的首领家庭构成，阶层差异有限且流动性较高。
socialClasses.en: Composed of ordinary households, skilled hunters, shamans, elders, and prestigious leader families, with limited hierarchy and relatively high social mobility.

allies.zh: 主要依赖亲族联姻与邻近营地的季节性互助，没有固定跨区域盟国。
allies.en: They relied mainly on marriage ties and seasonal mutual aid with neighboring camps, without fixed large-scale allies.

enemies.zh: 敌对者多为争夺猎场和水源的周边猎采群体，而非持久国家对手。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually nearby hunter-gatherer groups competing over hunting grounds and water sources rather than enduring state-level rivals.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以避免长期冲突、维持交换路线和在资源紧张时灵活迁徙为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on avoiding prolonged conflict, maintaining exchange routes, and migrating flexibly during resource stress.

era.zh: 北美晚期古风至林地前期游猎技术阶段
era.en: Late Archaic to early Woodland-level North American hunting technology

keyInnovations.zh: 集体围猎、肉干和皮张保存、石制投射器、骨角工具与高机动性皮帐营地。
keyInnovations.en: Communal surround hunts, preservation of dried meat and hides, stone projectile technology, bone and antler tools, and highly mobile hide-lodge camps.

strengths.zh: 机动性强、生态知识丰富、集体狩猎效率高，且社会弹性较强。
strengths.en: They possessed strong mobility, deep ecological knowledge, efficient communal hunting, and high social resilience.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、技术层次较低、缺乏集中组织，易受气候和兽群变化影响。
weaknesses.en: They were constrained by low population density, modest technology, and weak central organization, making them vulnerable to climate and herd fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 只要草原生态稳定并维持迁徙自由，此类社会可长期延续，但难以形成大规模国家结构。
outlook.en: As long as prairie ecology remained stable and mobility was preserved, this way of life could endure for long periods, though it was unlikely to develop into a large centralized state.

description.zh: 这是一个依靠野牛狩猎、季节性迁徙和亲族协商维系的北美平原游猎社会。
description.en: This was a North American plains hunting society sustained by bison procurement, seasonal migration, and kin-based consensus.

governmentForm.zh: 以希达共同体为基础、由长者和家户代表通过协商管理资源与迁徙的分散式部落治理。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal system based on siida communities, in which elders and household representatives managed resources and movement through consensus.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以希达为基本单位，成员共同使用渔场、猎场和季节性迁徙路线。家庭是生产核心，但资源利用往往经由共同体协商安排。威望来自年龄、狩猎捕鱼能力、仪式知识和调解纠纷的能力，而非严格贵族世袭。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around the siida as its core unit, with members sharing fishing grounds, hunting areas, and seasonal migration routes. The household was the basic productive unit, but resource use was often arranged through community deliberation. Prestige came from age, hunting and fishing skill, ritual knowledge, and ability to mediate disputes rather than from a strict hereditary nobility.

rulingClass.zh: 没有固定贵族阶层，希达长老、资深猎手、渔业组织者和萨满式宗教人物构成实际领导层。其权威多基于经验与信誉。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed noble class; siida elders, seasoned hunters, fishery organizers, and shamanic ritual specialists formed the practical leadership. Their authority was based largely on experience and reputation.

succession.zh: 领导地位通常由家族声望与共同体认可共同决定，长者去世后常由近亲中的资深成员接替。若意见不合，希达可重组或分裂。
succession.en: Leadership was shaped by both family prestige and community recognition; after an elder's death, a senior kinsman often succeeded. If disagreements deepened, a siida could reorganize or divide.

structure.zh: 政治组织松散，没有单一首都，各地希达在渔猎季节独立运作，在大型集会和交换时保持联络。共同事务主要包括分配捕鱼点、协调狩猎、处理婚姻和对外接触。
structure.en: Political organization was loose, with no single capital; local siidas operated independently through the fishing and hunting seasons while maintaining contact during larger gatherings and exchanges. Shared concerns included allocation of fishing spots, coordination of hunts, marriage matters, and outside contact.

function.zh: 协调共同资源使用、迁徙安排和内部调解。
function.en: Coordinated communal resource use, migration schedules, and internal mediation.

function.zh: 组织季节性捕捞、狩猎合作与重要祭仪活动。
function.en: Organized seasonal fishing, cooperative hunting, and major ritual observances.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理由各希达自行承担，家户代表与长老共同决定营地位置和资源利用秩序。缺乏超地方的常设行政机构。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by each siida itself, with household representatives and elders deciding camp placement and rules of resource use. There was no permanent supra-local administrative apparatus.

legalSystem.zh: 法律为习惯法，重点在渔场与猎场权、婚姻关系、赔偿和禁忌。争端通常通过长者调解与赔礼解决，极少依赖强制惩罚。
legalSystem.en: Law was customary, focusing on rights to fishing and hunting areas, marriage relations, compensation, and taboos. Disputes were normally settled by elder mediation and reparative gifts, with little reliance on coercive punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税收，公共事务依靠共同劳动、渔猎分成和仪式性馈赠承担。对领导者的供给更多是敬赠而非强制征收。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; communal affairs were supported through shared labor, portions of fishing and hunting yields, and ritual gifting. Support for leaders was more honorary contribution than compulsory levy.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 形成了适应北极—亚北极环境的渔猎生活、雪地与森林迁徙经验以及富于象征意义的鼓乐与仪式文化。其口头传统对自然地景和祖先记忆有极高依赖。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed a fishing and hunting lifeway adapted to Arctic and sub-Arctic conditions, as well as extensive experience in snow and forest mobility and a symbolic drum-and-ritual culture. Their oral traditions relied heavily on natural landmarks and ancestral memory.

languageFamily.zh: 乌拉尔语系芬兰-乌戈尔语族萨米语支前身
languageFamily.en: Ancestral Sámi branch of the Uralic language family

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰、萨满实践和对山川、湖泊、动物精灵的敬畏为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animistic belief, shamanic practice, and reverence for the spirits of mountains, lakes, and animals.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，以口传史诗、歌谣、鼓仪和地名记忆保存传统。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; tradition was preserved through oral poetry, songs, drum ritual, and remembered place-names.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以捕鱼、狩猎、采集、毛皮和小规模手工业为主，交换网络沿河湖和北方海岸展开。由于人口稀少且环境严酷，产出总量不高，但生态适应性很强。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on fishing, hunting, gathering, furs, and small-scale handicrafts, with exchange networks running along rivers, lakes, and northern coasts. Because the population was small and the environment harsh, total output was modest, though ecological adaptation was strong.

mainIndustries.zh: 捕鱼、狩猎、毛皮采集、骨角木器制作、采集
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, hunting, fur procurement, bone-antler-wood crafts, and gathering

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、鱼干、兽骨器、树脂、羽毛、琥珀与石料
tradeGoods.en: Furs, dried fish, bone tools, resin, feathers, amber, and stone materials

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富体现在渔具、船只或雪地交通工具、毛皮储备、优良营具和稳定的资源使用权。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth appeared in fishing gear, boats or snow-travel equipment, fur stocks, good camp equipment, and secure access to resource zones.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 以共同体资源管理、家庭生产和低强度远距离交换结合的渔猎经济。
economicSystem.en: A fishing-hunting economy combining communal resource management, household production, and low-intensity long-distance exchange.

source.en: Communal shares of fishing and hunting

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent in reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备主要以毛皮、鱼干、工具材料和礼仪物品形式分散存放于各希达，而非集中金属库藏。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves were held mainly as furs, dried fish, tool materials, and ritual goods dispersed across siidas rather than as a concentrated metal treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政偏向维持生计稳定与共同体互助，避免对家户施加过重负担。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized subsistence stability and communal mutual aid while avoiding heavy burdens on households.

description.zh: 熟悉森林、湖泊与冰雪环境，擅长伏击、追踪和小规模冲突。
description.en: Accustomed to forests, lakes, and snow terrain, they specialized in ambush, tracking, and small-scale conflict.

description.zh: 负责守卫营地、资源点和迁徙路线，多为临时召集。
description.en: They guarded camps, resource sites, and migration routes, usually on a temporary basis.

commanderInChief.zh: 希达资深长老
commanderInChief.en: Senior siida elder

technology.zh: 武备以弓箭、投矛、骨角木制器械和小舟机动为主，擅长环境适应但缺乏重武器与组织化大兵团作战能力。
technology.en: Armament centered on bows, spears, bone-antler-wood equipment, and boat mobility; they were highly adapted to their environment but lacked heavy weapons and organized large-unit warfare.

threats.zh: 主要威胁为资源竞争、外来掠夺、小规模边地冲突及严酷气候。
threats.en: The main threats were resource competition, outside raiding, small frontier conflicts, and severe climate.

populationDescription.zh: 人口散居于斯堪的纳维亚北部与科拉半岛周边的森林、湖沼和苔原过渡地带。聚落规模普遍较小，多为季节性营地和资源点居住。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across northern Scandinavia and adjacent Kola regions in forest, lake, marsh, and tundra transition zones. Settlements were generally small and mostly seasonal camps tied to resource areas.

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层较弱，主要包括普通家户、资深渔猎者、长老和萨满式宗教人物。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was weak, consisting mainly of ordinary households, experienced fishers and hunters, elders, and shamanic ritual specialists.

allies.zh: 主要盟友为邻近希达共同体及若干芬诺-乌戈尔语族边缘群体。
allies.en: Their main allies were neighboring siida communities and certain adjacent Finno-Ugric groups.

enemies.zh: 并无固定国家级宿敌，主要摩擦来自周边资源竞争者和偶发掠夺者。
enemies.en: They had no fixed state-level archenemy; frictions came mainly from neighboring resource competitors and occasional raiders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系重在保持资源边界、维持交换渠道并避免高成本冲突。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations aimed to preserve resource boundaries, maintain exchange channels, and avoid costly conflict.

era.zh: 北欧亚铁器时代边缘渔猎技术阶段
era.en: Northern European Iron Age frontier fishing-hunting technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 高纬环境迁徙知识、湖海捕鱼技术、毛皮加工、轻型舟具和复杂口述导航体系。
keyInnovations.en: High-latitude mobility knowledge, lake and sea fishing techniques, fur processing, light watercraft, and complex oral navigation systems.

strengths.zh: 环境适应能力极强、资源管理灵活、共同体凝聚力较高。
strengths.en: They had very strong environmental adaptation, flexible resource management, and substantial communal cohesion.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、产出有限、组织分散，面对外来高技术势力时防御能力不足。
weaknesses.en: They were limited by small population, modest output, and dispersed organization, leaving them vulnerable to outside groups with more advanced technology.

outlook.zh: 在北方生态环境相对稳定的条件下，此类希达体系可持续存在，但难形成集中型国家。
outlook.en: Under relatively stable northern ecological conditions, the siida system could persist for a long time, though it was unlikely to become a centralized state.

description.zh: 这是一个以希达共同体、渔猎经济和萨满信仰维系的亚北极萨米社会。
description.en: This was a sub-Arctic Sámi society sustained by siida communities, a fishing-hunting economy, and shamanic belief.

governmentForm.zh: 由分散部族和地方长老会议组成、以亲族关系和季节性资源管理为核心的部落治理。
governmentForm.en: A tribal system of dispersed communities and local elder councils, centered on kinship ties and seasonal resource management.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由若干氏族、家族营地和区域性部落组成，人口分散于热带草原与疏林地带。多数成员从事采集、狩猎、捕鱼与小规模植物管理，社会差异较小。长者、仪式专家和善于组织群体行动者拥有较高威望，但很少形成固定贵族。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of clans, family camps, and regional tribes spread across tropical savannas and woodland margins. Most people engaged in gathering, hunting, fishing, and small-scale plant management, with limited social inequality. Elders, ritual specialists, and those skilled in organizing collective action held higher prestige, but rarely formed a fixed nobility.

rulingClass.zh: 无严格统治阶级，部族长老、猎队首领和宗教祭司构成领导核心。其权威依赖协调能力、祖先威望和分配资源的公平性。
rulingClass.en: There was no strict ruling class; tribal elders, hunt leaders, and ritual priests formed the leadership core. Their authority depended on coordination skill, ancestral prestige, and fairness in distribution.

succession.zh: 领导权通常在有声望家系中产生，但需经长老和成年成员认可。若首领失德或无法保障群体利益，部族可另推新领袖或分群迁移。
succession.en: Leadership often emerged from prestigious lineages but required recognition from elders and adult members. If a leader lost esteem or failed to protect group interests, the tribe could choose another or divide and relocate.

structure.zh: 政治结构呈地方化和分段式，缺乏统一国家机器。区域性集会会处理婚姻交换、祭祀、资源边界与冲突调停，但日常治理由各部族自行负责。
structure.en: Political structure was local and segmentary, lacking a unified state apparatus. Regional gatherings handled marriage exchange, ritual, resource boundaries, and conflict mediation, while daily governance remained in the hands of individual tribes.

function.zh: 处理部族事务、边界争议、婚姻安排与对外协商。
function.en: Handled tribal affairs, boundary disputes, marriage arrangements, and external negotiation.

function.zh: 协调季节性采集、集体狩猎、渔猎点分配和粮食储备。
function.en: Coordinated seasonal gathering, communal hunts, allocation of fishing and hunting sites, and food storage.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由各部族与营地自主执行，长老和家族首领负责资源使用秩序与内部纠纷。更大范围的权力仅在季节性大会上短暂显现。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out autonomously by each tribe and camp, with elders and lineage heads overseeing resource order and internal disputes. Wider authority appeared only briefly during seasonal assemblies.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为基础，重视婚姻、血亲、猎场水源使用权、赔偿和禁忌。纠纷多通过调停、赔礼或分居解决，暴力报复虽存在但通常受长者约束。
legalSystem.en: Customary law governed marriage, kinship, rights to hunting grounds and water, compensation, and taboos. Disputes were often resolved through mediation, gifts, or separation, though retaliatory violence did occur and was usually restrained by elders.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，公共事务依赖共同劳动、祭礼贡献和猎获分配。部分地区首领可在丰收或大猎后获得象征性贡品。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; communal affairs relied on shared labor, ritual contributions, and distribution of hunting yields. In some areas, chiefs received symbolic tribute after abundant harvests or major hunts.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出对热带草原季节节律高度敏感的采集、火利用和群体狩猎传统，并形成丰富的歌舞、面饰与成年礼文化。其生态知识对植物、动物迁徙和水源变化把握精细。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed gathering practices, controlled fire use, and communal hunting traditions finely tuned to the seasonal rhythms of the tropical savanna, along with rich song, dance, masking, and initiation cultures. Their ecological knowledge of plants, animal movement, and shifting water sources was highly detailed.

languageFamily.zh: 多种非洲本土语系，可能包括尼日尔-刚果语系、尼罗-撒哈拉语系或科伊桑相关语言群
languageFamily.en: Various indigenous African language families, possibly including Niger-Congo, Nilo-Saharan, or Khoisan-related groups

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵论、祖灵崇拜和人与草原生态循环相互依存的观念为主。
philosophy.en: Their worldview was shaped by animism, ancestor veneration, and a sense of mutual dependence between people and savanna ecological cycles.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，以口述神话、舞蹈、身体装饰和岩画或器物纹样传承知识。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral myth, dance, body ornamentation, and rock art or object designs.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以采集、狩猎、捕鱼、蜂蜜采集和局部植物管理为主，部分地区与邻近农牧群体发生交换。整体财富水平不高，但在多样生态资源支持下具有较强韧性。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on gathering, hunting, fishing, honey collection, and localized plant management, with some exchange with neighboring farming or herding groups. Overall wealth levels were modest, but the economy was resilient thanks to diversified ecological resources.

mainIndustries.zh: 采集、狩猎、捕鱼、蜂蜜与蜡采集、木骨器制作、皮革加工
mainIndustries.en: Gathering, hunting, fishing, honey and wax collection, wood-and-bone crafts, and hide processing

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、蜂蜜、蜂蜡、羽毛、药用植物、象牙原料、干肉与石料
tradeGoods.en: Hides, honey, beeswax, feathers, medicinal plants, ivory raw material, dried meat, and stone materials

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富常体现为稳定水源附近的居住权、储存食物、优质猎具、装饰品和跨部族亲属关系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth often appeared in secure access to water-adjacent living areas, stored food, good hunting equipment, ornaments, and intertribal kin ties.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭采猎生产、部族共享和低度区域交换构成的部落采集经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal foraging economy combining household production, tribal sharing, and low-level regional exchange.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent in reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备主要以干肉、蜂蜜、蜡、皮张、象牙原料和礼仪装饰物的形式分散保存，不存在统一金属国库。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves were kept mainly as dried meat, honey, wax, hides, ivory raw material, and ritual ornaments, with no single metal treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重保障季节缺粮时期的互助、组织大规模采猎并维持部族间礼仪交换。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on mutual aid during seasonal scarcity, organizing large-scale foraging and hunting, and sustaining ritual exchange between tribes.

description.zh: 以步战为主，使用弓箭、矛、投掷武器和木盾，适合草原与林缘机动作战。
description.en: Primarily infantry using bows, spears, thrown weapons, and wooden shields, suited for mobile fighting across savanna and woodland margins.

description.zh: 负责守卫营地、水源和迁徙路线，多由成年成员轮值。
description.en: They guarded camps, water sources, and migration routes, often through rotating service by adult members.

description.zh: 负责追踪外来者、观察兽群与预警敌对部落活动。
description.en: They tracked outsiders, observed animal movements, and warned of hostile tribal activity.

commanderInChief.zh: 部族联席长老与猎战首领
commanderInChief.en: Council of tribal elders and hunt-war leaders

technology.zh: 军备以木、石、骨和少量区域交换得来的金属器为主，长于侦察、伏击和环境机动，但缺少重装、防城和持续会战能力。
technology.en: Armament was based mainly on wood, stone, bone, and in some areas small amounts of exchanged metal goods; they excelled in scouting, ambush, and environmental mobility but lacked heavy equipment, fortifications, and sustained battle capacity.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括邻近部族争夺水源与猎场、旱灾、疫病以及来自农牧扩张群体的边缘压力。
threats.en: Main threats included competition over water and hunting grounds with neighboring tribes, drought, disease, and pressure from expanding farming or pastoral groups.

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于非洲热带草原和疏林地区，居住形态以小型营地、季节性聚落和河湖边临时定居点为主。尽管总人口较高，但聚落密度普遍偏低。
populationDescription.en: The population was widely distributed across African tropical savannas and open woodlands, living mostly in small camps, seasonal settlements, and temporary lakeside or riverside sites. Despite the relatively large total population, settlement density was generally low.

socialClasses.zh: 以普通家户为主，辅以长老、祭司、猎战首领和少量专精工匠，整体阶层差异不大。
socialClasses.en: Most people belonged to ordinary households, alongside elders, ritual priests, hunt-war leaders, and a small number of specialized craftspeople, with limited class differentiation overall.

allies.zh: 盟友通常为通婚部族、共享水源的邻近群体及偶有交换关系的农牧社区。
allies.en: Allies usually included intermarrying tribes, neighboring groups sharing water access, and occasionally nearby farming or pastoral communities linked by exchange.

enemies.zh: 敌对者多为争夺资源的周边部落，或对狩猎采集地带施压的外来农牧人群。
enemies.en: Enemies were mainly surrounding tribes competing for resources or outside farming and pastoral peoples pressing into foraging zones.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策强调水源共享、婚姻结盟、局部交换与在冲突时迅速迁移避险。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized shared water access, marriage alliances, local exchange, and rapid relocation to avoid costly conflict.

era.zh: 非洲铁器时代早期边缘地带的采集—狩猎技术阶段
era.en: A foraging-hunting technological stage on the margins of early African Iron Age zones

keyInnovations.zh: 火利用管理、蜂蜜采集技术、区域性植物知识、弓箭狩猎、轻型营地构筑与季节性资源调度。
keyInnovations.en: Controlled fire management, honey-collecting techniques, regional botanical knowledge, bow hunting, light camp construction, and seasonal resource scheduling.

strengths.zh: 生态适应面广、食物来源多样、迁徙灵活且共同体互助较强。
strengths.en: They had broad ecological adaptability, diverse food sources, flexible mobility, and strong communal mutual support.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权和高密度生产基础，易受旱灾、疾病及外来扩张压力影响。
weaknesses.en: They lacked unified political authority and dense productive bases, making them vulnerable to drought, disease, and pressure from expanding outsiders.

outlook.zh: 若环境波动可控并保持与农牧邻居的交换空间，此类部族社会可持续存在，但在国家形成竞争中处于劣势。
outlook.en: If environmental shocks remained manageable and exchange with neighboring farmers and herders continued, these tribal societies could persist, though they were disadvantaged in competition with state-forming polities.

description.zh: 这是一个分散于非洲热带草原、依靠采集狩猎和部族长老协商维系的广域部落社会。
description.en: This was a wide-ranging tribal society of the African savanna sustained by foraging, hunting, and governance through councils of elders.

dynasty.en: Era of the Samnite Tribal Confederacies

governmentForm.zh: 以部族联盟为基础，由各部落贵族会议与年度高级执政官共同运作的山地部族政体。
governmentForm.en: A highland tribal confederate polity based on clan alliances, governed through councils of nobles and annually selected chief magistrates.

socialStructure.zh: 萨莫奈社会以氏族、部落与战士共同体为核心，自由武装成年男子在政治与军事上具有重要地位。贵族家族掌握土地、牲畜与声望，并通过婚姻和盟约维系统治。依附者、牧民与农民构成基层社会，战时可迅速动员。
socialStructure.en: Samnite society centered on clans, tribes, and warrior communities, with free adult armed males holding significant political and military weight. Noble families controlled land, herds, and prestige, maintaining power through marriage ties and compacts. Dependents, herders, and farmers formed the social base and could be mobilized quickly in war.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是各部落的贵族武士家族与祭司性长老。被称为“图提库斯”的高级官员通常出自声望最高的氏族。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted chiefly of noble warrior lineages and priestly elders of the tribes. Senior magistrates known as meddices tutici were typically drawn from the most prestigious clans.

succession.zh: 最高权力通常并非严格世袭，而是在贵族家族之间经部落会议推举或协商产生。地方首领职位则常由显赫家族延续，但仍需获得战士与长老认可。
succession.en: Supreme authority was not strictly hereditary, but usually emerged through selection or negotiation among noble families in tribal councils. Local chieftaincies often remained within prominent lineages, yet still required acceptance by warriors and elders.

structure.zh: 萨莫奈人没有高度集中的国家官僚体系，而是由彭特里、考迪尼、希尔皮尼与卡劳切尼等诸部组成松散联盟。战时由共同推举的高级执政官协调军事与外交，平时各部与聚落保有很强自治。宗教权威、长老会议与武装公民大会共同影响决策。
structure.en: The Samnites lacked a highly centralized bureaucratic state and instead formed a loose confederation of tribes such as the Pentri, Caudini, Hirpini, and Caraceni. In wartime, a jointly chosen chief magistrate coordinated military and diplomatic affairs, while tribes and settlements retained strong autonomy in peacetime. Religious authority, councils of elders, and assemblies of armed citizens all shaped decision-making.

function.zh: 协调各部盟约、裁决重大争端并推举高级执政官。
function.en: Coordinated intertribal compacts, judged major disputes, and selected the chief magistrate.

function.zh: 统计可征发战士、组织集结、分配武备与战利品。
function.en: Recorded eligible warriors, organized musters, and distributed arms and spoils.

function.zh: 维护部族圣地、监督誓约祭仪并保管部分贡献。
function.en: Maintained tribal sanctuaries, oversaw oath rituals, and held part of ritual offerings.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依靠聚落长老、氏族首领与战士集会。博维阿努姆等中心聚落在联盟中具有更高威望，但对周边山地社区并无完全直接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on settlement elders, clan heads, and warrior assemblies. Centers such as Bovianum enjoyed higher prestige within the confederacy but did not exercise fully direct rule over surrounding upland communities.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、誓约和赔偿原则为基础，重视血亲关系、土地使用与荣誉义务。重大案件通常由长老与贵族裁断，必要时辅以宗教宣誓。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice, oaths, and compensation, with emphasis on kinship, land use, and obligations of honor. Major cases were usually judged by elders and nobles, often reinforced by religious oath-taking.

taxationSystem.zh: 萨莫奈人缺乏固定普遍税制，公共收入多来自战利品分配、盟部贡赋、牲畜征发与圣所贡献。战时可临时向各部落征收粮食、武器与役使。
taxationSystem.en: The Samnites lacked a fixed universal tax system; public income came mainly from shares of war booty, tribal contributions, livestock levies, and sanctuary offerings. In wartime, food, weapons, and labor could be requisitioned from the tribes.

religion.zh: 意大利多神教
religion.en: Italic polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 萨莫奈人以山地要塞聚落、防御工事、武器装饰与富有地方特色的宗教圣所著称。其武士文化和部族联盟传统对中部—南部意大利政治格局影响深远。
culturalAchievements.en: The Samnites were noted for hillfort settlements, defensive works, decorated weaponry, and distinctive regional sanctuaries. Their warrior culture and confederate traditions had a lasting impact on the political landscape of central and southern Italy.

languageFamily.zh: 奥斯坎—翁布里亚语支，属意大利语族，印欧语系
languageFamily.en: Oscan-Umbrian branch of the Italic languages, Indo-European family

philosophy.zh: 以意大利山地部族传统伦理为主，强调勇武、忠诚、祖先荣誉与对誓约的敬畏，而非系统哲学学派。
philosophy.en: It was shaped by Italic highland tribal ethics emphasizing valor, loyalty, ancestral honor, and reverence for oaths rather than formal philosophical schools.

writingSystem.zh: 使用奥斯坎语，书写时采用源自伊特鲁里亚—古意大利体系的奥斯坎字母，但识字范围有限。
writingSystem.en: They used the Oscan language, written in an Oscan alphabet derived from the Etruscan and Old Italic tradition, though literacy remained limited.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以山地农牧混合为主，辅以地方手工业和小规模区域贸易。人均产出高于纯狩猎采集社会，但低于同期高度城市化的地中海国家。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on mixed highland farming and herding, supplemented by local crafts and modest regional trade. Output per head was higher than that of pure hunter-gatherer societies but below that of more urbanized Mediterranean states.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、牧羊与养牛、铁器锻造、木工、陶器
mainIndustries.en: grain cultivation, sheep and cattle herding, ironworking, carpentry, pottery

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、皮革、羊毛、铁器、木材、盐、谷物
tradeGoods.en: livestock, hides, wool, iron goods, timber, salt, grain

unitName.en: livestock, bronze ingots, and silver coins

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜头数、武器装备与亲族网络。贵族家庭拥有更多牧场、奴仆或依附者，并能积累金属器与战利品。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured chiefly in land-use rights, herd size, weaponry, and kin connections. Noble households held larger pastures, dependents or servants, and greater stocks of metal goods and booty.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以自给性部族经济为主，结合贡纳、互惠交换和有限市场贸易。
economicSystem.en: A largely self-sufficient tribal economy combining tribute, reciprocal exchange, and limited market trade.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非集中于单一宫廷，而是分散保存在重要圣所、贵族据点与联盟中心。其储备多为银器、青铜器、牲畜与可重新分配的贡物。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not centralized in a single court but dispersed among major sanctuaries, noble strongholds, and confederate centers. Reserves consisted largely of silverware, bronze goods, livestock, and redistributable tribute.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政偏向战时动员型，在冲突时期扩大征发，和平时期则维持较低常设负担。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was oriented toward wartime mobilization, expanding levies in conflict while keeping peacetime burdens relatively low.

description.zh: 以持矛、盾牌和短剑的部族步兵为主体，适合山地作战与伏击。
description.en: The main arm was tribal infantry armed with spears, shields, and short swords, well suited for mountain warfare and ambush.

description.zh: 由较年轻或装备较轻者组成，负责侦察、骚扰与地形掩护作战。
description.en: Made up of younger or more lightly equipped men, they handled scouting, harassment, and terrain-based fighting.

description.zh: 人数不多，但由贵族与富裕战士组成，用于联络、追击与突袭。
description.en: Small in number but composed of nobles and wealthy warriors, they served in communication, pursuit, and raiding roles.

commanderInChief.zh: 梅迪克斯·图提库斯
commanderInChief.en: Meddix Tuticus

technology.zh: 处于意大利铁器时代军事水平，使用铁矛、剑、盾与皮甲或青铜护具，擅长山地机动和防御工事。
technology.en: Their military stood at an Italic Iron Age level, using iron spears, swords, shields, and leather or bronze protection, with strengths in mountain mobility and field fortification.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自拉丁人与罗马势力的扩张、坎帕尼亚平原竞争以及邻近意大利诸部的冲突。
threats.en: The principal threats came from the expanding Latin and Roman sphere, rivalry over the Campanian plain, and conflict with neighboring Italic peoples.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在亚平宁山地谷地与丘陵聚落，密度不高但具有较强动员能力。真正的大型城市有限，多为设防聚落与部族中心。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across Apennine valleys and hill settlements, with low density but strong mobilization potential. True large cities were few; most centers were fortified settlements and tribal hubs.

socialClasses.zh: 贵族武士家族、自由战士农牧民、依附者与仆役、少量奴隶或战俘。
socialClasses.en: noble warrior lineages, free warrior-farmers and herders, dependents and servants, and a small number of slaves or war captives

allies.zh: 与部分奥斯坎语部族及亚平宁山地社群保持亲缘或盟约联系。
allies.en: They maintained kin-based or treaty ties with some Oscan-speaking tribes and other Apennine upland communities.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌手包括罗马、拉丁势力、部分坎帕尼亚城邦及竞争性意大利部族。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included Rome, Latin powers, some Campanian city-states, and rival Italic tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策重在维护山地自治、控制通道并防止低地强权深入内陆。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving upland autonomy, controlling passes, and preventing lowland powers from penetrating inland.

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制武器与工具、山地设防聚落、部族动员体系、奥斯坎字母的有限使用。
keyInnovations.en: iron weapons and tools, fortified hill settlements, tribal mobilization systems, and limited use of the Oscan script

strengths.zh: 山地地形优势明显，战士传统强，部落动员速度快，社会韧性高。
strengths.en: They possessed strong terrain advantages, a robust warrior tradition, rapid tribal mobilization, and high social resilience.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、财政有限、城市化不足，难以长期维持统一战略与攻城能力。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, limited finances, and low urbanization made it difficult to sustain unified strategy and prolonged siege warfare.

outlook.zh: 若能维持联盟一致，萨莫奈人足以长期主导意大利中南部山地；但面对更集中化国家时将逐渐处于劣势。
outlook.en: If confederate unity held, the Samnites could dominate much of the central-southern Italian uplands for a long period; against more centralized states, however, they would increasingly be disadvantaged.

description.zh: 萨莫奈人是意大利中南部亚平宁山区强悍而分散的奥斯坎语部族联盟。
description.en: The Samnites were a formidable yet decentralized Oscan-speaking tribal confederacy of the central-southern Apennines.

dynasty.zh: 古西伯利亚氏族联盟时期

governmentForm.zh: 以亲族氏族和季节性营地为基础、由多位首领与萨满共同维系的分散部落社会。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal society based on kin-clans and seasonal camps, sustained by multiple chiefs and shamans.

socialStructure.zh: 社会组织以小型氏族和家庭营地为核心，人口稀少且高度分散。狩猎技术、迁徙经验和对环境的熟悉程度决定个人威望。萨满、年长猎手与成功的家庭首领在资源分配和迁徙决策中影响较大。
socialStructure.en: Social organization centered on small clans and family camps, with sparse and highly dispersed populations. Prestige depended on hunting skill, migratory knowledge, and mastery of the environment. Shamans, elder hunters, and successful household heads had greater influence over resource distribution and movement decisions.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在固定世袭贵族，权威主要来自经验、亲族声望、猎获能力与灵性权威。部分大型氏族中的长者家系在跨季节协商中更有话语权。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed hereditary nobility; authority derived mainly from experience, kin prestige, hunting success, and spiritual power. In some larger clans, elder lineages carried more weight in seasonal negotiations.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常由氏族内部协商形成，继承偏向有能力的成年亲属而非严格长子制。萨满身份则多通过师承、神启或群体认可确立。
succession.en: Leadership usually emerged through clan consultation, with succession favoring capable adult relatives rather than strict primogeniture. Shamanic status was established through apprenticeship, visions, or communal recognition.

structure.zh: 古西伯利亚猎人没有中央国家机构，政治结构表现为多个氏族营地在季节性会合时进行协商。对外冲突、婚姻交换与猎场边界多由长者和首领口头协调。平时各营地高度自治，仅在大型狩猎、迁徙或危机时形成临时联盟。
structure.en: Paleo-Siberian hunters had no central state apparatus; politically they consisted of multiple clan camps that consulted one another during seasonal gatherings. External conflict, marriage exchange, and hunting boundaries were coordinated orally by elders and chiefs. In normal times camps were highly autonomous, forming temporary alliances only for large hunts, migrations, or crises.

function.zh: 商议迁徙路线、猎场分配、婚姻联络与纠纷调解。
function.en: Discussed migration routes, hunting allocation, marriage ties, and dispute mediation.

function.zh: 主持治病、祭灵、占兆并维护族群禁忌。
function.en: Conducted healing, spirit rites, divination, and maintenance of communal taboos.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即营地自治，由家庭长者和猎队首领处理日常事务。不存在固定行政区，活动范围依河流、森林与苔原季节变化而调整。
localAdmin.en: Local administration amounted to camp self-rule, with household elders and hunt leaders managing daily affairs. There were no fixed districts; activity zones shifted seasonally across rivers, forests, and tundra.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是口传习惯法，围绕猎物分配、婚姻交换、血亲义务与禁忌展开。严重冲突多通过赔偿、迁离或氏族调解解决。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted mainly of oral customary practice focused on game distribution, marriage exchange, kin obligations, and taboos. Serious conflicts were usually resolved through compensation, separation, or clan mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税收制度，公共需求通过共享猎获、互助劳动与仪式贡献满足。大型联合狩猎时，各营地需按能力提供食物、骨器或皮货。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; collective needs were met through shared game, mutual labor, and ritual offerings. During large cooperative hunts, camps contributed food, bone tools, or hides according to ability.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就体现在极地与亚寒带环境适应、复杂狩猎知识、皮革缝制、骨角器制作与仪式表演传统。口头叙事和萨满仪式在维系群体记忆方面十分重要。
culturalAchievements.en: Their achievements lay in adaptation to Arctic and sub-Arctic environments, sophisticated hunting knowledge, leather sewing, bone and antler craftsmanship, and ritual performance traditions. Oral narratives and shamanic rites were crucial for preserving collective memory.

languageFamily.zh: 多种古西伯利亚语系或孤立语言群，可能包括楚科奇—堪察加及其他前通古斯成分
languageFamily.en: Various Paleo-Siberian language groups or isolates, possibly including Chukotko-Kamchatkan and other pre-Tungusic elements

philosophy.zh: 世界观以万物有灵、祖灵与自然循环为核心，强调人与动物、土地和灵界的平衡关系。
philosophy.en: Their worldview centered on animism, ancestral spirits, and natural cycles, stressing balance between humans, animals, the land, and the spirit world.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识依靠口传、歌谣、神话、纹饰和记忆性地标传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system existed; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, song, myth, ornament, and mnemonic landmarks.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总量极低但与人口规模相符，核心价值来自肉类、鱼类、皮毛和骨角制品。由于几乎没有城市和固定市场，产出主要用于直接消费与互惠交换。
gdpDescription.en: Total output was very low but appropriate to the small population, with core value coming from meat, fish, furs, and bone-antler goods. With almost no towns or fixed markets, production was used mainly for direct consumption and reciprocal exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、捕鱼、采集、皮毛加工、骨角器制作
mainIndustries.en: hunting, fishing, gathering, fur processing, bone and antler toolmaking

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、海兽制品、鱼干、骨角器、燧石与装饰物
tradeGoods.en: furs, marine mammal products, dried fish, bone-antler tools, flint, and ornaments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为猎具、皮衣、雪橇或舟具、储藏食物和稳定的亲族互助网络。能长期保存食物和拥有优质毛皮的家庭更具安全性。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed in hunting gear, skin clothing, sled or boat equipment, stored food, and reliable kin-support networks. Families able to preserve food and hold high-quality furs enjoyed greater security.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭采猎自给为主，辅以氏族共享、互惠赠与和低强度边缘交换。
economicSystem.en: A household foraging economy based on self-provisioning, supplemented by clan sharing, reciprocal gifting, and low-intensity frontier exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无固定金属国库，储备主要是干鱼、肉类、皮货、骨器和少量交换珍品。财富分散于各氏族营地之中。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no fixed metallic treasury; reserves consisted mainly of dried fish, meat, hides, bone tools, and small exchange valuables. Wealth was dispersed across clan camps.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践以生存保障为核心，强调储备、共享和在灾年重新分配资源。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice was survival-oriented, emphasizing storage, sharing, and redistribution in hard years.

description.zh: 平时为猎队，冲突时转为轻装战斗单位，使用长矛、弓箭和短刃。
description.en: Normally hunting parties, they turned into light combat units in conflict, using spears, bows, and short blades.

description.zh: 熟悉雪地、森林和冻原环境，擅长追踪、伏击与预警。
description.en: Expert in snow, forest, and tundra conditions, they specialized in tracking, ambush, and early warning.

commanderInChief.zh: 氏族首领联席
commanderInChief.en: Council of Clan Chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术极为简朴，以木骨复合猎具、石骨刃器、弓矛和皮革护具为主，优势在环境适应与隐蔽机动。
technology.en: Military technology was very simple, relying on wood-and-bone hunting implements, stone and bone blades, bows, spears, and skin protection, with strength in environmental adaptation and stealth mobility.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自严酷气候、猎物波动、邻近氏族冲突及区域性资源紧张，而非大规模国家战争。
threats.en: The main threats came from severe climate, fluctuations in game, neighboring clan conflict, and regional resource stress rather than large state warfare.

populationDescription.zh: 人口极其稀疏，分散于河谷、林地、海岸和苔原边缘的季节性营地。出生率与存活率都受气候和食物供给强烈制约。
populationDescription.en: The population was extremely sparse, scattered across seasonal camps in river valleys, forests, coasts, and tundra margins. Birth and survival rates were heavily constrained by climate and food supply.

socialClasses.zh: 无严格阶级制，可区分为首领家庭、萨满、熟练猎手、普通家庭成员与收养或依附者。
socialClasses.en: There was no rigid class system; distinctions existed among chief families, shamans, skilled hunters, ordinary household members, and adoptees or dependents.

allies.zh: 盟友关系多体现为邻近氏族之间的通婚、互助狩猎和冬季共享网络。
allies.en: Alliance relations mostly took the form of intermarriage, cooperative hunting, and winter sharing networks among neighboring clans.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象通常是争夺河段、海岸点位或迁徙通道的周边小群体。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually nearby small groups competing for river stretches, coastal points, or migration corridors.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外策略以避免消耗性冲突、维持交换联系和保护关键猎场为主。
foreignPolicy.en: External strategy aimed at avoiding costly conflict, maintaining exchange ties, and protecting key hunting grounds.

era.zh: 北亚晚期新石器—早期金属接触阶段
era.en: North Asian late Neolithic to early metal-contact stage

keyInnovations.zh: 骨角复合工具、寒地皮衣缝制、鱼类储藏技术、轻型舟具与雪地迁徙经验。
keyInnovations.en: bone-antler composite tools, cold-weather skin clothing, fish preservation, light watercraft, and snow-country mobility knowledge

strengths.zh: 环境适应力极强，社会结构灵活，生存知识深厚，能在极端气候下维持连续定居网络。
strengths.en: They were highly adaptive to harsh environments, socially flexible, rich in survival knowledge, and capable of maintaining continuity under extreme climatic conditions.

weaknesses.zh: 人口过少、技术积累缓慢、缺乏集中储备与常设武装，抗外来压力能力有限。
weaknesses.en: Their very small population, slow technological accumulation, lack of centralized reserves, and absence of standing armed forces limited resistance to outside pressure.

outlook.zh: 在未受大规模南来扩张影响时可长期稳定存在，但面对更先进的牧业或金属器文化渗透将逐步边缘化。
outlook.en: They could endure stably for long periods in the absence of major outside expansion, but would gradually be marginalized by incoming pastoral or metal-using cultures.

description.zh: 古西伯利亚猎人是分散于北亚寒冷地带、依赖狩猎渔猎与萨满传统维生的氏族社会。
description.en: The Paleo-Siberian hunters were dispersed clan societies of cold northern Asia, sustained by hunting, fishing, and shamanic traditions.

governmentForm.zh: 以小型流动游群为基础，由资深猎手、家长与仪式人物临时协商治理的部落社会。
governmentForm.en: A tribal society of small mobile bands governed through ad hoc consultation among senior hunters, household heads, and ritual specialists.

socialStructure.zh: 社会单位通常是数十人规模的流动游群，依水源、季节果实与猎物迁徙而变化。权威较为平等，但善于寻水、狩猎和调解纠纷者更受尊重。婚姻联系把多个游群松散地连接起来，形成广域互助网络。
socialStructure.en: The normal social unit was a mobile band of several dozen people, shifting with water sources, seasonal plants, and animal movement. Authority was relatively egalitarian, but those skilled in finding water, hunting, and mediating disputes were more respected. Marriage links loosely connected multiple bands into wider mutual-support networks.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在固定统治阶级，仅有在特定时刻发挥领导作用的资深家长、猎手和祭仪人物。个人影响力建立在慷慨、经验与口碑之上。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed ruling class, only senior household heads, hunters, and ritual figures who exercised leadership in particular situations. Personal influence rested on generosity, experience, and reputation.

succession.zh: 领导地位通常由共识产生，可能偏向某些有威望家系，但并不严格世袭。若首领失去信任，成员可通过分群迁移来削弱其权力。
succession.en: Leadership generally arose by consensus and might favor certain respected families, but it was not strictly hereditary. If a leader lost trust, followers could simply split off, reducing his authority.

structure.zh: 沙漠采集者没有固定都城或常设政体，治理围绕营地协商、共享规则和迁徙安排展开。旱季时各游群可能围绕永久泉眼形成较大的集会，处理婚配、交换和冲突调解。雨季或资源分散时则重新分成小群活动。
structure.en: The desert hunter-gatherers had no fixed capital or permanent state structure; governance centered on camp consultation, sharing rules, and migration planning. In dry seasons, bands could gather around permanent springs in larger assemblies to handle marriages, exchange, and dispute settlement. In wetter periods or when resources spread out, they divided again into smaller groups.

function.zh: 决定季节性路线、泉眼使用次序和营地间协作。
function.en: Determined seasonal routes, spring-use order, and cooperation among camps.

function.zh: 主持成年礼、治病祈祷、禁忌维护和冲突调解。
function.en: Oversaw initiation rites, healing prayers, taboo maintenance, and conflict mediation.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即营地自治，按水源点、猎场和采集区临时组织。不存在固定边界，但各游群通常承认传统使用区与亲缘联系范围。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was simply camp self-rule, organized temporarily around water points, hunting grounds, and gathering zones. No fixed borders existed, though bands generally recognized traditional use areas and kin-linked ranges.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法重视共享食物、尊重水源次序、婚配规则与避免内部暴力。纠纷多通过长者劝解、礼物补偿或分群迁移解决。
legalSystem.en: Customary law emphasized food sharing, respect for water order, marriage rules, and avoidance of internal violence. Disputes were commonly settled through elder mediation, gift compensation, or band fission.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，公共事务靠共同劳动、猎获分享和在集会中提供食物或器物来承担。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; public needs were met through common labor, shared game, and the provision of food or tools during gatherings.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就在于对极端干旱环境的知识积累，包括识别水源、植物利用、追踪技术和简洁而高效的迁徙组织。岩画、身体装饰和仪式舞蹈可能构成重要象征表达。
culturalAchievements.en: Their achievements lay in accumulated knowledge of extreme arid environments, including water location, plant use, tracking skill, and efficient mobile organization. Rock art, body ornament, and ritual dance likely formed important symbolic expressions.

languageFamily.zh: 可能属于多种前文字沙漠语言群，具体谱系难以确证
languageFamily.en: Likely belonged to several pre-literate desert language groups whose exact affiliations are uncertain

philosophy.zh: 世界观以萨满式自然灵信仰为核心，强调水源神圣、动物灵性与祖先路径记忆。
philosophy.en: Their worldview was rooted in shamanic reverence for nature, emphasizing the sacredness of water, the spirits of animals, and ancestral route memory.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，知识通过口述传说、地景记忆、歌谣和岩画符号传承。
writingSystem.en: They had no writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tales, remembered landscapes, songs, and rock-art symbols.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模极小，价值主要体现在食物获取、生存知识和可携带器具，而非可积累余粮。由于环境限制，剩余产出和外贸都十分有限。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was extremely small, with value embodied mainly in food acquisition, survival knowledge, and portable tools rather than storable surplus. Environmental limits kept both surplus production and external trade very low.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、野生植物采集、皮革处理、石器制作、简易篮编
mainIndustries.en: hunting, wild plant gathering, leather preparation, stone toolmaking, simple basketry

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、羽毛、石料、药用植物、贝饰与少量赭石
tradeGoods.en: animal skins, feathers, stone materials, medicinal plants, shell ornaments, and small amounts of ochre

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在耐用携行器具、皮囊、水袋、采集知识和与其他游群的互助关系。拥有可靠水源记忆和婚姻网络的家庭更为安全。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured in durable portable gear, skin bags, water containers, gathering knowledge, and ties of mutual aid with other bands. Families with dependable water knowledge and marriage networks were more secure.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以流动采猎自给和群体共享为核心的生存型经济，辅以极低强度的邻近交换。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence economy centered on mobile foraging and communal sharing, supplemented by very low-intensity neighboring exchange.
