treasuryDescription.zh: 没有集中金库，公共储备主要是干制食物、皮袋、水囊、石器和象征性装饰物，分散存放于各游群。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; common reserves consisted mainly of dried food, skin bags, water containers, stone tools, and symbolic ornaments dispersed among bands.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践以风险分担为主，优先保障旱季生存与水源点周边的共享秩序。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on risk-sharing, prioritizing dry-season survival and orderly sharing around water sources.

description.zh: 由成年猎手组成，武器为投矛、弓箭、石刃和木棒，擅长短程伏击。
description.en: Composed of adult hunters armed with javelins, bows, stone blades, and clubs, they specialized in short-range ambushes.

description.zh: 善于辨识足迹和远距离寻水，可执行警戒、追踪和避战行动。
description.en: Skilled in reading tracks and locating distant water, they handled watch, pursuit, and conflict-avoidance operations.

commanderInChief.zh: 游群首领联席
commanderInChief.en: Council of Band Leaders

technology.zh: 技术层次接近沙漠狩猎采集社会常态，以石器、骨器、木器和皮革装备为主，长于生存机动而非正面作战。
technology.en: Their technological level was typical of desert foragers, relying on stone, bone, wood, and leather equipment, with strengths in survival mobility rather than open battle.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁是旱灾、水源争夺、邻近游群冲突和猎物减少，而不是制度化战争。
threats.en: The main threats were drought, competition over water, neighboring band conflict, and declining game rather than institutionalized warfare.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀少且流动性极高，分布在沙漠边缘、绿洲周边和季节性水道附近。聚落概念很弱，多为暂时营地。
populationDescription.en: The population was small and highly mobile, spread across desert margins, oasis zones, and seasonal watercourses. Settlement was weakly developed and usually took the form of temporary camps.

socialClasses.zh: 整体趋于平等，可区分为资深首领家庭、祭仪人物、成年猎采者、儿童与年老依赖者。
socialClasses.en: Society was broadly egalitarian, though one may distinguish senior leader families, ritual specialists, adult foragers, children, and elderly dependents.

allies.zh: 与邻近游群通过通婚、共享泉眼和礼物交换维持脆弱盟谊。
allies.en: Fragile alliances with neighboring bands were maintained through intermarriage, shared springs, and gift exchange.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象通常是争夺关键水源和采集区的其他小群体。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually other small groups competing for key water sources and gathering zones.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外方针以回避、协商和季节性共享为主，只有在水源生死攸关时才发生激烈冲突。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy emphasized avoidance, negotiation, and seasonal sharing, with serious conflict emerging only when vital water access was at stake.

era.zh: 沙漠地区晚期石器—早期金属接触阶段
era.en: Desert late lithic to early metal-contact stage

keyInnovations.zh: 水源识别知识、便携皮囊与容器、追踪技术、旱地植物利用、岩画与路线记忆系统。
keyInnovations.en: water-location knowledge, portable skin containers, tracking techniques, arid-plant use, and route memory systems expressed through rock art

strengths.zh: 机动性高、环境知识深厚、内部等级较弱，能灵活适应资源变化。
strengths.en: They were highly mobile, deeply knowledgeable about the environment, and socially flexible due to weak internal hierarchy.

weaknesses.zh: 人口极少、储备有限、技术积累不足，对长期旱灾和外来定居力量缺乏抵抗能力。
weaknesses.en: Their tiny population, limited reserves, and modest technological base left them vulnerable to prolonged drought and intrusive settled powers.

outlook.zh: 在生态平衡尚可时可长期延续，但一旦水源受控或周边农业—牧业扩张加强，其生存空间将迅速收缩。
outlook.en: They could persist for long periods under ecological balance, but their living space would contract quickly once water sources were controlled or neighboring agro-pastoral expansion intensified.

description.zh: 沙漠采集者是依赖泉眼、季节性植物与小型猎物，在极端干旱地带流动生存的游群社会。
description.en: The desert hunter-gatherers were mobile band societies surviving in extreme arid lands through springs, seasonal plants, and small game.

dynasty.zh: 南印度巨石文化诸酋邦
dynasty.en: Chiefdoms of the South Indian Megalithic Culture

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族与部落联盟为基础的分散酋邦体制，由地方首领在祭祀与战争中行使权威。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized chiefdom system based on clans and tribal confederations, with local chiefs exercising authority in ritual and warfare.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、家族与聚落共同体为核心，上层为掌握牛群、铁器与葬仪资源的酋长家族。普通成员多从事农耕、放牧、采集与手工业，另有依附性的战士与祭司群体。巨石墓葬显示社会分化已较明显，但尚未形成高度官僚化国家。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on clans, lineages, and village communities, with elite chiefly families controlling cattle, iron goods, and funerary prestige. Common people engaged mainly in farming, herding, gathering, and craft production, alongside dependent warrior and ritual specialist groups. Megalithic burials indicate clear social differentiation, though not yet a highly bureaucratic state.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是部落酋长、战士贵族与主持祖灵祭祀的宗教首领。其权威建立在血缘、战功、财富分配与葬仪声望之上。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted mainly of tribal chiefs, warrior elites, and ritual leaders overseeing ancestor worship. Their authority rested on kinship, martial success, redistribution of wealth, and funerary prestige.

succession.zh: 继承通常由酋长家族内部推举，偏向父系世袭，但须获得长老与武士支持。若继承不稳，常由旁支或更有战功者取代。
succession.en: Succession was usually decided within chiefly lineages, tending toward patrilineal inheritance but requiring approval from elders and warriors. When inheritance was disputed, collateral branches or more successful war leaders could replace the claimant.

structure.zh: 政治组织以聚落酋长、氏族长老与祭司共同协商为主，缺乏固定首都与常设官僚体系。较大的酋邦可在季节性集会时协调战争、贸易与葬仪活动。
structure.en: Political organization relied on consultation among settlement chiefs, clan elders, and ritual specialists, without a fixed capital or permanent bureaucracy. Larger chiefdoms could coordinate warfare, trade, and funerary ceremonies during seasonal assemblies.

function.zh: 处理部落联盟决策、争端调解、战争与盟约事务。
function.en: Handled confederational decisions, dispute mediation, warfare, and alliance matters.

function.zh: 负责祭仪组织、公共储粮、贡品保管与大型葬礼筹备。
function.en: Oversaw rituals, communal grain storage, tribute custody, and major funerary preparations.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由各聚落首领和长老会直接执行，村社内部事务依习惯法处理。较远地区仅在进贡、军事动员和婚盟时与上级酋长发生稳定联系。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out directly by settlement heads and councils of elders, with village affairs managed through customary law. More distant communities maintained regular ties to higher chiefs mainly for tribute, military levies, and marriage alliances.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为口传习惯法，重视血亲赔偿、牲畜补偿与仪式性和解。严重冒犯祖灵、盗牛与杀人行为会受到放逐、报复或祭仪处罚。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily oral customary law, emphasizing kin compensation, cattle payment, and ritual reconciliation. Grave offenses such as sacrilege against ancestors, cattle theft, and homicide could lead to exile, feud, or ritual punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 并无标准化税制，酋长主要通过贡赋、战利品分成、牲畜献纳和劳役获得资源。大型祭祀和墓葬工程时，附属聚落需提供粮食、石材、金属器与人力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no standardized tax system; chiefs derived resources mainly from tribute, shares of war booty, cattle offerings, and labor dues. During major rituals and tomb construction, subordinate settlements supplied grain, stone, metal goods, and manpower.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 巨石墓、石圈、石室墓与铁器随葬品反映出发达的葬仪文化和区域工艺传统。黑红陶、珠饰和铁制农兵器显示南印度在铁器时代早期已形成鲜明的物质文化圈。
culturalAchievements.en: Megalithic tombs, stone circles, cists, and iron grave goods reflect an advanced funerary culture and regional craft traditions. Black-and-red ware, beadwork, and iron tools and weapons show that early Iron Age South India had formed a distinctive material cultural zone.

languageFamily.zh: 早期达罗毗荼语族方言为主，可能夹杂部分蒙达或其他地方语言成分。
languageFamily.en: Primarily early Dravidian dialects, possibly with some Munda or other local linguistic elements.

philosophy.zh: 以祖先崇拜、自然灵信仰和英雄式战士伦理为核心，重视墓葬、纪念石与牲畜象征。
philosophy.en: Centered on ancestor veneration, animistic beliefs, and a heroic warrior ethic, with strong emphasis on burials, memorial stones, and cattle symbolism.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无本地广泛使用的成熟文字，信息主要依靠口述传统、符号记忆与器物标记传承。
writingSystem.en: No widely used mature local writing system existed; information was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, mnemonic symbols, and marks on objects.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以旱作农业、放牧和小规模铁器生产为主，整体仍属低剩余的区域性部落经济。财富更多表现为牲畜、谷物、珠饰和金属器，而非统一货币存量。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on dry farming, herding, and small-scale iron production, remaining a low-surplus regional tribal economy. Wealth was expressed more in cattle, grain, beads, and metal goods than in unified monetary holdings.

mainIndustries.zh: 旱作农业、放牧、铁冶炼、陶器制作、珠饰加工、石葬建筑
mainIndustries.en: Dry agriculture, herding, iron smelting, pottery making, bead production, megalithic stone construction

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、黑红陶、玛瑙与半宝石珠、牲畜、谷物、林产物、盐
tradeGoods.en: Iron tools, black-and-red ware, agate and semi-precious beads, cattle, grain, forest products, salt

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在牛羊数量、储粮、铁制工具和陶器。精英家庭则拥有更多珠饰、武器、战利品和用于墓葬展示的奢侈物。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly measured in livestock, stored grain, iron tools, and pottery. Elite households held more beads, weapons, booty, and prestige goods used in funerary display.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给、酋长再分配和区域交换网络并存的部落经济为主。部分物资通过长距离交换流动，但大多数生产仍服务于本地消费与祭祀需求。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy combining village self-sufficiency, chiefly redistribution, and regional exchange networks. Some goods moved through long-distance exchange, but most production served local consumption and ritual needs.

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要以储粮、牲畜、铁器和珠饰形式分散保存在各核心聚落，并非集中铸币金库。酋长通过控制这些储备维持威望和再分配能力。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was held mainly as grain stores, livestock, iron goods, and beads dispersed among core settlements rather than as a centralized coin reserve. Chiefs maintained prestige and redistributive power through control of these stores.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持酋长权威、保障祭祀和在歉收时进行有限再分配为主，缺乏固定预算制度。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining chiefly authority, supporting ritual obligations, and conducting limited redistribution in poor harvest years, without a fixed budget system.

description.zh: 由酋长直属战士组成，装备较好的铁矛、刀剑和盾牌，负责护卫与突袭。
description.en: Composed of warriors directly attached to chiefs, equipped with better iron spears, blades, and shields for protection and raiding.

description.zh: 战时从各聚落征集，多为农牧民，承担防御、掠袭和护送任务。
description.en: Raised from settlements in wartime, mostly farmer-herders serving in defense, raiding, and escort duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 联盟大酋长或最强部落首领
commanderInChief.en: Confederation high chief or leading tribal chief

technology.zh: 军备以铁矛、短剑、弓箭、木盾为主，缺乏复杂攻城器械和正规骑兵。作战以伏击、劫掠、聚落冲突和短期集结为主要形式。
technology.en: Armament centered on iron spears, short swords, bows, and wooden shields, with no advanced siege machinery or formal cavalry. Warfare consisted mainly of ambushes, raiding, settlement conflicts, and short-term mustering.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近酋邦争夺牧场、水源、铁矿和贸易通道，也面临内部继承冲突。生态波动和旱灾会迅速转化为武装掠夺。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring chiefdoms competing over pasture, water, iron sources, and trade routes, as well as internal succession conflicts. Ecological fluctuations and drought could quickly turn into armed raiding.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布以德干高原南部和泰米尔地区内陆聚落为主，多为中小型村社和季节性据点。居民生活方式兼具农业、畜牧与林地资源利用。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in inland settlements of the southern Deccan and Tamil regions, mostly in small to medium villages and seasonal sites. Livelihoods combined agriculture, pastoralism, and use of woodland resources.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长与战士贵族、祭司与长老、自由农牧民、手工业者、依附者与俘虏。
socialClasses.en: Chiefs and warrior elites, ritual specialists and elders, free farmer-herders, artisans, dependents, and captives.

allies.zh: 盟友主要是通过婚盟、共祭与交换网络联系的邻近酋邦，没有长期固定的跨区域同盟。
allies.en: Allies were mainly neighboring chiefdoms linked through marriage ties, joint rituals, and exchange networks, with no long-term formal interregional alliances.

enemies.zh: 敌对者多为争夺土地、牲畜和资源的邻近部落与竞争酋长。
enemies.en: Enemies were chiefly nearby tribes and rival chiefs competing for land, livestock, and resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持交换通道、通过婚姻和礼物稳固关系、在必要时发动报复性袭击为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining exchange routes, securing ties through marriage and gift exchange, and launching retaliatory raids when necessary.

keyInnovations.zh: 铁冶炼与锻造、黑红陶生产、石墓建筑技术、珠饰加工、旱地农耕工具改进。
keyInnovations.en: Iron smelting and forging, black-and-red ware production, megalithic stone construction techniques, bead manufacture, and improved dry-farming tools.

strengths.zh: 拥有较强的地方适应能力、成熟的铁器使用和稳固的氏族网络。酋长通过再分配和葬仪展示能够有效整合多个聚落。
strengths.en: It possessed strong local adaptability, established iron use, and resilient clan networks. Chiefs could integrate multiple settlements effectively through redistribution and funerary display.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、文书与行政能力薄弱，难以长期维持大规模统一行动。经济剩余有限，易受气候和内部纷争影响。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation and weak record-keeping and administration made sustained large-scale coordination difficult. Economic surplus was limited and vulnerable to climate stress and internal disputes.

outlook.zh: 未来可能向更稳定的区域酋邦和早期王权演化，并逐步融入南印度历史时期国家形成过程。
outlook.en: It was likely to evolve toward more stable regional chiefdoms and early kingdoms, gradually entering the state-formation processes of historic South India.

description.zh: 南印度巨石文化是在铁器、祖先崇拜与酋长权威基础上发展的分散部落—酋邦社会。
description.en: The South Indian Megalithic Culture was a dispersed tribal-chiefdom society built on iron use, ancestor veneration, and chiefly authority.

governmentForm.zh: 以亲族猎队和季节性营地为基础的松散部落组织，由威望型首领临时协调行动。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal organization based on kin hunting bands and seasonal camps, with prestige leaders coordinating action temporarily.

socialStructure.zh: 社会规模较小，以家庭、扩大家族和季节性猎队为基本单位。经验丰富的猎手、萨满和长者享有较高声望，但整体等级分化有限。资源分享和互助是维持严酷环境生存的核心机制。
socialStructure.en: Society was small in scale, organized around households, extended families, and seasonal hunting bands. Experienced hunters, shamans, and elders held high prestige, but overall stratification remained limited. Resource sharing and mutual aid were central to survival in the harsh environment.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在严格意义上的世袭贵族，领导层主要由最有经验的猎手、船队组织者和萨满构成。其权威来自技能、慷慨和灵性威望。
rulingClass.en: There was no strict hereditary aristocracy; leadership was mainly held by the most experienced hunters, boat organizers, and shamans. Their authority came from skill, generosity, and spiritual prestige.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常并非固定世袭，而是由群体默认最能带领狩猎和协调分配者担任。某些家族因声望较高而更易持续出任首领。
succession.en: Leadership was usually not fixed by strict heredity, but assumed by those recognized as most capable in hunting leadership and redistribution. Some families more easily retained leadership because of accumulated prestige.

structure.zh: 治理结构极为简约，营地内部依靠长者协商与首领威望处理事务。跨营地合作主要发生在大型捕猎、婚配交换和危机应对时期。
structure.en: The governing structure was highly minimal, with camp affairs managed through elder consultation and the prestige of leaders. Inter-camp cooperation occurred mainly during major hunts, marriage exchange, and times of crisis.

function.zh: 安排季节性海猎、迁徙路线与猎获分配。
function.en: Organized seasonal marine hunts, migration routes, and distribution of catch.

function.zh: 主持仪式、处理禁忌与群体内部冲突。
function.en: Conducted rituals and handled taboos and internal group conflicts.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政即营地自治，各家庭在首领与长者建议下安排狩猎和物资使用。没有固定疆界，活动范围依海冰、动物迁徙和季节变化而调整。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was essentially camp self-rule, with households organizing hunting and resource use under advice from leaders and elders. There were no fixed borders, and activity zones shifted with sea ice, animal migration, and the seasons.

legalSystem.zh: 法律为习惯法，重点在分享义务、婚姻规则、禁忌和冲突调停。严重争端多通过赔偿、分离营地或仪式性和解处理。
legalSystem.en: Law was customary, focusing on sharing obligations, marriage rules, taboos, and conflict mediation. Serious disputes were resolved through compensation, camp separation, or ritual reconciliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税收，首领获得的资源主要来自猎获分配优先权、礼物和集体协助。公共事务依赖志愿劳作与季节性共享储备。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; leaders received resources mainly through priority shares in catches, gifts, and collective support. Public functions depended on voluntary labor and seasonally shared stores.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 在严寒海岸环境中发展出高效的海兽捕猎、骨角器制作、皮舟与雪屋或半地穴居住技术。口述史、仪式歌曲和猎礼规范构成其核心文化成就。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed efficient sea-mammal hunting, bone and antler toolmaking, skin boats, and snow-house or semi-subterranean dwelling techniques suited to severe coastal conditions. Oral history, ritual songs, and hunting codes formed central cultural achievements.

languageFamily.zh: 古爱斯基摩—阿留申语系相关方言群
languageFamily.en: Dialects related to the ancient Eskimo-Aleut language family

philosophy.zh: 世界观以万物有灵、猎物灵魂平衡和人与海冰环境的相互敬畏为核心。
philosophy.en: The worldview centered on animistic-spiritual balance, the souls of prey animals, and reverent reciprocity between humans and the sea-ice environment.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣、神话和技艺示范代代相传。
writingSystem.en: There was no writing; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, myth, and practical demonstration.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济高度依赖海兽捕猎、渔业与季节性采集，市场化程度极低。物质价值主要体现为食物安全、皮毛、骨角器与迁徙装备，而非金属财富。
gdpDescription.en: The economy depended heavily on marine hunting, fishing, and seasonal gathering, with extremely low marketization. Material value lay mainly in food security, hides, bone tools, and mobility equipment rather than metal wealth.

mainIndustries.zh: 海豹与鲸类捕猎、渔业、骨角器制作、皮革加工、雪橇与皮舟制作
mainIndustries.en: Seal and whale hunting, fishing, bone-tool production, hide processing, sled and skin-boat making

tradeGoods.zh: 海兽皮、鲸脂、骨角器、燧石、漂木制品、装饰品
tradeGoods.en: Marine mammal hides, whale oil, bone tools, flint, driftwood goods, ornaments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富取决于舟具、雪橇、骨角器、皮帐和食物储备。拥有更多猎具和亲族协作网络的家庭在冬季更具安全性。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth depended on boats, sleds, bone tools, skin tents, and food stores. Families with better hunting equipment and stronger kin support networks were more secure in winter.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系以家庭生产、营地共享和礼物交换为主，只有有限的邻近群体物资互换。资源分配更多受生存伦理而非利润动机支配。
economicSystem.en: The economic system rested on household production, camp sharing, and gift exchange, with only limited barter between neighboring groups. Resource allocation was governed more by survival ethics than by profit motives.

category.en: Communal hunting gear and boat maintenance

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备主要表现为干肉、脂油、皮张和备用工具，并由多个营地分散保存。其规模很小，主要用于度过灾年或狩猎失败季节。
treasuryDescription.en: Communal reserves consisted mainly of dried meat, oil, hides, and spare tools, stored across several camps. They were very small and intended chiefly for years of scarcity or failed hunts.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践实为生存性再分配，强调共享储备和在危机中优先救助弱者。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice was essentially survival redistribution, emphasizing shared stores and priority aid to vulnerable members during crises.

description.zh: 由最有经验的成年猎手组成，平时从事海猎，冲突时承担防御和报复行动。
description.en: Made up of the most experienced adult hunters, engaged in marine hunting in normal times and defense or retaliatory action in conflict.

description.zh: 由其他成年男性和部分女性后勤成员组成，负责警戒、运输和支援。
description.en: Composed of other adults, with some women in logistical roles, responsible for watch, transport, and support.

commanderInChief.zh: 资深猎首或营地联合首领
commanderInChief.en: Senior hunt leader or combined camp head

technology.zh: 武备与狩猎工具高度重合，以骨角矛头、石刃、弓箭、投矛器和小舟机动为主。冲突规模通常很小，缺乏重装与攻城能力。
technology.en: Weaponry largely overlapped with hunting tools, centering on bone and antler spearheads, stone blades, bows, throwing gear, and small-boat mobility. Conflicts were generally very small in scale, with no heavy equipment or siege ability.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自食物短缺、气候剧变、海冰异常和局部群体冲突，而非大规模战争。环境危机往往比敌对人群更致命。
threats.en: Main threats came from food shortage, abrupt climate shifts, unstable sea ice, and localized group conflict rather than large-scale war. Environmental crisis was often deadlier than hostile human groups.

populationDescription.zh: 人口极为稀疏，分散于北极海岸、河口与海冰边缘地带，以季节性迁徙营地为主。高婴幼儿死亡率与环境风险限制了人口增长。
populationDescription.en: Population was extremely sparse, scattered across Arctic coasts, river mouths, and sea-ice margins, with seasonal camps predominating. High infant mortality and environmental risk constrained growth.

socialClasses.zh: 首领与资深猎手、萨满与长者、普通家庭成员、依附亲属与孤弱者。
socialClasses.en: Leaders and senior hunters, shamans and elders, ordinary household members, dependent kin, and vulnerable individuals.

allies.zh: 盟友主要是通婚和共享猎场的邻近营地群体。
allies.en: Allies were mainly neighboring camp groups linked by marriage and shared hunting grounds.

enemies.zh: 敌对者通常只是偶发性争夺资源和报复冲突的邻近群体，并无固定宿敌国家。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually neighboring groups involved in occasional resource disputes and retaliatory clashes, not permanent rival states.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以避免冲突、维持通婚和必要物资互换为主，在资源压力上升时可能发生短期敌对。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations aimed mainly at avoiding conflict, preserving marriage ties, and exchanging needed goods, though short-term hostility could arise under resource stress.

era.zh: 北极海猎石骨器时代晚期
era.en: Late Arctic marine hunting stone-and-bone age

keyInnovations.zh: 皮舟与雪橇、骨角复合工具、脂油照明、海冰导航经验、半地穴保温居所。
keyInnovations.en: Skin boats and sleds, composite bone-antler tools, oil lighting, sea-ice navigation knowledge, and semi-subterranean insulated dwellings.

strengths.zh: 对极端环境有卓越适应力，社会共享机制强，迁徙灵活。海猎知识和工具体系使其能够长期在严寒地区生存。
strengths.en: They had exceptional adaptation to extreme environments, strong sharing institutions, and high mobility. Their marine hunting knowledge and tool systems enabled long-term survival in severe cold.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、物资储备薄弱、技术冗余有限，一旦连续狩猎失败便易陷入危机。政治整合能力很低，无法形成大规模军事或行政组织。
weaknesses.en: Population was sparse, reserves were thin, and technological redundancy was limited, making repeated hunting failure dangerous. Political integration was very low, preventing large-scale military or administrative organization.

outlook.zh: 只要生态条件允许，这类社会可长期稳定延续；但任何持续气候异常都可能迫使迁徙、分裂或人口下降。
outlook.en: Such societies could remain stable for long periods if ecological conditions held, but prolonged climatic anomalies could force migration, fragmentation, or population decline.

description.zh: 北极海猎民是以海兽捕猎、共享储备和萨满信仰维系的高流动性小规模部落社会。
description.en: The Arctic marine hunters were highly mobile small-scale tribal societies sustained by sea-mammal hunting, shared reserves, and shamanic belief.

dynasty.zh: 加勒比诸岛前陶与早期陶器部落群
dynasty.en: Pre-Ceramic and Early Ceramic Tribal Groups of the Caribbean

governmentForm.zh: 以岛屿聚落和亲族网络为核心的松散酋长制或部落制，由地方酋长主持分配与仪式。
governmentForm.en: A loose island-based chiefdom or tribal system centered on settlements and kin networks, with local chiefs presiding over distribution and ritual.

socialStructure.zh: 社会组织以岛上村落、扩大家族和海上联系网络为主，规模普遍较小。酋长、长者和祭仪主持者拥有较高威望，普通成员从事捕鱼、采集、园圃种植和航海。部分岛屿已出现较初步的等级差异，但整体仍较平缓。
socialStructure.en: Social organization was based on island villages, extended families, and maritime contact networks, generally at a small scale. Chiefs, elders, and ritual leaders enjoyed higher prestige, while ordinary members engaged in fishing, gathering, gardening, and seafaring. Some islands show early status differences, but overall hierarchy remained mild.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层主要由岛上酋长家族、资深航海者和宗教权威构成。其权力依赖亲族纽带、对外交换能力和祭祀声望。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted mainly of island chiefly families, experienced navigators, and ritual authorities. Their power depended on kin ties, external exchange ability, and ceremonial prestige.

succession.zh: 继承多在显赫家族内部进行，可由长子、兄弟或经长者支持的近亲接替。若岛上联盟松散，强势婚盟也能决定首领更替。
succession.en: Succession generally occurred within prominent families, with sons, brothers, or close kin backed by elders succeeding. Where island alliances were loose, strong marriage ties could also shape leadership transitions.

structure.zh: 政治结构以单个岛屿或数个相邻聚落为单位，酋长在长者协助下管理公共劳动、航海联系和仪式事务。没有跨加勒比范围的统一政权，地区关系高度分散。
structure.en: Political structure operated at the level of a single island or a few adjacent settlements, with chiefs and elders managing communal labor, maritime links, and ritual affairs. There was no unified polity across the Caribbean, and regional relations were highly fragmented.

function.zh: 组织渔猎、独木舟航行、岛际联络和公共工程。
function.en: Organized fishing, canoe travel, inter-island communication, and communal works.

function.zh: 主持祖灵或自然灵仪式，并管理干鱼、木薯和贝壳物资储备。
function.en: Oversaw ancestral or nature-spirit rituals and managed stores of dried fish, cassava, and shell goods.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由各岛或各村落酋长自行处理，依靠长者会议与亲族纽带维持秩序。岛际影响力主要体现在交换网络和婚姻联盟，而非直接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled separately by each island or village chief, using councils of elders and kin ties to maintain order. Inter-island influence was expressed mainly through exchange networks and marriage alliances rather than direct rule.

legalSystem.zh: 法律为口传习惯法，重视婚姻、渔场使用、财物分享和伤害赔偿。严重违背群体规范者可能遭受放逐、赔偿或仪式性惩戒。
legalSystem.en: Law was oral customary law, emphasizing marriage, use of fishing grounds, sharing of goods, and compensation for injury. Serious violators of group norms might face exile, restitution, or ritual sanction.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税制，酋长通过礼物、劳动贡献、渔获分配与节庆献纳获得资源。灾后或大型航海活动前，聚落会集中征集食物和木材。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; chiefs obtained resources through gifts, labor contributions, fish-share allocations, and festival offerings. After disasters or before major voyages, settlements pooled food and timber.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出适应群岛环境的独木舟航海、渔猎工具、贝壳工艺和早期园圃种植传统。岛际交流促成了器物风格、装饰品和仪式形式的传播。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed dugout canoe navigation, fishing gear, shell craftwork, and early horticultural traditions adapted to island environments. Inter-island contacts spread artifact styles, ornaments, and ritual forms.

languageFamily.zh: 可能包括阿拉瓦克语系前驱方言及其他早期加勒比地方语言
languageFamily.en: Likely included precursor Arawakan dialects and other early local Caribbean languages

philosophy.zh: 以自然灵、祖灵敬畏和海洋导航经验相结合的宇宙观为主，强调群体分享与季节节律。
philosophy.en: The worldview combined reverence for nature spirits, ancestors, and maritime navigation knowledge, with emphasis on communal sharing and seasonal rhythms.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，主要通过口述传说、仪式歌曲、航海记忆和器物风格传承知识。
writingSystem.en: There was no writing; knowledge was preserved through oral tales, ritual songs, navigational memory, and artifact styles.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以捕鱼、贝类采集、狩猎和小规模园圃农业为基础，辅以岛际交换。总体剩余有限，但海洋资源使部分聚落较为稳定。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on fishing, shellfish gathering, hunting, and small-scale horticulture, supplemented by inter-island exchange. Overall surplus was limited, but marine resources made some settlements relatively stable.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、贝类采集、木薯与园圃种植、独木舟制造、贝壳和石器加工
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, shellfish gathering, cassava and garden cultivation, canoe building, shell and stone craft production

tradeGoods.zh: 贝壳饰品、石器、鱼干、木薯制品、羽饰、独木舟木材、树脂
tradeGoods.en: Shell ornaments, stone tools, dried fish, cassava products, feather ornaments, canoe timber, resin

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为独木舟、渔网、园圃产出、储存食物和装饰品。掌握远航技能或稀有原料来源的家庭通常地位更高。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed through canoes, nets, garden output, food stores, and ornaments. Families controlling long-distance voyaging skills or rare raw materials usually held higher status.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系以村落自给、亲族共享与岛际礼物交换并存，部分岛屿已出现较稳定的海上交换航线。生产以维持生计和社会关系为主，而非追求积累。
economicSystem.en: The economic system combined village self-sufficiency, kin sharing, and inter-island gift exchange, with some islands already participating in regular maritime exchange routes. Production aimed mainly at subsistence and social relations rather than accumulation.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备主要以鱼干、木薯饼、贝壳饰物、木材和独木舟部件形式存在，分散保存在岛上核心聚落。其作用在于支持节庆、航海和飓风后的恢复。
treasuryDescription.en: Communal reserves existed mainly as dried fish, cassava bread, shell ornaments, timber, and canoe parts, dispersed among island core settlements. They supported festivals, voyaging, and recovery after storms.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践重在维持公共航海、祭祀活动和灾后再分配，依赖礼物经济而非固定税额征收。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining communal voyaging, ritual life, and post-disaster redistribution, relying on gift economy rather than fixed taxes.

description.zh: 由酋长近亲和精干战士组成，负责护卫、航海护送和突袭。
description.en: Composed of the chief's close kin and able fighters, responsible for protection, escort on voyages, and raiding.

description.zh: 由各聚落成年成员临时动员，使用木矛、棍棒、石器和弓箭进行防御。
description.en: Temporarily mobilized from settlement adults, using wooden spears, clubs, stone weapons, and bows for defense.

commanderInChief.zh: 主岛酋长或战时航海首领
commanderInChief.en: Principal island chief or wartime voyaging leader

technology.zh: 军事技术较简单，以独木舟机动、近战武器和小规模伏击为主。作战多为岛际报复、俘获和资源争夺，缺乏持久围攻能力。
technology.en: Military technology was simple, relying on canoe mobility, close-combat weapons, and small-scale ambush. Fighting usually involved inter-island retaliation, captive-taking, and resource disputes, without sustained siege capacity.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻岛冲突、飓风灾害、渔场波动和食物储备不足。自然灾害在许多年份比战争更具破坏性。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring island conflicts, hurricanes, fluctuating fishing grounds, and insufficient food reserves. Natural disasters were more destructive than warfare in many years.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于多个岛屿海岸和河口聚落，绝大多数为小村社，依海洋资源生活。人口密度总体偏低，但资源较丰富岛屿能维持较稳定聚居。
populationDescription.en: Population was dispersed among coastal and estuarine settlements across multiple islands, mostly in small villages dependent on marine resources. Overall density was low, though richer islands could sustain more stable habitation.

name.zh: 小安的列斯独木舟港聚落

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族与航海精英、祭仪主持者与长者、普通渔猎与园圃家庭、依附者与战俘。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families and navigation elites, ritual leaders and elders, ordinary fishing and gardening households, dependents, and captives.

allies.zh: 盟友多为通婚和经常交换器物、食物与航海信息的邻近岛屿群体。
allies.en: Allies were mostly neighboring island groups linked by marriage and regular exchange of goods, food, and voyaging knowledge.

enemies.zh: 敌对者通常是争夺渔场、登陆点和声望资源的邻岛部落。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually neighboring island tribes competing over fishing grounds, landing sites, and prestige resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持航路、交换婚盟和控制关键登陆点为主，必要时通过小规模袭击恢复威望。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining sea routes, exchanging marriage alliances, and controlling key landing points, with small raids used when necessary to restore prestige.

era.zh: 加勒比前国家阶段的海洋部落时代
era.en: Maritime tribal age of the pre-state Caribbean

keyInnovations.zh: 独木舟航海、近海渔猎工具、木薯加工、贝壳工艺、岛际导航知识。
keyInnovations.en: Dugout canoe voyaging, coastal fishing gear, cassava processing, shell craftwork, and inter-island navigation knowledge.

strengths.zh: 海洋适应力强，岛际联系灵活，能够利用多样海岸资源。小规模社会结构在平时具有较高韧性和机动性。
strengths.en: They were strongly adapted to maritime life, with flexible inter-island links and effective use of diverse coastal resources. Their small-scale social structure gave them resilience and mobility in normal conditions.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度低，储备能力有限，极易受飓风、海况变化和局部冲突破坏。缺乏文字与大型组织，也限制了长期集中动员。
weaknesses.en: Political integration was low and reserve capacity limited, making them vulnerable to hurricanes, changing sea conditions, and local conflict. The absence of writing and large institutions also limited sustained centralized mobilization.

outlook.zh: 随着园圃农业和岛际交换加强，部分岛屿未来可能发展出更稳定的酋邦与更明显的社会分层。
outlook.en: As horticulture and inter-island exchange intensified, some islands were likely to develop more stable chiefdoms and clearer social stratification.

description.zh: 加勒比采集者是依托海洋资源、独木舟航海与亲族交换网络维系的群岛部落社会。
description.en: The Caribbean hunter-gatherers were archipelagic tribal societies sustained by marine resources, dugout canoe voyaging, and kin-based exchange networks.

dynasty.zh: 乔雷拉文化晚期酋邦网络

governmentForm.zh: 由多个沿海与河谷聚落组成的松散酋邦体系，权力集中于地方酋长与祭仪领袖。
governmentForm.en: A loose chiefdom system of coastal and riverine settlements, with authority centered on local chiefs and ritual leaders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、村落与区域性交换网络为基础，上层为酋长家族、祭司与工艺精英，中层为农民、渔民与手工业者，下层为附属家庭与俘获者。社会分化已经出现，但尚未形成完备的国家官僚阶层。仪式宴饮与奢侈品分配是维系等级秩序的重要方式。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on kinship, village communities, and regional exchange networks. At the top stood chiefly families, ritual specialists, and craft elites; below them were farmers, fishers, and artisans, with dependent households and captives at the bottom. Social differentiation was evident, but a full state bureaucracy had not emerged. Ritual feasting and the redistribution of prestige goods were key means of maintaining hierarchy.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由地方酋长家族及掌握祭祀权威的宗教人物构成。其权力来自血缘声望、财富再分配能力与对远距贸易品的控制。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted mainly of local chiefly lineages and religious figures who controlled ritual authority. Their power derived from genealogical prestige, the ability to redistribute wealth, and control over long-distance exchange goods.

succession.zh: 继承通常在显赫家族内部进行，多为世袭并辅以长老认可。若缺乏明确继承人，常由最有威望的亲族支系或婚姻联盟推举新酋长。
succession.en: Succession usually took place within prominent families, generally hereditary but validated by elders. If no clear heir existed, a new chief was often chosen from the most prestigious kin branch or marriage alliance.

structure.zh: 乔雷拉并无统一中央政权，而是由若干区域酋邦组成，各聚落在礼仪与贸易上互相联系。酋长主持分配、祭祀与纠纷调停，实际管理依赖家族首领和聚落长老。大型仪式中心在政治上具有号召力，但不构成固定首都。
structure.en: Chorrera had no unified central state, but rather several regional chiefdoms linked through ritual and trade. Chiefs oversaw redistribution, ceremonies, and dispute mediation, while practical administration depended on lineage heads and settlement elders. Major ceremonial centers carried political influence but did not function as a fixed capital.

function.zh: 管理贡纳、储藏、宴饮分配与劳役组织。
function.en: Managed tribute, storage, feast redistribution, and labor organization.

function.zh: 负责祭祀活动、声望物品流通及跨区域贸易联络。
function.en: Handled ceremonial activities, prestige-goods circulation, and interregional trade contacts.

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理以村落为单位，由家族长者和聚落首领共同处理日常事务。河口、海岸与内陆节点聚落拥有较强自治性，但会在祭祀和交换季节服从较大酋邦中心协调。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated at the village level, where lineage elders and settlement heads handled daily affairs. Estuarine, coastal, and inland nodal settlements retained strong autonomy, but during ritual and exchange seasons they followed coordination from larger chiefdom centers.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要表现为习惯法，由长老会议与酋长裁决。纠纷多通过赔偿、婚姻安排、仪式和解或驱逐来解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, with decisions rendered by councils of elders and chiefs. Disputes were usually settled through compensation, marriage arrangements, ritual reconciliation, or expulsion.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在固定货币税制，酋长主要通过贡纳、季节性劳役和仪式供品获取资源。税负常以玉米、木薯制品、渔获、陶器和劳动力形式体现。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed monetary tax system; chiefs obtained resources mainly through tribute, seasonal labor obligations, and ritual offerings. Burdens were paid in maize, manioc products, fish, pottery, and labor.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 乔雷拉文化以精美抛光陶器、笛哨形陶瓶和区域风格统一的工艺传统著称。其工艺与交换网络对后续厄瓜多尔沿海文化具有深远影响。
culturalAchievements.en: The Chorrera culture was known for finely polished ceramics, whistle bottles, and a regionally coherent craft tradition. Its craftsmanship and exchange networks had lasting influence on later cultures of the Ecuadorian coast.

languageFamily.zh: 语言未有定论，可能属于已消失的前安第斯沿海语群。
languageFamily.en: Language affiliation is uncertain, possibly belonging to an extinct pre-Andean coastal language group.

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰、祖灵崇拜与自然循环观念为核心，重视水域、动物与祖先之间的神圣联系。
philosophy.en: Centered on animistic beliefs, ancestral veneration, and cyclical views of nature, with strong emphasis on sacred links among waterways, animals, and ancestors.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，信息主要通过口述传统、图像装饰和仪式记忆传承。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; information was transmitted through oral tradition, decorative imagery, and ritual memory.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以混合农业、渔猎和手工业为基础，市场化程度有限，但区域交换较活跃。财富更多体现在食物储备、声望物品与劳动力动员能力上，而非货币积累。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on mixed farming, fishing, hunting, and craft production, with limited marketization but active regional exchange. Wealth was expressed more in food reserves, prestige goods, and the ability to mobilize labor than in monetary accumulation.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、渔业、陶器制造、贝壳与石器工艺、区域交换
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, fishing, pottery production, shell and stone craftwork, regional exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 陶器、贝壳饰品、石珠、可可、干鱼、玉米、棉织品与热带林产
tradeGoods.en: Ceramics, shell ornaments, stone beads, cacao, dried fish, maize, cotton textiles, and tropical forest products

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要表现为耕地使用权、独木舟、渔具、陶器和储粮。精英家庭则拥有更好的装饰品、宴饮器物和更广的交换关系。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of access to fields, canoes, fishing gear, pottery, and stored food. Elite households possessed finer ornaments, feasting vessels, and broader exchange ties.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系属于酋邦式再分配经济，辅以村落自给生产和跨区域物物交换。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a chiefly redistributive economy, supplemented by village subsistence production and interregional barter.

source.zh: 贸易中转与声望物品控制

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要以储粮、陶器、贝饰和少量贵重材料形式存在，集中于酋长住宅与祭仪仓储点。其功能偏向礼物分配与灾荒缓冲，而非抽象财政储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” existed mainly as stored food, pottery, shell ornaments, and small amounts of valuable materials kept in chiefly residences and ritual storehouses. Its function was more redistributional and famine-buffering than an abstract fiscal reserve.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持酋长威望和社会整合为核心，强调通过再分配换取忠诚。丰年扩张宴饮与交换，歉年则优先保障核心聚落与亲族网络。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on maintaining chiefly prestige and social cohesion, emphasizing loyalty through redistribution. In good years feasting and exchange expanded; in poor years priority went to core settlements and kin networks.

description.zh: 由酋长直接召集的常备护卫与精干战士，负责护送、威慑和小规模冲突。
description.en: A core of guards and capable fighters directly mobilized by chiefs for escort, deterrence, and small-scale conflict.

description.zh: 战时由各聚落成年男子临时集结，装备以木矛、石制武器和投掷器具为主。
description.en: In wartime, adult men from each settlement were temporarily assembled, armed chiefly with wooden spears, stone weapons, and throwing implements.

commanderInChief.zh: 区域酋长
commanderInChief.en: Regional Chief

technology.zh: 军事技术较为基础，依赖木矛、石刃、棍棒、护具和地形熟悉度，缺乏专业攻城与大规模防御工事。
technology.en: Military technology was basic, relying on wooden spears, stone blades, clubs, simple protection, and knowledge of terrain, with no specialized siege methods or large defensive works.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近酋邦的资源竞争、河口航道控制与报复性袭击。环境波动和粮食短缺也可能迅速转化为部落冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from competition with neighboring chiefdoms over resources, control of estuarine routes, and retaliatory raiding. Environmental fluctuation and food shortages could also quickly turn into tribal conflict.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约四万，多分布于厄瓜多尔沿海河口、低地和若干内陆通道聚落。人口密度不高，但在资源丰富的海岸与河谷地区形成较稳定聚居。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 40,000, spread across estuaries, lowlands, and some inland corridor settlements on the Ecuadorian coast. Population density was modest, but relatively stable concentrations formed in resource-rich coastal and riverine zones.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长与祭司精英、工艺与交换专门者、普通农渔家庭、附属者与俘获者
socialClasses.en: Chiefly and priestly elites, craft and exchange specialists, ordinary farming-fishing households, dependents, and captives

allies.zh: 与邻近沿海和河谷聚落存在婚姻联盟、礼物交换和阶段性合作关系，无固定国家级盟友。
allies.en: Maintained marriage alliances, gift exchange, and occasional cooperation with neighboring coastal and riverine settlements, without permanent state-level allies.

enemies.zh: 无长期固定宿敌，但常与相邻酋邦在贸易通道、渔场和耕地上发生摩擦。
enemies.en: Had no permanent hereditary enemy, but often clashed with neighboring chiefdoms over trade routes, fishing grounds, and cultivable land.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制交换网络、维持婚姻联盟和避免破坏性长期战争为主。通过礼物与仪式联系扩大影响力，胜于直接领土吞并。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling exchange networks, maintaining marriage alliances, and avoiding destructive prolonged wars. Influence was expanded more through gifts and ritual ties than direct territorial conquest.

era.en: Late Formative Period of South America

keyInnovations.zh: 高水平抛光陶器、哨音瓶、沿海与内陆复合生计、独木舟交通与区域工艺传播。
keyInnovations.en: High-quality polished ceramics, whistle bottles, combined coastal-inland subsistence, canoe transport, and regional diffusion of craft styles.

strengths.zh: 工艺精湛、交换网络广、海岸与河谷资源多样，社会整合依赖有效的礼仪再分配机制。
strengths.en: Strong craftsmanship, broad exchange networks, and diverse coastal-riverine resources, with social cohesion supported by effective ritual redistribution.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度有限，缺少稳定中央政权与专业军队，对环境变化和区域冲突较为脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Political integration was limited, lacking a stable central authority and professional army, making it vulnerable to environmental shifts and regional conflict.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍可维持稳定的区域酋邦体系，但未来可能分化为更具地方特色的后继文化。其工艺与交换传统将继续影响厄瓜多尔沿海社会。
outlook.en: In the short term it could maintain a stable regional chiefdom system, though it would likely fragment into more localized successor cultures. Its craft and exchange traditions would continue to shape coastal Ecuadorian societies.

description.zh: 乔雷拉文化是厄瓜多尔沿海以精美陶器、礼仪交换和松散酋邦组织为特征的形成期社会。
description.en: The Chorrera culture was a Formative-era society of coastal Ecuador marked by fine ceramics, ritual exchange, and loosely organized chiefdoms.

dynasty.zh: 古因纽特晚期狩猎群体

governmentForm.zh: 由季节性迁徙的狩猎群与若干亲族营地构成的部落式松散组织，缺乏固定中央权力。
governmentForm.en: A loosely organized tribal system of seasonally mobile hunting bands and kin-based camps, lacking fixed central authority.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以小型亲族群体为核心，成员多按家庭和合作狩猎需要组成营地。地位差异有限，优秀猎手、年长者和萨满拥有较高声望，但其权力主要建立在劝导和技能上。资源分享与互助是维持群体生存的关键规范。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on small kin groups, with camps formed around households and cooperative hunting needs. Status differences were limited; skilled hunters, elders, and shamans held prestige, but their authority rested mainly on persuasion and ability. Sharing resources and mutual aid were essential norms for survival.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在固定意义上的统治阶级，较有影响力者为资深猎手、长者与萨满。其地位并非绝对，若无法提供食物、经验或精神引导，影响力会迅速下降。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed ruling class; the most influential figures were experienced hunters, elders, and shamans. Their status was not absolute, and influence could quickly decline if they failed to provide food, judgment, or spiritual guidance.

succession.zh: 领导权不严格世袭，通常由最有经验、最善狩猎或最受敬重者在具体情境中担任首领。家庭内部的声望传承存在，但必须得到群体认可。
succession.en: Leadership was not strictly hereditary; it usually fell in specific situations to the most experienced, skilled, or respected person. Prestige could pass within families, but only with group acceptance.

structure.zh: 古因纽特社会没有国家机构，治理主要依赖营地协商、长者建议与萨满权威。不同狩猎群在季节性交会时会交换信息、安排婚配并调解争端。政治组织高度分散，适应北极环境中的高流动性生计模式。
structure.en: Paleo-Inuit society lacked state institutions; governance relied mainly on camp consensus, elder advice, and shamanic authority. During seasonal gatherings, different hunting bands exchanged information, arranged marriages, and mediated disputes. Political organization was highly decentralized, fitting a mobile Arctic way of life.

function.zh: 协调猎物分享、迁徙路线和季节性营地安排。
function.en: Coordinated food sharing, migration routes, and seasonal camp organization.

function.zh: 主持祭仪、处理禁忌问题并调解营地内外纠纷。
function.en: Conducted rituals, handled taboo matters, and mediated disputes within and between camps.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即营地治理，由一至数个家庭联合决定日常事项。冬夏营地规模变化较大，权威也随狩猎季节和资源条件灵活调整。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was simply camp administration, with one or several families jointly deciding daily matters. Winter and summer camps varied considerably in size, and authority shifted flexibly with hunting seasons and resource conditions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体现为习俗、禁忌与群体共识，重点在于资源分享、婚姻规则和避免破坏集体生存的行为。严重冲突多通过公开指责、分营、赔偿或驱逐解决。
legalSystem.en: Law took the form of custom, taboo, and group consensus, focusing on food sharing, marriage rules, and preventing behavior harmful to collective survival. Serious conflicts were usually resolved through public reproach, camp separation, compensation, or expulsion.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，领导者获得的“收入”主要来自猎物分配中的尊荣份额、礼物与互惠支持。公共事务依靠自愿协作和生存义务，而非制度化征税。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; leaders' “income” came mainly from honor shares in game distribution, gifts, and reciprocal support. Collective needs were met through voluntary cooperation and survival obligations rather than institutionalized taxes.

religion.zh: 萨满教
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 古因纽特发展出适应极地环境的精细石器与骨角器传统，并形成高效的季节性迁徙生存模式。其居住、狩猎与精神实践为后来北极人群奠定了重要文化基础。
culturalAchievements.en: The Paleo-Inuit developed finely adapted stone, bone, and antler tool traditions and an efficient seasonally mobile survival system for Arctic conditions. Their settlement, hunting, and spiritual practices laid important cultural foundations for later Arctic peoples.

languageFamily.zh: 语言未详，可能属于已消失的古北极语言群，未必与后来的因纽特-阿留申语系相同。
languageFamily.en: Language is uncertain, possibly belonging to an extinct Paleo-Arctic language group and not necessarily identical with the later Inuit-Aleut family.

philosophy.zh: 以萨满信仰、万物有灵和人与动物灵体互惠关系为核心，强调遵守狩猎禁忌与尊重环境。
philosophy.en: Centered on shamanism, animistic belief, and reciprocal relations between humans and animal spirits, emphasizing hunting taboos and respect for the environment.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，知识通过口述叙事、狩猎示范和仪式传统传递。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral narratives, hunting demonstration, and ritual tradition.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济几乎完全建立在狩猎、渔捞、采集和家庭加工之上，剩余极少。财富不以积累为主，而体现为工具质量、皮毛储备、亲族互助和对资源地的熟悉程度。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested almost entirely on hunting, fishing, gathering, and household processing, with very little surplus. Wealth was not primarily accumulative, but expressed in tool quality, skin reserves, kin support, and knowledge of resource zones.

mainIndustries.zh: 海兽狩猎、捕鱼、驯鹿及小型动物猎捕、皮毛加工、骨角器制造
mainIndustries.en: Marine mammal hunting, fishing, caribou and small-game hunting, skin processing, bone and antler toolmaking

tradeGoods.zh: 皮毛、骨角器、石料半成品、海象牙、脂油与少量装饰品
tradeGoods.en: Furs, bone and antler tools, worked stone blanks, walrus ivory, blubber, and small ornaments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富体现在皮衣、雪屋或帐居装备、猎具、犬力有限使用资源以及保存的肉脂和皮毛。较富裕家庭通常拥有更好的狩猎器具和更广的人际互助网络。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was embodied in clothing, snowhouse or tent equipment, hunting gear, limited dog-power resources, and stored meat, fat, and skins. Better-off households usually possessed superior hunting tools and wider networks of mutual support.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系属于以家庭自给和营地共享为核心的采猎经济，并辅以低强度的邻近群体交换。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a foraging economy centered on household subsistence and camp sharing, supplemented by low-intensity exchange with neighboring groups.

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无固定国库，公共储备主要表现为营地共同保留的皮毛、脂油、干肉和备用工具。其规模极小，主要用于渡过恶劣季节和应对狩猎失败。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no fixed treasury; communal reserves consisted mainly of stored skins, fat, dried meat, and spare tools held by camps. These reserves were very small and intended chiefly to survive harsh seasons and hunting failure.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实践本质上是生存性再分配，优先保障寡弱者和关键猎手。资源充裕时加强礼物往来，资源紧张时压缩仪式支出并分散迁徙风险。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice was essentially survival-oriented redistribution, prioritizing the vulnerable and key hunters. In times of abundance gift exchange expanded; in scarcity ritual outlays were cut and migration risk was dispersed.

description.zh: 由最熟练的成年猎手组成，平时用于大型猎捕，冲突时承担前锋与防卫任务。
description.en: Composed of the most skilled adult hunters, used in normal times for major hunts and in conflict as the main defensive and assault element.

description.zh: 由其他可作战成年人临时集结，主要保卫营地、器具和食物储备。
description.en: A temporary body of other able adults assembled mainly to defend camps, equipment, and food stores.

commanderInChief.zh: 资深猎队首领
commanderInChief.en: Senior Hunting Leader

technology.zh: 军事技术与狩猎技术高度重合，使用石刃矛头、骨角鱼叉、弓矢、刀具和简易护身装具。作战规模很小，侧重伏击、追踪和营地防御。
technology.en: Military technology overlapped heavily with hunting technology, using stone-tipped spears, bone and antler harpoons, bows, knives, and simple protective gear. Warfare was small in scale and emphasized ambush, tracking, and camp defense.

threats.zh: 主要威胁不是大规模战争，而是资源竞争、局部营地冲突、严寒、海冰变化和猎物波动。环境风险常比人际敌对更致命。
threats.en: The main threats were not large-scale war but resource competition, local camp conflicts, severe cold, shifting sea ice, and prey fluctuation. Environmental risks were often deadlier than human hostility.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约五千，分散于北极与亚北极海岸、岛屿及内陆边缘营地。绝大多数人口生活在小型季节性聚落中，常随海兽、鱼群和驯鹿路线迁移。
populationDescription.en: The total population was around 5,000, dispersed across Arctic and sub-Arctic coasts, islands, and inland-edge camps. The great majority lived in small seasonal settlements, moving with marine mammals, fish, and caribou routes.

socialClasses.zh: 无严格阶级；可区分为长者与萨满、优秀猎手、普通家庭成员、孤弱依附者
socialClasses.en: No strict classes; distinctions existed among elders and shamans, skilled hunters, ordinary household members, and vulnerable dependents

allies.zh: 盟友关系主要体现为邻近狩猎群之间的婚姻、共享猎场信息和灾年互助。
allies.en: Alliances mainly took the form of marriages, shared hunting information, and mutual aid between neighboring hunting bands.

enemies.zh: 无固定国家敌人，偶有与相邻群体因猎场、迁徙路线或报复而发生冲突。
enemies.en: There were no fixed state enemies, though occasional conflicts arose with neighboring groups over hunting grounds, migration routes, or revenge.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以避免高成本冲突、维持婚姻联系和确保资源信息流通为主。生存条件决定了合作通常比征服更有价值。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations aimed mainly to avoid costly conflict, maintain marriage ties, and ensure the flow of resource information. Survival conditions made cooperation generally more valuable than conquest.

keyInnovations.zh: 微型石刃技术、骨角复合工具、极地服装与居住技术、季节性海陆资源整合。
keyInnovations.en: Microblade technology, composite bone and antler tools, Arctic clothing and dwelling techniques, and seasonal integration of marine and terrestrial resources.

strengths.zh: 对严酷环境适应力极强，社会组织灵活，资源共享与迁徙知识丰富，能在低人口密度下维持生存。
strengths.en: Highly adapted to harsh environments, socially flexible, rich in sharing practices and migration knowledge, and capable of surviving at very low population densities.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、剩余极低、技术扩散缓慢，对气候波动和猎物减少极其敏感。
weaknesses.en: Population was sparse, surplus minimal, technological diffusion slow, and the society was extremely sensitive to climatic shifts and prey decline.

outlook.zh: 若环境维持可预测，其群体能继续稳定存在；但长期气候与生态变化可能推动人口迁移、文化转型或被后续北极人群取代。
outlook.en: If environmental conditions remained predictable, these communities could persist stably; over the long term, however, climatic and ecological change could drive migration, cultural transformation, or replacement by later Arctic peoples.

description.zh: 古因纽特是北极地区以季节性迁徙、海陆采猎和萨满信仰为核心的小型亲族社会。
description.en: The Paleo-Inuit were small kin-based Arctic societies centered on seasonal mobility, marine-terrestrial foraging, and shamanic belief.

governmentForm.zh: 双王制、革鲁西亚（长老院）、五位监察官（五长老）和公民大会组成的混合寡头制。公民实行严格的军事化集体生活。
governmentForm.en: A mixed oligarchy combining dual kingship, Gerousia (Council of Elders), five Ephors, and citizens' assembly (Apella). Citizens practiced strict militarized communal living.

socialStructure.zh: 斯巴达人（公民战士）享有完全权利但受严格纪律约束，庇里阿西人（边民）从事手工业和贸易，黑劳士（农奴）承担全部农业生产。阶层壁垒极其森严。
socialStructure.en: Spartiates (citizen-warriors) enjoyed full rights under strict discipline; Perioikoi (peripherals) practiced crafts and trade; Helots (serfs) performed all agricultural labor. Class barriers were extremely rigid.

rulingClass.zh: 双王家族、革鲁西亚成员和监察官共同构成最高权力层。
rulingClass.en: The dual royal families, Gerousia members, and Ephors jointly formed the supreme power structure.

succession.zh: 双王位分别在阿基亚德和欧里庞提德家族内世袭。监察官由公民大会年选。
succession.en: Dual thrones were hereditary within the Agiad and Eurypontid families. Ephors were elected annually by the citizens' assembly.

structure.zh: 斯巴达政治体制由双王、革鲁西亚（28名年逾六十的长老加两王）、五名监察官和公民大会组成。监察官此时已拥有强大实权，可制约王权。
structure.en: The Spartan polity consisted of dual kings, the Gerousia (28 elders over sixty plus the two kings), five Ephors, and the citizens' assembly. By this time Ephors wielded great real power and could check royal authority.

function.zh: 监督王权、主持外交、管理司法和公共秩序。
function.en: Supervised the kings, conducted diplomacy, managed justice and public order.

function.zh: 管理全部斯巴达公民从七岁到三十岁的军事教育体系。
function.en: Managed the entire military education system for Spartan citizens from age seven to thirty.

function.zh: 管理克里普提亚（秘密警察）和黑劳士安全控制。
function.en: Managed the Krypteia (secret police) and Helot security control.

localAdmin.zh: 拉科尼亚和美塞尼亚由斯巴达直辖，庇里阿西人城镇享有有限自治。伯罗奔尼撒同盟成员国保持内政独立。
localAdmin.en: Laconia and Messenia were directly ruled by Sparta; Perioikoi towns had limited autonomy. Peloponnesian League members retained internal independence.

legalSystem.zh: 基于吕库古大律法的习惯法体系，监察官和革鲁西亚共同行使司法权。
legalSystem.en: A customary-law system based on the Great Rhetra of Lycurgus, with judicial power shared between Ephors and Gerousia.

taxationSystem.zh: 黑劳士缴纳固定份额贡赋，公民缴纳公共餐（西西提亚）份额。不缴纳者丧失公民资格。
taxationSystem.en: Helots paid fixed tribute shares; citizens contributed syssitia (public mess) dues. Non-payment meant loss of citizenship.

religion.zh: 多神信仰（奥林匹斯诸神）
religion.en: Polytheism (Olympian gods)

culturalAchievements.zh: 斯巴达以阿果盖教育制度、重装步兵方阵战术和严格的集体生活方式著称。其军事纪律被全希腊敬畏。温泉关之战（即将到来）将使斯巴达名垂千古。
culturalAchievements.en: Sparta was renowned for its agoge education system, hoplite phalanx tactics, and austere communal lifestyle. Its military discipline was revered across Greece. The coming Battle of Thermopylae would immortalize Sparta.

languageFamily.zh: 多利安希腊语。
languageFamily.en: Doric Greek.

philosophy.zh: 以集体纪律、勇武荣誉、简朴和牺牲精神为核心的吕库古式理想。
philosophy.en: The Lycurgan ideal centered on collective discipline, martial honor, austerity, and self-sacrifice.

writingSystem.zh: 希腊字母（但文字使用有限，重视口头传统）。
writingSystem.en: Greek alphabet (but limited written culture, emphasizing oral tradition).

gdpDescription.zh: 经济完全依赖黑劳士农业劳动和庇里阿西人手工业。公民不从事任何经济活动。刻意使用铁币棒以阻止财富积累和商业。
gdpDescription.en: The economy depended entirely on Helot agricultural labor and Perioikoi crafts. Citizens engaged in no economic activity. Iron-spit currency was deliberately used to discourage wealth accumulation and commerce.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业（黑劳士耕作）、畜牧、铁器与武器制造（庇里阿西人）
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture (Helot labor), herding, iron and weapon manufacture (by Perioikoi)

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、橄榄油（少量出口）
tradeGoods.en: Ironware, olive oil (limited export)

householdWealth.zh: 公民名义上平等持有克莱罗斯份地，但实际财富差异正在扩大。
householdWealth.en: Citizens nominally held equal kleroi, but actual wealth differences were widening.

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的集体经济，排斥商业和财富积累。
economicSystem.en: A state-directed collective economy hostile to commerce and wealth accumulation.

source.zh: 庇里阿西人手工业与贡赋

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备以谷物和武器为主，刻意避免贵金属积累。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves were mainly grain and weapons, with deliberate avoidance of precious metals.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 维持军事体系和黑劳士控制为绝对优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Maintaining the military system and Helot control was the absolute priority.

description.zh: 经阿果盖训练的职业公民战士，希腊世界最精锐的步兵。
description.en: Agoge-trained professional citizen-warriors, the finest infantry in the Greek world.

description.zh: 边民提供的辅助步兵。
description.en: Auxiliary infantry provided by Perioikoi.

commanderInChief.zh: 双王之一
commanderInChief.en: One of the Dual Kings

technology.zh: 方阵战术已臻完善。铜盾、长矛（多律）和短剑为标准装备。指挥、编制和纪律远超同时代。
technology.en: Phalanx tactics had reached perfection. Bronze shield, long spear (dory), and short sword were standard. Command, organization, and discipline far surpassed contemporaries.

threats.zh: 波斯帝国入侵是首要外部威胁。黑劳士叛乱是永恒内忧。
threats.en: Persian invasion was the primary external threat. Helot revolt was the perennial internal concern.

populationDescription.zh: 约二十万（含黑劳士），其中斯巴达公民约八千至九千户，庇里阿西人数万，黑劳士人数远超公民。
populationDescription.en: About 200,000 (including Helots), with roughly 8,000–9,000 Spartiate households, tens of thousands of Perioikoi, and Helots far outnumbering citizens.

socialClasses.zh: 斯巴达公民、庇里阿西人、黑劳士。
socialClasses.en: Spartiates, Perioikoi, Helots.

allies.zh: 伯罗奔尼撒同盟盟主，盟友包括科林斯、墨伽拉等。
allies.en: Hegemon of the Peloponnesian League; allies included Corinth, Megara, and others.

enemies.zh: 波斯帝国是即将到来的大敌。阿尔戈斯为宿敌。
enemies.en: The Persian Empire was the imminent great enemy. Argos was the perennial rival.

foreignPolicy.zh: 维持伯罗奔尼撒霸权，抵抗波斯扩张，保守但在必要时果断出击。
foreignPolicy.en: Maintain Peloponnesian hegemony, resist Persian expansion, conservative but decisive when necessary.

era.en: Eve of the Greek Classical Period

keyInnovations.zh: 完善的方阵战术、阿果盖军事教育体系、克里普提亚秘密警察制度。
keyInnovations.en: Perfected phalanx tactics, agoge military education, Krypteia secret police system.

strengths.zh: 陆军无敌于希腊，公民纪律和军事训练首屈一指，伯罗奔尼撒同盟提供战略纵深。
strengths.en: Army unmatched in Greece, citizen discipline and training second to none, Peloponnesian League providing strategic depth.

weaknesses.zh: 公民人口持续减少（Oliganthropia），经济封闭，黑劳士随时可能叛乱。
weaknesses.en: Citizen population in steady decline (oliganthropia), economic isolation, and Helots could revolt at any time.

outlook.zh: 波斯战争将使斯巴达威名达到巅峰，但内在矛盾终将导致衰落。
outlook.en: The Persian Wars would bring Sparta's prestige to its zenith, but inherent contradictions would eventually lead to decline.

description.zh: 斯巴达是以双王制、阿果盖训练和重装步兵方阵著称的希腊最强军事城邦。
description.en: Sparta was the strongest military city-state in Greece, renowned for its dual kingship, agoge training, and hoplite phalanx.

dynasty.zh: 克利斯提尼民主改革后

governmentForm.zh: 克利斯提尼改革后的早期民主制——五百人议事会、公民大会和十将军制构成政治核心。
governmentForm.en: An early democracy following Cleisthenes' reforms—the Council of Five Hundred (Boulē), Citizens' Assembly (Ekklesia), and Board of Ten Generals (Strategoi) formed the political core.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由公民（含不同财产等级）、外邦人（梅托伊科斯）和奴隶三大阶层组成。公民权利依据财产分为四个等级，但政治参与权正在扩大。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of citizens (in several property classes), metics (resident foreigners), and slaves. Citizen rights were graded by wealth into four classes, but political participation was expanding.

rulingClass.zh: 旧贵族家族仍具影响力，但克利斯提尼的德莫（区）制度使普通公民获得更多政治权力。
rulingClass.en: Old noble families still held influence, but Cleisthenes' deme system gave ordinary citizens greater political power.

succession.zh: 执政官和将军由选举或抽签产生，一年一届。
succession.en: Archons and generals were chosen by election or lot for one-year terms.

structure.zh: 五百人议事会负责日常政务和立法准备，公民大会拥有最高决策权，十将军负责军事指挥。执政官虽仍存在但权力已大为削弱。陶片放逐法已经确立。
structure.en: The Council of Five Hundred handled daily affairs and legislative preparation; the Citizens' Assembly held supreme decision-making power; the Ten Generals commanded military forces. Archons still existed but with greatly reduced power. Ostracism had been established.

function.zh: 准备议案、管理日常行政和外交事务。
function.en: Prepared legislation, managed daily administration, and handled diplomacy.

function.zh: 统领军事行动和防务规划。
function.en: Commanded military operations and defense planning.

function.zh: 管理公共财政、劳里翁银矿收入和宗教基金。
function.en: Managed public finance, Laurion silver mine revenues, and religious funds.

localAdmin.zh: 阿提卡地区划分为十个部落（弗利），每个部落由若干德莫（区）组成。地方事务由德莫大会和德莫长处理。
localAdmin.en: Attica was divided into ten tribes (phylai), each composed of several demes. Local affairs were handled by deme assemblies and demarchs.

legalSystem.zh: 以梭伦法典和克利斯提尼改革为基础的成文法体系，由陪审法庭审理案件。
legalSystem.en: A written-law system based on Solon's code and Cleisthenes' reforms, with cases tried by jury courts.

taxationSystem.zh: 公民按财产等级承担军事义务（代替税收），外邦人缴纳梅托伊科斯税。劳里翁银矿收入是重要公共财源。
taxationSystem.en: Citizens bore military obligations by property class (in lieu of tax); metics paid the metoikion. Laurion silver mine revenues were an important public revenue source.

religion.zh: 多神信仰（雅典娜为主保护神）
religion.en: Polytheism (Athena as patron)

culturalAchievements.zh: 雅典正处于文化爆发前夕——悲剧艺术正在诞生（泰斯庇斯、埃斯库罗斯），哲学和自然哲学蓬勃发展，黑绘和红绘陶器达到巅峰，帕特农神庙的时代即将到来。
culturalAchievements.en: Athens stood on the eve of a cultural explosion—tragedy was being born (Thespis, Aeschylus), philosophy and natural philosophy flourished, black-figure and red-figure pottery reached their peak, and the age of the Parthenon was approaching.

languageFamily.zh: 阿提卡-爱奥尼亚希腊语。
languageFamily.en: Attic-Ionic Greek.

philosophy.zh: 民主、理性探究和公民美德的理念正在形成，即将催生苏格拉底、柏拉图和亚里士多德的传统。
philosophy.en: Ideas of democracy, rational inquiry, and civic virtue were forming, soon to give rise to the tradition of Socrates, Plato, and Aristotle.

writingSystem.zh: 希腊字母（阿提卡变体）。
writingSystem.en: Greek alphabet (Attic variant).

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以劳里翁银矿、爱琴海贸易、陶器出口和橄榄油生产为支柱。比雷埃夫斯港正成为地中海最繁忙的商港之一。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on Laurion silver mines, Aegean trade, pottery export, and olive oil production. Piraeus was becoming one of the busiest commercial ports in the Mediterranean.

mainIndustries.zh: 银矿开采、海上贸易、陶器生产、橄榄油加工、造船
mainIndustries.en: Silver mining, maritime trade, pottery production, olive oil processing, shipbuilding

tradeGoods.zh: 银、陶器、橄榄油、葡萄酒、蜂蜜、大理石
tradeGoods.en: Silver, pottery, olive oil, wine, honey, marble

householdWealth.zh: 财富差距显著：富裕公民拥有大片田地、银矿份额和船只，贫穷公民依赖日工和少量土地。
householdWealth.en: Wealth differences were notable: rich citizens held large estates, mine shares, and ships; poor citizens depended on day labor and small plots.

economicSystem.zh: 以私有制和自由市场为基础的混合经济，银矿和港口贸易为公共收入支柱。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on private property and free markets, with silver mines and port trade as pillars of public revenue.

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备以银和谷物为主，存于卫城和神庙。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves mainly silver and grain, stored on the Acropolis and in temples.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 平衡军事扩张与公共建设，银矿收入为关键。
fiscalPolicy.en: Balancing military expansion with public works; silver mine income was critical.

description.zh: 装备齐全的公民战士，自备武装。
description.en: Well-equipped citizen warriors providing their own arms.

description.zh: 三列桨战舰船员（正在扩建中）。
description.en: Trireme crews (fleet under expansion).

commanderInChief.zh: 十将军之首
commanderInChief.en: Chief Strategos

technology.zh: 重装步兵方阵与三列桨战舰并重。马拉松战役即将证明雅典步兵的战斗力。
technology.en: Hoplite phalanx and trireme navy in tandem. The Battle of Marathon was about to prove Athenian infantry capability.

threats.zh: 波斯帝国入侵是迫在眉睫的威胁。
threats.en: Persian imperial invasion was the imminent threat.

populationDescription.zh: 含阿提卡全境约二十五万，其中约三万男性公民、数万外邦人和大量奴隶。
populationDescription.en: About 250,000 across Attica, including roughly 30,000 male citizens, tens of thousands of metics, and a large slave population.

socialClasses.zh: 富裕公民、中等公民、贫穷公民（泰特斯）、外邦人、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Wealthy citizens, middling citizens, poor citizens (thetes), metics, slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分爱奥尼亚城邦和普拉提亚有盟约关系。
allies.en: Had alliances with some Ionian cities and Plataea.

enemies.zh: 波斯帝国是即将到来的大敌。与埃吉纳和庇奥提亚有长期竞争。
enemies.en: The Persian Empire was the imminent great enemy. Had long-term rivalry with Aegina and Boeotia.

foreignPolicy.zh: 支持爱奥尼亚起义、准备抵抗波斯入侵、争夺爱琴海贸易主导权。
foreignPolicy.en: Supporting the Ionian Revolt, preparing to resist Persian invasion, and contesting Aegean trade dominance.

era.en: Eve of the Greek Classical Period

keyInnovations.zh: 民主制度、三列桨战舰、红绘陶器技术、自然哲学、悲剧艺术、城市规划。
keyInnovations.en: Democracy, trireme warships, red-figure pottery, natural philosophy, tragic drama, and urban planning.

strengths.zh: 民主制激发公民积极性，经济多元发达，文化创造力爆发在即，银矿财富充裕。
strengths.en: Democracy energized citizens, the economy was diversified, cultural creativity was about to explode, and silver wealth was ample.

weaknesses.zh: 社会阶层矛盾潜在，依赖粮食进口，陆军不及斯巴达。
weaknesses.en: Latent social-class tensions, dependence on grain imports, and army inferior to Sparta's.

outlook.zh: 波斯战争将把雅典推上希腊世界的领导地位，开启古典时代的黄金岁月。
outlook.en: The Persian Wars would propel Athens to leadership of the Greek world, opening the golden years of the Classical age.

description.zh: 雅典是克利斯提尼民主改革后正迅速崛起的希腊文化与商业中心。
description.en: Athens was a rapidly rising Greek cultural and commercial center following Cleisthenes' democratic reforms.

ideology.zh: 民主政治（德谟克拉提亚），公民大会至上，五百人议事会，部落改革，陶片放逐法
ideology.en: Demokratia, sovereignty of the Citizens' Assembly (Ekklesia), Council of Five Hundred, tribal reorganization, ostracism

headquarters.zh: 雅典城 / 普尼克斯山

relationship.zh: 民主派是公元前508年克利斯提尼改革后雅典的主导政治力量。通过打破旧贵族部落体系、建立以德莫（区）为基础的十部落制度，克利斯提尼将政治权力从少数贵族转移到广大公民手中。公民大会成为最高决策机关，五百人议事会主导日常政务。民主派获得了多数公民的支持，并通过陶片放逐法这一制度工具压制寡头势力的复辟企图。
relationship.en: The democratic faction was the dominant political force in Athens after Cleisthenes' reforms of 508 BCE. By breaking up the old aristocratic tribal system and establishing a new ten-tribe structure based on demes, Cleisthenes transferred political power from a narrow aristocracy to the broader citizenry. The Citizens' Assembly became the supreme decision-making body, with the Council of Five Hundred managing daily affairs. The democrats commanded majority citizen support and used ostracism as an institutional tool to suppress oligarchic restoration attempts.

description.zh: 雅典民主派代表了古代世界最革命性的政治实验。克利斯提尼在公元前508年的改革彻底重组了雅典政治结构：以一百三十九个德莫为基础单位，按地域而非血缘重新编制十个部落，使所有男性公民不论贫富均可参与政治决策。公民大会（厄克勒西亚）每年召开约四十次会议讨论国策，五百人议事会（布莱）由各部落抽签产生，陶片放逐法则防止任何个人权力膨胀。这一制度正激发雅典空前的政治活力和公民认同感。
description.en: The Athenian democratic faction represented the most revolutionary political experiment in the ancient world. Cleisthenes' reforms of 508 BCE thoroughly reorganized Athens' political structure: using 139 demes as basic units, he reassigned citizens into ten tribes by geography rather than kinship, enabling all male citizens regardless of wealth to participate in political decisions. The Citizens' Assembly (Ekklesia) met about forty times a year to deliberate policy; the Council of Five Hundred (Boulē) was selected by lot from each tribe; and ostracism prevented any individual from amassing excessive power. This system was generating unprecedented political vitality and civic identity.

role.zh: 公元前508年改革的设计者，建立了德莫部落制与陶片放逐法
role.en: Architect of the 508 BCE reforms, establishing the deme-tribe system and ostracism

leader.zh: 旧贵族家族（欧帕特里德）

ideology.zh: 寡头政治，贵族特权，传统血统门第优先，部分倾向亲波斯立场
ideology.en: Oligarchy, aristocratic privilege, precedence of traditional bloodlines, some pro-Persian sympathies

relationship.zh: 贵族/寡头派由雅典旧贵族（欧帕特里德）中不满民主化改革的势力组成。他们怀念庇西特拉图斯僭主时代或更早的贵族寡头统治，认为政治权力应由出身高贵、财富充裕的精英掌控。部分贵族与波斯帝国暗通款曲，希望借外力恢复寡头统治。他们在民主制度下仍通过财富、社会声望和宗教祭祀网络维持相当影响力。
relationship.en: The aristocratic-oligarchic faction consisted of old Athenian nobles (Eupatridae) discontented with democratic reforms. They longed for the era of Peisistratid tyranny or earlier aristocratic rule, believing political power should rest with well-born, wealthy elites. Some aristocrats maintained covert links with the Persian Empire, hoping to restore oligarchy through foreign intervention. Under democracy they still maintained considerable influence through wealth, social prestige, and religious-ceremonial networks.

description.zh: 雅典的贵族/寡头派代表了被民主化浪潮边缘化的旧精英势力。欧帕特里德（「出身优良者」）是雅典最古老的贵族阶层，曾在梭伦改革前垄断政治、宗教和司法权力。克利斯提尼的部落改革打破了他们的组织基础，但这些家族仍然掌握大量土地、矿产和商业财富。他们中的一些人对波斯帝国抱有同情，认为波斯可能支持他们恢复寡头政权。伊萨戈拉斯曾在公元前508年试图借助斯巴达推翻克利斯提尼改革但失败，反映了这一派系的顽强抵抗。
description.en: The aristocratic-oligarchic faction represented the old elite marginalized by the wave of democratization. The Eupatridae ('well-born') were Athens' oldest aristocratic class, having monopolized political, religious, and judicial power before Solon's reforms. Cleisthenes' tribal reorganization broke their organizational base, but these families still controlled vast landholdings, mining interests, and commercial wealth. Some harbored sympathies for Persia, believing it might support an oligarchic restoration. Isagoras had attempted to use Spartan intervention to overthrow Cleisthenes' reforms in 508 BCE but failed, reflecting this faction's stubborn resistance.

role.zh: 反民主派领袖，曾试图借斯巴达兵力推翻克利斯提尼改革
role.en: Anti-democratic leader who attempted to use Spartan forces to overthrow Cleisthenes' reforms

leader.zh: 庇西特拉图家族流亡者

ideology.zh: 民粹威权主义——以僭主个人权力为基础，绕过贵族与民主制度直接诉诸民众支持，承诺公共建设与底层福利
ideology.en: Populist authoritarianism — basing power on the tyrant's personal authority, bypassing aristocratic and democratic institutions to appeal directly to popular support, promising public works and lower-class welfare

headquarters.zh: 流亡地（波斯宫廷与北方殖民地）
headquarters.en: Exile locations (Persian court and northern colonies)

relationship.zh: 庇西特拉图僭主家族于公元前510年被推翻后流亡海外。希庇亚斯逃往波斯宫廷，企图借波斯军事力量重返雅典。虽然克利斯提尼的民主改革已使僭主制度失去了制度基础，但庇西特拉图家族在部分底层民众中仍有怀旧式的支持，代表了民主制度面临的一种潜在威胁。
relationship.en: The Peisistratid tyrant family was overthrown in 510 BCE and fled into exile. Hippias took refuge at the Persian court, seeking to use Persian military power to return to Athens. Though Cleisthenes' democratic reforms had eliminated the institutional basis for tyranny, the Peisistratids still retained nostalgic support among some lower-class citizens, representing a latent threat to the democratic order.

description.zh: 庇西特拉图僭主制在雅典持续了约半个世纪（前561-前510年），是雅典从贵族寡头制向民主制过渡的关键阶段。庇西特拉图本人虽以非法手段夺权，却实行了许多有利于普通民众的政策——重新分配土地、资助公共建设、推动文化繁荣、举办泛雅典娜节和戏剧竞赛。这些政策使僭主在底层民众中获得了相当好感。庇西特拉图之子希庇亚斯则在兄弟希帕库斯被暗杀后变得暴虐多疑，最终在斯巴达干预下被驱逐。希庇亚斯流亡至波斯大流士宫廷，积极游说波斯军队帮助他复辟。他在马拉松战役（前490年）时甚至亲随波斯军队登陆阿提卡。虽然庇西特拉图家族的复辟企图终告失败，但僭主制的记忆深刻影响了雅典民主政治——陶片放逐法的设立正是为了防止新的僭主出现。在公元前500年，这一威胁仍被雅典人视为现实存在的危险。
description.en: The Peisistratid tyranny had lasted about half a century in Athens (561-510 BCE) and was a critical phase in the transition from aristocratic oligarchy to democracy. Peisistratos himself, though seizing power illegally, implemented many policies favorable to common citizens — redistributing land, funding public construction, promoting cultural flourishing, and organizing the Panathenaia and dramatic competitions. These policies earned the tyrant considerable goodwill among the lower classes. His son Hippias became suspicious and cruel after the assassination of his brother Hipparchus, and was ultimately expelled with Spartan intervention. Hippias fled to the court of Darius and actively lobbied for Persian military assistance to restore him. He even accompanied the Persian invasion force that landed at Marathon in 490 BCE. Though the Peisistratid restoration ultimately failed, the memory of tyranny profoundly shaped Athenian democratic politics — the institution of ostracism was created specifically to prevent new tyrants from arising. In 500 BCE, this threat was still perceived as a real and present danger by the Athenians.

role.zh: 庇西特拉图之子，前雅典僭主，正在波斯宫廷谋求复辟
role.en: Son of Peisistratos, former tyrant of Athens, seeking restoration from the Persian court

name.zh: 庇西特拉图家族支持者

role.zh: 留在雅典的僭主家族旧部和底层支持者，暗中维系对旧政权的忠诚
role.en: Former retainers and lower-class supporters of the tyrant family remaining in Athens, covertly maintaining loyalty to the old regime

governmentForm.zh: 由阿尔吉德王朝统治的世袭君主国，国王权力受贵族和伙伴骑兵制约。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchy under the Argead dynasty, with royal power checked by aristocratic companions and cavalry.

socialStructure.zh: 王室和贵族伙伴（赫泰罗伊）构成上层，自由农牧民居中，附属民居下。马其顿人被南方希腊人视为半蛮族。
socialStructure.en: The royal house and noble companions (hetairoi) formed the top; free farmers and herders the middle; dependents the bottom. Macedonians were viewed by southern Greeks as semi-barbarian.

rulingClass.zh: 阿尔吉德王族和伙伴贵族。
rulingClass.en: The Argead royals and companion nobility.

succession.zh: 在阿尔吉德家族内世袭，但需军队认可。
succession.en: Hereditary within the Argead family, but requiring army endorsement.

structure.zh: 国王拥有军事指挥权和司法权，但重大决策需咨询伙伴贵族。军队大会在紧急时刻具有影响力。
structure.en: The king held military command and judicial power but consulted companion nobles on major decisions. The army assembly had influence in emergencies.

function.zh: 管理骑兵部队、王室庄园和军事动员。
function.en: Managed cavalry forces, royal estates, and military mobilization.

function.zh: 管理祭祀、外交使节和与希腊世界的关系。
function.en: Handled ritual, diplomatic missions, and relations with the Greek world.

localAdmin.zh: 各地区由贵族伙伴代管，边疆地区半自治。
localAdmin.en: Regions were overseen by noble companions; frontier areas were semi-autonomous.

legalSystem.zh: 王室司法为主，辅以部族习惯法。
legalSystem.en: Royal justice was primary, supplemented by tribal custom.

taxationSystem.zh: 以农牧贡赋和矿业利润为主。
taxationSystem.en: Based mainly on agricultural tribute and mining profits.

religion.zh: 多神信仰（奥林匹斯诸神与地方神灵）
religion.en: Polytheism (Olympian and local gods)

culturalAchievements.zh: 亚历山大一世积极推进希腊化，参加奥林匹克运动会并资助希腊诗人。马其顿正从边缘逐步融入希腊文化圈。
culturalAchievements.en: Alexander I actively pursued Hellenization, competed at Olympia, and patronized Greek poets. Macedon was gradually integrating into the Greek cultural sphere from its periphery.

languageFamily.zh: 古马其顿语（可能为希腊语方言或近缘语言）。
languageFamily.en: Ancient Macedonian (possibly a Greek dialect or closely related language).

philosophy.zh: 吸收希腊文化但保留本土传统。重视勇武和宴饮。
philosophy.en: Absorbed Greek culture while retaining native traditions. Valued martial prowess and feasting.

writingSystem.zh: 使用希腊字母。
writingSystem.en: Greek alphabet in use.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农牧业和木材出口为基础，矿业潜力巨大但尚未充分开发。
gdpDescription.en: Based on farming, herding, and timber export, with great but not yet fully exploited mining potential.

mainIndustries.zh: 农牧业、木材、矿业、马匹繁育
mainIndustries.en: Farming, herding, timber, mining, horse breeding

tradeGoods.zh: 木材、谷物、马匹、金银矿产、畜产品
tradeGoods.en: Timber, grain, horses, gold-silver ore, pastoral products

householdWealth.zh: 贵族拥有大片庄园，普通农民耕作自有或国王分配的土地。
householdWealth.en: Nobles held large estates; ordinary farmers tilled their own or royally allocated land.

economicSystem.zh: 王室主导的混合经济，私有农业与王室采邑并存。
economicSystem.en: A royally-directed mixed economy with private farming alongside royal estates.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以银、谷物和木材储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as silver, grain, and timber reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以军事建设和希腊化为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Military building and Hellenization took priority.

description.zh: 贵族组成的精锐骑兵，马其顿军力核心。
description.en: Elite cavalry of nobles, the core of Macedonian military strength.

description.zh: 自由农民组成的步兵，装备尚不统一。
description.en: Infantry of free farmers, equipment not yet standardized.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王亚历山大一世
commanderInChief.en: King Alexander I

technology.zh: 骑兵战术为强项，步兵装备和训练落后于希腊城邦。
technology.en: Cavalry tactics were a strength; infantry equipment and training lagged behind Greek city-states.

threats.zh: 波斯帝国、色雷斯部族和伊利里亚人构成三面威胁。
threats.en: The Persian Empire, Thracian tribes, and Illyrians posed a three-sided threat.

populationDescription.zh: 约二十万，分布于马其顿平原和山地。
populationDescription.en: About 200,000, spread across the Macedonian plain and highlands.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与伙伴贵族、自由农民、附属民。
socialClasses.en: Royals and companion nobles, free farmers, dependents.

allies.zh: 在波斯与希腊之间保持微妙平衡。
allies.en: Maintained a delicate balance between Persia and Greece.

enemies.zh: 色雷斯部族和伊利里亚人为主要边境威胁。
enemies.en: Thracian tribes and Illyrians were the main border threats.

foreignPolicy.zh: 推进希腊化以获得南方承认，同时维护对波斯的独立性。
foreignPolicy.en: Pursued Hellenization for southern recognition while maintaining independence from Persia.

era.en: Late Iron Age of Northern Greece

keyInnovations.zh: 伙伴骑兵战术、王室赞助的文化引进、早期城市化。
keyInnovations.en: Companion cavalry tactics, royally-patronized cultural imports, and early urbanization.

strengths.zh: 骑兵精锐，王权较集中，木材和矿业资源丰富。
strengths.en: Elite cavalry, relatively centralized kingship, and rich timber and mineral resources.

weaknesses.zh: 步兵落后，贵族离心倾向，被南方希腊人视为蛮族。
weaknesses.en: Infantry lagged, nobles had centrifugal tendencies, and it was regarded as barbarous by southern Greeks.

outlook.zh: 潜力巨大——一个半世纪后将产生征服世界的亚历山大大帝。
outlook.en: Enormous potential—a century and a half later it would produce Alexander the Great, conqueror of the known world.

description.zh: 马其顿是北希腊的世袭君主国，正通过希腊化政策从边缘向希腊文化核心靠近。
description.en: Macedon was a hereditary kingdom of northern Greece, drawing closer to the Greek cultural core through Hellenization.

governmentForm.zh: 由色雷斯奥德里西亚王族为核心的部族王国联盟，正在从松散部族联盟向中央集权过渡。
governmentForm.en: A tribal kingdom confederation centered on the Odrysian royal house, transitioning from loose tribal alliance toward centralization.

socialStructure.zh: 王族和战士贵族占据上层，自由农牧民居中，依附民和俘获者居下。
socialStructure.en: Royals and warrior nobles held the top; free farmers and herders the middle; dependents and captives the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 奥德里西亚王族和色雷斯各部族的贵族战士。
rulingClass.en: The Odrysian royals and noble warriors of the Thracian tribes.

succession.zh: 在王族内世袭，但竞争激烈。
succession.en: Hereditary within the royal family, but with intense competition.

structure.zh: 色雷斯尚处于从部族联盟向王国过渡阶段。奥德里西亚王在战时和贡赋征收中权力较大，但地方首领保有高度自治。
structure.en: Thrace was still transitioning from tribal confederation to kingdom. The Odrysian king held stronger power in wartime and tribute collection, but local chiefs retained high autonomy.

function.zh: 征收贡赋和管理王室庄园。
function.en: Collected tribute and managed royal estates.

function.zh: 组织骑兵和部族征召。
function.en: Organized cavalry and tribal levies.

function.zh: 主持狄奥尼索斯崇拜等仪式和对外交涉。
function.en: Conducted Dionysian worship and other rituals and external dealings.

localAdmin.zh: 各部族地区由本地首领自治，在贡赋和军事上服从王权。
localAdmin.en: Tribal areas were self-governed by local chiefs, deferring to royal authority on tribute and military matters.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法为主，王权裁决重大案件。
legalSystem.en: Primarily custom; the king adjudicated major cases.

taxationSystem.zh: 以牲畜、金属、谷物和劳役形式征收贡赋，贵族赠礼补充王室收入。
taxationSystem.en: Tribute collected in livestock, metals, grain, and labor; noble gift-giving supplemented royal income.

religion.zh: 多神信仰（狄奥尼索斯崇拜突出）
religion.en: Polytheism (Dionysus worship prominent)

culturalAchievements.zh: 精美的金银器和墓葬文化（如帕纳久里什泰宝藏），色雷斯骑兵传统和狄奥尼索斯崇拜影响了整个希腊世界。
culturalAchievements.en: Fine gold and silver work and burial culture (e.g. the Panagyurishte Treasure), and Thracian cavalry tradition and Dionysus worship influenced the entire Greek world.

languageFamily.zh: 色雷斯语（印欧语系）。
languageFamily.en: Thracian (Indo-European family).

philosophy.zh: 以狄奥尼索斯神秘崇拜和灵魂不灭信念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on Dionysian mystery cult and belief in soul immortality.

writingSystem.zh: 使用希腊字母书写少量铭文。
writingSystem.en: Greek alphabet used for limited inscriptions.

gdpDescription.zh: 以农牧业为主，金银矿开采和对希腊的贸易日益重要。
gdpDescription.en: Based mainly on farming and herding, with gold-silver mining and trade with Greeks growing in importance.

mainIndustries.zh: 农牧、金银矿、马匹繁育、木材、葡萄酒
mainIndustries.en: Farming-herding, gold-silver mining, horse breeding, timber, wine

tradeGoods.zh: 金银、马匹、谷物、蜂蜜、木材、奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Gold, silver, horses, grain, honey, timber, slaves

name.zh: 银币（仿希腊）与实物交换

householdWealth.zh: 贵族极富，控制大片牧场和矿区。普通农民较贫困。
householdWealth.en: Nobles were extremely rich, controlling vast pastures and mines. Ordinary farmers were relatively poor.

economicSystem.zh: 王室-贵族主导的再分配经济与家庭自给农牧并行。
economicSystem.en: A royal-noble redistributive economy alongside household subsistence farming and herding.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以金银和谷物储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as gold, silver, and grain.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以军事和王权巩固为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Military and royal consolidation took priority.

description.zh: 贵族组成的精锐骑兵，以勇猛著称。
description.en: Elite cavalry of nobles, renowned for ferocity.

description.zh: 各部族征召的轻步兵和标枪手。
