description.en: Light infantry and javelinmen levied from the tribes.

commanderInChief.zh: 色雷斯王
commanderInChief.en: Thracian King

technology.zh: 骑兵和标枪轻步兵为传统强项。缺乏重装方阵但极善袭扰战。
technology.en: Cavalry and javelin light infantry were traditional strengths. Lacked heavy phalanx but excelled at raiding warfare.

threats.zh: 波斯帝国扩张、斯基泰人南下和马其顿的崛起。
threats.en: Persian expansion, Scythian southward movement, and Macedonian rise.

populationDescription.zh: 约五十万，分布于巴尔干半岛东南部广大地区。
populationDescription.en: About 500,000, spread across a large area of the southeastern Balkans.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族骑士、自由农牧民、依附民与俘虏。
socialClasses.en: Royals and noble horsemen, free farmers and herders, dependents and captives.

allies.zh: 与部分希腊城邦有贸易关系。
allies.en: Had trade ties with some Greek city-states.

enemies.zh: 波斯帝国和斯基泰人。
enemies.en: The Persian Empire and Scythians.

foreignPolicy.zh: 在波斯、希腊和斯基泰之间保持独立。
foreignPolicy.en: Maintained independence between Persia, Greece, and Scythia.

keyInnovations.zh: 金银冶金、骑兵战术、设防王陵。
keyInnovations.en: Gold-silver metallurgy, cavalry tactics, fortified royal tombs.

strengths.zh: 人口众多，骑兵精锐，金银资源丰富。
strengths.en: Large population, elite cavalry, rich gold-silver resources.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂，部族离心倾向严重，缺乏统一行政体系。
weaknesses.en: Politically fragmented, strong tribal centrifugal tendencies, lacking unified administration.

outlook.zh: 奥德里西亚王国即将统一大部分色雷斯，但会持续受到马其顿和后来罗马的压力。
outlook.en: The Odrysian kingdom was about to unify most of Thrace, but would face continued Macedonian and later Roman pressure.

description.zh: 色雷斯奥德里西亚是巴尔干东南部正在向集权过渡的部族王国联盟。
description.en: Odrysian Thrace was a tribal kingdom confederation in the southeastern Balkans transitioning toward centralization.

dynasty.zh: 叙拉古与西西里希腊城邦群

governmentForm.zh: 由叙拉古等希腊殖民城邦组成的城邦体系，各城邦政体不一（民主、寡头或僭主制）。
governmentForm.en: A city-state system of Greek colonies including Syracuse, with varied constitutions (democracy, oligarchy, or tyranny).

socialStructure.zh: 希腊殖民者构成公民阶层，原住民和外来人口从事手工业和农业，奴隶制发达。
socialStructure.en: Greek colonists formed the citizen class; indigenous and immigrant populations engaged in crafts and farming; slavery was well developed.

rulingClass.zh: 殖民者贵族和商业精英。
rulingClass.en: Colonist aristocrats and commercial elites.

succession.zh: 因城邦不同而异——选举、世袭或政变。
succession.en: Varied by city-state—election, hereditary, or coup.

structure.zh: 各城邦独立运行。叙拉古人口最多、军事最强，是西西里希腊世界的核心。
structure.en: Each city-state operated independently. Syracuse had the largest population and strongest military, forming the core of Greek Sicily.

function.zh: 管理公共事务、税收和司法。
function.en: Managed public affairs, taxation, and justice.

function.zh: 管理陆军和海军力量。
function.en: Managed land and naval forces.

function.zh: 管理主要神庙和宗教节日。
function.en: Administered major temples and religious festivals.

localAdmin.zh: 各城邦治理周边农业区域。
localAdmin.en: Each city-state governed surrounding agricultural territory.

legalSystem.zh: 希腊成文法传统。
legalSystem.en: Greek written-law traditions.

taxationSystem.zh: 关税、市场税和农业什一税。
taxationSystem.en: Customs, market taxes, and agricultural tithes.

religion.zh: 多神信仰（奥林匹斯诸神）
religion.en: Polytheism (Olympian gods)

culturalAchievements.zh: 叙拉古和阿格里根顿的神庙建筑宏伟壮观。西西里希腊诗歌和戏剧传统兴盛。农业技术先进，小麦产量冠绝地中海西部。
culturalAchievements.en: Temple architecture at Syracuse and Agrigentum was magnificent. Sicilian Greek poetry and drama traditions flourished. Advanced agriculture produced the finest wheat yield in the western Mediterranean.

languageFamily.zh: 多利安/爱奥尼亚希腊语。
languageFamily.en: Doric/Ionic Greek.

philosophy.zh: 受母邦文化影响的希腊哲学传统，恩培多克勒即将诞生于此。
philosophy.en: Greek philosophical tradition influenced by mother-city culture; Empedocles would soon be born here.

writingSystem.zh: 希腊字母。
writingSystem.en: Greek alphabet.

gdpDescription.zh: 以肥沃的农业（小麦出口重地）、海上贸易和手工业为支柱。
gdpDescription.en: Rested on fertile agriculture (a major wheat export region), maritime trade, and crafts.

mainIndustries.zh: 小麦种植、橄榄油与葡萄酒、海上贸易、陶器、神庙建筑
mainIndustries.en: Wheat cultivation, olive oil and wine, maritime trade, pottery, temple construction

tradeGoods.zh: 小麦、橄榄油、葡萄酒、陶器、硫磺
tradeGoods.en: Wheat, olive oil, wine, pottery, sulfur

householdWealth.zh: 城邦公民较富裕，大地产主尤其富有。原住民较贫穷。
householdWealth.en: City-state citizens were relatively wealthy, large landowners especially so. Indigenous people were poorer.

economicSystem.zh: 以城邦市场经济为核心。
economicSystem.en: Centered on city-state market economy.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以银和谷物储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as silver and grain.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以军事和神庙建设为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Military and temple construction took priority.

description.zh: 希腊公民重装步兵。
description.en: Greek citizen hoplites.

description.zh: 三列桨战舰船员。
description.en: Trireme crews.

description.zh: 贵族骑兵和原住民辅助部队。
description.en: Noble cavalry and indigenous auxiliaries.

commanderInChief.zh: 叙拉古将军
commanderInChief.en: Syracusan General

technology.zh: 方阵和三列桨战舰并用。
technology.en: Phalanx and trireme combination.

threats.zh: 迦太基是最大威胁，原住民抵抗也持续存在。
threats.en: Carthage was the greatest threat; indigenous resistance also persisted.

populationDescription.zh: 含希腊殖民者和原住民约五十万。
populationDescription.en: About 500,000 including Greek colonists and indigenous peoples.

socialClasses.zh: 希腊公民、外邦人、原住民、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Greek citizens, metics, indigenous peoples, slaves.

allies.zh: 与母邦科林斯保持密切关系。
allies.en: Maintained close ties with mother-city Corinth.

enemies.zh: 迦太基及其在西西里西部的盟友。
enemies.en: Carthage and its allies in western Sicily.

foreignPolicy.zh: 抵抗迦太基扩张，控制地中海西部贸易。
foreignPolicy.en: Resisting Carthaginian expansion and controlling western Mediterranean trade.

keyInnovations.zh: 宏伟神庙建筑、先进农业技术、三列桨战舰、城市规划。
keyInnovations.en: Magnificent temple architecture, advanced agriculture, trireme warships, urban planning.

strengths.zh: 农业极其肥沃，城邦经济发达，军事力量强大。
strengths.en: Extremely fertile agriculture, developed city-state economy, and strong military.

weaknesses.zh: 城邦间竞争激烈，与迦太基的冲突持续不断。
weaknesses.en: Intense inter-city rivalry and ongoing conflict with Carthage.

outlook.zh: 叙拉古将成为西地中海最强大的希腊城邦，但迦太基威胁长期存在。
outlook.en: Syracuse would become the most powerful Greek city-state in the western Mediterranean, though the Carthaginian threat would persist.

description.zh: 西西里希腊殖民城邦是以叙拉古为核心的地中海西部希腊世界繁荣中心。
description.en: Greek Sicily was a prosperous center of the Greek world in the western Mediterranean, with Syracuse at its core.

governmentForm.zh: 希腊殖民城邦王政，巴图斯王朝统治约公元前500年正面临改革压力。
governmentForm.en: A Greek colonial city-state monarchy; the Battiad dynasty circa 500 BCE was facing reform pressure.

socialStructure.zh: 希腊殖民者公民阶层、利比亚原住民和奴隶。
socialStructure.en: Greek colonist citizen class, indigenous Libyans, and slaves.

rulingClass.zh: 巴图斯王族和殖民者贵族。
rulingClass.en: The Battiad royal house and colonist aristocrats.

succession.zh: 在巴图斯家族内世袭。
succession.en: Hereditary within the Battiad family.

structure.zh: 王制正面临贵族和民众要求改革的压力。城邦运作仍以王权为核心。
structure.en: The monarchy was under pressure from nobles and commons for reform. The city-state still operated under royal authority.

function.zh: 管理税收、外交和公共事务。
function.en: Managed taxation, diplomacy, and public affairs.

function.zh: 管理阿波罗神庙及宗教节日。
function.en: Administered the Temple of Apollo and religious festivals.

localAdmin.zh: 城市及周边农业区域由王室官员管理。
localAdmin.en: City and surrounding farmland managed by royal officials.

legalSystem.zh: 希腊法律传统。
legalSystem.en: Greek legal traditions.

taxationSystem.zh: 农业税、贸易关税和西非草药（西尔菲翁）垄断利润。
taxationSystem.en: Agricultural tax, trade duties, and monopoly profits from silphium herb.

religion.zh: 多神信仰（阿波罗崇拜为核心）
religion.en: Polytheism (Apollo worship central)

culturalAchievements.zh: 以西尔菲翁草药贸易闻名地中海，阿波罗神庙是重要圣所。
culturalAchievements.en: Famous across the Mediterranean for silphium herb trade; the Temple of Apollo was an important sanctuary.

languageFamily.zh: 多利安希腊语。
languageFamily.en: Doric Greek.

philosophy.zh: 希腊殖民地的文化传统。
philosophy.en: Cultural traditions of a Greek colony.

writingSystem.zh: 希腊字母。
writingSystem.en: Greek alphabet.

gdpDescription.zh: 以西尔菲翁垄断贸易和农业为经济支柱。
gdpDescription.en: Economic pillars were the silphium monopoly trade and agriculture.

mainIndustries.zh: 西尔菲翁采收与出口、谷物种植、畜牧、海上贸易
mainIndustries.en: Silphium harvesting and export, grain cultivation, herding, maritime trade

tradeGoods.zh: 西尔菲翁、谷物、马匹、羊毛
tradeGoods.en: Silphium, grain, horses, wool

householdWealth.zh: 因西尔菲翁贸易而相当富裕。
householdWealth.en: Fairly wealthy due to silphium trade.

economicSystem.zh: 城邦市场经济与王室垄断并存。
economicSystem.en: City-state market economy combined with royal monopoly.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以银和西尔菲翁储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as silver and silphium reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 维持西尔菲翁垄断和军事防御。
fiscalPolicy.en: Maintain silphium monopoly and military defense.

description.zh: 希腊公民战士。
description.en: Greek citizen warriors.

description.zh: 当地利比亚部族提供的骑兵。
description.en: Cavalry provided by local Libyan tribes.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王
commanderInChief.en: King

technology.zh: 希腊方阵步兵与利比亚骑兵结合。
technology.en: Greek phalanx infantry combined with Libyan cavalry.

threats.zh: 利比亚部族和波斯帝国扩张。
threats.en: Libyan tribes and Persian imperial expansion.

populationDescription.zh: 约十万，含希腊殖民者和利比亚原住民。
populationDescription.en: About 100,000, including Greek colonists and indigenous Libyans.

socialClasses.zh: 希腊公民、利比亚人、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Greek citizens, Libyans, slaves.

allies.zh: 与母邦锡拉和埃及保持联系。
allies.en: Maintained ties with mother-city Thera and Egypt.

enemies.zh: 利比亚部族和迦太基。
enemies.en: Libyan tribes and Carthage.

foreignPolicy.zh: 维持独立和西尔菲翁贸易垄断。
foreignPolicy.en: Maintain independence and silphium trade monopoly.

era.en: Greek Colonial Period of North Africa

keyInnovations.zh: 西尔菲翁栽培知识、希腊建筑和农业技术。
keyInnovations.en: Silphium cultivation knowledge, Greek architectural and agricultural techniques.

strengths.zh: 西尔菲翁垄断带来巨额财富，农业条件优越。
strengths.en: Silphium monopoly brought great wealth; agricultural conditions were excellent.

weaknesses.zh: 人口有限，依赖单一高价值商品，政治不稳定。
weaknesses.en: Limited population, dependence on a single high-value commodity, and political instability.

outlook.zh: 短期繁荣可维持，但巴图斯王朝将面临政治改革压力。
outlook.en: Short-term prosperity was maintainable, but the Battiad dynasty faced political reform pressure.

description.zh: 昔兰尼是北非利比亚海岸以西尔菲翁贸易闻名的富裕希腊殖民城邦。
description.en: Cyrene was a wealthy Greek colonial city-state on the Libyan coast of North Africa, famous for its silphium trade.

ruler.zh: 迦太基总督/努拉格首领

dynasty.zh: 迦太基治下与努拉格残余并存
dynasty.en: Under Carthaginian rule with Nuragic remnants

governmentForm.zh: 沿海地区已被迦太基控制，内陆仍由努拉格部族首领管辖。二元结构并存。
governmentForm.en: Coastal areas were controlled by Carthage while the interior remained under Nuragic tribal chiefs. A dual structure coexisted.

socialStructure.zh: 沿海：迦太基殖民者和商人占上层；内陆：努拉格首领和战士仍掌权。
socialStructure.en: Coast: Carthaginian colonists and merchants held the upper tier; interior: Nuragic chiefs and warriors still held power.

rulingClass.zh: 沿海为迦太基官员，内陆为努拉格首领。
rulingClass.en: Carthaginian officials on the coast, Nuragic chiefs in the interior.

succession.zh: 迦太基官员由母城任命，努拉格首领在家族内世袭。
succession.en: Carthaginian officials appointed by the mother city; Nuragic chiefs hereditary within families.

structure.zh: 二元结构：迦太基控制港口和沿海城镇，努拉格社会在内陆山区维持独立。两者时有冲突也有贸易往来。
structure.en: Dual structure: Carthage controlled ports and coastal towns; Nuragic society maintained independence in the mountainous interior. The two had both conflicts and trade.

function.zh: 管理港口贸易和殖民行政。
function.en: Managed port trade and colonial administration.

function.zh: 内陆石塔首领自治管理。
function.en: Interior tower-chief self-governance.

localAdmin.zh: 沿海由迦太基官员管理，内陆由努拉格首领自治。
localAdmin.en: Coast managed by Carthaginian officials; interior self-governed by Nuragic chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 沿海：迦太基法律；内陆：努拉格习惯法。
legalSystem.en: Coast: Carthaginian law; interior: Nuragic custom.

taxationSystem.zh: 迦太基区征收贸易关税和贡赋；努拉格区以传统方式运行。
taxationSystem.en: Carthaginian zones collected trade duties and tribute; Nuragic areas operated traditionally.

religion.zh: 混合信仰（迦太基巴力崇拜与努拉格水神崇拜并存）
religion.en: Mixed beliefs (Carthaginian Baal worship and Nuragic water cult coexisting)

culturalAchievements.zh: 努拉格石塔文化在内陆延续，迦太基带来了新的建筑和工艺传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Nuragic tower culture persisted in the interior; Carthage brought new architectural and craft traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 迦太基腓尼基语与古撒丁语并用。
languageFamily.en: Carthaginian Phoenician and Paleo-Sardinian in parallel use.

philosophy.zh: 两种文化传统并存。
philosophy.en: Two cultural traditions coexisting.

writingSystem.zh: 腓尼基字母（迦太基区）。
writingSystem.en: Phoenician alphabet (Carthaginian zones).

gdpDescription.zh: 沿海经济融入迦太基贸易网络，内陆维持农牧自给。矿业资源丰富。
gdpDescription.en: Coastal economy integrated into Carthaginian trade networks; interior maintained farming-herding self-sufficiency. Mineral resources were rich.

mainIndustries.zh: 矿业、农牧、海上贸易、金属冶铸
mainIndustries.en: Mining, farming-herding, maritime trade, metal casting

tradeGoods.zh: 矿石、谷物、金属制品、皮革
tradeGoods.en: Ore, grain, metalware, leather

name.zh: 迦太基银币与实物交换

householdWealth.zh: 沿海商人较富，内陆依传统标准。
householdWealth.en: Coastal merchants were richer; interior followed traditional standards.

economicSystem.zh: 二元经济——迦太基市场经济与努拉格自给经济并存。
economicSystem.en: Dual economy—Carthaginian market economy and Nuragic subsistence economy coexisting.

treasuryDescription.zh: 分散于迦太基港口仓储和内陆石塔。
treasuryDescription.en: Distributed across Carthaginian port storerooms and interior towers.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 迦太基以矿业开发和贸易利润为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Carthage prioritized mining development and trade profits.

description.zh: 迦太基派驻的常备军队。
description.en: Standing forces stationed by Carthage.

description.zh: 内陆努拉格首领的武装力量。
description.en: Armed forces of interior Nuragic chiefs.

commanderInChief.zh: 迦太基总督
commanderInChief.en: Carthaginian Governor

technology.zh: 迦太基带来先进武器和攻城技术，努拉格保留石塔防御传统。
technology.en: Carthage brought advanced weapons and siege techniques; Nuragic side retained tower defense tradition.

threats.zh: 内陆叛乱和希腊势力渗透。
threats.en: Interior revolts and Greek penetration.

populationDescription.zh: 约二十万，沿海为迦太基殖民者和混合人口，内陆为努拉格原住民。
populationDescription.en: About 200,000; coast held Carthaginian colonists and mixed populations, interior held Nuragic indigenous people.

socialClasses.zh: 迦太基殖民者、努拉格首领、农牧民、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Carthaginian colonists, Nuragic chiefs, farmers-herders, slaves.

allies.zh: 迦太基母城是最大后盾。
allies.en: Carthage was the primary backer.

enemies.zh: 努拉格抵抗势力和希腊竞争者。
enemies.en: Nuragic resistance and Greek competitors.

foreignPolicy.zh: 作为迦太基殖民帝国的一部分运作。
foreignPolicy.en: Operated as part of the Carthaginian colonial empire.

era.zh: 西地中海迦太基殖民时代
era.en: Carthaginian Colonial Period of the Western Mediterranean

keyInnovations.zh: 迦太基港口工程、腓尼基冶金、努拉格石塔传统延续。
keyInnovations.en: Carthaginian port engineering, Phoenician metallurgy, continued Nuragic tower tradition.

strengths.zh: 矿业资源丰富，迦太基贸易网络提供市场，内陆防御体系坚固。
strengths.en: Rich mineral resources, Carthaginian network providing markets, and solid interior defense systems.

weaknesses.zh: 内部分裂（迦太基vs努拉格），殖民管理成本高。
weaknesses.en: Internal division (Carthaginian vs. Nuragic) and high colonial management costs.

outlook.zh: 迦太基控制将逐步深化，但内陆努拉格文化将长期延续。
outlook.en: Carthaginian control would deepen, but interior Nuragic culture would persist long-term.

description.zh: 撒丁岛是迦太基沿海殖民与努拉格内陆自治并存的西地中海岛屿。
description.en: Sardinia was a Western Mediterranean island where Carthaginian coastal colonization coexisted with Nuragic interior autonomy.

governmentForm.zh: 喀尔巴阡山区和特兰西瓦尼亚高原上多个达契亚部族王国的松散联盟。
governmentForm.en: A loose alliance of several Dacian tribal kingdoms in the Carpathians and Transylvanian Plateau.

socialStructure.zh: 贵族战士（塔拉波伊）和祭司（扎尔莫克西斯信徒）构成上层，自由农民居中，依附民居下。
socialStructure.en: Noble warriors (taraboi) and priests (Zalmoxis followers) formed the upper tier; free farmers the middle; dependents the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 部族王和祭司阶层。
rulingClass.en: Tribal kings and the priestly class.

succession.zh: 在王族内世袭，祭司阶层具有影响力。
succession.en: Hereditary within the royal family; the priestly class held influence.

structure.zh: 多个部族王国独立运行，在战争和重大祭祀时联合。扎尔莫克西斯祭司在精神和政治上具有独特地位。
structure.en: Multiple tribal kingdoms operated independently, uniting for war and major rituals. Zalmoxis priests held a unique spiritual and political position.

function.zh: 组织山地防御和部族征召。
function.en: Organized mountain defense and tribal levies.

function.zh: 主持扎尔莫克西斯崇拜、天文历法和法律仲裁。
function.en: Conducted Zalmoxis worship, astronomical calendar, and legal arbitration.

function.zh: 管理金银矿和贡赋。
function.en: Managed gold-silver mines and tribute.

localAdmin.zh: 各山谷由地方王自治。
localAdmin.en: Each valley was self-governed by local kings.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法与祭司裁决并行。
legalSystem.en: Custom and priestly adjudication in parallel.

taxationSystem.zh: 以金银、谷物和劳役形式征收。
taxationSystem.en: Collected in gold, silver, grain, and labor.

religion.zh: 扎尔莫克西斯崇拜
religion.en: Zalmoxis worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 扎尔莫克西斯信仰是独特的灵魂不灭与禁欲主义宗教。金银冶金工艺精湛。山地要塞建筑发达。
culturalAchievements.en: Zalmoxis faith was a unique religion of soul immortality and asceticism. Gold-silver metallurgy was sophisticated. Mountain fortress construction was advanced.

languageFamily.zh: 达契亚语（印欧语系色雷斯-达契亚支）。
languageFamily.en: Dacian (Indo-European, Thraco-Dacian branch).

philosophy.zh: 扎尔莫克西斯教义强调灵魂不灭、道德约束和来世观念。
philosophy.en: Zalmoxis doctrine emphasized soul immortality, moral discipline, and afterlife concepts.

writingSystem.zh: 使用希腊字母的少量铭文。
writingSystem.en: Limited inscriptions using Greek alphabet.

gdpDescription.zh: 以农牧和金银矿业为基础。与希腊世界的贸易日益频繁。
gdpDescription.en: Based on farming-herding and gold-silver mining. Trade with the Greek world was growing.

mainIndustries.zh: 金银矿、农牧、铁器制造、木材
mainIndustries.en: Gold-silver mining, farming-herding, ironworking, timber

tradeGoods.zh: 金银、蜂蜜、蜂蜡、皮革、谷物
tradeGoods.en: Gold, silver, honey, beeswax, leather, grain

householdWealth.zh: 取决于田地、牲畜和矿产权。
householdWealth.en: Depended on fields, livestock, and mineral rights.

economicSystem.zh: 部族再分配与市场交换混合。
economicSystem.en: Tribal redistribution and market exchange combined.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以金银和谷物储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as gold, silver, and grain.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 军事防御和祭祀体系为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Military defense and ritual system took priority.

description.zh: 装备精良的贵族战士和首领亲兵。
description.en: Well-equipped noble warriors and chiefly retainers.

description.zh: 自由民组成的步兵，善于山地作战。
description.en: Infantry of free men, skilled in mountain warfare.

commanderInChief.zh: 联盟首席王
commanderInChief.en: Senior Confederate King

technology.zh: 铁器广泛使用，达契亚弯刀（法尔克斯）是特色武器。山地要塞防御坚固。
technology.en: Iron was widely used; the Dacian falx (curved sword) was a distinctive weapon. Mountain fortress defense was formidable.

threats.zh: 斯基泰人、凯尔特人东扩和马其顿的崛起。
threats.en: Scythians, Celtic eastward expansion, and Macedonian rise.

populationDescription.zh: 约五十万，分布于喀尔巴阡山脉及周边地区。
populationDescription.en: About 500,000, spread across the Carpathians and surrounding areas.

name.zh: 特兰西瓦尼亚矿区聚落

socialClasses.zh: 贵族战士、祭司、自由农民、依附民。
socialClasses.en: Noble warriors, priests, free farmers, dependents.

allies.zh: 与色雷斯部族有文化联系。
allies.en: Had cultural ties with Thracian tribes.

enemies.zh: 斯基泰人和凯尔特入侵者。
enemies.en: Scythians and Celtic invaders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 保卫山地家园，发展与希腊世界的贸易。
foreignPolicy.en: Defend the mountain homeland and develop trade with the Greek world.

keyInnovations.zh: 达契亚弯刀（法尔克斯）、山地要塞建筑、金银冶金。
keyInnovations.en: Dacian falx, mountain fortress construction, gold-silver metallurgy.

strengths.zh: 山地防御优势，金银资源，强悍战士传统，独特宗教凝聚力。
strengths.en: Mountain defensive advantages, gold-silver resources, strong warrior tradition, and unique religious cohesion.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂，缺乏统一指挥。
weaknesses.en: Politically fragmented, lacking unified command.

outlook.zh: 统一的潜力巨大，布雷比斯塔时代将证明这一点。
outlook.en: Enormous potential for unification, which the age of Burebista would prove.

description.zh: 达契亚部族王国是喀尔巴阡山区以扎尔莫克西斯信仰和金银矿业著称的部族联盟。
description.en: The Dacian tribal kingdoms were a Carpathian confederation known for Zalmoxis worship and gold-silver mining.

governmentForm.zh: 以费赞绿洲群为基础、由世袭王统治的撒哈拉王国。
governmentForm.en: A Saharan kingdom based on the Fezzan oasis cluster and ruled by a hereditary king.

socialStructure.zh: 王族和灌溉精英在上，自由绿洲农民居中，奴隶和依附劳动者（负责挖掘福加拉水渠）在下。
socialStructure.en: Royals and irrigation elites at the top; free oasis farmers in the middle; slaves and dependent laborers (digging foggara channels) at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 王族和控制灌溉与贸易的精英。
rulingClass.en: Royals and elites controlling irrigation and trade.

succession.zh: 在王族内世袭。
succession.en: Hereditary within the royal family.

structure.zh: 以加拉马为中心的集权化程度已显著提高，王权通过灌溉控制和贸易垄断得到强化。
structure.en: Centralization around Garama had significantly increased; royal power was strengthened through irrigation control and trade monopoly.

function.zh: 管理福加拉水渠网络的扩建和维护。
function.en: Managed expansion and maintenance of the foggara channel network.

function.zh: 管理跨撒哈拉贸易和与北非的外交。
function.en: Managed trans-Saharan trade and diplomacy with North Africa.

localAdmin.zh: 各绿洲由王任命的管理者治理。
localAdmin.en: Each oasis was governed by royally-appointed administrators.

legalSystem.zh: 王室司法为主。
legalSystem.en: Royal justice was primary.

taxationSystem.zh: 以农产品、劳役和贸易税为主。
taxationSystem.en: Based on agricultural produce, labor, and trade tax.

religion.zh: 多神信仰与祖先崇拜
religion.en: Polytheism and ancestor worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 福加拉灌溉网络已发展为撒哈拉最精密的工程体系之一。使用战车和骆驼进行跨撒哈拉贸易。
culturalAchievements.en: The foggara irrigation network had become one of the Sahara's most sophisticated engineering systems. Used chariots and camels for trans-Saharan trade.

languageFamily.zh: 柏柏尔语。
languageFamily.en: Berber.

philosophy.zh: 以祖先崇拜和水神信仰为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on ancestor worship and water-deity belief.

writingSystem.zh: 提非纳格字母。
writingSystem.en: Tifinagh alphabet.

gdpDescription.zh: 以灌溉农业和跨撒哈拉贸易为支柱。
gdpDescription.en: Rested on irrigation agriculture and trans-Saharan trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、畜牧、跨撒哈拉贸易、盐业、宝石中转
mainIndustries.en: Irrigation farming, herding, trans-Saharan trade, salt, gemstone transit

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、椰枣、宝石、黄金中转、牲畜
tradeGoods.en: Salt, dates, gemstones, gold transit, livestock

householdWealth.zh: 取决于灌溉田地和贸易参与。
householdWealth.en: Depended on irrigated fields and trade participation.

economicSystem.zh: 王室主导的绿洲经济。
economicSystem.en: A royally-directed oasis economy.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以谷物、盐和金属储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as grain, salt, and metal reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 灌溉维护和贸易通道保障为核心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Irrigation maintenance and trade-route security were central.

description.zh: 加拉马的常备武装。
description.en: Standing force at Garama.

description.zh: 战时征召的绿洲和沙漠战士。
description.en: Oasis and desert warriors called up in wartime.

commanderInChief.zh: 加拉曼特王
commanderInChief.en: Garamantian King

technology.zh: 使用马拉战车和骆驼，铁制武器，擅长沙漠战术。
technology.en: Used horse-drawn chariots and camels, iron weapons, and excelled at desert tactics.

threats.zh: 游牧袭击和北方大国的潜在干预。
threats.en: Nomadic raids and potential intervention by northern powers.

populationDescription.zh: 约十万，集中于费赞绿洲群。
populationDescription.en: About 100,000, concentrated in the Fezzan oasis cluster.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与灌溉精英、自由农民、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royals and irrigation elites, free farmers, slaves.

allies.zh: 与柏柏尔部族和南部黑非洲贸易伙伴。
allies.en: Berber tribes and southern sub-Saharan trade partners.

enemies.zh: 游牧劫掠者。
enemies.en: Nomadic raiders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 保障贸易通道安全和灌溉系统运行。
foreignPolicy.en: Securing trade routes and irrigation system operation.

keyInnovations.zh: 大规模福加拉灌溉网络、提非纳格文字、战车和骆驼运输。
keyInnovations.en: Large-scale foggara irrigation network, Tifinagh script, chariot and camel transport.

strengths.zh: 独一无二的撒哈拉灌溉技术，控制跨撒哈拉贸易要道。
strengths.en: Unique Saharan irrigation technology; control of key trans-Saharan trade routes.

weaknesses.zh: 人口有限，孤立于地中海文明中心。
weaknesses.en: Limited population; isolated from Mediterranean civilization centers.

outlook.zh: 将在未来几个世纪发展为更繁荣的撒哈拉王国。
outlook.en: Would develop into a more prosperous Saharan kingdom over the coming centuries.

description.zh: 加拉曼特王国是费赞绿洲以福加拉灌溉和跨撒哈拉贸易著称的新兴撒哈拉国家。
description.en: The Garamantian Kingdom was an emerging Saharan state in the Fezzan oases, distinguished by foggara irrigation and trans-Saharan trade.

governmentForm.zh: 以耶哈为中心的早期王国，融合南阿拉伯和本土非洲元素。
governmentForm.en: An early kingdom centered on Yeha, blending South Arabian and indigenous African elements.

socialStructure.zh: 王族和祭司精英构成上层，农民和工匠居中，依附者居下。文化融合了南阿拉伯（萨巴）和本土埃塞俄比亚传统。
socialStructure.en: Royals and priestly elites at the top; farmers and artisans in the middle; dependents below. Culture blended South Arabian (Sabaean) and indigenous Ethiopian traditions.

rulingClass.zh: 王族和神庙祭司。
rulingClass.en: Royals and temple priests.

succession.zh: 在王族内世袭。
succession.en: Hereditary within the royal family.

structure.zh: 以耶哈为中心，王权与神庙权力并行。南阿拉伯式行政和本土传统混合。
structure.en: Centered on Yeha, with royal and temple power running in parallel. Mixed South Arabian-style administration and indigenous traditions.

function.zh: 管理税收和地方事务。
function.en: Managed taxation and local affairs.

function.zh: 管理阿尔马卡神庙和宗教仪式。
function.en: Administered the Almaqah temple and religious ceremonies.

localAdmin.zh: 由地方首领在王权框架内管理。
localAdmin.en: Managed by local chiefs within the royal framework.

legalSystem.zh: 混合南阿拉伯和本土法律传统。
legalSystem.en: Mixed South Arabian and indigenous legal traditions.

taxationSystem.zh: 以农产品和贸易税为主。
taxationSystem.en: Based mainly on agricultural produce and trade tax.

religion.zh: 阿尔马卡月神崇拜（南阿拉伯式）
religion.en: Almaqah moon-god worship (South Arabian style)

culturalAchievements.zh: 耶哈的大神庙是非洲之角最早的纪念性建筑之一。
culturalAchievements.en: The great temple at Yeha was one of the earliest monumental buildings of the Horn of Africa.

languageFamily.zh: 盖埃兹语（南闪米特语）与本土库什语。
languageFamily.en: Ge'ez (South Semitic) and indigenous Cushitic languages.

philosophy.zh: 南阿拉伯宗教与本土信仰融合。
philosophy.en: Blended South Arabian religion with indigenous beliefs.

writingSystem.zh: 南阿拉伯文字（萨巴字母）。
writingSystem.en: South Arabian script (Sabaean alphabet).

gdpDescription.zh: 以灌溉农业和红海贸易为支柱。
gdpDescription.en: Rested on irrigation agriculture and Red Sea trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、乳香和没药贸易、铁器制造
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, herding, frankincense and myrrh trade, ironworking

tradeGoods.zh: 乳香、没药、象牙、兽皮、黄金
tradeGoods.en: Frankincense, myrrh, ivory, hides, gold

householdWealth.zh: 取决于田地和贸易参与。
householdWealth.en: Depended on fields and trade participation.

economicSystem.zh: 王室和神庙主导的混合经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy directed by royalty and temples.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以贸易品和谷物储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as trade goods and grain.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 神庙建设和贸易发展为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Temple construction and trade development took priority.

description.zh: 常备武装力量。
description.en: Standing armed force.

description.zh: 战时征召。
description.en: Called up in wartime.

commanderInChief.zh: 达木特王
commanderInChief.en: D'mt King

technology.zh: 铁制武器和简单防御工事。
technology.en: Iron weapons and simple fortifications.

threats.zh: 周边部族和南阿拉伯势力的竞争。
threats.en: Competition from surrounding tribes and South Arabian powers.

populationDescription.zh: 约二十万，分布于厄立特里亚和埃塞俄比亚北部高原。
populationDescription.en: About 200,000, spread across the highlands of Eritrea and northern Ethiopia.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与祭司、农民与工匠、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Royals and priests, farmers and artisans, dependents.

allies.zh: 与萨巴王国保持文化和贸易联系。
allies.en: Maintained cultural and trade ties with the Sabaean kingdom.

enemies.zh: 周边竞争部族。
enemies.en: Competing surrounding tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 维持红海贸易通道和南阿拉伯联系。
foreignPolicy.en: Maintain Red Sea trade access and South Arabian connections.

era.zh: 非洲之角铁器时代早期
era.en: Early Iron Age of the Horn of Africa

keyInnovations.zh: 南阿拉伯式石造建筑、盖埃兹文字、灌溉农业。
keyInnovations.en: South Arabian-style stone architecture, Ge'ez script, and irrigation agriculture.

strengths.zh: 独特的文化融合，红海贸易通道，耶哈神庙的宗教号召力。
strengths.en: Unique cultural fusion, Red Sea trade access, and the religious appeal of the Yeha temple.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合有限，依赖外部贸易联系。
weaknesses.en: Limited political integration; dependent on external trade connections.

outlook.zh: 达木特的传统将为后来的阿克苏姆帝国奠定基础。
outlook.en: D'mt's traditions would lay the foundation for the later Aksumite Empire.

description.zh: 达木特王国是非洲之角以耶哈神庙和南阿拉伯-非洲文化融合著称的早期王国。
description.en: The D'mt Kingdom was an early state in the Horn of Africa distinguished by the Yeha temple and South Arabian-African cultural fusion.

governmentForm.zh: 由喀麦隆-尼日利亚边境地区向外扩散的农耕部族组成的松散社会，以村落自治为主。
governmentForm.en: A dispersed society of farming tribes expanding outward from the Cameroon-Nigeria border area, mainly self-governing at the village level.

socialStructure.zh: 以血缘和村落为基本单位，首领、长老和铁匠/祭司拥有较高地位。铁器使用正在带来生产力变革。
socialStructure.en: Based on kinship and village units, with chiefs, elders, and smiths/ritual specialists holding higher status. Iron use was bringing a productivity revolution.

rulingClass.zh: 村落首领和仪式专家。
rulingClass.en: Village chiefs and ritual specialists.

succession.zh: 在首领家系内传承。
succession.en: Passed within chiefly lineages.

structure.zh: 没有超越村落的中央政权。各村落独立运行，通过亲缘网络和迁徙方向松散联系。
structure.en: No centralized authority beyond the village. Villages operated independently, loosely connected through kin networks and migration patterns.

function.zh: 处理村务、纠纷和迁徙决策。
function.en: Handled village affairs, disputes, and migration decisions.

function.zh: 管理冶铁和宗教仪式。
function.en: Managed ironworking and religious ceremonies.

localAdmin.zh: 村落自治。
localAdmin.en: Village self-governance.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法与长老裁决。
legalSystem.en: Custom and elder adjudication.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，互助和贡献为基础。
taxationSystem.en: No formal taxation; based on mutual aid and contributions.

religion.zh: 祖先崇拜与万物有灵
religion.en: Ancestor worship and animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 铁器冶炼技术的独立发展和传播是其最深远的成就，将彻底改变撒哈拉以南非洲的人口和文化格局。
culturalAchievements.en: The independent development and spread of iron-smelting technology was its most far-reaching achievement, destined to transform the demographic and cultural landscape of sub-Saharan Africa.

languageFamily.zh: 原始班图语（尼日尔-刚果语系）。
languageFamily.en: Proto-Bantu (Niger-Congo family).

philosophy.zh: 以祖先崇拜、自然灵力和社区和谐为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on ancestor worship, nature spirits, and community harmony.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统。
writingSystem.en: No writing system.

gdpDescription.zh: 以刀耕火种农业和铁器生产为基础的自给经济。铁器正在大幅提高农业生产力。
gdpDescription.en: A subsistence economy based on slash-and-burn agriculture and iron production. Iron tools were dramatically increasing agricultural productivity.

mainIndustries.zh: 刀耕火种农业、冶铁、渔业、采集狩猎
mainIndustries.en: Slash-and-burn farming, ironworking, fishing, gathering-hunting

tradeGoods.zh: 铁器、棕榈油、薯芋、陶器
tradeGoods.en: Iron tools, palm oil, yams, pottery

householdWealth.zh: 取决于田地大小和铁器拥有量。
householdWealth.en: Depended on field size and iron-tool possession.

economicSystem.zh: 村落自给经济为主。
economicSystem.en: Mainly village subsistence economy.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以谷物、铁器和牲畜形式储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as grain, iron tools, and livestock.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以确保食物安全和冶铁持续为核心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Centered on ensuring food security and sustaining ironworking.

description.zh: 各村落最有经验的武装男子。
description.en: The most experienced armed men from each village.

description.zh: 战时征召的壮年男子。
description.en: Able-bodied men called up in wartime.

commanderInChief.zh: 区域首领联合
commanderInChief.en: Joint regional chiefs

technology.zh: 铁制矛和斧是主要武器，利用森林地形进行防御。
technology.en: Iron spears and axes were main weapons; forest terrain was used for defense.

threats.zh: 与原住民狩猎采集者的冲突以及部族间竞争。
threats.en: Conflict with indigenous hunter-gatherers and inter-tribal competition.

populationDescription.zh: 约一百万，从喀麦隆-尼日利亚起源地向东、南方向扩散。人口增长推动了持续迁徙。
populationDescription.en: About one million, expanding eastward and southward from the Cameroon-Nigeria homeland. Population growth drove continuous migration.

socialClasses.zh: 村落首领与铁匠精英、普通农民、新定居者。
socialClasses.en: Village chiefs and smith elites, ordinary farmers, new settlers.

allies.zh: 亲缘部族之间保持合作。
allies.en: Cooperation maintained among kindred tribes.

enemies.zh: 与被迁入地区的原住民可能发生冲突。
enemies.en: Potential conflict with indigenous peoples of receiving areas.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以寻找新耕地和建立新村落为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on finding new farmland and establishing new villages.

era.en: Early Iron Age of West Africa

keyInnovations.zh: 独立发展的铁器冶炼技术、刀耕火种农业、班图语的扩散。
keyInnovations.en: Independently developed iron-smelting technology, slash-and-burn agriculture, and the spread of Bantu languages.

strengths.zh: 铁器技术优势，人口增长迅速，农业生产力高于周边采集社会。
strengths.en: Iron-technology advantage, rapid population growth, and higher agricultural productivity than surrounding foraging societies.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分散，缺乏任何统一组织。
weaknesses.en: Extremely politically dispersed, lacking any unified organization.

outlook.zh: 班图扩张将是人类历史上最大规模的农业人口迁徙之一，彻底重塑撒哈拉以南非洲。
outlook.en: The Bantu expansion would become one of the largest agricultural population migrations in human history, fundamentally reshaping sub-Saharan Africa.

description.zh: 班图早期扩张代表了以铁器农业为驱动的人类史上最大规模迁徙之一的起始阶段。
description.en: The Early Bantu Expansion represented the initial phase of one of history's largest migrations, driven by iron-age agriculture.

governmentForm.zh: 以“大人物”制度为核心的分散部族社会。各山谷独立运作，领导者通过个人威望和财富再分配获得地位。
governmentForm.en: A dispersed tribal society centered on the 'Big Man' system. Each valley operated independently, with leaders gaining status through personal prestige and wealth redistribution.

socialStructure.zh: 以氏族和山谷为基本单位。“大人物”通过组织盛宴、猪群交换和战争领导获得威望。社会相对平等，但仪式知识和猪群数量决定地位高低。
socialStructure.en: Organized by clan and valley. Big Men gained prestige through organizing feasts, pig exchanges, and war leadership. Society was relatively egalitarian, but ritual knowledge and pig herds determined status.

rulingClass.zh: “大人物“和仪式长老。”, “en“: “Big Men and ritual elders.

succession.zh: 不世袭，每代”大人物“须通过个人能力重新获得地位。
succession.en: Not hereditary; each generation's Big Man had to re-earn status through personal ability.

structure.zh: 没有中央政权，各山谷和氏族独立运行。”大人物“通过影响力而非强制力来领导。部族间的猪群交换仪式（如莫卡）是重要的政治活动。
structure.en: No central authority; each valley and clan operated independently. Big Men led through influence rather than coercion. Inter-tribal pig exchange ceremonies (such as moka) were important political events.

function.zh: 处理纠纷、婚姻安排和部族间关系。
function.en: Handled disputes, marriage arrangements, and inter-tribal relations.

function.zh: 管理园艺周期、猪群交换仪式和祖先祭祀。
function.en: Managed horticultural cycles, pig exchange ceremonies, and ancestor rituals.

localAdmin.zh: 各山谷由地方”大人物“和长老自治。
localAdmin.en: Each valley was self-governed by local Big Men and elders.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法，纠纷通过赔偿（猪群和贝壳）或仪式性战争解决。
legalSystem.en: Customary law; disputes resolved through compensation (pigs and shells) or ritual warfare.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，以互惠义务和仪式供奉为基础。
taxationSystem.en: No formal taxation; based on reciprocal obligations and ritual offerings.

religion.zh: 祖先崇拜与万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Ancestor worship and animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 新几内亚高地是世界上最早的独立农业起源地之一，约9000年前就开始了沼泽排水和芋头种植。复杂的园艺梯田和灌溉系统在高地广泛分布。
culturalAchievements.en: The New Guinea highlands were one of the world's earliest independent centers of agriculture, with swamp drainage and taro cultivation beginning about 9,000 years ago. Complex horticultural terraces and irrigation systems were widespread across the highlands.

languageFamily.zh: 跨新几内亚语系为主，另有多个独立语系
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Trans-New Guinea languages, alongside multiple independent language families

philosophy.zh: 以祖先灵力、自然精灵和互惠义务为核心的宇宙观
philosophy.en: A cosmology centered on ancestral power, nature spirits, and reciprocal obligations

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，以口传传统和仪式符号传递知识
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge transmitted through oral tradition and ritual symbols

gdpDescription.zh: 以沼泽排水园艺农业（芋头、山药、甘蔗）和猪群饲养为基础。猪群是最重要的财富形式和交换媒介。
gdpDescription.en: Based on swamp drainage horticulture (taro, yam, sugarcane) and pig husbandry. Pig herds were the most important form of wealth and exchange medium.

mainIndustries.zh: 园艺农业、猪群饲养、石器制造、盐业、羽毛和贝壳加工
mainIndustries.en: Horticultural farming, pig husbandry, stone tool making, salt production, feather and shell working

tradeGoods.zh: 猪、芋头、盐块、石斧、鸟羽装饰、贝壳
tradeGoods.en: Pigs, taro, salt blocks, stone axes, bird feather ornaments, shells

householdWealth.zh: 以猪群数量、园艺地面积和贝壳饰品为主要衡量标准。
householdWealth.en: Measured mainly by pig herd size, garden plot area, and shell ornaments.

economicSystem.zh: 以猪群交换和互惠义务为核心的自给农业经济。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence agricultural economy centered on pig exchange and reciprocal obligations.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以猪群、贝壳和盐块形式储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as pig herds, shells, and salt blocks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持猪群交换网络和仪式义务为核心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining pig exchange networks and ritual obligations.

description.zh: 各氏族的勇士和”大人物“的追随者。
description.en: Clan warriors and Big Man followers.

description.zh: 部族冲突时征召的壮年男子。
description.en: Able-bodied men called up during tribal conflicts.

commanderInChief.zh: 战时”大人物“联合
commanderInChief.en: Wartime Big Man coalition

technology.zh: 以石制和木制武器为主（石斧、木矛、弓箭、盾牌）。仪式性战争较为常见，伤亡通常有限。
technology.en: Mainly stone and wood weapons (stone axes, wooden spears, bows, shields). Ritual warfare was common, with casualties usually limited.

threats.zh: 山谷间的部族冲突和资源争夺。
threats.en: Inter-valley tribal conflicts and resource competition.

populationDescription.zh: 约二十万人分布于新几内亚高地各山谷。人口密度因农业发展而相对较高。
populationDescription.en: About 200,000 people distributed across the New Guinea highland valleys. Population density was relatively high due to agricultural development.

socialClasses.zh: “大人物”、仪式长老、普通农民、妇女和儿童。”, “en“: “Big Men, ritual elders, ordinary farmers, women and children.

allies.zh: 通过猪群交换仪式与相邻山谷保持联盟关系。
allies.en: Maintained alliances with neighboring valleys through pig exchange ceremonies.

enemies.zh: 与竞争山谷的部族间存在周期性仪式战争。
enemies.en: Periodic ritual warfare with competing valley tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维持猪群交换网络和领土完整为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining pig exchange networks and territorial integrity.

era.zh: 新几内亚高地新石器农业时代
era.en: New Guinea Highland Neolithic Agricultural Era

keyInnovations.zh: 沼泽排水技术、芋头和山药栽培、石斧制造、猪群驯化管理。
keyInnovations.en: Swamp drainage technology, taro and yam cultivation, stone axe manufacturing, and pig domestication management.

strengths.zh: 世界最早的独立农业传统之一，高效的高地园艺系统，密集的社会交换网络。
strengths.en: One of the world's earliest independent agricultural traditions, efficient highland horticultural systems, and dense social exchange networks.

weaknesses.zh: 极度政治分散，缺乏统一组织，山地地形限制交流，无金属和文字。
weaknesses.en: Extremely politically fragmented, lacking unified organization, mountain terrain limiting communication, and no metals or writing.

outlook.zh: 高地农业社会将在相对隔离中持续发展数千年，保持独特的“大人物”制度和园艺文化。
outlook.en: Highland agricultural society would continue developing in relative isolation for thousands of years, maintaining its distinctive Big Man system and horticultural culture.

description.zh: 巴布亚高地农耕社会是公元前500年新几内亚高地以沼泽排水农业和“大人物”制度为特征的部族社会，拥有世界上最古老的独立农业传统之一。
description.en: The Papuan Highland Farmers were a tribal society in the New Guinea highlands around 500 BCE, characterized by swamp drainage agriculture and the Big Man system, possessing one of the world's oldest independent agricultural traditions.

name.zh: 晚期拉皮塔/原始波利尼西亚
晚期拉皮塔-原始波利尼西亚

governmentForm.zh: 以汤加和萨摩亚为中心的早期酋邦社会，拉皮塔文化正在向波利尼西亚文化转化。世袭酋长制度正在形成。
governmentForm.en: An early chiefdom society centered on Tonga and Samoa, with Lapita culture transforming into Polynesian culture. Hereditary chieftainship was forming.

socialStructure.zh: 社会等级化程度逐渐增加，世袭酋长家族开始出现。长子继承制和等级制开始取代拉皮塔时代较为平等的社会结构。仪式专家和航海家享有较高地位。
socialStructure.en: Social stratification was gradually increasing, with hereditary chiefly families beginning to emerge. Primogeniture and ranked systems were beginning to replace the more egalitarian Lapita-era social structure. Ritual specialists and navigators held high status.

rulingClass.zh: 世袭酋长家族和仪式专家。
rulingClass.en: Hereditary chiefly families and ritual specialists.

succession.zh: 长子或高等级继承人优先，正在形成世袭制。
succession.en: Firstborn or high-ranking heir preferred; hereditary succession was forming.

structure.zh: 各岛屿由世袭酋长统治，汤加和萨摩亚的酋长家族逐渐形成更复杂的政治体系。岛屿间通过航海联系和婚姻联盟保持关系。
structure.en: Each island was ruled by hereditary chiefs, with Tongan and Samoan chiefly families gradually forming more complex political systems. Inter-island relations were maintained through maritime connections and marriage alliances.

function.zh: 处理岛屿事务、资源分配和对外关系。
function.en: Handled island affairs, resource allocation, and external relations.

function.zh: 维护祖先祭祀、航海传统和社会等级仪式。
function.en: Maintained ancestor worship, navigation traditions, and social ranking ceremonies.

localAdmin.zh: 各岛屿由世袭酋长家族管理。
localAdmin.en: Each island was managed by hereditary chiefly families.

legalSystem.zh: 酋长裁决和习惯法。禁忌（塔布）体系逐渐成为社会控制的核心机制。
legalSystem.en: Chiefly adjudication and customary law. The taboo (tabu) system was gradually becoming the core mechanism of social control.

taxationSystem.zh: 以向酋长贡献首获食物和仪式供品为基础。
taxationSystem.en: Based on contributing first fruits and ritual offerings to the chief.

religion.zh: 南岛祖先崇拜与原始波利尼西亚多神信仰
religion.en: Austronesian ancestor worship and Proto-Polynesian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 拉皮塔陶器传统正在退化，但航海技术和社会组织日益复杂。汤加和萨摩亚的口传传统正在形成波利尼西亚神话体系的基础。塔布（禁忌）制度开始成为社会核心规范。
culturalAchievements.en: The Lapita pottery tradition was declining, but navigation technology and social organization were growing more complex. Tongan and Samoan oral traditions were forming the foundation of the Polynesian mythological system. The tabu (taboo) system was becoming a core social norm.

languageFamily.zh: 原始波利尼西亚语（南岛语系大洋洲语族）
languageFamily.en: Proto-Polynesian (Austronesian, Oceanic branch)

philosophy.zh: 以玛纳（神力）、塔布（禁忌）和祖先崇拜为核心的宇宙观正在形成
philosophy.en: A cosmology centered on mana (spiritual power), tabu (taboo), and ancestor veneration was forming

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，以口传谱系和航海歌谣传递知识
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge transmitted through oral genealogies and navigation chants

gdpDescription.zh: 以园艺农业（芋头、面包果、山药）、渔业和岛屿间贸易为核心。猪和鸡的饲养日益重要。
gdpDescription.en: Centered on horticultural agriculture (taro, breadfruit, yam), fishing, and inter-island trade. Pig and chicken husbandry was growing in importance.

mainIndustries.zh: 园艺农业、渔业、独木舟建造、树皮布制作、贝壳加工
mainIndustries.en: Horticultural farming, fishing, canoe building, bark cloth making, shell working

tradeGoods.zh: 芋头、鱼干、贝壳饰品、树皮布、独木舟
tradeGoods.en: Taro, dried fish, shell ornaments, bark cloth, canoes

householdWealth.zh: 以独木舟、猪群、园艺地和贝壳饰品为主要衡量标准。酋长家族拥有更多资源。
householdWealth.en: Measured mainly by canoes, pig herds, garden plots, and shell ornaments. Chiefly families held more resources.

economicSystem.zh: 以酋长再分配和互惠交换为核心的岛屿经济。
economicSystem.en: An island economy centered on chiefly redistribution and reciprocal exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以贝壳、树皮布和食物储备形式保存。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as shells, bark cloth, and food reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持航海能力和酋长权威为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Navigation capability and chiefly authority took priority.

description.zh: 酋长的常备武装。
description.en: The chief's standing armed force.

description.zh: 冲突时征召的壮年男子。
description.en: Able-bodied men called up during conflict.

commanderInChief.zh: 最高酋长
commanderInChief.en: Paramount Chief

technology.zh: 以石制和木制武器为主（木棍、石锤、矛），善于利用独木舟进行岛屿间突袭。
technology.en: Mainly stone and wood weapons (clubs, stone hammers, spears), skilled at using canoes for inter-island raids.

threats.zh: 岛屿间的酋邦竞争和外来入侵风险。
threats.en: Inter-island chiefdom competition and risk of external invasion.

populationDescription.zh: 约三万人口分布于汤加和萨摩亚群岛。人口在增长，正在形成更密集的岛屿社会。
populationDescription.en: About 30,000 people distributed across the Tonga and Samoa archipelagos. Population was growing, forming denser island societies.

socialClasses.zh: 世袭酋长家族、仪式专家、航海家、普通农渔民。
socialClasses.en: Hereditary chiefly families, ritual specialists, navigators, ordinary farmer-fishermen.

allies.zh: 汤加与萨摩亚之间通过婚姻联盟和贸易保持联系。
allies.en: Tonga and Samoa maintained connections through marriage alliances and trade.

enemies.zh: 岛屿间的酋邦竞争，但尚未形成大规模战争。
enemies.en: Inter-island chiefdom competition, but no large-scale warfare had yet developed.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维持岛屿间航海联系和酋长联盟为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining inter-island maritime connections and chiefly alliances.

era.zh: 太平洋拉皮塔晚期/原始波利尼西亚

keyInnovations.zh: 双体独木舟改进、星象导航术精化、树皮布制造、园艺集约化。
keyInnovations.en: Improved double-hulled canoes, refined stellar navigation, bark cloth manufacturing, and agricultural intensification.

strengths.zh: 卓越的航海传统，正在形成的等级社会组织，丰富的岛屿资源。
strengths.en: Outstanding navigational traditions, forming hierarchical social organization, and rich island resources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口规模小，陶器技术退化，岛屿资源有限。
weaknesses.en: Small population, declining pottery technology, and limited island resources.

outlook.zh: 原始波利尼西亚社会将在未来几个世纪发展为完整的波利尼西亚文化，并开始向东太平洋的伟大远航。
outlook.en: Proto-Polynesian society would develop into full Polynesian culture over the coming centuries, and begin the great voyages eastward into the Pacific.

description.zh: 晚期拉皮塔/原始波利尼西亚是公元前500年汤加-萨摩亚地区正在从拉皮塔文化向波利尼西亚文化转化的早期酋邦社会，塔布和玛纳等核心概念正在形成。
description.en: The Late Lapita / Proto-Polynesian was an early chiefdom society in the Tonga-Samoa region around 500 BCE, transforming from Lapita to Polynesian culture, with core concepts like tabu and mana taking shape.

governmentForm.zh: 以游猎营地为基础的分散部族社会，由经验丰富的猎人领袖与家族长者治理。
governmentForm.en: A dispersed tribal society based on mobile hunting camps, governed by experienced hunt leaders and family elders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会高度平等，以扩展家庭为基本单位，领导权依赖狩猎技能和分配公正性。
socialStructure.en: Highly egalitarian, organized around extended families, with leadership based on hunting skill and fair distribution.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 完善了北方森林生存技术，独木舟与雪鞋工艺更加成熟。
culturalAchievements.en: Refined boreal forest survival techniques, with more sophisticated canoe and snowshoe craftsmanship.

languageFamily.zh: 阿萨巴斯卡语系与阿尔冈昆语系
languageFamily.en: Athabaskan and Algonquian language families

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 以驯鹿狩猎、捕鱼、毛皮采集和季节性采集为经济基础。
gdpDescription.en: Economy based on caribou hunting, fishing, fur collecting, and seasonal foraging.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、捕鱼、毛皮加工、独木舟制造
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, fur processing, canoe building

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、肉干、桦皮、骨角工具
tradeGoods.en: Furs, dried meat, birch bark, bone and antler tools

householdWealth.zh: 财富体现在独木舟、雪鞋、猎具、毛皮储备上。
householdWealth.en: Wealth embodied in canoes, snowshoes, hunting gear, fur stores.

grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"主要为首领控制的食物与工具储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 冬季饥荒、猎物迁徙变化、领地摩擦。
threats.en: Winter famine, prey migration shifts, territorial friction.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于北美亚寒带森林的流动营地中。
populationDescription.en: Population dispersed in mobile camps across North American subarctic forests.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 改进的雪鞋设计、桦皮独木舟、高效陷阱技术。
keyInnovations.en: Improved snowshoe designs, birch-bark canoes, efficient trap technology.

strengths.zh: 极端环境适应力强，机动性高。
strengths.en: Excellent extreme environment adaptation, high mobility.

weaknesses.zh: 人口密度极低，政治组织松散。
weaknesses.en: Extremely low population density, loose political organization.

outlook.zh: 游猎生活方式将持续数千年。
outlook.en: The mobile hunting lifestyle would persist for millennia.

description.zh: 亚北极森林猎人是北美北部寒带森林中依赖驯鹿狩猎的游猎部族。
description.en: The Subarctic Forest Hunters were mobile hunting peoples in the boreal forests of northern North America, dependent on caribou hunting.

name.zh: 北美太平洋沿岸采集者
北美太平洋沿岸采集者

governmentForm.zh: 以村社和世袭首领为核心的复合采集社会。
governmentForm.en: Complex foraging societies centered on villages and hereditary leaders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为贵族、自由民和奴隶，夸富宴强化等级分配。
socialStructure.en: Divided into nobles, commoners, and slaves; potlatch feasts reinforced hierarchy.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 图腾柱雕刻和独木舟工艺进一步发展。
culturalAchievements.en: Totem pole carving and canoe craftsmanship further developed.

languageFamily.zh: 萨利希语系、瓦卡什语系等
languageFamily.en: Salishan, Wakashan, and other families

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 以丰富的鲑鱼捕捞和海洋资源为基础。
gdpDescription.en: Based on abundant salmon fishing and marine resources.

mainIndustries.zh: 鲑鱼捕捞、海产采集、木工、编织
mainIndustries.en: Salmon fishing, seafood gathering, woodworking, weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 鲑鱼干、鲸油、贝壳、雪松制品
tradeGoods.en: Dried salmon, whale oil, shells, cedar products

householdWealth.zh: 财富集中在独木舟、铜器、毯子上。
householdWealth.en: Wealth concentrated in canoes, coppers, and blankets.

populationDescription.zh: 人口沿太平洋西北海岸定居。
populationDescription.en: Population settled along the Pacific Northwest coast.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 高级木工、鲑鱼保存、大型独木舟。
keyInnovations.en: Advanced woodworking, salmon preservation, large canoes.

strengths.zh: 资源极丰富，文化复杂度高。
strengths.en: Extremely abundant resources, high cultural complexity.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散，各村社独立。
weaknesses.en: Politically fragmented, villages independent.

outlook.zh: 太平洋沿岸文化将持续繁荣。
outlook.en: Pacific coast cultures would continue to flourish.

description.zh: 北美太平洋沿岸采集者以鲑鱼捕捞为核心，拥有北美最复杂的非农业社会结构。
description.en: The Pacific Foragers centered on salmon fishing, with the most complex non-agricultural social structures in North America.

governmentForm.zh: 以季节性渔猎营地和半永久村社为基础的部族社会。
governmentForm.en: Tribal society based on seasonal fishing-hunting camps and semi-permanent villages.

socialStructure.zh: 社会相对平等，首领地位依赖捕鱼技能。
socialStructure.en: Relatively egalitarian, with leadership based on fishing skill.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 河鱼捕捞、编篮和岩画进一步发展。
culturalAchievements.en: River fishing, basket weaving, and rock art further developed.

languageFamily.zh: 萨利希语系内陆支、萨哈普廷语系
languageFamily.en: Interior Salishan, Sahaptian

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 部族间突袭、洪水。
threats.en: Intertribal raids, flooding.

populationDescription.zh: 人口沿亚马逊河网分布。
populationDescription.en: Population distributed along the Amazon river network.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 木薯毒素去除、独木舟、陶器烧制。
keyInnovations.en: Manioc detoxification, canoe building, pottery firing.

strengths.zh: 食物稳定，河流网络广泛。
strengths.en: Stable food supply, extensive river networks.

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，无金属技术。
weaknesses.en: Highly fragmented politically, no metal technology.

outlook.zh: 亚马逊农业社会将继续扩展。
outlook.en: Amazonian agricultural societies would continue expanding.

description.zh: 亚马逊木薯农民以木薯园圃农业和河流渔业为生计的部族群体。
description.en: Amazonian Manioc Farmers were tribal peoples living by manioc garden agriculture and river fishing.

governmentForm.zh: 以村社和区域仪式中心为核心的农业部族社会。
governmentForm.en: Agricultural tribal societies organized around villages and regional ritual centers.

socialStructure.zh: 等级分化进一步发展，仪式精英阶层形成。
socialStructure.en: Social differentiation further developed, with a ritual elite stratum forming.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 玉米农业更加集约化，早期城镇和仪式中心扩展。
culturalAchievements.en: Maize agriculture became more intensive, early towns and ritual centers expanded.

languageFamily.zh: 奥托-曼格语系、马雅语系等
languageFamily.en: Oto-Manguean, Mayan, and other families

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。

description.en: Patagonian Hunters were mobile hunting peoples of southernmost South America living by guanaco pursuit.

governmentForm.en: Small tribal societies centered on coastal shell midden settlements.

socialStructure.zh: 平等社会，以家庭为单位沿海岸生活。
